ANIMAL MASTERY.1: Puts any animal into a magical sleep.
ANIMAL MASTERY.2: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of Small size or smaller.
ANIMAL MASTERY.3: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any one animal species.
ANIMAL MASTERY.4: As Animal Sleep I, except up to 3 animals may be made to sleep.
ANIMAL MASTERY.5: Allows caster to control the actions of any one animal.
ANIMAL MASTERY.6: Caster can locate members of any one species of animal within the area of effect or he can find out what species are in the area.
ANIMAL MASTERY.7: All animals within 10' will act friendly toward caster. This does not control the animals.
ANIMAL MASTERY.8: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of Medium size or smaller or heal fatal damage in an animal of Small size or smaller.
ANIMAL MASTERY.9: Caster can understand and/or visualize the thoughts and emotions of any one animal.
ANIMAL MASTERY.10: Caster magically summons a 1st level animal from the surrounding area. The caster can specify the species of creature or simply a general type, but not a particular individual. If the animal can understand the caster (e.g., through use of Animal Tongues), the creature can be controlled through normal communications. Otherwise, control is maintained only through concentration by the caster. If the animal summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in the animal’s perception), the duration of this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the animal is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level.
ANIMAL MASTERY.11: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control up to three animals.
ANIMAL MASTERY.12: As Animal Summons I , except the caster chooses two of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).
ANIMAL MASTERY.13: Caster can “call” any one known, individual animal; if it is physically possible, the animal will come to the caster.
ANIMAL MASTERY.14: This spell will return any animal of no more than Small size from the dead. The animal cannot have been dead for more days than its own level plus the caster’s level. If this spell is ever cast unsuccessfully on an animal (i.e., if an attempt to cast this spell ends in spell failure), then that animal can never be revived. The animal will be at -100 to all actions for a number of days equal to 20 days per day dead.
ANIMAL MASTERY.15: When cast upon any domesticated animal, that animal will return to its “wild” state. The animal will have no difficulty in adapting to the harsh requirements of the wilderness (and have no desire to return to domestication). Most animals will be willing targets of this spell, but those with a strong tie to a particular human may prefer domestication.
ANIMAL MASTERY.16: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control up to five animals.
ANIMAL MASTERY.17: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of any size or heal fatal damage in an Animal of Medium size or smaller.
ANIMAL MASTERY.18: As Animal Summons III , except four options can be chosen.
ANIMAL MASTERY.19: As Animal Reviving I , except animal may be of up to Medium size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead.
ANIMAL MASTERY.20: As Animal Summons III , except nine options can be chosen.
ANIMAL MASTERY.25: As Animal Mastery I , except caster can control all animals of a given species within 10'R per level.
ANIMAL MASTERY.30: As Animal Reviving I , except animal may be of up to Big size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead for a Big animal, with no recovery period for smaller animals.
ANIMAL MASTERY.35: Caster may heal any damage in any animal of any size.
ANIMAL MASTERY.40: As Animal Reviving I , except animal may be of any size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead for a Large or larger animal, with no recovery period for smaller animals.
ANIMAL MASTERY.50: As Animal Summons III , except caster can select one option for every level of experience. Note: When allowed to choose more than one option on summoning spells, the same option may be chosen more than once. For example, with an Animal Summons III, two options are available. This means that if both options are used for the same thing, then three first-level creatures could be summoned, one third-level creature could be summoned, or one first-level creature could be summoned for four times as long. See Creature Law for more information on animals. For purposes of this list consider anything in the “Animals” chapter of Creature Law, and nothing outside that chapter, to be an animal. While Summons spells on this list are of type ‘U’, the creatures summoned are automatically considered willing to serve as described.   

ANTICIPATIONS.1: Caster becomes aware of his primary foe’s likely attack. If the caster parries, the DB gained from the parry is doubled.
ANTICIPATIONS.2: Caster becomes aware of his primary foe’s likely defense. If the foe parries, the DB gained from the parry is halved.
ANTICIPATIONS.3: When faced with a choice about which the caster has little or no information (e.g., which corridor leads to the wine cellar), the caster may cast this spell and the Gamemaster will determine which choice the caster actually makes, biasing the choice favorably by 25% (e.g., if there are two choices, roll 1-100: correct on 26-100, incorrect on 01-25).
ANTICIPATIONS.4: Once this spell is cast, the next time that the caster needs to cast a spell, he will have already subconsciously readied himself to do so. Treat the caster as having prepared that next spell for two rounds.
ANTICIPATIONS.5: Caster gains a vision of what will probably happen in the next minute if the caster takes a specified action.
ANTICIPATIONS.6: Caster is aware of any being within the range of the spell that has hostile intentions towards him. The hostility must be directed specifically at the caster or the spell reveals nothing. The being’s hostility must be active (i.e., driving the being to perform aggressive actions), not passive (i.e., hate exists, but no action is planned).
ANTICIPATIONS.7: Caster has a second sense for the coming actions of his competitor(s) in any game involving skill (possibly with some significant element of chance, but the game must allow meaningful tactical choices, so games such as chess, go, poker, qualify; simply dicing for the highest number would not), providing a +30 bonus to all maneuvers related to that game. Duration is one game or playing session. Does not apply to essentially solitaire games or gambling, regardless of degree of skill involved.
ANTICIPATIONS.8: Caster has a dream relating to a topic decided upon just before retiring; limited to once per night. The caster must sleep for a normal sleep cycle (minimum of four hours) for this spell to work.
ANTICIPATIONS.9: Caster has a vision of what has occurred in a room or place; limited to up to 1 min/level into the past. The room must fit within a cube with edges equal to twice the caster’s level in feet (i.e., if the caster is 9th level, then the room must fit inside an 18' x 18' x 18' cube).
ANTICIPATIONS.10: Predicts most probable action of a being in the next round; specifics are not learned (e.g., attack in melee, cast a spell; but no detail on attack target or what spell).
ANTICIPATIONS.11: As Intuitions I, except caster gets to gaze 2 minutes into the future.
ANTICIPATIONS.12: As Room Feel I, except limit is 1 hour/level.
ANTICIPATIONS.13: As Anticipate Hostility , except for area of effect, duration, and range.
ANTICIPATIONS.14: As Dream I, except limit is 2 dreams/night on different topics.
ANTICIPATIONS.15: As Anticipations, except if action is to cast a spell, the spell to be cast and target(s) are learned.
ANTICIPATIONS.16: As Room Feel I, except limit is 1 day/level.
ANTICIPATIONS.17: As Dream I, except limit is 3 dreams/night on different topics.
ANTICIPATIONS.18: Caster can predict with 95% accuracy the time, type, and severity of the weather to come over the next seven days assuming no spells or magical phenomena interfere.
ANTICIPATIONS.19: As Room Feel I, except limit is 1 week/level.
ANTICIPATIONS.20: As Intuitions I , except caster may gaze into the future for a number of minutes equal to the caster’s level.
ANTICIPATIONS.25: As Anticipations, except caster can predict actions of all beings within 100' of himself.
ANTICIPATIONS.30: As Dream I, except limit is 5 dreams/night on any combination of topics the caster chooses.
ANTICIPATIONS.35: As Dream V, except limit is one dream/night per every five levels of the caster (i.e., a 35th level caster may have seven dreams).
ANTICIPATIONS.40: As Room Feel I, except there is no time limit.
ANTICIPATIONS.50: As Anticipations True, except spell types and targets are learned.

ASSASSINATION MASTERY.1: Target object (no larger than a dagger) is camouflaged to appear as part of the caster’s clothing or skin. This is an illusion to all senses (including feel), and can be detected as an illusion. The object will remain concealed until taken from the location, or the duration expires.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.2: [RR Mod: -50] When this spell is cast on a person, it remains active for 24 hours. After 24 hours has passed (but before another 24 hours has passed), the caster must cast this spell again on the same person. During the time the spell is active, it is recording information concerning the activities of the target. When the spell is cast the second time, the information about those activities is revealed to the caster. This information is used for spells later on this list and spells on other lists (see the Misdirections and Disguise Mastery lists). The caster can only have one “patsy” studied at a time.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.3: The next attack made by the caster (within one minute of casting this spell) receives a bonus of +25.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.4: If the caster’s next attack (made within one minute of casting this spell), gets a “flank” or “rear” bonus, he may use his Ambush skill even if the target is aware of the caster. However, the maximum change to the critical roll from Ambush is ±2.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.5: The caster is able to make use of whatever weapons are available, including non-standard weapons such as a candlestick. Whatever penalties are applied due to the use of “similar skill”, limitations of the item when employed as a weapon, or unsuitability for use due a mismatch between the caster and the user for which a weapon is designed (as from Size) are reduced by 20.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.6: Caster may cast this spell to create one piece of physical evidence that implicates the currently studied patsy (see Study Patsy). This could include a fingerprint, a footprint, etc., but must be something that cannot normally be moved from the crime scene. The evidence should be treated as circumstantial (i.e., a good alibi should still get the patsy off the hook).
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.7: As Concealed Object I, except will affect an object up to the size of a one-handed sword.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.8: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +50.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.9: As Open Ambush I , except the limit on critical roll modification is ±4.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.10: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 40.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.11: Caster gains a +20 to his initiative roll this round. This must be the caster’s first action and the +20 applies to the casting of this spell.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.12: As Create Evidence I, except two pieces of evidence are created; one piece of evidence is incriminating (i.e., patsy will need more than a good alibi).
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.13: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +75.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.14: As Open Ambush I, except the limit on critical roll modification is ±6.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.15: As Improvised Attack I , except penalties are reduced by 60.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.16: As Concealed Object I, except object may be of any size, so long as the largest dimension of the object is less than the caster’s height.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.17: As Study Patsy , except the information it gathers is required for use with the Slaying Attack spells later on this list.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.18: As Create Evidence I , except three pieces of evidence are created; two pieces of evidence are incriminating.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.19: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +100.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.20: The caster’s next attack (within one minute of casting this spell) against the currently studied victim (see Study Victim) will be resolved as a Slaying IV attack (+20 to the critical roll).
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.25: As Open Ambush I , except the limit on critical roll modification is ±10.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.30: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 90.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.35: As Open Ambush I , except the limit on critical roll modification is ±14, the caster has 10 minutes in which to make the attack, and he does not have to have a positional bonus.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.40: As Improvised Attack I , except penalties are reduced by 120.
ASSASSINATION MASTERY.50: As Slaying Attack, except all of the caster’s attacks made against the victim are resolved as Slaying IV attacks, and the duration is 1 minute per level.

ATTACK AVOIDANCE.1: Caster adds +20 to his DB versus one missile fired at the caster. The caster must be able to see the attack.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.2: Caster adds +20 to his DB versus one melee attack on the caster. The caster must be able to see the attack.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.3: Creates an invisible force shield in front of the caster; it subtracts 25 from up to 3 attacks per round from the caster’s front. This does not occupy a hand, but is not usable with a physical shield (nor does it benefit from the Shield skill).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.4: Caster adds +20 to his DB versus one directed spell attack on the caster. The caster must be able to see the attack.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.5: Deflects one missile fired at the caster; that missile has 50 subtracted from its attack (missile must be in caster’s field of vision).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.6: As Deflect I, except its effect is against one melee attack.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.7: As Shield, except force shield encircles caster, protecting against any number of attacks from all directions. It can be combined with actual armor, but not with either a physical shield or the Shield spell.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.8: As Deflect I, except missile automatically misses.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.9: As Shield, but the defensive bonus is 40 against up to 4 attacks per round.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.10: Creates a pocket of still air 1”/level on all sides of the caster, that no outside gas can come into (duration is the time for the oxygen to be exhausted by one person).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.11: As Deflect I, except affects 2 missiles.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.12: Allows caster to get full Quickness-based DB against all attacks, including those of which the caster is unaware.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.13: Deflects one directed elemental spell against caster; attack roll has -50 added to it (must be in field of vision).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.14: Sets up vibrations in any weapon used to attack the caster in melee. This causes the fumble number of such weapons to be increased by 5.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.15: As Bladeturn I, except affects 2 melee attacks.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.16: Caster’s parry is doubled in value (i.e., if the caster used 15 OB to parry with, the caster would gain 30 DB).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.17: The caster moves to avoid the area of effect of an elemental area effect spell or innate power (typically a ball attack or breath weapon). This results in a -50 to the attack roll as applied to the caster (and caster cannot be subjected to the attack bonus for being at the center of the attack).
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.18: As Deflect I, except affects 3 missiles.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.19: As Aim Untrue I, except affects 2 missiles.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.20: As Spell Deflect I, except affects 2 directed elemental attack spells.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.25: As Bladeturn I, except affects 3 melee attacks.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.30: As Spell Deflect I, except affects 3 directed elemental attack spells.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.35: As Spell Deflect I, except penalty to attacking spell is -100.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.40: As Enhanced Parry, except defensive value is tripled.
ATTACK AVOIDANCE.50: As Deflect I, Bladeturn I, and Spell Deflect I; except any 3 attacks can be deflected.

BARRIER LAW.1: Creates a black, insubstantial wall up to 20'x20'x1" that light cannot pass into or through (i.e., it does not hinder movement, but it blocks vision). Note that this provides Full Soft Cover (see RMCL, Section 9.6). This will not block Darkvision, which does not require light to function.
BARRIER LAW.2: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty.
BARRIER LAW.3: As Shadow Wall I, except for duration.
BARRIER LAW.4: Creates a wall of water up to 10'x10'x1'. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Run going in would be a Walk pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty.
BARRIER LAW.5: Creates a wall of wood up to 10'x20'x2". It can be burned through or chopped through or toppled if one end is not against a wall.
BARRIER LAW.6: Target lock becomes harder to pick or to force, giving a -20 penalty to any attempt to do so. Note that this will give a lock an RR against Heaven’ s Key.
BARRIER LAW.7: Creates a wall of packed earth up to 10'x10'x (3' at base, 1' at top); it can be dug through only (20 man-rounds at top).
BARRIER LAW.8: Creates a wall of ice up to 10'x10'x (2' at base, 1' at top); it can be melted through or chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
BARRIER LAW.9: Creates a sanctified entryway that will attempt to prevent “unholy” things from passing through the gate. The gate is defined by a closed loop that is either freestanding or at an entrance/exit point. A pair of pillars does not define a gate, because the top is open. Putting a crosspiece that connects that pair of pillars would close the loop and allow that to become a Sacred Gate. The gate does not need to have straight sides; a cave mouth could become a Sacred Gate. There is no size limit; the Gateway Arch in St. Louis could be made into a Sacred Gate. When an unholy being tries to pass through the Sacred Gate, it must make an RR. If it fails, then it takes an A Holy critical and is unable to pass through. If it succeeds, it passes through the gateway and the Sacred Gate spell is dispelled. If the Sacred Gate ever ceases to be a closed loop (e.g., the crosspiece falls off the pillars, leaving the top unconnected), the spell ends. It may also be canceled or dispelled normally.
BARRIER LAW.10: Opens a pit (up to 500 cubic feet in stone; 1000 cubic feet in earth or ice). The entire pit must be within 50' of caster and the pit can only be opened on a horizontal surface with an incline of less than 45°.
BARRIER LAW.11: As Airwall, except has a set duration of 1 min/level and does not require concentration.
BARRIER LAW.12: Creates a wall of non-precious stone up to 10'x10'x1'; it can be chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
BARRIER LAW.13: As Waterwall, except caster need not concentrate and the duration is 1 minute per level.
BARRIER LAW.14: As Sacred Gate I, except for duration and it does a B Holy critical.
BARRIER LAW.15: As Woodwall, except duration is permanent.
BARRIER LAW.16: Unlocks one lock without triggering any associated traps. There is no chance of failure (other than normal spell failure), unless the lock has an RR.
BARRIER LAW.17: As Earthwall, except duration is permanent.
BARRIER LAW.18: As Icewall I, except size is up to 20'x20'x(4' at base, 2' at top).
BARRIER LAW.19: As Sacred Gate I, except for duration and it does a C Holy critical.
BARRIER LAW.20: As Stonewall, except duration is permanent and dimensions may be up to 20'x20'x1'.
BARRIER LAW.25: Fuses touching walls (seam can be up to 20' long) or fuses a section of stone blocks (up to 100 cubic feet).
BARRIER LAW.30: As any one of the lower level “wall” Spells on this list, except the wall can be curved up to a semicircle.
BARRIER LAW.35: As Sacred Gate I, except for duration and it does a D Holy critical.
BARRIER LAW.40: As Sacred Gate I, except for duration and it does an E Holy critical.
BARRIER LAW.50: Creates a transparent wall of “force” that is absolutely impassable by anyone or anything. It is up to 10'x20'x1".

BEASTLY WAYS.1: Caster’s vision in low light is as a cat. In near darkness (e.g., a dark night - but not in situations with no light), caster sees with normal clarity and over normal distance, but cannot see color.
BEASTLY WAYS.2: Caster gains a +40 bonus to any maneuvers primarily involving jumping.
BEASTLY WAYS.3: Caster gains an increased sense of smell and hearing (subtle smells and higher sonic ranges included). Adds +50 to Perception maneuvers using only these senses or +20 to Perception maneuvers using these senses combined with others.
BEASTLY WAYS.4: Caster gains a +5 bonus to initiative rolls.
BEASTLY WAYS.5: Caster gains improved ability to maneuver without his sight. When blinded or otherwise without sight, this spell adds +50 to maneuver rolls in the dark. Note that this spell does not negate penalties for blindness; it simply decreases them.
BEASTLY WAYS.6: Caster can safely hold his breath for the duration of this spell.
BEASTLY WAYS.7: Caster can “glide” through the air. The product of caster’s altitude and horizontal speed cannot exceed 100 (with altitude in feet and speed in feet per round). For example, a caster can glide along at a rate of 5' per round if he were 20' off the ground, or he could glide at the rate of 50' per round if he were 2' off the ground. Note that the product must equal 100 (i.e., the caster cannot choose to have a result less than or more than 100). Elevation may be increased up to 5' or decreased up to 10' each round, with the highest elevation being used to calculate the horizontal speed. There is a minimum elevation of 2'.
BEASTLY WAYS.8: Caster gains the visual acuity of a hawk. Adds +50 to all Perceptions involving only sight; +20 to all Perceptions involving this sense combined with others.
BEASTLY WAYS.9: Caster’s skin has the resilience of a large cat (effective AT of 3).
BEASTLY WAYS.10: Caster gains the ferocity of a wild boar. He gains +20 to his melee OB and does double concussion hit damage in melee, but cannot parry or use missiles for the duration of the spell.
BEASTLY WAYS.11: Caster gains the ability to make claw attacks as a lion. He may attack using the Claw Attack Table (size of attack is caster’s own size). The caster must develop skill with these claws as an Unarmed combat skill, but does gain a special bonus equal to his ranks in this list.
BEASTLY WAYS.12: Caster’s skin has the resilience of a bear (effective AT of 4).
BEASTLY WAYS.13: Caster gains an extra 1 AP each round and a +10 bonus to initiative rolls.
BEASTLY WAYS.14: Caster gains the ability to make a grappling attack with the strength of a bear. When the caster makes a Grappling/Wrestling attack, it does double normal concussion hit damage and any attempt to break free from a hold the caster has established is done with a -30 penalty. His Grapple attack also gains a special bonus to OB equal to the caster’s ranks in this list.
BEASTLY WAYS.15: Caster’s skin has the resilience of a rhinoceros (effective AT of 6).
BEASTLY WAYS.16: As Glide I, except product is 300.
BEASTLY WAYS.17: Caster gains the ability to make bite attacks like a shark. He may attack using the Bite Attack Table (size of attack is caster’s own size). The caster must develop skill with this bite as an Unarmed combat skill, but does gain a special bonus equal to his ranks in this list.
BEASTLY WAYS.18: Caster’s skin takes on the resilience of a minor drake (providing the protection of Armor Type 8).
BEASTLY WAYS.19: Caster gains an extra 2 AP each round and a +15 bonus to initiative rolls.
BEASTLY WAYS.20: Caster gains the strength of an ox. He gains +40 to his melee OB and does triple concussion hit damage in melee, but cannot parry or use missiles for the duration of the spell.
BEASTLY WAYS.25: As Glide I, except product is 500.
BEASTLY WAYS.30: Caster gains an extra 3 AP each round and a +20 bonus to initiative rolls.
BEASTLY WAYS.35: For the duration of this spell, all of the caster’s physical attacks (whether ranged or melee) deliver a poison upon achieving a critical. An RR is made against poison based on the caster’s level and the effects are felt in 1-5 rounds. The poison may be of any type of the caster’s choosing (the same poison for the duration of any given casting of this spell). While fast-acting, the poison is not powerful and can achieve no more than Moderate effect.
BEASTLY WAYS.40: Caster gains a +40 bonus to all Ambush rolls and gains an extra five effective ranks in Ambush in order to determine how much he can modify the roll for any critical done.
BEASTLY WAYS.50: Caster’s skin takes on the resilience of a lesser drake (providing the protection of Armor Type 10).

BLOOD LAW.1: Reduces target’s blood loss by up to 1 hit per round. Until the injury is actually treated by some means (e.g., Medicine maneuver or a Flesh Relief spell), the target cannot move or be moved without the bleeding resuming. Multiple Staunch Wound, Flowstop, Clotting, or Cut Repair spells may not be cast upon the same wound.
BLOOD LAW.2: As Staunch Wound I, except the caster may choose one option: reduce the bleeding by an additional 2 hits/round, or cause the wound to clot so that it is only at risk of reopening during the first hour and only if the target engages in activity more vigorous than walking.
BLOOD LAW.3: One poison or disease afflicting the target is reduced by one level of severity (e.g. from Extreme to Severe, or from Mild to cured), and, in the case of disease, anyone subsequently infected by the target gains a bonus of +20 on their RR.
BLOOD LAW.4: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
BLOOD LAW.5: Prevents the deterioration of 1 limb (even while severed); also requires the use of Limb Preservation from the Bone, Muscle, and Nerve spell lists.
BLOOD LAW.6: As Staunch Wound II, except two options may be chosen, including multiple reductions in the amount of bleeding. If the clotting option is selected twice, bleeding is instantly stopped with no danger of reopening. In addition, one option may be spent to split the effect of the spell between multiple bleeding injuries (e.g., if the reduction will be 3 hits per round, it may be applied to reduce bleeding of one wound by 1 hit per round and another by 2 hits per round).
BLOOD LAW.7: Target receives an additional RR vs either poison or disease, as specified by the caster at the time of casting.
BLOOD LAW.8: As Light Flesh Relief, except a wound incurring a penalty up to -40.
BLOOD LAW.9: As Staunch Wound III, except three options may be chosen.
BLOOD LAW.10: As Medium Flesh Relief, except a wound incurring any penalty.
BLOOD LAW.11: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
BLOOD LAW.12: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spells from the Bone, Muscle, and Nerve spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BLOOD LAW.13: As Staunch Wound III, except four options may be chosen.
BLOOD LAW.14: As Light Flesh Healing , except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
BLOOD LAW.15: As Staunch Wound III, except five options may be chosen.
BLOOD LAW.16: Removes any one disease from the target.
BLOOD LAW.17: As Light Flesh Healing , except an injury incurring any penalty.
BLOOD LAW.18: Removes any one poison from the target.
BLOOD LAW.19: As Staunch Wound III, except seven options may be chosen.
BLOOD LAW.20: Removes all disease from one target/level.
BLOOD LAW.25: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
BLOOD LAW.30: As Light Flesh Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.
BLOOD LAW.35: Removes all poison from one target/level.
BLOOD LAW.40: As Medium Flesh Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.
BLOOD LAW.50: As Severe Flesh Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.

BLOOD MASTERY.1: Reduces target’s blood loss by up to 3 hits per round. Until the injury is actually treated by some means (e.g., Medicine maneuver or a Flesh Relief spell), the target cannot move or be moved without the bleeding resuming. Multiple Flowstop spells may not be cast upon the same wound.
BLOOD MASTERY.2: Instantly stops bleeding from a wound that bleeds at a rate of 1 hit/round.
BLOOD MASTERY.3: One poison or disease afflicting the target is reduced by one level of severity (e.g. from Extreme to Severe, or from Mild to cured), and, in the case of disease, anyone subsequently infected by the target gains a bonus of +20 on their RR. This spell may not be used multiple times on the same target to treat the same disease.
BLOOD MASTERY.4: Stops bleeding on a wound bleeding up to 3 hits/round. For one hour, the target can engage in activity no more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume.
BLOOD MASTERY.5: Instantly stops bleeding on a wound that bleeds at a rate up to 3 hits/round.
BLOOD MASTERY.6: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury or any severity is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll. Note that this treats wounds that incur injury penalties, but does not treat bleeding.
BLOOD MASTERY.7: Target receives an additional RR vs either poison or disease, as specified by the caster at the time of casting.
BLOOD MASTERY.8: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -40 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
BLOOD MASTERY.9: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spells from the Bone, Muscle, and Nerve and Organ Mastery spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BLOOD MASTERY.10: As Flowstop III, except the caster may stop all blood loss in the target.
BLOOD MASTERY.11: Target is kept in a state of suspended animation and prevented from dying for the duration of the spell (i.e., the body is “preserved” and the “soul” will not leave the body). Also requires the use of the other Suspend Life I spell (on the Nerve and Organ list).
BLOOD MASTERY.12: As Clotting III, except a wound bleeding at any rate.
BLOOD MASTERY.13: Instantly stops bleeding on a wound that bleeds at a rate up to 5 hits/round.
BLOOD MASTERY.14: Removes any one disease from the target.
BLOOD MASTERY.15: Instantly stops bleeding on a wound that bleeds at any rate.
BLOOD MASTERY.16: As Medium Flesh Healing, except a wound incurring any penalty.
BLOOD MASTERY.17: As Suspend Life I , except for duration and the requirement of Suspend Life II from Nerve & Organ Mastery.
BLOOD MASTERY.18: Removes any one poison from the target.
BLOOD MASTERY.19: As Clotting III , except stops total bleeding of up to 1 hit/round per level of the caster in any number of targets within range.
BLOOD MASTERY.20: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
BLOOD MASTERY.25: Caster can instantly stop bleeding from multiple wounds (in a single target) up to a total hits/round of bleeding equal to his level.
BLOOD MASTERY.30: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to rounds rather than days.
BLOOD MASTERY.35: As Light Flesh Healing True, except an injury incurring a penalty up to 40.
BLOOD MASTERY.40: As Light Flesh Healing True, except an injury with any penalty.
BLOOD MASTERY.50: Caster can regrow 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the Regrow Limb spells from Bone, Muscle, and Nerve and Organ Mastery for the limb to become fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

BLOOD WAYS.1: Stops bleeding on a wound that is bleeding at rate of up to 2 hits/round. For one hour, the caster can engage in no activity more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume at the prior rate.
BLOOD WAYS.2: Instantly stops bleeding from 1 wound that bleeds at rate of 1 hit/round.
BLOOD WAYS.3: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days. Note that this treats wounds that incur injury penalties, but does not treat bleeding.
BLOOD WAYS.4: As Clotting II, except caster can heal a wound bleeding up to 5 hits/round.
BLOOD WAYS.5: Instantly stops bleeding from 1 wound that bleeds at rate of up to 3 hits/round.
BLOOD WAYS.6: As Light Flesh Healing , except a wound that incurs a penalty up to -40.
BLOOD WAYS.7: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BLOOD WAYS.8: Stops bleeding on a wound that is bleeding at any rate. For one hour, the caster can engage in no activity more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume at the prior rate.
BLOOD WAYS.9: As Light Flesh Healing , except a wound that incurs any penalty.
BLOOD WAYS.10: Instantly stops bleeding from 1 wound regardless of bleeding rate.
BLOOD WAYS.11: Adjusts caster’s blood contents to prepare for exertions. All Endurance rolls made while this spell is in effect are made with a +50 bonus.
BLOOD WAYS.12: As Clotting I, except caster can heal bleeding in multiple wounds up to a total hits/round of bleeding equal to his level.
BLOOD WAYS.13: As Medium Flesh Healing , except up to one wound per level.
BLOOD WAYS.14: Caster can instantly stop bleeding from multiple wounds up to a total hits/round of bleeding equal to his level.
BLOOD WAYS.15: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.
BLOOD WAYS.16: A flesh wound or skin/tissue injury that incurs a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to rounds rather than days.
BLOOD WAYS.17: As Severe Flesh Healing, except up to one wound per level.
BLOOD WAYS.18: As Light Flesh Healing True, except a wound that incurs a penalty up to -40.
BLOOD WAYS.19: Caster must take a full day in a healing trance to use this spell, which will cleanse the body of accumulated toxins and other ills. Caster physically becomes 5 years younger (to a minimum of half the caster’s true age).
BLOOD WAYS.20: Caster can regulate his own blood flow and completely eliminate any blood loss without fear of harm. The duration of this spell is for as long as the caster is immobile. He can cast other spells, although spells not listed as subconscious take the normal penalties for casting a spell without words or gestures.
BLOOD WAYS.25: As Light Flesh Healing True , except a wound that incurs any penalty.
BLOOD WAYS.30: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BLOOD WAYS.35: As Severe Flesh Healing True , except up to one wound per level.
BLOOD WAYS.40: Caster must take a full day in a healing trance to use this spell, which will cleanse the body of accumulated toxins and other ills. Caster physically becomes 10 years younger (to a minimum of one third the caster’s true age).
BLOOD WAYS.50: Caster can use any 1 of the lower spells on this list in a given round at will.

BODY REINS.1: Adds +50 to any rolls for any moving maneuvers performed at a walking pace or less (e.g., walking a 3" beam).
BODY REINS.2: Allows the caster’s muscles, limbs, and torso to be slightly altered; this facilitates escaping from bonds and small places. Gives a +25 to +50 bonus to maneuvers to perform such escapes.
BODY REINS.3: Adds +20 to any one maneuver (not combat), no other action can be performed the round of this maneuver (except for casting this spell).
BODY REINS.4: Caster is able to sustain an additional 25% of his total concussion hits (round off fractional hits) before passing out; hits are still taken and remain when the spell lapses.
BODY REINS.5: Alters the caster’s face to resemble someone else, but not so closely as to be confused on a close inspection.
BODY REINS.6: Caster can breathe water, but not air.
BODY REINS.7: As Concentration I, except bonus is +30.
BODY REINS.8: This spell increases the caster’s effective strength. In melee, the caster does double normal concussion hit damage and gains a +10 bonus to OB.
BODY REINS.9: As Unpain I, except 50% additional hits may be sustained.
BODY REINS.10: Alters the caster’s body slightly to the general shape and size of the desired humanoid race (must be within 25% of the caster’s normal mass and same Size category).
BODY REINS.11: As Concentration I, except bonus is +40.
BODY REINS.12: As Monk’ s Strength II, except caster delivers triple concussion hit damage and has a +15 OB bonus.
BODY REINS.13: This spell will awaken the caster from sleep the round following casting (caster can preset conditions under which the spell will activate).
BODY REINS.14: Caster can breathe any gas as normal air.
BODY REINS.15: As Unpain I, except 75% additional hits may be sustained.
BODY REINS.16: As Concentration I, except bonus is +50.
BODY REINS.17: This spell halves the normal amount of time needed for rest. For example, after a normal day, instead of needing eight hours of sleep, the caster will need only four hours of sleep. This spell will remain in effect for the duration of one sleep session.
BODY REINS.18: As Unpain I, except 100% additional hits may be sustained.
BODY REINS.19: During a normal sleep, the caster may make Perception rolls (that are not based on sight) at no penalty. Duration is one sleep session.
BODY REINS.20: Upon receiving a death blow, the caster goes into a state of suspended animation, until cured or killed by brain destruction.
BODY REINS.25: As Monk’ s Sleep and Meditative Sleep operating at the same time.
BODY REINS.30: As Monk’ s Strength II, except caster’s effective strength is increased dramatically. In melee, the caster delivers four times normal concussion damage and gains an OB bonus of +20.
BODY REINS.35: As Concentration I, except bonus is +70.
BODY REINS.40: As Monk’ s Strength II, except caster’s effective strength is increased dramatically. In melee, the caster delivers five times normal concussion damage and gains an OB bonus of +25.
BODY REINS.50: Caster ignores all pain; he ignores the penalties for lost hits and will never become unconscious due to hits taken, nor will he die from hit loss while this spell is in effect.

BONE LAW.1: An injury to cartilage incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
BONE LAW.2: Caster acquires complete understanding of any bone damage, including the tools and methods required for healing. Caster does not receive the skill or power necessary to perform the cure, but this diagnostic does provide a +20 bonus to treatment if supervised by the caster. Alternatively, this spell can be used to identify bones, including the approximate age, species, sex, and in some cases cause of death.
BONE LAW.3: As Light Cartilage Relief, except a bone injury.
BONE LAW.4: Target’s bones become flexible, granting +50 to escape maneuvers.
BONE LAW.5: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Blood, Muscle, and Nerve Limb Preservation spells as well.
BONE LAW.6: As Light Cartilage Relief, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE LAW.7: As Medium Cartilage Relief , except a bone injury.
BONE LAW.8: Target’s bones are strengthened, and the injury penalty incurred by any bone injury sustained within the duration is halved. Injuries suffered before the spell was cast are not affected.
BONE LAW.9: As Medium Cartilage Relief, except an injury incurring any penalty.
BONE LAW.10: As Medium Cartilage Relief , except a bone injury incurring any penalty.
BONE LAW.11: A bone injury is temporarily braced and immobilized such that the injury penalty from that injury is halved for the duration.
BONE LAW.12: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spell from the Blood, Muscle, and Nerve spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BONE LAW.13: An injury to cartilage incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
BONE LAW.14: As Light Cartilage Healing , except for a bone injury.
BONE LAW.15: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE LAW.16: As Medium Cartilage Healing, except for a bone injury.
BONE LAW.17: As Strong Bones I, except the duration is 10 minutes per level.
BONE LAW.18: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
BONE LAW.19: As Severe Cartilage Healing, except for a bone injury.
BONE LAW.20: Target’s bones are greatly strengthened, granting a physical RR to prevent any new injury penalties incurred from bone injuries.
BONE LAW.25: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
BONE LAW.30: As Medium Cartilage Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE LAW.35: As Medium Bone Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE LAW.40: Any new bone injury suffered by the target will not result in any injury penalty. Other effects of the attack (hits, stun, etc.) are not prevented by this spell.
BONE LAW.50: Caster can use any lower level spell (on this list) once per round.

BONE MASTERY.1: An injury to cartilage incurring a penalty up to -40 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
BONE MASTERY.2: Target’s bones become flexible, granting +50 to escape maneuvers.
BONE MASTERY.3: As Medium Cartilage Relief , except an injury to bones incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE MASTERY.4: As Medium Cartilage Relief, except an injury incurring any penalty.
BONE MASTERY.5: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Muscle Mastery Limb Preservation spell as well.
BONE MASTERY.6: An injury to cartilage incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
BONE MASTERY.7: As Medium Cartilage Relief, except a bone injury incurring any penalty.
BONE MASTERY.8: Target’s bones are strengthened, and the injury penalty incurred by any bone injury sustained within the duration is halved. Injuries suffered before the spell was cast are not affected.
BONE MASTERY.9: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spells from the Blood, Muscle, and Nerve and Organ Mastery spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BONE MASTERY.10: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE MASTERY.11: As Medium Cartilage Healing, except for a bone injury.
BONE MASTERY.12: A bone injury is temporarily braced and immobilized such that the injury penalty from that injury is halved for the duration.
BONE MASTERY.13: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
BONE MASTERY.14: As Severe Cartilage Healing, except for a bone injury.
BONE MASTERY.15: As Strong Bones I, except the duration is 10 minutes per level.
BONE MASTERY.16: Target regenerates any one bone. Recovery time is 1-10 hours.
BONE MASTERY.17: As Medium Bone Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE MASTERY.18: As Severe Cartilage Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE MASTERY.19: Target’s bones are greatly strengthened, granting a physical RR to prevent any new bone injuries.
BONE MASTERY.20: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
BONE MASTERY.25: As Severe Bone Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE MASTERY.30: Target’s bones become unbreakable, and any new bone injury which would incur an injury penalty is prevented.
BONE MASTERY.35: As Severe Bone Healing , except for both cartilage and bone injuries, up to a number of injuries equal to the caster’s level (on one target).
BONE MASTERY.40: Target regenerates all of the lost bone and/or cartilage in his body; recovery time is 2-20 days.
BONE MASTERY.50: Caster can regrow 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the Regrow Limb spells from Blood, Muscle, and Nerve and Organ Mastery for the limb to become fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

BONE WAYS.1: An injury to cartilage incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
BONE WAYS.2: As Light Cartilage Healing , except an injury to bone other than the skull incurring a penalty up to -20.
BONE WAYS.3: As Light Cartilage Healing, except a cartilage injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE WAYS.4: As Light Cartilage Healing , except an injury to the skull incurring a penalty up to -20.
BONE WAYS.5: As Light Cartilage Healing , except an injury to bone other than the skull incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE WAYS.6: As Light Cartilage Healing, except a cartilage injury incurring any penalty.
BONE WAYS.7: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BONE WAYS.8: As Light Cartilage Healing , except an injury to the skull incurring a penalty up to -40.
BONE WAYS.9: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury to bone other than the skull incurring any penalty.
BONE WAYS.10: As Medium Cartilage Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.11: As Light Cartilage Healing, except an injury to the skull incurring any penalty.
BONE WAYS.12: As Medium Osseous Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.13: As Light Skull Healing , except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.14: Caster can regenerate any 1 bone that is not part of the skull. Recovery time is 1-10 hours.
BONE WAYS.15: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.
BONE WAYS.16: As Medium Skull Healing , except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.17: As Severe Cartilage Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.18: Caster’s skull regenerates. Recovery time is 1-10 hours.
BONE WAYS.19: As Severe Osseous Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.20: As Severe Skull Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
BONE WAYS.25: Any number of injuries to bone (including the skull) and cartilage up to the caster’s level are cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the caster receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
BONE WAYS.30: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
BONE WAYS.35: Caster can regenerate any 1 bone that is not part of the skull. Recovery is instantaneous.
BONE WAYS.40: As Mass Skeletal Relief , and the recovery time is reduced from days to hours.
BONE WAYS.50: Caster can regenerate all of the lost bone and/or cartilage in his body; recovery is instantaneous.

BRILLIANCE.1: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.
BRILLIANCE.2: Causes a small, 1" diameter point of light that will glow softly. This spell provides enough light for Nightvision to work properly, but not enough light to allow much mobility in the dark.
BRILLIANCE.3: Lights a 10'R area about the point touched.
BRILLIANCE.4: Causes a bright aura about the caster, providing a more powerful appearance and subtracting 10 from all attacks on the caster.
BRILLIANCE.5: [RR Mod: -10] Causes a 10'R sphere of intense light, all within are stunned (-50) for 1 round/10 failure.
BRILLIANCE.6: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
BRILLIANCE.7: Reduces illumination to a dark night in a 10'R area about the point touched.
BRILLIANCE.8: As Aura, except it produces an extremely powerful appearance and subtracts 15 from all attacks.
BRILLIANCE.10: As Light I, except radius is 50'.
BRILLIANCE.11: As Darkness I, except radius is 50'.
BRILLIANCE.12: As Shock Bolt I, but with a range increment of 75'.
BRILLIANCE.13: Causes any object up to 1,000 cubic feet to glow with any desired color.
BRILLIANCE.14: As Glow, except produces one light per caster level and these can be directed by concentration anywhere within 5' of the caster. Illumination will still be quite soft, but ten of them provide enough light to read by even without Nightvision.
BRILLIANCE.15: Ray of light of any color springs from caster’s hand, can be up to 5 miles long.
BRILLIANCE.16: As Darkness I , except radius is 1000'.
BRILLIANCE.17: As Shock Bolt I, but with a range increment of 125'.
BRILLIANCE.18: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 100'R, and lights that area as full daylight.
BRILLIANCE.19: Darkens a 100'R area, no light (except Utterlight) can exist within this radius.
BRILLIANCE.20: Focuses sunlight as a lens, must be focused initially on a fixed point. The lens can be moved at a rate of 1'/round, after the first round. It will burn through a 1' diameter at a rate of: 1' wood/round, 4" stone/round, or 1" metal/round. This spell cannot be used effectively in combat (as foes can easily move out of the way). However, if used on an immobile foe, see Hand of Fire for damage resolution.
BRILLIANCE.25: As Sunfires, except does not require sunlight.
BRILLIANCE.30: As Sunfires, except the initial focus is 6" from open palm of caster (caster’s arm is immune) and it can be moved 10'/round. If used in melee, it strikes on the Fire Bolt table (with concussion hits doubled) using the caster’s Directed Spell: Elemental Bolts skill; concentration is not required when focus is fixed 6" from palm. If the attack is fumbled (01-02 UM), the caster takes an automatic C Heat Critical.
BRILLIANCE.35: As Beacon, except that the beam will rotate through a full circle, repeatedly, and it has the listed duration and stays centered at the initial location, rather than requiring the caster to stay and concentrate.
BRILLIANCE.40: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; resolve attack on the Lightning Bolt Attack Table as a Medium-sized attack.
BRILLIANCE.50: As Hand of Fire, except does not require sunlight.

CALM SPIRITS.1: Caster is placed in a state of calm. This provides a +25 RR bonus against Fear or any spells that provoke an emotional response, a +10 bonus to any maneuvers that benefit from calm (including the use of missile weapons), while giving a -15 penalty to any activity to which calm is detrimental (e.g., most moving maneuvers above a walking pace, melee combat, thrown weapons)
CALM SPIRITS.2: Target will take no aggressive/offensive action and will fight only if attacked.
CALM SPIRITS.3: Target will not be able to rise as undead or house any animating spirit or otherwise be made to occupy a state other than peaceful death or true resurrection that returns the body to life and rehouses the soul.
CALM SPIRITS.4: As Calm I, except 2 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.5: Humanoid target has its Action Points per round reduced by 3 (i.e., normally, the target will have 1 AP each round).
CALM SPIRITS.6: As Calm I, except 3 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.7: As Restful Spirit I, except for duration.
CALM SPIRITS.8: As Calm I, except 4 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.9: As Calm l, except 5 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.10: Caster can calm (as Calm I) a number of animals equal to the caster’s level.
CALM SPIRITS.11: Any target has its Action Points per round reduced by 3 (i.e., normally, the target will have 1 AP each round).
CALM SPIRITS.12: As Calm I, except that 10 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.13: As Hold Kind I , except 3 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.14: The caster enters a deep relaxing meditation from which he cannot be woken until a specified time has passed (up to the duration of the spell). While meditating the caster regains Hits and PP at twice the normal rate and receives +50 to any Meditation maneuvers.
CALM SPIRITS.15: All targets in radius must resist or they are calmed (as Calm I).
CALM SPIRITS.16: As True Hold I, except 2 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.17: As Restful Spirit I, except for duration.
CALM SPIRITS.18: [RR Mod.: -20] As Mass Animal Calm I, except RRs are modified by -20 and the range is 10'/lvl.
CALM SPIRITS.19: As Hold Kind I , except 5 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.20: [RR Mod.: -20] As Calm I, except that 20 targets may be affected.
CALM SPIRITS.25: [RR Mod.: -20] As Calm I, except that calmness lasts for 1 day/level.
CALM SPIRITS.30: [RR Mod.: -20] As Calm I, except that caster can calm a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
CALM SPIRITS.35: As Hold Kind I , except that caster can affect a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
CALM SPIRITS.40: As True Hold I, except that caster can affect a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
CALM SPIRITS.50: As Calm I, except that calmness is permanent.

CHANNELS.1: When this spell is cast, up to four fluid ounces of clean, clear water are transformed into holy water. This spell takes one hour to cast. If the water is not placed into a clean receptacle, its holy nature is ruined.
CHANNELS.2: Caster points his arm (elbow locked and fist clenched) at the target and channels raw power from his power source (usually a deity). The target of the spell may take no aggressive/offensive action, and will only fight if attacked.
CHANNELS.3: When this spell is cast, the 1st level spell that the caster begins to cast (or prepare) the next round will be “channeled” to another spell user of the Channeling realm. The “channeled” spell is transmitted to the other spell user when it would normally take effect, and must be used by the receiving spell user immediately or delayed as described in Section 4.8 (treating the receiving spell user as the caster of the spell). All normal restrictions on the transmitted spell apply. The caster of the Channels spell must be able to see the receiver or know exactly where the receiver is (direction and distance or specific place). The receiver must know exactly when the spell is coming (but need not know what the spell sent is). For example, if a Cleric was at a certain altar at a certain time (midnight of full moon) ready to receive a spell; then another spell user (or deity) who knew this could use Channels I to send a spell to that Cleric. Because the spell is passed through the aura of the receiving character, the spell is cast at the level of the receiving character (including any level-based parameters).
CHANNELS.4: As Channels I, except instead of sending a spell, the caster may send up to 1 PP/level to the other spell user (who need not use them right away). If the PP sent put the receiver over his normal PP maximum, excess PP are lost and the receiving individual suffers one hit of damage per PP in excess of his maximum.
CHANNELS.5: As Calming, except target is stunned (-50).
CHANNELS.6: As Channels I, except a 1st-3rd level spell may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.7: As Channels I, except the caster can send up to
CHANNELS.8: As Stunning, except target falls into a deep sleep. The spell duration of this sleep is magical (and the target cannot be awakened in any normal fashion). After this magical sleep, the target may be awakened normally, but if undisturbed, the target will sleep for a normal length sleep period (eight hours for humans).
CHANNELS.9: As Create Holy Water, except that the holy water is simply created without the need for clean water to transform.
CHANNELS.10: As Channels I, except a 1st-5th level spell may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.11: As Stunning, except target is blinded for 1 minute per 5 failure.
CHANNELS.12: [RR Mod: -20] Target takes a B Holy critical.
CHANNELS.13: As Channels I, except a 1st-8th level spell may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.14: [RR Mod: -20] Target’s “soul” is torn from the target’s body (or other physical location if the soul is otherwise housed); it remains in whatever place “souls” go for 1 week/10 failure. The soul can be returned early only through Lifegiving or Reuniting. Target is unconscious until soul is restored; even subconscious activity (i.e., healing, etc.) suffers a -75 penalty.
CHANNELS.15: All beings not of the caster’s own “alignment” (religious persuasion) are affected. All targets failing to resist by 1-40 are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; those failing by more than 40 are unconscious for 1 rnd/5 failure.
CHANNELS.16: Target item (weighing no more than 10 pounds) will return to a designated location. This spell has two possible durations (determined at the time of casting). The caster may send (i.e., teleport) the target item to a designated location at the time of casting (with an instantaneous duration), or the caster can designate that the target item will return to the caster when the possessor of the item has completed a specific task (specified at the time of casting).
CHANNELS.17: As Channels I, except a 1st-10th level spell may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.18: Caster is returned to a predefined place (this is a type of teleportation). Caster must have lived in the place for 30 days (meditating 16 hours each day) to define it as the caster’s “sanctuary”. Only one such place may be defined at any one time; to change it, the new place must be defined as above.
CHANNELS.19: As Item Returning, except object will stay at the designated location (set at the time of casting) for up to 1 round per level. At the end of this time, the object will go back to where it “returned” from.
CHANNELS.20: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution, except the “soul” can only be brought back through Lifegiving or Reuniting.
CHANNELS.25: For up to 1 round/level after having used Sanctuary, the caster may cast this spell and be returned to within 20' of the point at which the Sanctuary spell was cast.
CHANNELS.30: As Channels I, except a 1st-20th level spell may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.35: [RR Mod: -20] Target takes an E Holy critical.
CHANNELS.40: As Channels I, except there is no limit on the level of spell that may be transmitted.
CHANNELS.50: Opens a direct channel to the caster’s deity; results depend on the deity’s personality, motives, and powers. Any spell the deity can cast may be transmitted through the gate.

CLEANSING.1: Caster taps into the mind of a target to diagnose the target’s wounds, injuries, and diseases allowing a +25 bonus to all Medicine maneuvers made by the caster to treat the target’s diagnosed condition(s). Caster can take a round to advise someone else on treating the target, providing a +15 bonus to treat the target.
CLEANSING.2: Caster transfers one injury, wound, state of death, soul departure (not soul destruction), disease, or poison from the target to himself. With an Easy Spell Mastery maneuver, the caster may elect to only transfer certain components of an injury to himself, but he cannot break up an individual component of the injury (e.g., if an injury has done 18 hits, a round of Stun (-50), 2 hits/round of bleeding, and inflicted a -30 penalty, the caster might elect to transfer the Stun or bleeding as the most urgent components, and leave the target with 18 hits of damage and a -30 penalty; the caster could not, however, choose to only transfer 12 of the hits or 1 hit/round of bleeding, or -20 worth of penalty). The caster immediately suffers all effects transferred, while the target is immediately and completely cured of all such effects. However, if the caster would be killed by the transferred damage, they go into a coma and death is delayed for one minute (during which time subconscious healing spells may be cast to prevent death from occurring at all).
CLEANSING.3: Caster transfers one mental illness or addiction from the target to himself.
CLEANSING.4: One poison or disease is totally removed from the caster’s system. This does not undo any damage already done by the poison or disease, which must be cured separately.
CLEANSING.6: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 weeks during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions.
CLEANSING.7: Caster transfers one curse from the target to himself.
CLEANSING.8: As Transference, except that it this is an instantaneous spell.
CLEANSING.9: [RR Mod.: -10] Caster rids himself of one curse. If the curse makes its RR, the caster cannot try again to remove it until he has gained a rank in this list. (Note that if the caster has a different curse removal spell from a different list, then he does get a second chance with that spell.)
CLEANSING.10: Caster is cured of one mental illness or malady. This will require a 1-10 day recovery period to take effect.
CLEANSING.12: For the caster’s next Transference spell, cast within one round/level of casting this spell, he may instead transfer the affliction to another (willing) target with a range of touch.
CLEANSING.13: Caster may suspend all life functions from his body, effectively entering suspended animation for the duration of the spell or until a fixed specified time has passed or a specified triggering event occurs (e.g., body brought to a specific person). While suspended the caster does not age, require food, water, or air, nor will any herbs work on his body although healing spells may continue to be used, but no further harm will come to him from any injury, toxin, curse, etc. until life resumes. The caster is not protected from further harm by external causes.
CLEANSING.14: As Diagnostics, except each round caster may target any one individual within range of this spell.
CLEANSING.15: Caster may transfer any number of afflictions from 1 target.
CLEANSING.16: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 days during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions.
CLEANSING.17: All poisons and diseases are totally removed from the caster’s system. This does not undo any damage already done by a poison or disease, which must be cured separately.
CLEANSING.18: Caster is instantly cured of one mental illness or malady.
CLEANSING.19: As Suspend Life, except for duration.
CLEANSING.20: [RR Mod.: -50] As Curse Cure, except curse’s RR is at a -50 penalty.
CLEANSING.25: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 hours during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions.
CLEANSING.30: Caster may transfer up to 1 condition/level to himself from any number of targets within range. A condition in this context is a wound, injury, disease, poison, curse, mental illness, or addiction.
CLEANSING.35: Caster transfers one possessing demon, spirit, soul, or like entitity from the target to himself.
CLEANSING.40: [RR Mod.: -35] Caster expels one possessing demon, spirit, soul, or like entity from his person. If this fails, the caster cannot cast this spell again for 1-10 days, though he will get whatever normal RRs may be allowed to resist/expel the possessing entity.
CLEANSING.50: Caster may use 1 spell from this list per round.

CLOAKING.1: Causes caster to appear blurred to attackers, granting a +10 to the caster’s DB.
CLOAKING.2: Caster and objects on the caster’s person appear to be in shadow and thus are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus of between +25 and +75).
CLOAKING.3: The next Cloaking spell cast by the caster of this spell, within a minute of completing this spell, will make any noises produced by the caster inaudible to the target(s). This means that the caster can then speak or otherwise make a sound without allowing additional RRs.
CLOAKING.4: [RR Mod = -50] Target makes an RR. Failure results in the caster being invisible to the target; others see the caster normally. If the caster makes an obvious action (e.g., talking, moving an object, etc.), the target may make another RR (the GM may deem it appropriate to apply modifiers to this RR based upon the action the caster takes). If the caster attacks the target, the target may make another RR with a +50 modifier. If the caster begins to concentrate, he can extend the duration of this spell indefinitely (as long as he can concentrate).
CLOAKING.5: Caster has a limited illusion on himself that allows him to look like any humanoid race within 20% of his own size. The illusion moves as the caster moves.
CLOAKING.6: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except the caster can affect 3 targets.
CLOAKING.7: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except every target in the area of effect must make an RR. New targets entering the sphere while the spell is still in effect will need to make an RR or be affected as well. Once a target makes a successful RR, he need not make another RR against the spell; however, once affected, leaving the sphere does not cancel the effect.
CLOAKING.8: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except the caster can affect 5 targets.
CLOAKING.9: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking Sphere I, except for the area of effect.
CLOAKING.10: Creates a duplicate of the caster; if the caster concentrates, it will move as willed; otherwise, it does exactly as the caster does. The duplicate must be created within line of sight of the caster and the caster can only control it within line of sight. If the caster concentrates, he can “merge” the duplicate with himself and then split back apart again (making it difficult to discern which one is the “real” caster).
CLOAKING.11: As Façade I, except illusion may include altering the caster’s voice and any other sounds generated.
CLOAKING.12: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except the caster can affect 10 targets.
CLOAKING.13: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking Sphere I , except for the area of effect.
CLOAKING.14: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.
CLOAKING.15: Caster and objects on his person take on the visual texture, color, and form of the surrounding area. Caster is nearly invisible when motionless (+200 to Stalking maneuvers) and gives a +50 bonus to Stalking attempts even when moving (requires concentration). This spell is not cumulative with Shadow.
CLOAKING.16: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except the caster can affect 20 targets.
CLOAKING.17: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking III, except for duration.
CLOAKING.18: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 20%.
CLOAKING.19: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking Sphere I , except for the area of effect.
CLOAKING.20: As Shadow Mentalist I , except there are two duplicates.
CLOAKING.25: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 30%.
CLOAKING.30: As Camouflage, except does not require concentration while moving and the Stalking bonus when moving is +75.
CLOAKING.35: [RR Mod = -50] As Extended Cloak III, except the caster can affect 8 targets.
CLOAKING.40: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except for duration and that the caster can make any number of associates (up to a maximum of the caster’s level) invisible to the target. Additional RRs due to actions of one of these “invisible” persons are resolved as for Cloaking spells, but if one of them is revealed to the target, this does not expose the others. (Only one RR is made initially to determine if the target resists having the entire group be invisible to him.)
CLOAKING.50: [RR Mod = -50] As Cloaking I, except the caster can affect as many targets as he has levels and the range is only limited to the caster’s field of vision.

COMBAT MASTERY.1: The caster analyzes the fighting style of one opponent he is in melee combat with. After he has both attacked and been attacked by the foe, the target is considered ‘studied’ for the purposes of using Strike spells on this list. Target then remains ‘studied’ until spell duration ends.
COMBAT MASTERY.2: After being hit by a studied opponent the caster may cast this spell to get a +10 to his OB on his next attack.
COMBAT MASTERY.3: The caster’s next attack against a studied opponent causes an additional Unbalancing critical of 2 severities less.
COMBAT MASTERY.4: The caster receives +25 to his Combat Expertise: Reverse Strike skill.
COMBAT MASTERY.5: The caster’s next attack receives +50 to his Combat Expertise: Disarm or Subdual skill.
COMBAT MASTERY.6: The caster receives +25 to his Combat Expertise: Multiple Attacks skill.
COMBAT MASTERY.7: Allows caster to ignore the penalties for making a called shot attack on his next attack.
COMBAT MASTERY.8: Any combatants making attacks directed at the caster have their fumble numbers increased by 3 (after any reduction for their number of ranks in the Combat Training specialization).
COMBAT MASTERY.9: The caster’s next attack is treated as
COMBAT MASTERY.10: The caster’s next attack is treated as a Slaying I attack.
COMBAT MASTERY.11: As Unbalancing Strike I , except critical is 1 severity less.
COMBAT MASTERY.12: As All-around Attacks I, except bonus is +50.
COMBAT MASTERY.13: As Adept Strike I, except bonus is +75.
COMBAT MASTERY.14: As Multiple Attacks I , except bonus is +50.
COMBAT MASTERY.15: As Foiling Attacks I, except fumble numbers are increased by 6.
COMBAT MASTERY.16: Caster’s next attack ignores any DB due to parry.
COMBAT MASTERY.17: As Mighty Strike I, except attack is treated as 2 Sizes larger than normal.
COMBAT MASTERY.18: As Vitals Strike I, except attack is treated as a Slaying II attack.
COMBAT MASTERY.19: As Unbalancing Strike I , except critical is of equal severity.
COMBAT MASTERY.20: As All-around Attacks I, except bonus is +75.
COMBAT MASTERY.25: As Adept Strike I , except bonus is +100
COMBAT MASTERY.30: As Multiple Attacks I , except bonus is +75.
COMBAT MASTERY.35: As Foiling Attacks I, except fumble numbers are increased by 9.
COMBAT MASTERY.40: As All-around Attacks I and Multiple Attacks I, except bonus is +100 to both skills.
COMBAT MASTERY.50: Caster’s attack is treated as a Confusing Strike, Mighty Strike II, Vitals Strike II, and Unbalancing Strike III.

COMMUNAL WAYS.1: When faced with a choice about which the caster has little or no information (e.g., which corridor leads to the wine cellar), the caster may cast this spell and the Gamemaster will determine which choice the caster actually makes, biasing the choice favorably by 25% (e.g., if there are two choices, roll 1-100: correct on 26-100, incorrect on 01-25). The caster must have a limited number of options (i.e., finite and countable). After the spell is cast, the caster may not cast another Guess for the same information until his experience level has increased.
COMMUNAL WAYS.2: Caster can speak and understand the language(s) of any supernatural entities favorable to his religion with the same fluency as he has in his native language.
COMMUNAL WAYS.3: Caster gains a vision of what will probably happen in the next minute if the caster takes a specified action.
COMMUNAL WAYS.4: Caster has a dream relating to a topic decided upon just before retiring; limited to once per night. The caster must sleep for a normal sleep cycle (minimum of four hours) for this spell to work.
COMMUNAL WAYS.5: As Intuitions I, except the caster gets to gaze 3 minutes into the future.
COMMUNAL WAYS.6: Caster gets a vision of the events surrounding the death of any 1 dead being through the eyes of the deceased, including a view of the killer, if any. Caster must be within 10' of the corpse. Corpse must have died within a number of years equal to the caster’s level.
COMMUNAL WAYS.7: Caster can open a channel with any 1 known being the caster is acquainted with. This spell will enable the caster to learn the specific location (direction and distance only) of the other being, making it possible for the caster to use a Channels spell (see Channels list). Note that this spell does not allow any sort of communication, although the target will know that a Channel has been opened to him.
COMMUNAL WAYS.8: As Intuitions I, except caster gets to gaze 5 minutes into the future.
COMMUNAL WAYS.9: Caster gains a +50 to his Attunement skill as applied to magic items tied to his religion (i.e., those based in Channeling magic and usable by the caster).
COMMUNAL WAYS.10: As Dream I, except the limit is 3 dreams/night on different topics.
COMMUNAL WAYS.11: As Intuitions I , except caster gets to gaze 10 minutes into the future.
COMMUNAL WAYS.12: Caster receives a “yes” or “no” to a question on a single topic; usable but once a day. This answer normally comes from the caster’s deity or some supernatural subordinate of that deity.
COMMUNAL WAYS.13: The target of this spell is a small pool of clear, clean water. The water must be calm (and remain so for the duration of the spell). The caster may designate a topic and a vision will appear in the pool that relates to the topic. This vision normally takes 5-50 rounds. This vision may be seen by anyone looking into the pool.
COMMUNAL WAYS.14: Caster can mentally communicate with one other person, who must be a member of the same religion.
COMMUNAL WAYS.15: As Channel Opening I, except range is 10 miles per level.
COMMUNAL WAYS.16: As Commune I, except caster may ask two questions.
COMMUNAL WAYS.17: As Dream I, except the limit is five dreams/night on different topics.
COMMUNAL WAYS.18: As Magic Basin I, except up to two topics may be designated (5-50 rounds are required for each vision).
COMMUNAL WAYS.19: Caster gets a vision of the events surrounding the death of any 1 dead being within 10 feet. Caster gets an understanding of the reason(s) the deceased died, exactly who the killer was (if any), and who was ultimately responsible (if anyone). The vision of who is ultimately responsible will be a quick montage of the connections between the killer and the person ultimately responsible. Corpse may be of any age.
COMMUNAL WAYS.20: As Commune I, except caster can receive a more detailed answer (e.g., a name or short phrase) to a question involving only one concept.
COMMUNAL WAYS.25: As Intuitions I, except caster may gaze into the future for a number of minutes equal to the caster’s level.
COMMUNAL WAYS.30: As Channel Opening I, except caster can open a channel to anyone the caster chooses, regardless of whether or not the caster is acquainted with the being. Caster must specify the type of being sought (e.g., a priest of a specific deity, a warrior with blue eyes, etc.), range is 1 mile per level. Channel will open to the nearest being matching the description, if any.
COMMUNAL WAYS.35: As Channel Opening I, except range is 100 miles per level.
COMMUNAL WAYS.40: As Magic Basin I, except up to one topic for every 10 levels (or fraction thereof) of the caster may be designated, and the visions take less objective time (although they will seem as subjectively long to the viewer), requiring only 1 round per vision.
COMMUNAL WAYS.50: Caster receives detailed information on a single topic question. This information comes directly from the caster’s deity.

CONCEALMENT MASTERY.1: Caster and objects on the caster’s person appear to be in shadow and thus are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus of between +25 and +75).
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.2: Up to three objects are made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or the object is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or the object makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or casts a spell of type E or F.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.3: Target inanimate object takes on the visual appearance of a similar object. For example, a small red key may appear as a small blue spoon. The object cannot vary in dimensions by more than 100% of actual size in any given dimension. This is strictly a visual illusion (and can be detected as such).
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.4: As Unseen III, except it covers one target and everything within 1' of the target.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.5: As Conceal I , except the caster may choose two options — affect one additional object, double the duration, increase the range to 50' for one object, or add a tactile sense to one concealed object.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.6: As Invisibility I, except the caster can vary the radius up to 1'. This makes the invisible target harder to see by reducing the “fringe effect.”
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.7: One melee attack (armed or unarmed) is covered by a moment of invisibility. Unless the target of that attack has the power to see the invisible, the target’s parry is subject to a -100 penalty (down to a minimum of 0 parry DB).
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.8: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.9: As Conceal III, except choose four options.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.10: As Unseen III, except up to 10 objects.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.11: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 20%.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.12: As Invisibility II, except caster can vary the radius up to 10', determined at the time of casting.
