CONCEALMENT MASTERY.13: Caster becomes almost impossible to read as to honesty or emotions (-100 to all attempts to use Social Awareness skill on the caster) and spells to read the caster’s mind or emotional state must make an RR roll against this spell or simply fail.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.14: Caster can see invisible things anywhere the caster can normally see; the caster’s attacks on invisible targets get no penalty (as they are not invisible to him).
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.15: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 30%.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.16: As Invisibility Sphere II, except caster can vary the radius by up to 20'.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.17: As Unseen Blow, except affects all melee attacks by the target during the spell’s duration.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.18: As Hide Thoughts, except for duration.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.19: As See Invisible, except the duration is 10 minutes per level.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.20: As Conceal III, except choose nineteen options.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.25: As Unseen III, except up to 1 object per level.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.30: As Invisibility I, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be the centers of constant 1' radii of invisibility.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.35: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 50%.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.40: As Conceal III , except choose one option per caster level.
CONCEALMENT MASTERY.50: As Invisibility I, except the caster can vary the radius of invisibility up to 1' and if the caster attacks he is only visible for the 5 seconds immediately following the attack. Likewise, he may cast a type E or F spell and become visible for the following 5 seconds. Violent blows do not affect this spell.

CONCUSSION MASTERY.1: Removes up to -30 in penalties from fatigue.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.3: Target is relieved of 1 round’s worth of accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.4: Reduces target’s concussion hit damage by 2 hits every round as long as caster concentrates; if caster is unconscious, this spell will operate on the caster without concentration.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.6: An injury from burns, chemical burns, scalding, or frostbite incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.8: Target is relieved of 3 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.9: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.10: As Regeneration I, except 4 hits per round.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.12: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.13: Target is relieved of 5 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.14: As Stun Relief I, except the range is 200'.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.15: As Regeneration I, except 6 hits per round.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.16: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.18: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing True, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.19: Target is relieved of 10 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.20: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing True , except an injury incurring any penalty.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.25: Target is healed of all concussion hit damage.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.30: As Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing, except all such injuries on the target.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.35: As Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing True, except all such injuries on the target.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.40: Target is relieved of all accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION MASTERY.50: Caster can use one of the lower level spells on this list each round.

CONCUSSION'S WAY.1: Any foreign matter causing an irritation (e.g., itching powder, poison ivy chemical, a splinter, allergen, or the like) is removed from the skin and exposed minor wounds of the target.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.3: An injury from burns, chemical burns, scalding, or frostbite incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.5: Target is relieved of 1 round of accumulated stun effects.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.6: As Light Exposure Relief, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.7: Reduces target’s concussion hit damage by 2 hits every round as long as caster concentrates; if caster is unconscious, this spell will operate on the caster without concentration.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.9: As Light Exposure Relief, except an injury incurring any penalty.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.12: As Regeneration I, except 4 hits per round.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.13: As Light Exposure Relief, except the recovery time is reduced from days to hours.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.16: As Medium Exposure Relief, except the recovery time is reduced from days to hours.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.17: As Stun Relief, except the range is 200'.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.18: As Regeneration I, except 6 hits per round.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.19: As Severe Exposure Relief, except the recovery time is reduced from days to hours.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.25: As Regeneration I, except 10 hits per round.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.30: Target is healed of all concussion hit damage.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.35: Up to one target per caster level is relieved of 1 round of stun effects.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.40: As Severe Exposure Relief, except the recovery time is reduced from days to rounds.
CONCUSSION'S WAY.50: Allows caster to heal all concussion hits in a number of targets equal to the level of the caster.

CONFUSING WAYS.1: Target is at -30 for all actions.
CONFUSING WAYS.2: Target is incapable of making decisions or initiating action, but may continue to fight current foes or in self-defense. The target may also follow orders from the target’s leader (if any), but will only be able to follow them so long as no ambiguities arise.
CONFUSING WAYS.3: Target has a -100 OB modification for missile attacks and a -50 penalty for all other actions involving sight.
CONFUSING WAYS.4: Target fears caster and attempts to flee. (This does not involve a Fear RR or the usual fear mechanics. Use a normal Essence or Mentalism RR and the result is flight for the specified duration.)
CONFUSING WAYS.5: Target becomes unbalanced. If the target is moving, the target trips and falls (prone and stunned (-50) for 1-5 rounds); any maneuver that the target was performing when affected by this spell automatically fails.
CONFUSING WAYS.6: As Stumble, except target also fumbles any weapon or item currently held; roll on the appropriate RMCL Chapter 11 Fumble Table.
CONFUSING WAYS.7: Target sees a nonexistent foe and must fight this foe until it is “defeated” (i.e., takes damage that would drop the target). Foe has same capabilities as target but does no damage (i.e., always misses).
CONFUSING WAYS.8: Target is spun about a few times, ending up facing 180° away from his original facing, then spends the rest of the round recovering from the disorientation.
CONFUSING WAYS.9: Target perceives the target’s weapon(s) as changed into some other similar weapon(s), which lacks the right balance even if familiar to the target. Target will only have half normal OB with the weapon(s) so long as this delusion remains in effect.
CONFUSING WAYS.10: Target sees all moving things clearly, but the caster can control the fixed scene that the target “sees”.
CONFUSING WAYS.12: Target sees moving things shifted between 6" and 18" from where they really are; any attack the target makes against a moving foe has a 50% chance of having no effect.
CONFUSING WAYS.13: A number of targets equal to the caster’s level can be distracted as in Distraction; they must all be in the caster’s field of vision.
CONFUSING WAYS.14: As Fear, except the spell is instantaneous and the duration is 1 minute per 5 failure.
CONFUSING WAYS.15: Target has amnesia, but loses no skills or abilities. This is a loss of personal memories and knowledge of own identity and background.
CONFUSING WAYS.16: As Confusion, except the spell is instantaneous and all beings (other than the caster) within an area encompassed by a radius of 50' are targets.
CONFUSING WAYS.17: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except the range is increased to 300'.
CONFUSING WAYS.18: As Fear, except the spell is instantaneous and the duration is 1 minute per 5 failure and all beings (other than the caster) within a fifty foot radius are targets.
CONFUSING WAYS.19: As Weapon Alteration, except target must also make an RR each round or fumble his weapon. If the target is using two weapons, he must make a separate RR for each weapon and may fumble twice in a round due to the spell. He may still fumble normally if he gets to make an attack.
CONFUSING WAYS.20: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except affects up to 20 targets.
CONFUSING WAYS.25: As Amnesia, except duration is permanent (unless dispelled).
CONFUSING WAYS.30: Target sees and hears a world in which everything real has an image, but the image is changed or distorted (e.g., a Dwarf might seem to be an Elf, a house might seem to be a cave, etc.).
CONFUSING WAYS.35: As Amnesia, except the spell is instantaneous and all beings (other than the caster) within an area encompassed by a radius of 50' are targets.
CONFUSING WAYS.40: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except the range is increased to 300' and it affects up to one target per level of the caster.
CONFUSING WAYS.50: As any single spell from this list of no more than 20th level, except affects up to one target per level of the caster and the range is 300'.

CONTROLLING SONGS.1: Targets will not take aggressive (offensive) action towards the caster.
CONTROLLING SONGS.2: Targets are calmed and cannot take aggressive (offensive) action during the performance. This spell also soothes pain and can offset up to -10 in penalties due to injury (until the spell ends).
CONTROLLING SONGS.3: Targets must dance (to the best of their ability). This requires Concentration and the dancers cannot choose to leave the area of effect unless a second RR is made. All dance-related maneuvers by the targets have a +25 bonus.
CONTROLLING SONGS.4: Targets are stunned (-50) while song lasts.
CONTROLLING SONGS.5: As Calming Song , except targets are only non-aggressive with respect to the caster and targets will be drawn to the caster. They will approach the caster as close as possible without touching the caster or having to fight for position, and they will follow the caster if he is moving.
CONTROLLING SONGS.6: Targets fall into a light sleep. Targets get another RR if someone attempts to wake them while this spell is still active. When the duration is up, the target can be awakened normally.
CONTROLLING SONGS.7: Targets regard the caster as a good friend.
CONTROLLING SONGS.8: Targets must make an RR versus Essence magic, but the results are treated as an RR versus Fear (see RMCL Section 14.3). Normal modifications against Fear do not apply.
CONTROLLING SONGS.9: Targets become emotionally open and regard the caster favorably. This adds +10 to any maneuver made to seduce a target of this spell by someone other than the caster and adds +10 to any Influence skill maneuver or +30 to any seduction attempt made by the caster to affect a target of this spell.
CONTROLLING SONGS.10: As Calming Song, except for area of effect, and it can offset up to -20 in injury-related penalties.
CONTROLLING SONGS.11: As Dancing Song, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.12: As Lullaby, except targets experience a sleep troubled by nightmares. For 24 hours after awakening, they will have a -40 penalty to any RR against fear or fear-inducing magic.
CONTROLLING SONGS.13: [RR Mod.: -25] As Fear’ s Song, except targets suffer a -25 modification to their RRs.
CONTROLLING SONGS.14: As Stunning Song, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.15: As Luring Song, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.16: Targets will attack nearest individual other than the caster. If two or more individuals are equidistant, the target will attack the one he would normally be most inclined to fight. If a target injures (inflicts a critical on) a loved one, a close friend, or a longtime comrade in arms, he may make a new RR.
CONTROLLING SONGS.17: As Lullaby, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.18: As Charming Song, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.19: [RR Mod.: -25] As Panic’ s Song, except for area of effect.
CONTROLLING SONGS.20: Targets will obey the caster’s commands, unless the action commanded is totally alien to the target’s nature (e.g., no suicidal or self-mutilating commands unless the target is already self-destructive). If a target cannot understand the command, it will simply take no action.
CONTROLLING SONGS.25: [RR Mod: -50] As Panic’ s Song, except for area of effect and RR modifier.
CONTROLLING SONGS.30: [RR Mod: -10] As Calming Song, except for area of effect, RR modifier, and a maximum of -30 in offset penalties.
CONTROLLING SONGS.35: [RR Mod: -10] As Seduction’ s Song, except for area of effect, RR modifier, and the caster’s bonuses are +50 to seduction attempts and +25 to other Influence maneuvers, while those other than the caster gain a +25 to seduction attempts where the object of the seduction is under the influence of this spell.
CONTROLLING SONGS.40: [RR Mod: -10] As Song of Command, except for area of effect and RR modifier.
CONTROLLING SONGS.50: [RR Mod: -10] As Fight Song, except for area of effect and RR modifier. Note: Area of Effect and Duration are treated unusually and there are other special rules for this list. See Section 10.

CREATIONS.1: Creates (out of thin air) a cloth up to 3'x6' in area, the cloth is made of typical local textile materials (of an inexpensive kind), suitable for use as either a blanket or as a simple garment.
CREATIONS.2: Allows caster to go without food or water and not suffer damage (i.e., provides nutrition and water for 1 day).
CREATIONS.3: Caster can produce sufficient water in any available receptacle to supply a being for one day.
CREATIONS.4: Caster can produce sufficient food from the surrounding area to feed one hearty appetite for one day.
CREATIONS.5: Allows caster to set a fire of 1'R; fire will then last as long as there is fuel. A target in the 1'R will take an A Heat critical. Caster may only target a specific location, not an individual, with this spell.
CREATIONS.6: Caster can produce a loaf of blessed bread that weighs half a pound and will support one being for one day; the loaf will lose potency in one month.
CREATIONS.7: As Water Production I, except caster can produce three days of water.
CREATIONS.8: As Food Production I, except caster can produce sufficient food to feed three beings for one day.
CREATIONS.9: Allows caster to enhance the efficacy of any one herb (growing or dead) with healing or durational effects. For healing herbs, recovery rolls receive an extra +25 bonus. For herbs with a durational effect, the duration is doubled. This spell may be employed only once per herb.
CREATIONS.10: As Water Production I, except caster can produce five days supply of water.
CREATIONS.11: As Food Production I, except caster can produce sufficient food to feed five beings for one day.
CREATIONS.12: As Nutrient Conjure I, except caster can produce three loaves of blessed bread.
CREATIONS.13: Caster can double the potency of any one herb (growing or dead). This has the effect of halving the quantity of herb needed for a full dose, i.e. if a normal dose requires one full leaf to be effective, following this spell, only half a leaf would be required. This spell may be employed only once/herb, and may not be combined with Herb Enhancement.
CREATIONS.14: Caster creates a plain tent, capable of sheltering up to four persons comfortably (or six uncomfortably) against local weather conditions. At the end of this spell, the tent simply disappears.
CREATIONS.15: Caster can produce a plant up to 10' in height and/or width; plant must be representative of the region.
CREATIONS.16: As Water Production I, except caster can create one day of water per level (e.g., a 17th level Cleric could create 17 days of water).
CREATIONS.17: As Food Production I, except caster can produce sufficient food to feed a number of beings equal to the caster’s level.
CREATIONS.18: As Herb Mastery, except caster may enhance one herb per level. Herbs must all be of the same type.
CREATIONS.19: As Tent of the Gods, except creates a log cabin suitable for 12 persons in comfort or 20 persons uncomfortably.
CREATIONS.20: Caster can create an animal representative of the surrounding region and weighing no more than 10 lbs; animal will be friendly to caster.
CREATIONS.25: As Nutrient Conjure I, except caster can produce a number of loaves of blessed bread equal to the caster’s level.
CREATIONS.30: As Lesser Plant Production , except that caster can create one plant with a height and/or width of one foot per caster level (e.g., a 30th level could produce a 30' high tree with a 30' maximum width); plant must be representative of the local area.
CREATIONS.35: As Tent of the Gods, except creates a stone tower suitable for 16 persons in comfort or 30 uncomfortably. This is a defensive structure, with a barred door and whatever standard structural design the caster’s culture would use for a fortified stone tower.
CREATIONS.40: As Greater Plant Production, except plant need not be native to the local area.
CREATIONS.50: As Lesser Animal Production, except that caster can produce an animal with a maximum weight of one pound per caster level. The animal must be a native of the area.

CURSEBREAKING.1: When the caster is a target of a spell from an Evil spell list, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
CURSEBREAKING.2: As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
CURSEBREAKING.3: As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
CURSEBREAKING.4: As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
CURSEBREAKING.5: Any active spell from an Evil spell list that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
CURSEBREAKING.6: [RR Mod.: -20] Nullifies a curse for the duration of this spell. The curse is not dispelled and takes effect after the duration of this spell.
CURSEBREAKING.7: As Dispel Evil I , except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
CURSEBREAKING.8: As Dispel Evil I, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
CURSEBREAKING.9: As Dispel Evil I, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
CURSEBREAKING.10: Caster can determine nature and origin of any one curse, including the name of the one who bequeathed it.
CURSEBREAKING.11: Any active Evil spell that is (at least partly) within the radius of effect must make an RR (use the spell’s level for the RR). If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
CURSEBREAKING.12: Caster learns the current location of the caster of a given curse, so long as the caster is within range. Location is direction plus distance. The curse must have been previously analyzed with Curse Tales.
CURSEBREAKING.13: [RR Mod.: -20] As Neutralize Curse I, except curse is nullified for one hour per level.
CURSEBREAKING.14: As Dispel Evil Sphere I, except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
CURSEBREAKING.15: As Dispel Evil Sphere I , except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
CURSEBREAKING.16: As Dispel Evil Sphere I, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
CURSEBREAKING.17: As Find Curser I, except range is 10 miles per level.
CURSEBREAKING.18: [RR Mod: -20] As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects all spells, “incoming” spells make their RR at -20, and duration is 10 minutes per level.
CURSEBREAKING.19: As Dispel Evil Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
CURSEBREAKING.20: Dispels a curse if the curse fails an RR. The curse’s level is the level of the caster of the curse. If the curse is not dispelled, the caster cannot try again until he has gone up another level of experience.
CURSEBREAKING.25: As Dispel Channeling Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
CURSEBREAKING.30: [RR Mod.: -50] As Uncurse, except curse makes its RR with a -50 penalty.
CURSEBREAKING.35: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
CURSEBREAKING.40: As Dispel Mentalism Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
CURSEBREAKING.50: As Dispel Evil Sphere V, except affects all spells and duration is one minute per level. Any given spell need only resist this spell once, but any spell coming into the area of effect while this spell is active must make its RR or be dispelled. Sphere moves with the caster.

CURSES.1: Detects a curse on an item or person.
CURSES.2: Target can see at night as if it were day, but he can only see in the day as if it were the darkest night.
CURSES.3: Target will never voluntarily fly by any means, nor climb any greater height than 100'.
CURSES.4: Target can only eat bread or dairy products. Anything else will aggravate his ulcer (unless he makes a physical RR vs 4th level) and he will be incapacitated for 1-10 hours.
CURSES.5: Target cannot move faster than a walking pace on his own (he may be carried or ride at a faster pace).
CURSES.6: Target can only eat green leaves. Eating other food incapacitates him (if he fails a physical RR vs 6 th level) for 1-10 hours.
CURSES.7: Target will never voluntarily swim, enter any water (even a bath), or get into a boat.
CURSES.8: All of target’s fumble numbers (before any reduction due to skill) when attacking are doubled (e.g., a fumble number of 4 becomes 8). When rolling absolute maneuvers, any result of 50 or less is absolute failure. When rolling percentage maneuvers, an “F” result always results in some additional problem arising.
CURSES.9: Target loses all Power Points if target is a Channeling spell user. He cannot cast any spells from the Channeling realm, even by use of magic items, including Runes. A hybrid user of Channeling will only lose half of his Power Points, but cannot cast his Base lists as well as those which are pure Channeling. These PP cannot be regained until the duration of the spell ends or the curse is removed.
CURSES.10: Target can only speak and write in his native language.
CURSES.12: Target loses 1 Power Point every 10 minutes. Power Points are recovered in a normal fashion, but begin to leak away as soon as they are regained.
CURSES.13: Target’s appearance becomes extremely attractive. If anyone sees the uncovered face of the target or gets within 3' (and is of an appropriate sexual orientation and fails an RR), that person will attempt to possess and carry off the target (fighting if necessary). Certain persons will get modifications to their RRs (positive or negative) based on outlook (a hedonist from a tribe that routinely kidnaps brides would be at a large penalty, an ascetic who had taken an oath of celibacy would have a bonus).
CURSES.14: Target’s blood will coagulate and create a blood clot around the area of any cut wound (bleeding at least one hit per round). The clot will cut off circulation to the area fed by the given vessel and target will lose the affected body section after 2-20 minutes due to system damage (e.g., a thigh wound might ultimately mean loss of leg; a neck wound would be fatal). An Extremely Hard (-30) Medicine maneuver, taking 1d10 rounds, will prevent loss of the body part, as will a Joining spell from Blood Law, Blood Mastery, or Blood Ways.
CURSES.15: Target’s appearance becomes hideous and horrifying. If a person sees the uncovered face of the target or gets within 3' of the target, he must make an RR. If he fails by more than 50, he attacks the target. If he fails by 50 or less, he will make every effort short of endangering his own safety to get away from the target. Even if he makes his RR, the viewer will experience extreme distaste for the target. Friends and family will gain bonuses to their RRs, but even they will regard the target with pity and disgust.
CURSES.16: Target will lie 50% of the time, regardless of the topic.
CURSES.17: Any time that the target rolls doubles (e.g., a natural roll of 44), the equipment he is using must check for breakage.
CURSES.18: Target may only speak the truth.
CURSES.19: Once per day (at a random time), the target will experience the pain from an old wound. For new characters, treat this as an A Krush critical strike (rerolling a lethal result) 50% of the time and d10 concussion hits the other 50% of the time. Alternatively, the GM can re-open a specific old wound.
CURSES.20: Target will move at fastest possible pace until he collapses of exhaustion (-100 total penalties). He may be restrained, but whenever freed, or he rests enough to recover from penalties to below -50 he will begin to run again.
CURSES.25: Every time the target is in a combat situation near friends who are within 10', there is a 10% chance each round (non-cumulative) that he will attack one of his friends (randomly determine which one if there are multiple possible targets) and attempt to kill that friend. For these purposes, a “friend” includes any companion, ally, or associate.
CURSES.30: As Excommunication, except effect is permanent until removed.
CURSES.35: Target becomes unable to tolerate magical herbs or oral medicines, vomiting them up without benefit. Healing times for all injuries are doubled.
CURSES.40: Target can never be a willing target of any spell cast by another.
CURSES.50: Target may not attack any “evil” creature. The target will obey the commands of any evil being who is 10 or more levels higher than himself, but can command (+30 to Influence and +60 Leadership) any “Evil” creature whose level is at least 10 below the target’s level. See Section 10.8 for definition of “Evil”.

DAMAGE RESISTANCE.1: Caster is protected from natural heat up to 176°F (80°C) (treat as if caster were in 68°F [20°C] temperature). For temperatures above 176°F, subtract 108°F (60°C) to determine the effective temperature for the target. Caster also receives +20 to all RRs vs. heat spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental fire/heat attacks.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.2: Caster is protected from natural cold down to 14°F (-10°C) (treat as if caster were in 68°F [20°C] temperature). For temperatures below 14°F, add 54°F (30°C) to determine the effective temperature for the target. Caster also receives +20 to all RRs vs. cold spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental cold/ice attacks.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.3: Caster is protected from natural light and light changes (instantly adjusting vision to changing light intensity to never be blinded by glare, sudden light, or sudden darkness). Caster also receives +20 to all RRs vs. light or electricity spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental light/electricity attacks.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.4: Caster is protected from falls of up to 50'. For falls longer than 50', subtract 50' to determine the effective length of the fall. Caster also receives +20 to all RRs vs. spells that do Impact criticals and +20 DB vs elemental attacks doing Impact criticals.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.5: Caster is able to sustain an additional 25% of his total concussion hits (round off fractional hits) before passing out; hits are still taken and remain when the spell lapses.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.6: Caster is relieved of 1 round’s worth of accumulated stun effects.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.7: Delays the effect of a poison on the caster. If the poison is not eliminated before the spell expires, the poison will affect the caster at that time.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.8: As Heat Resistance, except for duration, spell is not instantaneous, and bonus against magical attacks is only +10.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.9: As Cold Resistance, except for duration, spell is not instantaneous, and bonus against magical attacks is only +10.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.10: As Unpain I, except 50% additional hits may be sustained.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.11: Caster is relieved of 3 rounds of accumulated stun effects.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.12: Any poison inside the caster must make an RR with a -20 modifier or be neutralized. In any case, it delays the effects of the poison for the duration of the spell.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.13: Caster takes half hits damage from all physical (non-magical) attacks.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.14: This spell will awaken the caster from sleep the round following casting (caster can preset conditions under which the spell will activate).
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.15: As Unpain I, except 75% additional hits may be sustained.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.16: Caster is relieved of 5 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.17: As Heat Resistance, except caster is immune to natural heat and is treated as one Size larger when taking damage from heat spells.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.18: As Cold Resistance, except caster is immune to natural cold and is treated as one Size larger when taking damage from cold spells.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.19: As Light Resistance, except caster is immune to natural light/electricity and is treated as one Size larger when taking damage from electrical spells.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.20: As Unpain I, except 100% additional hits may be sustained.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.25: As Neutralize Poison, except poison has a -70 modifier to its RR.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.30: As Awake, except there is no delay and it can be triggered by any normally perceivable previously declared condition within 50' (e.g., anyone entering the area, a stranger entering the area, or a drawn weapon in the area).
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.35: As Heat Resistance True , Cold Resistance True, and Light Resistance True.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.40: Caster is relieved of all accumulated stun effects.
DAMAGE RESISTANCE.50: Caster ignores all pain; he ignores the penalties for lost hits and will never become unconscious due to hits taken, nor will he die from hit loss while this spell is in effect.

DARK CHANNELS.1: Detects any active spell or active magical item from an Evil source, or any entity capable of casting Evil spells; caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round. (See Section 10.4 for more about detection spells.)
DARK CHANNELS.2: [RR Mod.: -10] Caster points his arm (elbow locked and fist clenched) at the target and channels raw power. The target is stunned (-50).
DARK CHANNELS.3: When this spell is cast, the 1st level spell that the caster begins to cast (or prepare) the next round will be “channeled” to another spell user of the Channeling realm. The “channeled” spell is transmitted to the other spell user when it would normally take effect, and must be used by the receiving spell user immediately or delayed as described in Section 4.8 (treating the receiving spell user as the caster of the spell). All normal restrictions on the transmitted spell apply. The caster of the Channels spell must be able to see the receiver or know exactly where the receiver is (direction and distance or specific place). The receiver must know exactly when the spell is coming (but need not know what the spell sent is). For example, if a Cleric was at a certain altar at a certain time (midnight of full moon) ready to receive a spell; then another spell user (or deity) who knew this could use Channels I to send a spell to that Cleric. Because the spell is passed through the aura of the receiving character, the spell is cast at the level of the receiving character (including any level-based parameters).
DARK CHANNELS.4: Causes a “holy” being or a Channeler of a “good” power to be unable to approach closer than 100' to the caster and to retreat, if possible, to maintain that distance. If the target fails its RR by more than 30, it must flee (if of this plane) or be banished back to its home plane of existance (in which case
DARK CHANNELS.5: [RR Mod.: -10] Caster points his arm (elbow locked and fist clenched) at the target and channels raw power. The target falls into a deep, magical sleep.
DARK CHANNELS.6: As Channels I, except a 1st-3rd level spell may be transmitted.
DARK CHANNELS.7: Caster can open a channel with any 1 known being the caster is acquainted with. This spell will enable the caster to learn the specific location (direction and distance only) of the other being, making it possible for the caster to use a Channels spell. Note that this spell does not allow any sort of communication, although the target will know that a Channel has been opened to him.
DARK CHANNELS.8: [RR Mod.: -10] Caster points his arm (elbow locked and fist clenched) at the target and channels raw power. The target is blinded.
DARK CHANNELS.9: As Repel Holy I, except affects up to Type III or 15th level targets.
DARK CHANNELS.10: Caster may cast one of the Black Channel I spells (see Section 10.7).
DARK CHANNELS.11: As Channels I, except a 1st-5th level spell may be transmitted.
DARK CHANNELS.12: As Repel Holy I, except affects up to Type IV or 20th level targets.
DARK CHANNELS.13: [RR Mod: -20] Target’s “soul” is torn from the target’s body (or other physical location if the soul is otherwise housed); it remains in whatever place “souls” go for 1 week/10 failure. The soul can be returned early only through Lifegiving or Reuniting. Target is unconscious until soul is restored; even subconscious activity (i.e., healing, etc.) suffers a -75 penalty.
DARK CHANNELS.14: The next Channels spell cast by the caster of this spell may be sent to anyone, regardless of the realm of the other individual. However, the spell channeled must be from an Evil spell list.
DARK CHANNELS.15: Caster may cast one of the Black Channel II spells (see Section 10.7).
DARK CHANNELS.16: As Channel Opening I, except range is 10 miles per level.
DARK CHANNELS.17: As Channels I, except a 1st-7th level spell may be transmitted.
DARK CHANNELS.18: As Repel Holy I, except affects up to Type VI or 30th level targets.
DARK CHANNELS.19: As Channels I, except a 1st-10th level spell may be transmitted.
DARK CHANNELS.20: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution, except the “soul” can only be brought back through Lifegiving.
DARK CHANNELS.25: Caster may cast one of the Black Channel III spells (see Section 10.7).
DARK CHANNELS.30: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution, except the “soul” is utterly destroyed.
DARK CHANNELS.35: As Channels I , except a 1st-20th level spell may be transmitted.
DARK CHANNELS.40: As Channel Opening I, except range is 100 miles per level.
DARK CHANNELS.50: Opens a direct channel to the caster’s deity (or other power source); results depend on the deity’s personality, motives, and powers. Any spell the deity can cast may be transmitted through the gate. Note: See section 10.8 for guidelines for handling “good” and “evil”. Absolution does not kill the body, so Dark Absolution leaves a living, but soulless body.   

DARK SUMMONS.1: The caster can attune himself to a small creature of animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw) to serve as his familiar. The caster must obtain the creature (which can be no more than 10% of caster’s own mass) through normal means and cast the spell on the animal once each day for 1 week (associating with the animal for 2 hours each day). The caster can then control the familiar and view the world through its senses by concentrating on it (must be within 50' per caster level). If the animal is killed, the caster will have a -25 modification to all actions for 2 weeks.
DARK SUMMONS.2: Caster can instantly summon a first level non-intelligent (i.e., possessing the Animalistic or Mindless flaw) living creature, which appears somewhere with the spell’s range. The caster can specify the general type of creature, but exactly what the creature is should be determined randomly (e.g., the caster could specify “four-legged, hoofed” and could get a zebra, horse, camel, etc.). If the creature can understand the caster’s communication, the creature can be controlled by normal communication. Otherwise, control is maintained through concentration of the caster. If the creature summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in its own perception), the duration for this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the creature is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level. At the end of the spell’s duration, the summoned creature will disappear back to whence it came.
DARK SUMMONS.3: This spell is cast upon the caster’s familiar. However, the familiar must be a type of animal that is usually associated with evil in the caster’s culture. This spell transfers part of the caster’s “life” into the familiar. This spell must be cast upon the familiar every day for a month (associating with the animal for 2 hours a day). The familiar retains all of its old abilities and it gains special abilities. The familiar will become a malformed version of the animal it once was (more grotesque). The caster can view the world through the senses of the creature at a range of 250' per level. The creatures AT changes to 3 (if not already higher), and it gains a +10 to its DB and OB. The caster can cast spells through the familiar as if the caster were actually where the familiar is (this includes casting “self” spells on the familiar): range for this ability is 50' per level. The creature will make all of its RRs at half the caster’s level or its own level, whichever is higher. The familiar can be sent on long-range missions, although it will be limited in its ability to interpret ambiguous instructions or adapt to unexpected events. It can always find its way back to the caster through their bond unless the caster is magically hidden. If the familiar dies, the caster will lose one quarter of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks (and these cannot be restored by any means until the time has passed).
DARK SUMMONS.4: As Summons I, except the caster chooses one of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options to the higher level Summons spells, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).
DARK SUMMONS.5: Caster can bind one creature (up to second level) with animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw). The bound creature must follow the commands of the caster (the caster does not have to concentrate). The caster can only have as many creatures bound as he has levels. If the creature is ever outside the range of the spell, the spell is dispelled.
DARK SUMMONS.6: As Summons II, except two options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.7: The target of this spell is any creature with animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw) that has been bound (with a Binding spell). The caster can give a simple, straight-forward task for the creature to attempt to complete. The creature will do its best to complete the task (within the duration of the spell). When the duration of this spell expires, the creature returns to its normal “unbound” state. Once this spell is cast, the creature can leave the range of the spell without ending its duration.
DARK SUMMONS.8: As Summons II, except four options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.9: As Binding II, except binds up to a 5th level creature.
DARK SUMMONS.10: As Summons II, except six options may be chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.11: The target of this spell is a familiar that has had Investiture I successfully cast on it. This spell puts more of the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for six months (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 1,000 feet per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 200' per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 5, the DB bonus improves to +20 and
DARK SUMMONS.12: As Summons II, except nine options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.13: As Binding II, except binds up to a 10th level creature.
DARK SUMMONS.14: As Summons II, except eleven options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.15: As Order Bound II, except maximum duration is 10 hours.
DARK SUMMONS.16: As Summons II, except fourteen options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.17: As Binding II, except binds up to a 15th level creature.
DARK SUMMONS.18: As Summons II, except sixteen options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.19: The target of this spell is a familiar that has had Investiture II successfully cast on it. This spell puts even more of the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for one year (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 1 mile per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 1000' per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 7, the DB bonus improves to +30 and the OB bonus improves to +50, and it makes RRs at the higher of its own level or the caster’s level. It retains its venom from Investiture II. Its form becomes completely grotesque and could not be associated with the animal it once was. If the familiar dies, the caster loses three quarters of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks.
DARK SUMMONS.20: As Summons II , except nineteen options are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.25: As Binding II, except binds a creature of any level.
DARK SUMMONS.30: As Order Bound II , except maximum duration is 1 hour per caster’s level.
DARK SUMMONS.35: As Summons II, except a number of options equal to the caster’s level are chosen.
DARK SUMMONS.40: The target of this spell is a familiar that has had Investiture III successfully cast on it. This spell puts even more of the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for two years (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 10 miles per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 1 mile per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 9, the DB bonus improves to +50 and the OB bonus improves to +75, and it continues to make RRs at the higher of its own level or the caster’s level. It retains its venom from Investiture II, but potency is the higher of the familiar’s level or the caster’s level. It cannot really become more grotesque. If the familiar dies, the caster loses all of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks, spending that period in a coma.
DARK SUMMONS.50: Caster can use any lower level spell on this list, once per round.

DARKNESS.1: Reduces illumination to a dark night in a 20'R area about the point touched.
DARKNESS.2: Target can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.
DARKNESS.3: Caster can vary the intensity of darkness in the area, but he cannot lighten the area past its natural state.
DARKNESS.4: As Darkness II, except radius is 50'.
DARKNESS.5: As Darkness II, except radius is 100'.
DARKNESS.6: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.
DARKNESS.7: As Dark Control II, except range and radius are 50'.
DARKNESS.8: Caster can create the visual illusion of either shadowy figures or actual shadows (as many as the caster’s level); they will move when he concentrates.
DARKNESS.9: As Darkness II, except radius is 300'.
DARKNESS.10: Darkens a 20'R area, no light (except Utterlight) can exist within this radius.
DARKNESS.11: As Dark Control II, except range and radius are 100'.
DARKNESS.12: As Darkvision, except the target can see as far as he would in normal daylight.
DARKNESS.13: As Lesser Utterdark, except radius is 100'.
DARKNESS.14: As Darkness II , except radius is 500'.
DARKNESS.15: Creates a “cloud” of darkness that has a 100'R and drifts with the wind. The darkness can vary in intensity from an overcast day to the dark of night.
DARKNESS.16: As Nightvision, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
DARKNESS.17: As Lesser Utterdark, except radius is 200'.
DARKNESS.18: As Darkvision, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
DARKNESS.19: As Clouds of Darkness I, except for area of effect.
DARKNESS.20: As Darkness II, except radius is 1,000'.
DARKNESS.25: As Lesser Utterdark, except radius is 300'.
DARKNESS.30: As Darkness II, except radius is 100' per level of the caster.
DARKNESS.35: As Darkvision True, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
DARKNESS.40: As Darkness V, except duration is permanent.
DARKNESS.50: As Clouds of Darkness I , except radius is 1 mile per level of the caster.

DELVING.1: Caster receives basic idea of item’s purpose, if any.
DELVING.2: Detects magical power in an item, but not the realm or how much. A different item can be examined each round by concentrating.
DELVING.3: Gives a general idea of the place of origin of an item.
DELVING.4: Once the caster has detected magical power in an object, this spell will reveal what realm of magic is involved.
DELVING.5: The caster learns if there is a curse on the item.
DELVING.6: Caster learns the origins of an item’s power.
DELVING.7: Caster gets details of where, when, and how the examined stone was worked.
DELVING.8: Caster gets a vision of a significant event in the item’s past.
DELVING.9: Caster gets a vision of a significant event in the person’s past.
DELVING.10: Caster learns the significant details about an item’s construction and purpose (not specific powers).
DELVING.11: Caster gets a vision up to 1 hour/level into the past; desired time can be set within ±30 min. Vision must be associated with an item or place. Vision can last up to 1 min/level, if the caster concentrates and remains inactive. Once the caster breaks concentration, the spell ends.
DELVING.12: Gives details of where, when, how, and by whom the examined text was written. This does not provide the ability to read the text, but if the caster can read the text, this spell assists in comprehending it (+50 to any relevant maneuvers).
DELVING.13: Gives major abilities and powers of an item.
DELVING.14: Gives a vision of the caster and circumstances of the casting of an active spell. Caster must touch a physical manifestation of the spell or a person or object under the influence of the spell.
DELVING.15: Gives a vision of how someone died and an image of the killer, if any. This spell must be cast within 24 hours of the death and must be cast at the place of death or in the presence of the body. Vision occurs in real time and covers the dying process.
DELVING.16: Gives a complete rundown on an item’s non-magical construction, powers, and purpose. This spell will not reveal anything relating to magical powers the item may possess.
DELVING.17: When cast just before a Past Vision spell, it allows caster to lock onto a specific event in an item’s past, and then examine that event with the Past Vision.
DELVING.18: As Death’ s Memory, except that it may be cast within one week of the death.
DELVING.19: The caster gains complete information on the item’s construction, purpose, magical abilities, and any non-magical bonuses.
DELVING.20: As Past Vision I, except time range is 1 day/level (error: ±1 hour) and vision can last up to 10 min/level.
DELVING.25: As Past Vision I, except time range is 1 month/level (error: ±1 day) and vision can last up to 1 hour/level.
DELVING.30: As Past Vision I , except time range is 1 year/level (error: ±1 week) and vision can last up to 5 hours/level.
DELVING.35: Caster may revisit any past experience. The memory is accurately enhanced, allowing the caster to note details that were missed at the time and recall vividly details that had been forgotten or misremembered.
DELVING.40: As Item Analysis, except allows analysis of 1 item per round.
DELVING.50: As Past Vision I, except time range is 10 year/level (error: ±1 month) and vision can last up to 10 hour/level (each hour only takes 10 minutes of real time).

DELVING WAYS.1: Caster can measure a distance or length so long as both ends are within sight or caster can measure either the weight or the volume of one item by touching it or the caster can know both the temperature and barometric pressure.
DELVING WAYS.2: Caster can read text written in an unknown language, but only understands basic concepts of it.
DELVING WAYS.3: Gives nature and origin of natural stone, and when and how worked stone was obtained and worked.
DELVING WAYS.4: As Stone Analysis, except metal may be examined.
DELVING WAYS.5: As Stone Analysis, except a gas may be examined.
DELVING WAYS.6: As Stone Analysis, except wood or other plant materials may be examined.
DELVING WAYS.7: As Text Analysis I , except gives a complete technical analysis (vocabulary and syntax) but not an understanding of idioms, jargon, implications, or cultural references.
DELVING WAYS.8: As Stone Analysis , except a liquid may be examined.
DELVING WAYS.9: As Stone Analysis , except items composed of organic materials of animal origins (e.g., bone, leather, ivory) may be examined. This will identify rune paper.
DELVING WAYS.10: Gives significant details about an item’s construction and purpose (not specific powers).
DELVING WAYS.11: Provides analysis of an active spell, giving its duration and the profession of its caster and its type (not its level or exactly what spell it is).
DELVING WAYS.12: Caster gains a knowledge of any non-magical bonus possessed by the target object.
DELVING WAYS.13: Caster gains a +30 bonus to any maneuvers involving mathematical calculations.
DELVING WAYS.14: Gives information concerning a being’s death (weapon, spell, time since, etc.); must be cast in place of death (within 24 hr) or in the presence of body (no time limit).
DELVING WAYS.15: As Text Analysis II, except everything but implications is known (e.g., answers to riddles are not known automatically).
DELVING WAYS.16: One item, person, or place may be examined to see if it has magical power, which realm the power is from, a general idea of its origin, and its basic configuration.
DELVING WAYS.17: Caster’s awareness leaves the caster’s body (which is inactive) and may travel at 1 mile/min; however it can only travel 10'/rnd when in solid material or when observing the world. If the caster overstays the duration, he must make an RR (against Essence) modified by -50 or die (attack level is equal to the number of rounds overstayed). Caster’s awareness can see and hear normally.
DELVING WAYS.18: As Death Analysis, except gives a visual image of the killer (if any) and a vague reason for death (e.g., revenge, robbery, accident, etc.).
DELVING WAYS.19: Gives nature and origin of a work of art, including who the (primary) artist was, what materials and tools were used, when and where the work was done, and the intended purpose and message of the work.
DELVING WAYS.20: Any of the lower level spells may be used together on one item, person, or place (Conveyance is not included).
DELVING WAYS.25: As Analysis, except one item per round may be examined.
DELVING WAYS.30: As Power Analysis, except exact origin, creator, and detailed purpose are given.
DELVING WAYS.35: As Death’ s Delving, except caster can sense direction and distance to the killer, the body, and to the killing weapon (if any) so long as the distance does not exceed 1 mile per level of the caster.
DELVING WAYS.40: The caster gains complete information on the item’s construction, purpose, magical abilities, and any non-magical bonuses.
DELVING WAYS.50: As Conveyance, except rate is 10 mile/min (50'/rnd through solids or while observing).

DEMONIC PACTS.1: Caster’s mind is filled with dark secrets tugging at his subconscious mind. Any Lore or Science maneuvers on a demon-related topic are made by the caster with a +25 bonus.
DEMONIC PACTS.2: Caster and one demon seal an agreement whereby this spell binds both of them to keep the bargain they have made. The spell will last until the bargain expires due to a time limit agreed upon or simply no longer applies. It is extremely hard to break this spell; it receives a +100 to its RR to resist any form of Dispel or other magic to remove or nullify it. The two parties to the agreement cannot attempt to circumvent the Oath without triggering the punishment for violation of the bargain. Upon any violation of the bargain, the offending party takes an E Holy critical, then on the following round takes a D Holy critical and so on until taking an A Holy critical on the fifth round. These criticals are not modified by size.
DEMONIC PACTS.3: Allows a demon to stay on this plane while it performs a mission for the caster. Caster and demon must have already made the Oath of Binding . Duration is until the demon fulfills its mission (and returns to the caster if it has not yet received its reward and the mission has left it away from the caster), its mission ends in failure (e.g., the item it was sent to fetch is destroyed), or the maximum duration of 1 hour per caster level has elapsed. While this spell is active, the demon can only pursue its mission (or return to the caster once the mission is completed). When this spell ends, unless some other spell allows the demon to stay, it will return to its home plane after a grace period of 1 minute per level of the demon.
DEMONIC PACTS.4: For a period of one week, the caster must cast this spell and spill some of his own blood (taking 1-10 hits of damage in the process) each day. At the end of that week, the caster will have attracted a Stage I familiar spirit into this world and established a bond to it. See Section 10.3 for details on the familiar spirit and its relationship to the caster.
DEMONIC PACTS.5: Caster gains a +25 bonus to his choice of any one Composition, Crafting, Lore, Medical, Performance Art, or V ocation skill. This bonus lasts until the caster casts another Diabolical Skill spell (only one Diabolical Skill spell may be active at one time), the caster touches or is touched by a Holy object (includes Unholy object if the Unholiness is associated with a dark god rather than a demon), is on sacred ground, or is affected by any non-Informational spells from a non-Evil user of Channeling.
DEMONIC PACTS.6: Allows caster to observe any location on the same plane in a pool of blood (blood must not have congealed; spell will prevent congealing during spell duration). This spell has no limits on distance and can focus in on any known place, being, or object that the caster is personally familiar with or can describe well enough to uniquely identify. It is subject to magical protections against information spells and such protections are automatically successful. This spell is also unable to reveal anything on holy ground, though it can spy upon holy objects.
DEMONIC PACTS.7: As Demonic Mission I, except maximum duration is 1 day per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.8: This spell must be cast on the caster’s Stage I familiar spirit every day for two weeks. At the end of that time, more of the spirit’s strength will have been drawn from its own plane, allowing it to manifest as a Stage II familiar spirit. See Section 10.3 for the various Stages of the familiar spirit.
DEMONIC PACTS.9: As Diabolical Skill I, except bonus is +50.
DEMONIC PACTS.10: Frees a demon from any constraining Conjuring Circle and allows it to remain for 10 minutes per caster level until it returns to its home plane.
DEMONIC PACTS.11: As Demonic Vision I, except allows the caster to hear what is going on at the site looked upon.
DEMONIC PACTS.12: As Familiar Spirit II, except the caster’s Stage II familiar becomes Stage III in a three week process.
DEMONIC PACTS.13: As Demonic Mission I, except maximum duration is 1 week per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.14: As Diabolical Skill I, except bonus is +75.
DEMONIC PACTS.15: As Demonic Vision II , except caster sees the scene as if it were well lit, even if it is in total darkness.
DEMONIC PACTS.16: As Familiar Spirit II, except the caster’s Stage III familiar becomes Stage IV in a four week process.
DEMONIC PACTS.17: As Unleash Demon I , except duration is 1 hour per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.18: As Demonic Mission I, except maximum duration is 1 year per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.19: As Diabolical Skill I, except bonus is +100.
DEMONIC PACTS.20: As Familiar Spirit II, except the caster’s Stage IV familiar becomes Stage V in a five week process.
DEMONIC PACTS.25: As Unleash Demon I , except duration is 1 day per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.30: As Familiar Spirit II, except the caster’s Stage V familiar becomes Stage VI in a six week process.
DEMONIC PACTS.35: This spell is used only once, if ever. Using it, the caster transforms himself into a Lich, one of the most feared of the undead. If the caster fails when attempting this spell, he will simply die. With success, the caster becomes one of the undead without ever actually dying. At this point, the caster is sustained by magic and dwells in a place between life and death.
DEMONIC PACTS.40: As Unleash Demon I, except duration is 1 week per caster level.
DEMONIC PACTS.50: As Cheat Death, except caster’s organs are transferred into a ready container (jar, box, chest, etc.). The caster then becomes a somewhat more powerful Classic Lich and can only be truly destroyed if the container with his organs is destroyed. Note: The desires of demons vary just as those of mortals do, but they generally desire freedom upon the mortal plane, blood, and material goods of significant value. Their price for aid varies depending on what is asked, the kind and individual personality of the demon, and other factors. A foolish bargain can rebound on the caster. Some demons deal in “souls”, but as such can only be offered once, one should haggle most carefully before making that trade. A demon can only be affected by one Unleash Demon spell during any one stay on a given plane. See more about dealing with summoned demons in Section 10.

DEMONIC SUMMONS.1: Caster empowers a circular diagram (a simple pentacle will suffice, but many prefer something more elaborate) of between five and ten feet in radius to confine a demon within its borders. This conjuring circle will prevent any Type I demon from leaving or attacking (including casting any spells or using other magical abilities) across this circle. If a demon is left in the circle until the duration expires, the demon is freed and may spend 1 minute per demon level on this plane before returning to its home plane.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.2: One Type I demon is instantly banished back to the plane whence it came. If the demon is currently confined to a Conjuring Circle, it makes its RR with a penalty of -25.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.3: Summons a Type I demon which appears at a point within range designated by the caster (within a Conjuring Circle is usually a good idea). It takes the demon two rounds to materialize, during which it is possible to identify the kind of demon appearing (using Creature Lore: Demon). If the demon is not within a Conjuring Circle, it may do as it wishes and can remain for 1 minute per demon level before vanishing back to its home plane. If it is within a Conjuring Circle it remains there until something causes it to be released or banished. When casting this spell, the caster must roll a Spell Mastery maneuver (difficulty Easy). If the Spell Mastery is a Success, the kind of demon that appears is up to the caster; in the case of Absolute Success, the caster may select what level the demon is from the possible range of levels for that kind of demon (or specify a particular demon, if one is known). Partial Success means that the caster knows what kind of demon he is getting (does not need to attempt Creature Lore: Demon). Failure means a random demon of the correct Type is summoned. Absolute Failure means that the caster has summoned a random demon of the next higher Type (in the case of Demon Summoning I, this would be a Type II demon).
DEMONIC SUMMONS.4: Demon obeys the caster for the duration of this spell. The demon vanishes back to its home plane at the end of this spell. If the demon is trapped within a Conjuring Circle, it makes its RR with a -25 penalty. However, if the demon succeeds in its RR, it is freed from any confinement and may stay in this world for 1 minute per level of the demon. A failed control attempt will usually anger a demon. This spell affects demons of Type I only.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.5: As Conjuring Circle I, except will confine a demon of Type II or lower.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.6: As Banish Demon I, except a demon of Type II or lower may be banished.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.7: As Summon Demon I , except summons a demon of Type II and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Light difficulty.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.8: As Control Demon I, except a demon of Type II or lower may be controlled.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.9: As Conjuring Circle I, except will confine a demon of Type III or lower.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.10: As Banish Demon I, except a demon of Type III or lower may be banished.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.11: As Summon Demon I, except summons a demon of Type III and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Medium difficulty.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.12: As Control Demon I , except a demon of Type III or lower may be controlled.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.13: As Conjuring Circle I, except will confine a demon of Type IV or lower.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.14: As Banish Demon I, except a demon of Type IV or lower may be banished.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.15: As Summon Demon I , except summons a demon of Type IV and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Hard difficulty.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.16: As Control Demon I , except a demon of Type IV or lower may be controlled.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.17: As Conjuring Circle I, except will confine a demon of Type V or lower.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.18: As Banish Demon I, except a demon of Type V or lower may be banished.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.19: As Summon Demon I , except summons a demon of Type V and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Very Hard difficulty.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.20: As Control Demon I, except a demon of Type V or lower may be controlled.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.25: As Conjuring Circle I, except will confine a demon of Type VI or lower.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.30: As Banish Demon I, except a demon of Type VI or lower may be banished.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.35: As Summon Demon I, except summons a demon of Type VI and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Extremely Hard difficulty.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.40: As Control Demon I , except a demon of Type VI or lower may be controlled.
DEMONIC SUMMONS.50: As Summon Demon I, except summons a demon of greater than Type VI and the required Spell Mastery roll is of Sheer Folly difficulty, but since this is the most powerful class of demons, an Absolute Failure is the same as a normal failure. Note that demons this powerful cannot be controlled like those of lesser power, so this is a spell to consider carefully before using. Note: Statements about how long the demon can remain on this plane are based on the demon having arrived by means of a Summon Demon spell. If the demon arrived by some other means, it may well be able to stay longer. See Creature Law for much more on demons and Section 10.3 of this volume for more on demon summoning.   

DETECTING WAYS.1: Detects any active spell or active magical item from the Essence realm; caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round.
DETECTING WAYS.2: As Detect Essence, except realm is Mentalism.
DETECTING WAYS.3: As Detect Essence, except realm is Channeling.
DETECTING WAYS.4: As Detect Essence, except detects magic of any realm (does not reveal which realm is detected).
DETECTING WAYS.5: As Detect Essence, except detects invisible things; all attacks against something so detected are modified by -50.
DETECTING WAYS.6: As Detect Essence, except it gives a 75% chance of detecting a trap (modified by half the bonus or penalty difficulty of finding the trap through a Perception maneuver). If a trap is missed, the spell will not find it no matter how many times the caster scans the same area.
DETECTING WAYS.7: As Detect Essence, except detects any evil being or any item created by evil or long used by a very evil person.
DETECTING WAYS.8: Gives the direction and distance to any specific object or place that the caster is familiar with or has had described in detail. The caster must have the dimensions of the object exact within 10% of actual size and know at least two other attributes that make the item unique (e.g., color, texture, weight, etc.) within range of the spell. Note that sometimes the caster must know more than two other attributes to make the item unique.
DETECTING WAYS.9: As Detect Essence, except detects poisonous substances.
DETECTING WAYS.10: Gives the power (level) of the person or item or spell examined; caster can concentrate on a different target each round.
DETECTING WAYS.11: As Detect Essence, except detects dead bodies and whether anything has died in the radius in the last 24 hours.
DETECTING WAYS.12: As Location I, except range is 300'.
DETECTING WAYS.13: As Detect Essence, except detects one herb of the caster’s choice.
DETECTING WAYS.14: As Detect Essence, except detects one species of life chosen by the caster.
DETECTING WAYS.15: As Detect Essence , except detects any active or latent (awaiting a trigger event) spell that has been cast in the area examined. Realm(s) of spell(s) are revealed, as well as general type (Force, Elemental, etc.); also revealed is whether spell is active or awaiting a triggering event.
DETECTING WAYS.16: As Location I, except range is 500'.
DETECTING WAYS.17: As Detect Essence, except detects any area where magic does not act normally or where there is an abnormal structure to the local space and/or time (as with the presence of an interdimensional Gate).
DETECTING WAYS.18: As Perceive Power , except range is 300'.
DETECTING WAYS.19: As Detect Essence, except detects one metal of the caster’s choice.
DETECTING WAYS.20: As Location I, except range is 1 mile.
DETECTING WAYS.25: As Detect Essence, except detects any type of detection spell that is operating in the area examined and gives exact spell detected as well as the location of that spell’s caster if he is within 100'.
DETECTING WAYS.30: Detects as any one of the lower level Detect spells. Thing detected and area of effect can both be changed each round.
DETECTING WAYS.35: As Perceive Power I, except range is 100'/level and everything within a 5' radius can be “perceived” at once.
DETECTING WAYS.40: As any of the lower level Detect or Discover spells, except entire 100' radius around the caster is scanned instantly and the locations of any detected things are learned to within 1'.
DETECTING WAYS.50: As Location I, except range is 1 mile/level.

DETECTION MASTERY.1: Detects any active spell or active magical item from the Channeling realm; caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round.
DETECTION MASTERY.2: As Detect Channeling, except realm is Essence.
DETECTION MASTERY.3: As Detect Channeling, except realm is Mentalism.
DETECTION MASTERY.4: As Detect Channeling , except detects magic of any realm.
DETECTION MASTERY.5: As Detect Channeling, except detects living beings.
DETECTION MASTERY.6: As Detect Channeling , except detects curses on people or things.
DETECTION MASTERY.7: As Detect Channeling, except detects the presence of Undead.
DETECTION MASTERY.8: Analyzes one living being, giving race, age, and current state of health.
DETECTION MASTERY.9: As Detect Channeling, except detects invisible things; all attacks against something so detected are at -50.
DETECTION MASTERY.10: Gives the power (level) of the person or item or spell examined; caster can concentrate on a different target each round.
DETECTION MASTERY.11: Caster may analyze a poison to determine the exact effects and what antidotes are necessary. Poison may be on an item or in a being.
DETECTION MASTERY.12: As Detect Channeling , except it detects mechanical traps (but not unintentional hazards of any kind or magical protections).
DETECTION MASTERY.13: Detects any active magic on a target person or item. It also analyzes general spell type (in item or cast) and what profession cast or created it; in case of a person, it gives an exact profession.
DETECTION MASTERY.14: As Detect Channeling, except detects any holy or unholy creature/item, any items (including sacred texts) of religious significance, hallowed ground, and exceptionally pious individuals.
DETECTION MASTERY.15: As Detect Power , except detects any spell that has been cast in the area examined going back 1 day/level of caster. Caster learns approximate time (within an hour) that a detected spell was cast.
DETECTION MASTERY.16: Identifies any woman or animal that is with child, at any stage of pregnancy. Caster learns the developmental stage, number, and sex(es) of the preborn. Caster can scan a different area each round.
DETECTION MASTERY.17: As Perceive Power, except range is 500' and everyone/everything in a five foot radius can be “perceived” at once, with a different area targeted each round.
DETECTION MASTERY.18: Gives the direction and distance to any specific object or place that the caster is familiar with or has had described in detail. The caster must have the dimensions of the object exact within 10% of actual size and know at least two other attributes that make the item unique (e.g., color, texture, weight, etc.) within range of the spell. Note that sometimes the caster must know more than two other attributes to make the item unique.
DETECTION MASTERY.19: As Detect Undead , except caster learns exact numbers, types, and levels of Undead, as well as whether or not each Undead is functioning independently or is currently controlled (including “mastered”) by another.
DETECTION MASTERY.20: As Detect Curse, except any curse may be analyzed for approximate level, effect, and required cure.
DETECTION MASTERY.25: As Life Typing, except also gives exact profession, degree of religious feeling, religious affiliation, experience level, and other pertinent details.
DETECTION MASTERY.30: Detects as any one of the lower level Detect spells. Thing detected and area of effect can both be changed each round.
DETECTION MASTERY.35: As Perceive Power, except every single item, spell, or individual within 500' of the caster that has “power” (a level) is “perceived” in an instant.
DETECTION MASTERY.40: As Life Analysis, except applies to 1 target/level within a range of 100'.
DETECTION MASTERY.50: As Location III, except range is 1 mile/level.

DETECTIONS.1: Detects any active spell or active magical item from the Mentalism realm; caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round.
DETECTIONS.2: As Detect Mentalism, except realm is Essence.
DETECTIONS.3: As Detect Mentalism , except realm is Channeling.
DETECTIONS.4: Caster can detect any extreme emotion (e.g., hatred, love, sadness, fear, etc.). In addition, the caster learns which emotion(s) the target is feeling. If there are no extremes of emotion (i.e., only normal feelings), nothing is detected. Caster may change the spell’s target each round.
DETECTIONS.5: As Detect Mentalism, except detects invisible things; all attacks against something so detected are at -50.
DETECTIONS.6: As Detect Mentalism, except detects any evil being or any item created by evil or long used by a very evil person.
DETECTIONS.7: As Detect Mentalism , except it gives a 75% chance of detecting a trap (modified by half the bonus or penalty difficulty of finding the trap through a Perception maneuver). See Section 10.
DETECTIONS.8: Caster learns the power (level) of every person or item or spell within the area examined. Caster can focus on a different 5'R area each round.
DETECTIONS.9: As Detect Mentalism, except it detects any one substance (e.g., gold, silver, black pepper, goat’s milk, human blood, water) chosen by the caster at the time of casting.
DETECTIONS.10: Detects any active magic on a target person or item. It also analyzes general spell type (in item or cast) and what profession cast or created it; in case of a person, it gives an exact profession.
DETECTIONS.11: As Determine Power I, except range is 100' and area is a circle with a 10' radius.
DETECTIONS.12: As Detect Mentalism, except detects anything deliberately concealed by non-magical means, including secret doors and compartments, as well as hiding individuals. To be detected, a thing must have either been hidden within 1 day/level of the caster, or there must be someone currently within range who is aware of the concealment.
DETECTIONS.13: As Detect Invisible, except caster can see invisible things anywhere the caster can normally see; the caster’s attacks on invisible targets get no penalty (as they are not invisible to him).
DETECTIONS.14: Caster learns if any external influences or probes are in the caster’s own mind. The involved spell or effect is learned, but this spell does not reveal the identity of the “intruder”.
DETECTIONS.15: Detects any spell that has been cast in the area examined any time as far back as 1 day per caster level. For each spell, the caster learns the spell type and the list it was cast from. A different area may be probed each round.
DETECTIONS.16: As Determine Power I, except range is 500' and area is a circle with a 50' radius.
DETECTIONS.17: Each round, the caster can detect any poison, venom, disease, curse, parasitic infection, or mental illness afflicting one living target, gaining a general knowledge of the type(s) of each present (e.g., a Channeling curse and two mild nerve poisons).
DETECTIONS.18: Detects and exactly identifies every spell cast or active in the target area. It will also identify any magic item or spellcaster within the radius as if it were a target of Power Typing.
DETECTIONS.19: As See Invisible, except caster need not concentrate in order to see invisible things.
DETECTIONS.20: As Determine Power I, except range is 1 mile and area is a circle with a 300' radius.
DETECTIONS.25: Gives the direction and distance to any specific object or place that the caster is familiar with or has had described in detail. The caster must have the dimensions of the object exact within 10% of actual size and know at least two other attributes that make the item unique (e.g., color, texture, weight, etc.) within range of the spell. Note that sometimes the caster must know more than two other attributes to make the item unique.
DETECTIONS.30: Detects as any one of the lower level Detect spells. Thing detected and area of effect can both be changed each round.
DETECTIONS.35: As Location V, except for range.
DETECTIONS.40: As Spell Probe, except detects spells as far back as one year per caster level.
DETECTIONS.50: Caster may use any of the lower level spells on this list up to level 30 each round. Also, the caster may combine “detections”, allowing the detection of multiple things in the same targeted area during a single round (once the appropriate spells are active).

DISEASE.1: Target gets a cold. Treat as a mild Pneumonic disease that does not go away. (See RMCL, Section 15.6.)
DISEASE.3: Target has no sense of smell. Target never perceives by smell alone and suffers a -25 modifier to all Awareness maneuvers involving smell and other senses.
DISEASE.4: Target has no sense of hearing. Target never perceives by hearing alone and suffers a -25 modifier to all Awareness maneuvers involving hearing and other senses.
DISEASE.5: Over the course of one week, the target’s tongue will rot away. When the tongue is fully gone, the target will be unable to speak.
DISEASE.6: Target gets severe asthma. For every round of physical exersion (e.g., running or melee combat), the target must make an Endurance roll.
DISEASE.7: Target gets leprosy. This causes a gradual loss of feeling (and blood flow) in the extremities. Every day, the target suffers a cumulative -1 penalty to all perceptions involving touch to a maximum of -25. There is similarly a cumulative -1 penalty added each day to any roll depending on the target’s appearance (e.g., Charm maneuvers), but the maximum for this penalty is -50. After one week of leprosy, all concussion hit damage against the target is halved due to loss of feeling.
DISEASE.8: Target becomes slightly allergic to a substance of the caster’s choosing. When in the presence of this substance, the target loses 1 AP each round due to the distraction of runny nose, sneezing, etc.
DISEASE.9: The roots of any one plant wither and die and no new roots can be grown. The plant will eventually die.
DISEASE.10: The target becomes a hemophiliac. All concussion hits taken by the target are doubled and hits heal at half the normal rate. All attempts to stop bleeding by the target are made with a -30 penalty and even magical healing stops one less hit/round of bleeding than would normally be the case.
DISEASE.11: The target contracts malaria; after the initial bout of high fever, delerium, coma, and incapacitation (lasting 1 + 2d10 days), the target will be subject to random “relapses” similar to the initial bout (5% chance each month of a relapse). If the target receives no care during the initial bout, there is a 99% chance of death during this time (only 1% chance if given competent medical attention).
DISEASE.12: Caster may instantly kill any one plant he touches.
DISEASE.13: Target becomes an epileptic; in a tense situation, there is a 5% chance that he will go into an epileptic fit for 2d10 rounds. If someone is able to provide care (anyone with a rank in Medicine may do so), there is a 1% chance that the target will do an A Krush critical to himself by hitting some object; if there is nobody to do so, there is a 20% chance of taking a critical. In any case, he is treated as stunned (-75).
DISEASE.14: Target gets pneumonia; if the target is kept warm and completely immobile for 2 weeks, he will have only a 25% chance of dying (50% chance if not treated). The chance of dying is modified by the target’s Constitution stat bonus (e.g., if the target has a +7 Constitution stat bonus, his chance of dying if untreated is 43%). If the target survives this two week period, the lingering symptoms are as a mild Pneumonic disease (RMCL, Section 15.6).
DISEASE.15: As Minor Allergy, except 2 AP are lost each round.
DISEASE.16: The target suffers any one of the diseases from this list of up to 5th level (caster’s choice). In addition, the target is contagious. All who come into contact (5'R) of the target must make an RR (with a +25 modifier if only close to target, +0 if touching target, and at -25 if the individual has intimate contact with the target). If the original target makes his RR, he does not suffer the effects, but must make a second RR or will still be a carrier of the disease. All people who catch the disease from the target are not themselves contagious.
DISEASE.17: Target gets elephantiasis; one of his extremities (not his head) swells and grows up to five times normal size. Exact effect should be determined by the GM (but the extremity should be completely useless).
DISEASE.18: At the rise of the next full moon, the target becomes a Lesser Werewolf (see Creature Law).
DISEASE.19: As Carrier I, except disease may be chosen from spells up to 10th level.
DISEASE.20: Target gets leukemia; he will weaken at a rate of 2% per day (-2/day, cumulative, to all activities). When the total reachs 100% (-100 penalty), the target dies.
DISEASE.25: As Carrier I, except disease may be chosen from spells up to 15th level.
