DISEASE.30: Target gets rabies; after 21 days of no visible effect, the disease sets in. The target’s organs contract; he foams at the mouth, becomes very thirsty and violent; and any one he bites or scratches must resist or contract the disease. The target dies 5-7 days after the disease’s onset.
DISEASE.35: As Minor Allergy, except 3 AP are lost each round.
DISEASE.40: At the rise of the next full moon, the target becomes a Greater Werewolf (see Creature Law).
DISEASE.50: Target gets a “plague”. If he fails his RR, he will die 95% of the time (after 10 minutes). The other 5% of the time, he believes that he resisted (and suffers no effects), but becomes a carrier instead. Anyone coming within 5' of the carrier must resist (+25 RR; +0 RR if touching the target; -25 RR if in intimate contact with the target) or face the same chances as the original target. Only the original target is contagious. Note: It is an Easy maneuver with an appropriate medical skill to recognize any of these afflictions as the result of magic rather than natural disease. When selecting allergies, the caster cannot choose something that the target is in constant contact with. For example, the caster cannot choose for the target to be allergic to himself, air, dirt, etc. Once caught, diseases cannot be dispelled with Dispelling spells. However, they can be cured by Undisease or Remove Disease spells. Unless stated otherwise, the diseases gained through this list are not contagious. Unless stated otherwise, disease effects kick in d10 hours after the spell is cast.   

DISGUISE MASTERY.1: Caster studies a person for use with disguise spells later on this list. The caster can only have as many disguises studied as he has ranks in this list. He may forget a studied disguise at any time to make room for replacements.
DISGUISE MASTERY.2: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its visual appearance altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes. This spell cannot make an object cast light (although it may alter the appearance of light given off, whether naturally or from another spell).
DISGUISE MASTERY.3: Caster has a limited illusion on himself that allows him to look like any humanoid race within 20% of his own size. The illusion moves as the caster moves.
DISGUISE MASTERY.4: All deeds performed while this spell is active are “shadowed” to divination spells. All related visions gained through divination effect are shrouded in shadows. For example, a Death’ s Mastery cast on the scene of a murder might reveal a shadowy figure performing the deeds (but no details about the identity of the figure).
DISGUISE MASTERY.5: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, caster appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.
DISGUISE MASTERY.6: As Façade I, except one extra sense is added.
DISGUISE MASTERY.7: Caster physically changes to take on the form and visual appearance of any one studied person (using the Study Disguise spell). This is strictly a physical change.
DISGUISE MASTERY.8: As Shadow Assassin, except absolutely no information about the caster will be revealed. For example, a Death’s Memory cast on the scene of a murder might show the victim collapsing with a knife in his chest (but no sign of the assassin).
DISGUISE MASTERY.9: As Façade I, except two extra senses are added.
DISGUISE MASTERY.10: As Disguise I, except caster also takes on the vocal characteristics of the person mimicked.
DISGUISE MASTERY.11: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s profession may be misrepresented.
DISGUISE MASTERY.12: Caster may mentally speak with one target; to the target it will seem as if they are communicating normally (i.e., orally).
DISGUISE MASTERY.13: As Disguise II, except caster also takes on the obvious mannerisms of the person mimicked (e.g., left-handedness, limp).
DISGUISE MASTERY.14: As Façade I, except all five basic human senses (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch) are included.
DISGUISE MASTERY.15: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s level may be misrepresented (to an upper limit of twice the caster’s actual level).
DISGUISE MASTERY.16: As Disguise III, except caster also takes on the subtle mannerisms of the person mimicked. This makes him virtually undetectable to all but the closest of associates.
DISGUISE MASTERY.17: Target becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.
DISGUISE MASTERY.18: As Shadow Assassin, except all deeds performed while this spell is active will appear as if the currently studied patsy is performing the caster’s actions (see Study Patsy on Assassination Mastery list).
DISGUISE MASTERY.19: As Disguise IV, except even very subtle mannerisms are gained.
DISGUISE MASTERY.20: Allows caster to use all of the “Misfeel” spells at once.
DISGUISE MASTERY.25: Caster and items on his person cannot be detected by any Detect. . spell.
DISGUISE MASTERY.30: As Misfeel, except it has a ten minute/level duration and does not require concentration.
DISGUISE MASTERY.35: As Façade III, except duration is only ten minutes per level and it may be used on another person.
DISGUISE MASTERY.40: As Façade V , except with one round of concentration, the caster can change the façade to another.
DISGUISE MASTERY.50: Caster may use any lower level spell on this list, once per round.

DISPELLING WAYS.1: When the caster (of Cancel Essence) is the target of a spell from the Essence realm, the “incoming” spell must first make a successful RR before the caster needs to make an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster normally; otherwise, the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
DISPELLING WAYS.2: As Cancel Essence, except it affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
DISPELLING WAYS.3: As Cancel Essence , except it affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
DISPELLING WAYS.4: Any active spell from the realm of Essence that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
DISPELLING WAYS.5: As Dispel Essence, except affects spells of the realm of Mentalism.
DISPELLING WAYS.6: As Dispel Essence, except affects spells of the realm of Channeling.
DISPELLING WAYS.7: Any active spell from the realm of Essence that is (at least partly) within the radius of effect must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
DISPELLING WAYS.8: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except affects spells of the realm of Mentalism.
DISPELLING WAYS.9: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except affects spells of the realm of Channeling.
DISPELLING WAYS.10: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
DISPELLING WAYS.11: As Dispel Mentalism Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
DISPELLING WAYS.12: As Dispel Channeling Sphere I, except radius is 50'.
DISPELLING WAYS.13: As Cancel Essence , except affects spells from any realm.
DISPELLING WAYS.14: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except radius is 100'.
DISPELLING WAYS.15: As Dispel Mentalism Sphere I, except radius is 100'.
DISPELLING WAYS.16: As Dispel Channeling Sphere I, except radius is 100'.
DISPELLING WAYS.17: Target has no Essence Power Points and can cast no Essence spells for 24 hours (includes spells cast using power enhancers). This can also be cast against items that could normally cast spells.
DISPELLING WAYS.18: As Unessence, except Mentalism is affected.
DISPELLING WAYS.19: As Unessence, except Channeling is affected.
DISPELLING WAYS.20: As Dispel Essence Sphere I, except radius is 300'.
DISPELLING WAYS.25: As Dispel Mentalism Sphere I, except radius is 300'.
DISPELLING WAYS.30: As Dispel Channeling Sphere I, except radius is 300'.
DISPELLING WAYS.35: As Dispel Essence Sphere V , except all 3 realms are affected.
DISPELLING WAYS.40: As Dispel Sphere True V, except radius is 100'.
DISPELLING WAYS.50: As Dispel Sphere True V , except radius is 200'.

EARTH LAW.1: Caster transforms up to one cubic foot per level of clay into baked pottery. Firing clay in this manner will always be successful. This can also cook glazes onto earthenware. Quality of product depends on the skill of whomever shapes the clay.
EARTH LAW.2: Loosens 100 cubic feet of earth to the consistency of plowed ground.
EARTH LAW.3: Caster receives a +30 bonus on any maneuvers involving the working of stone.
EARTH LAW.4: Creates a wall of packed earth up to 10'x10'x (3' at base, 1' at top); it can be dug through only (20 man-rounds at top).
EARTH LAW.5: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.
EARTH LAW.6: Caster may mend a single break in a small (up to 2 pounds) inorganic object or multiple breaks, rips, or cracks in a larger (up to 10 pounds) organic object. All component parts of the object must be within a 10' radius.
EARTH LAW.7: Creates a wall of non-precious stone up to 10'x10'x1'; it can be chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
EARTH LAW.8: This spell causes the area of effect to bristle with hundreds of 6" to 12" sharp spikes and points. The exact composition of these spikes will depend on the material the ground is made of. The area of effect must be placed horizontally on a surface (i.e., it cannot be placed on walls). Most animals and creatures cannot be induced to walk through the area. Anyone moving through the area must make a maneuver roll (at least Extremely Hard difficulty) for every 5' moved through. Anyone who falls inside the area of effect receives an A Puncture critical strike.
EARTH LAW.9: Changes 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth. This change is gradual, taking three rounds to complete.
EARTH LAW.10: As Earthwall, except duration is permanent.
EARTH LAW.11: As Stone/Earth, except changes earth to soft mud.
EARTH LAW.12: As Stone/Earth, except changes packed earth to solid stone and loose earth to gravel.
EARTH LAW.13: As Stonewall, except duration is permanent and dimensions may be up to 20'x20'x1'.
EARTH LAW.14: As Stone/Earth, except changes mud to packed earth.
EARTH LAW.16: Fuses two touching walls together (seam can be up to 20' long) or fuses a section of stone blocks (up to 100 cubic feet).
EARTH LAW.17: As Stone/Earth, except changes stone to mud.
EARTH LAW.18: As Stonewall True, except wall may be curved to a semicircle.
EARTH LAW.19: Removes a flaw from a single gem, mineral, or stone. This can greatly enhance the value of some jewels.
EARTH LAW.30: Causes a very minor earthquake which could cause shoddy construction to collapse (and can be very terrifying). This is approximately 5.5 on the Richter scale.
EARTH LAW.35: As Stonewall True, except wall may be curved as desired so long as total length does not exceed 10' per level and wall is two feet thick.
EARTH LAW.40: Transforms one stone into a stone golem (sophistication of shape depending on caster’s artistic skills) for the duration of the spell, during which it will obey the caster faithfully unless control is wrested by another (in which case the caster can always cancel the spell and return the golem to boulder form before duration runs out). Size of the golem is based on the stone’s weight (which may be modified up to 50% in order to achieve desired Size) and may be up to Large.
EARTH LAW.50: Caster can use one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.1: Target is totally protected from all natural light (not lightning); +10 to all RRs or DBs vs. elemental light (electricity) attacks.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.2: Target is totally protected from all natural heat to 200° F; +10 to all RRs or DBs vs. elemental heat/fire attacks.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.3: Target is totally protected from all natural cold to -20° F; +10 to all RRs or DBs vs. elemental cold/ice attacks.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.4: As Resist Light, except all beings within 10'R of target are protected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.5: As Resist Heat, except all beings within 10'R of target are protected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.6: As Resist Cold, except all beings within 10'R of target are protected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.7: As Resist Heat and Resist Cold, except for duration, and only natural temperature extremes are protected against.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.8: As Resist Light, except protects against all natural light and lightning; +20 to all RRs vs light (electricity); -20 to elemental electricity attacks.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.9: As Lightarmor, except protects against heat.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.10: As Lightarmor, except protects against cold.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.11: As Lightarmor, except protects all beings within 10'R centered on target.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.12: As Heatarmor, except protects all beings within 10'R centered on target.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.13: As Coldarmor, except protects all beings within 10'R centered on target.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.14: As Resist Light, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.15: As Lightarmor, except it also reduces the effective Size of any electrical attack on the target by 1 (e.g., a Medium Lightning Bolt becomes a Small Lightning Bolt).
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.16: As Resist Heat , except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.17: As Lightning Armor, except fire and heat spells are affected (e.g., fire bolts and fire balls).
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.18: As Resist Cold , except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.19: As Lightning Armor, except cold and ice spells are affected (e.g., ice bolts and cold balls).
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.20: As Lightarmor, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.25: As Heatarmor, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.30: As Coldarmor, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.35: As Fire Armor, except protects all beings within 10'R centered on target.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.40: As Ice Armor, except protects all beings within 10'R centered on target.
ELEMENTAL SHIELDS.50: Acts as Lightning Armor, Fire Armor, and Ice Armor all at the same time.

ENTERTAINING WAYS.1: Caster is lit by a bright circle of light (between 5' and 10' diameter as desired). At the time of casting, the caster designates his “front” and the light will shine from that direction. The caster can change his “front” by concentrating (the light will blink off and another will blink on). The diameter and color of the light can also be changed and the light dimmed down to a soft mood light. All adjustable features can be changed at once.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.2: All non-magical light sources in the area of effect are dimmed to the luminosity of soft candle light.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.3: Caster can create colored lights that will shine in the area of effect. The caster can change the colors and forms of the light by concentrating. The light can take any form the caster desires, but the light will not illuminate more than the area of effect. For example, several small spheres of various colors can float around the caster’s feet, or five red beams can flash back and forth across the caster’s body.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.4: Caster receives a +5 bonus to all Performance Art maneuvers for the duration of this spell.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.5: Caster can make any one target (up to 1 pound per level of the caster) vanish. The caster must designate a location for the target to “reappear” in (the location must be within the caster’s line of sight and within 100' per level of the caster). The object is teleported to the designated location at the time of casting, where it may remain invisibly until the duration expires or the caster chooses for it to appear. When the item is teleported, it makes a sound only where it disappears.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.6: The target of this spell is enthralled with the caster (for as long as the caster is performing or for the duration of this spell, whichever is shorter). While enthralled, the target is considered to be Concentrating on the performance and has a -75 to Perception regarding anything else. Enthrallment is broken if the target is attacked.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.7: The caster can place any one inanimate object (up to 1 pound per level of the caster) into any available container. The container opening must be large enough to fit at least one dimension of the object. When the container is examined, it will appear empty to all normal perception (including feeling). The caster may pluck the item from the container at any time (up to the duration of the spell). If the object is not taken from the container before duration is up, the object appears in the container as if it were there all along.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.8: When the caster has experienced a fumble/failure result with an attack or physical maneuver, this spell allows the caster to turn tragedy into comedy. The caster suffers no negative effects beyond losing the opportunity to act as desired; the caster’s antics also bolster allied morale and the next friendly morale/fear RR made in the current situation receives a +20 bonus.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.9: As Showman I, except bonus is +10.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.10: Caster can read at three times the normal rate; this material is retained unusually well, granting a +10 bonus to any attempt to recall the information later.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.11: As Enthrall I, except affects up to five targets.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.12: Target will perform any actions that the caster dictates for the duration of this spell. If the target of this spell has previously been a “volunteer” for the caster, he suffers an RR modification of -1 for each previous time he was a successful target. If an action is commanded which is completely alien to the target, the target receives a new RR with a +50 bonus.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.13: Target of this spell (which may be the caster) gains an enhanced appearance. This spell enhances facial appearance, body appearance, and clothing appearance (roughly equivalent to a modern make-over, including fashion). Target gains a +10 to all rolls where personal appearance is a factor or +20 if appearance is a major factor.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.14: As Showman I, except bonus is +15.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.15: As Enthrall I, except affects up to ten targets.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.16: As Legerdemain, except affects any target up to the caster’s own mass.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.17: As Showman I, except bonus applies to a number of target up the caster’s level.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.18: As Showman I, except bonus is +20.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.19: As Quick Study, except caster can read a page at a glance (i.e., caster can read as fast as he can turn through the pages).
ENTERTAINING WAYS.20: As Enthrall I , except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.25: As Showman I, except bonus is +25.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.30: As Disappear, except may affect any one living creature or inanimate object up to 100 pounds per level of the caster.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.35: As Master of Ceremonies I, except bonus is +10.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.40: As Showman I, except bonus is +40.
ENTERTAINING WAYS.50: As Master of Ceremonies I, except bonus is +20.

ESCAPES.1: Gives the caster +20 bonus for picking the lock analyzed. If the caster takes a round to explain the lock to someone else, that person gets a +10 bonus to pick the lock.
ESCAPES.2: Any minor covering on the head of the caster made of organic material is loosened. This includes any gags or blindfolds.
ESCAPES.3: This spell allows the caster to perform one maneuver using a Craft or Technical skill as if the caster had a complete set of the appropriate tools with a bonus of +10.
ESCAPES.4: Caster quietly “teleports” a single inanimate object to a point up to 100' away from its current location. There can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes, a barrier is anything the object could not physically go through). The object has a maximum weight limit of 1 pound per level of the caster.
ESCAPES.5: Any ropes (or similar bindings) that are binding the caster are untied.
ESCAPES.6: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +20.
ESCAPES.7: Caster quietly “teleports” to a point up to 100' away, but there can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes a barrier is anything the caster could not physically go through; a closed door is a barrier, a pit is not).
ESCAPES.8: As Silenced Leave Item I, except the object can pass through barriers by specifying exact direction and distance. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the object does not move (though the caster still loses the PP).
ESCAPES.9: Each lock or shackle binding the caster has a 75% chance of opening (modified by the sophistication of the lock, see Section 10.14).
ESCAPES.10: As Silenced Leave Item I , except target can be moved 500'.
ESCAPES.11: Caster may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.
ESCAPES.12: As Silenced Leaving I, except caster can pass through barriers by specifying exact distances. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the caster does not move, instead being stunned (-50) 1-10 rounds, and the PP for this spell are expended.
ESCAPES.13: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +30.
ESCAPES.14: As Silenced Long Door Item I, except object can be moved up to 500'.
ESCAPES.15: As Unbind, except will also work on fixed shackles (i.e., those that have been rivetted shut).
ESCAPES.16: As Silenced Leaving I , except caster can move 300'.
ESCAPES.17: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +30.
ESCAPES.18: Caster is flattened until becoming virtually two-dimensional. This allows the caster to slide through cracks and makes the caster effectively invisible when viewed from the side.
ESCAPES.19: As Silenced Long Door I , except caster can move 300'.
ESCAPES.20: As Passing, except caster can pass through as much material as the duration allows at a rate of 10'/rnd.
ESCAPES.25: Opens a portal 3'x6'x3' in any solid surface, through which anyone can pass. At the end of the duration, the surface is returned to its previous state. Anyone in the area of effect when the spell ends will be forced back out to the point of entry.
ESCAPES.30: As Phantom Tools I , except bonus for tools is +50.
ESCAPES.35: As Silenced Long Door I , except caster can move 1000'.
ESCAPES.40: Caster leaves no physical trace of having passed: no footprints, no broken twigs, no scent. Non-magical tracking is impossible. No protection against magical tracking is provided, nor does the caster gain any unusual abilities (the caster will still sink in snow normally, it just will be left unmarked after the caster passes). Deliberate actions will still leave traces (e.g., the caster will not snap a twig off a tree in passing, but if the caster reaches out and breaks a twig, it will stay broken),
ESCAPES.50: As Silenced Long Door I, except movement range is 10 miles per level. Making a precise appearance is difficult, especially if unfamiliar with the destination. The following chances for error exist: Never seen place (only described) = 50%; been briefly (1 hr) = 25%; Studied (24 hr) = 10%; Studied carefully (1 week) =1%; Lived in (1 yr) = .01 %. In case of failure: first determine direction of error (randomly), then determine amount of error (make an open-ended d100 roll for the number of feet).

ESSENCE HAND.1: Causes an object of up to 1 lb. weight to vibrate rapidly; if the item is fragile, make a breakage check. If it is an object held by a being, the being must make an RR or fumble it (each round).
ESSENCE HAND.2: Exerts 5 pound of counterforce on a person or object. Object cannot be moved by Staying alone, which can only be used to counter another force, such as gravity, and it can only exert force in one direction. A person (or other animate target) must win a feat of strength based on their weight vs. the weight capacity of the spell in order to move against the force.
ESSENCE HAND.3: Caster can move one object at a distance, weighing up to 1 pound, up to 1'/second with no acceleration. If the caster stops concentration before the duration is up, the object remains stationary as if it had Staying I thrown on it. If the object is held, resolve any attempt to grab it as a feat of strength based on the weight capacity of the spell vs. the weight of the target holding the object.
ESSENCE HAND.4: As Vibrations I, except weight limit is 5 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.5: As Staying I, except force limit is 25 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.6: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 5 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.7: As Vibrations I, except weight limit is 25 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.8: As Staying I, except force limit is 125 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.9: As Vibrations I, except weight limit is 50 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.10: Causes +50 to be added to the attack roll of the missile. The caster must touch the firer and see the missile for the entire round that the missile is fired.
ESSENCE HAND.11: As Telekinesis I, except the weight limit is 25 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.12: As Staying I, except the force limit is 250 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.13: As Vibrations I, except as many 5 lb. objects as the caster’s level can be vibrated (they must all be within the field of vision of the caster).
ESSENCE HAND.14: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 50 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.15: As Staying I, except force limit is 500 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.16: Caster may “hurl” one object (that starts within 10' of the caster) of 1 lb. or less with sufficient force to deliver an attack on the Sling Attack Table with the ranges of a Short Bow. Base the caster’s OB on his directed spell skill with this spell. If a weapon is “hurled”, a medium Spell Mastery maneuver will allow use of the weapon’s normal attack table, retaining the Short Bow range penalties. If this is done, the weapon gets any normal OB bonuses (e.g., from superior materials).
ESSENCE HAND.17: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 100 lbs.
ESSENCE HAND.18: As Vibrations I, except weight limit is 10 lbs./level.
ESSENCE HAND.19: As Vibrations I, except as many 25 lb. objects as the caster’s level can be vibrated (they must all be within the field of vision of the caster).
ESSENCE HAND.20: As Aiming, except missile attack bonus is +100.
ESSENCE HAND.25: As Staying I, except force limit is 50 lbs./level.
ESSENCE HAND.30: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 10 lbs./level.
ESSENCE HAND.35: As Mass Vibrations I, except objects may be up to 10 lbs./level in weight.
ESSENCE HAND.40: As True Telekinesis, except within the weight limit, the caster may simultaneously manipulate up to one object for every five caster levels (i.e., a 40th level caster could control eight objects at once).
ESSENCE HAND.50: As Aiming, except missile attack automatically does maximum damage (roll the critical normally, except that it is treated as Slaying II).

ESSENCE TWISTING.1: The local Essence field is distorted, causing all RRs against spells from the Realm of Essence to be made at -10.
ESSENCE TWISTING.2: As Baneful Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Mentalism.
ESSENCE TWISTING.3: As Baneful Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Channeling.
ESSENCE TWISTING.4: The local Essence field is distorted, causing all RRs against spells from the Realm of Essence to be made at +10.
ESSENCE TWISTING.5: As Baneful Essence , except affects all spells.
ESSENCE TWISTING.6: As Weaken Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Mentalism.
ESSENCE TWISTING.7: As Weaken Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Channeling.
ESSENCE TWISTING.8: As Baneful Essence, except penalty is -20.
ESSENCE TWISTING.9: As Woeful Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Mentalism.
ESSENCE TWISTING.10: As Weaken Essence, except affects all spells.
ESSENCE TWISTING.11: As Woeful Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Channeling.
ESSENCE TWISTING.12: Any time an attempt to cast a spell from the Realm of Essence within the area of effect results in spell failure, +30 is added to the spell failure roll.
ESSENCE TWISTING.13: As Treacherous Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Mentalism.
ESSENCE TWISTING.14: As Woeful Essence, except affects all spells.
ESSENCE TWISTING.15: As Treacherous Essence, except affects spells from the Realm of Channeling.
ESSENCE TWISTING.16: Any attempt to cast a spell of the Realm of Essence will be made with a -20 penalty to the Spellcasting roll.
ESSENCE TWISTING.17: Any attempt to cast a spell of the Realm of Mentalism will be made with a -20 penalty to the Spellcasting roll.
ESSENCE TWISTING.18: Any attempt to cast a spell of the Realm of Channeling will be made with a -20 penalty to the Spellcasting roll.
ESSENCE TWISTING.19: As Treacherous Essence, except affects all spells.
ESSENCE TWISTING.20: Any attempt to cast any spell will be made with a -20 penalty to the Spellcasting roll.
ESSENCE TWISTING.25: As any of the above spells relating to the Realm of Essence, except for area of effect and duration.
ESSENCE TWISTING.30: As any of the above spells relating to the Realm of Mentalism, except for area of effect and duration.
ESSENCE TWISTING.35: As any of the above spells relating to the Realm of Channeling, except for area of effect and duration.
ESSENCE TWISTING.40: All spells cast that are from an Evil spell list are resisted at -10, while those from other lists suffer a -25 to their Spellcasting rolls and are resisted with a +10 bonus.
ESSENCE TWISTING.50: As any of the above spells of less than 40th level, except for area of effect and duration.

ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.1: Caster can pick a point up to 10' away and experience tactile sensations as if their hand was in that place, able to move as their hand might. The point may be on the other side of a barrier (e.g., in a chest, behind a door), but cannot move through solid objects from that starting point. Sensations of intense heat or cold will not cause pain, nor will touching something painful to the touch produce more than a dull ache (i.e., painful sensation is muted). Target cannot be moved and cannot feel the “touch”.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.2: Caster is aware of the presence of all sentient/thinking beings within 20'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.3: Caster can pick a point up to 10' away and will hear as if physically at that point (there can be intervening objects such as walls). Note that this means that the caster does not have hearing at his own location.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.4: As Listen I, except the caster’s sense of smell functions from the fixed point.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.5: Caster’s point of hearing may be moved independently up to 100' away (moves at 10'/md); the caster must be physically able to go there (e.g., the caster could not send this point of hearing through walls or closed doors). Note that this means that the caster does not have hearing at his own location.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.6: As Listen I, except the caster sees from the fixed point (it can rotate).
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.7: As Long Ear I, except the caster sees from the moving point (it can rotate).
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.8: As Listen I, except the range is 100'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.9: As Long Ear I , except the caster’s sense of smell functions from the moving point.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.10: Caster can read the surface thoughts of one target.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.11: As Watch I, except the range is 100'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.12: As Long Ear I , except the range is 300'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.13: As Smell I, except the range is 100'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.14: As Listen I, except the range is 500'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.15: As Long Eye I , except the range is 300'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.16: One of the caster’s senses has an extended spectrum of sensitivity. Vision may be extended into infrared or ultraviolet light or hearing extended into infrasonic or ultrasonic frequencies. Applicability to any other sense is up the Gamemaster.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.17: As Long Nose I , except the range is 300'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.18: As Watch I, except range is 500'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.19: As Smell I, except range is 500'.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.20: As Listen I, except the range is 1 mile/level.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.25: As Watch I, except the range is 1 mile/level.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.30: As Listen I, except the range is unlimited (the caster must be able to locate the desired point in terms of direction and distance).
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.35: As Smell I, except the range is 1 mile/level.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.40: When making a ranged attack, including any form of spell, all range penalties are reduced to one-tenth rounded down (e.g., a -25 penalty would be reduced to -2). Any Perception maneuver penalties based on sight should be made as if the caster were at one-tenth the actual distance from what is being observed.
ESSENCE'S PERCEPTIONS.50: As Listen True, except the caster sees instead of hears.

EVASIONS.1: Allows caster to leap up to 10' vertically or laterally, grasp and swing from a fixed object, and land perfectly (up to 20' away from the object). The object could be a tree, branch, rafter, rope, chandelier, etc. This swing is included as part of this instantaneous spell action (i.e., takes no additional AP).
EVASIONS.2: Caster is immune to stagger effects (includes “prone” or “knocked back” results) for the duration of this spell.
EVASIONS.3: If the caster performs a Full Dodge for a round, he gains +20 to his DB. If he uses a Partial Dodge, he gets half that bonus.
EVASIONS.4: As Swing I, except 3 swings may be executed in rapid succession.
EVASIONS.5: Allows caster to “flip” in any direction, landing up to 10' away (facing in any direction). This flip is included as part of this instantaneous spell action (i.e., takes no additional AP).
EVASIONS.6: Caster can tread very quietly, providing a +25 bonus to Stalking maneuvers when moving.
EVASIONS.7: As Dodging I, but bonus is +40.
EVASIONS.8: Caster may move at up to a Running pace and Dodge without suffering a penalty due to pace.
EVASIONS.9: As Swing I, except 5 swings may be executed in rapid succession.
EVASIONS.10: As Flip I, except 3 flips may be executed in rapid succession.
EVASIONS.11: Caster can tread extremely quietly, providing a +50 bonus to Stalking maneuvers when moving.
EVASIONS.12: As Dodging I, but bonus is +60.
EVASIONS.13: Caster can use his shield against two extra attacks per round (e.g., a full shield may be used against 5 attacks per round).
EVASIONS.14: Caster may move at any pace and Dodge without suffering a penalty due to pace.
EVASIONS.15: Caster can perform a Partial Dodge each round without Concentration.
EVASIONS.16: Caster reacts to an attack to take some control of the placement of an unavoidable blow. This allows him to change the result rolled on a critical taken by himself by up to ±5. This only applies to criticals that result from attacks using a Combat Training skill (e.g., it could be used against a sword attack or a crossbow bolt, but not a Fire Bolt or a Wall of Fire). This effect applies to the first applicable critical taken within the spell’s duration, after which the spell is cancelled.
EVASIONS.17: Caster is all but weightless when standing or treading on a surface of some kind (but falling is normal). This allows the caster to be supported easily by tiny twigs at the ends of tree branches, unsafe roofs, and other surfaces that would normally give under the pressure of his weight. The spell also greatly reduces the impact of the caster’s passing, giving a -75 penalty to attempts to track him by looking for signs (but not by scent or by magic).
EVASIONS.18: As Dodging I, but bonus is +80.
EVASIONS.19: Caster is able to immediately slip free of any Grapple and this spell will also negate any Hold spell or power to which the caster was subject.
EVASIONS.20: As Shield Hand, except allows the use of a shield against any number of attacks per round.
EVASIONS.25: As Dodging I, but bonus is +100.
EVASIONS.30: As Elusive Step, except duration is 1 round per level.
EVASIONS.35: As Defensive Contortion I, except adjustment is by up to ±10.
EVASIONS.40: As Defensive Contortion I, except adjustment is by up to ±15.
EVASIONS.50: Caster can use any one of the lower level spells on this list each round.

FIRE LAW.1: For each level of the caster, one cubic foot of liquid can be heated to boiling at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round. Once the caster ceases concentration, the liquid (if any remains) will cool normally.
FIRE LAW.2: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material (up to 1 cubic foot per level) can be warmed to 100°F (38°C) at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round.
FIRE LAW.3: Causes any wood to ignite and burn. All wood ignited must be within 1' of caster’s palm.
FIRE LAW.4: Creates an opaque wall of fire (up to 10'x10'x6"). Anyone passing through it takes an A Heat critical (no RR).
FIRE LAW.5: As Warm Solid, except for duration and material can be heated to 500°F (260°C) at a rate of one cubic foot heated by 100°F (56°C) per round. The caster must concentrate to increase the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).
FIRE LAW.6: A bolt of fire is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Fire Bolt Table.
FIRE LAW.7: Creates a cube of flame (up to 10'x10'x10'); it takes 1 complete round for the cube to form and be effective. Anyone passing through (or caught within) the cube takes an A Heat critical each round spent in the cube.
FIRE LAW.8: A 1' diameter ball of fire is shot from the palm of the caster. Upon reaching the selected target/location, it expands to affect a 10'R area. Resolve attack on the Fire Ball Table.
FIRE LAW.9: The caster may designate a type of light source to ignite or extinguish (e.g., candles, torches, fireplaces, or lanterns). This spell will affect all of the light sources of the specified type(s) within the area of effect.
FIRE LAW.10: As Wall of Fire, except “wall” is 10' high and forms a 10'R circle (6" thick) with the caster at the center (it is non-mobile).
FIRE LAW.11: As Fire Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
FIRE LAW.12: As Call Flame I , except cube is up to 20'x20'x20' in size and the range is 20'.
FIRE LAW.13: As Call Flame I , except the effect can be delayed for up to 24 hours. The effect can be triggered by one of the following (decided by the caster at time of casting): time period, certain movements, certain sounds, touch, violent actions in the area, or any other trigger so long as the caster would be able to sense it if the caster remained at the area of effect.
FIRE LAW.14: As Call Flame I, except small balls of flame rain down in a 10' cube causing a B Heat critical to all within or passing through the area of effect.
FIRE LAW.15: Causes a metal object to burst into flames, the object can be up to 1 lb/level in mass. If the object is on a being, it gets an RR, and if it fails, the being takes an automatic Heat critical of a severity to be determined by its location on the being’s body. If the object is touching exposed flesh, the being takes a C Heat critical; if the object is touching through cloth, the being takes a B Heat critical; any being within a 1'R takes an A Heat critical.
FIRE LAW.16: Three bolts of fire are shot from the palm of the caster. They can strike up to three different targets as long as they are all within 60° of each other and are within the caster’s field of vision. Caster may apply his Directed Spell skill by dividing it as he chooses among the three bolts (e.g., a +120 OB could be split evenly into three +40 bolts or one +120 bolt and two +0 bolts or one +60, one +40, and one +20 bolt).
FIRE LAW.17: As Fire Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
FIRE LAW.18: As Waiting Flame, except a Firestorm can be delayed.
FIRE LAW.19: As Call Flame I, except the size is up to a 50' cube and the range is 50'.
FIRE LAW.20: As Fire Bolt III, except caster can make the bolt turn once by up to 90° before striking the target (the caster must know the target’s location). The caster’s OB is modified by -25 when using this spell. However, this spell can be used to bypass a positional modifier – any DB bonus gained through the use of cover (partial/half/full soft cover; partial/half/full hard cover) is reduced to zero if the turn circumvents the cover; a target that otherwise would be unable to be hit can be attacked using this spell.
FIRE LAW.25: As Corner Fires, except bolt can make as many turns as necessary, with an OB penalty of -25 per turn, and go through openings (must be larger than 6" radius) to strike the target.
FIRE LAW.30: A stone surface of up to 300 sq. ft. bursts into flames, and a C Heat critical is given each round to anything passing through (or spending the round in) the 3' flames.
FIRE LAW.35: As Following Fires, except there is no penalty to OB.
FIRE LAW.40: A cone of fire resembling the breath weapon of a fire-breathing dragon shoots from the caster’s hand or face, with a diameter of 1' at its origin point at the caster increasing to a diameter of 50' at the termination of the cone 100' away. Results of this attack are determined using the Fire Ball table.
FIRE LAW.50: Caster can use any one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

FLESH DESTRUCTION.1: A random part of a random limb is sprained. If it is part of a leg: movement is cut by 25%, combat is at -10. If it is part of an arm, combat is modified by -20.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.2: A random limb is in intense pain. If it is a leg, the target cannot walk (target may crawl at 10% of walking movement rate). If it is an arm, that arm cannot be used.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.3: The bones and tissues of the target’s body are disrupted as if the target had fallen a long distance. If the target’s RR fails by 1-10, the target takes an A Impact critical; increase the severity of the critical for each 10 by which the RR is failed (i.e. 11-20 = B, 21-30 = C, 31-40 = D, 41+ = E).
FLESH DESTRUCTION.4: One of the target’s joints locks. Target suffers as in Limb Pain, and in addition a moving target that is afflicted with leg lock will fall down; if a target with arm lock is carrying anything, it is dropped.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.5: A random limb is broken. A broken arm incurs a -40 penalty, while a broken leg results in -25 and movement is reduced by 50%.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.6: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 20'.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.7: Target’s skin will flake and peel (on entire body). There is no tactical penalty, but it ruins the appearance of the target, resulting in a -25 to -75 on social interactions, depending on the importance of appearance. Lasts until dispelled and 1 month passes (recovery time may be reduced through magical healing).
FLESH DESTRUCTION.8: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 50'.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.9: One of the target’s ears (randomly chosen) is disrupted. Hearing is at 50% of normal until cured (-25 to Perception involving hearing, -50 to Perception only involving hearing). If both ears go, the target will be rendered deaf.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.10: One of the target’s limbs (random) will wither and die. Target loses 5% of the use of the limb per day; when the total reaches 100%, the limb falls off. Can be cured through flesh and nerve repair spells suitable for the degree of lost use (e.g., at 25% loss, treat as a -25 injury penalty, thus requiring Medium Flesh Relief and Medium Nerve Relief) before reaching 80%, after which it must be regenerated. If the limb is a leg, movement is reduced by half the percentage lost (e.g., if a leg is 60% withered, movement is cut by 30%). If the limb is an arm, combat suffers a -1 penalty per 2% withered (e.g., if an arm is 80% withered, all combat with that arm will suffer a -40 penalty).
FLESH DESTRUCTION.11: As Ear Disruption, except an eye is affected and the penalties are to Perception involving sight.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.12: One of the target’s lungs collapses. This restricts the target to half of normal activity for all physical activity (treat as 2 AP per round and half normal BMR) until the lung is repaired.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.13: As Limb Death, except a random muscle is affected (not muscle organs, e.g., not the heart).
FLESH DESTRUCTION.14: As Limb Death, except a random bone (not part of spine or skull) gradually disintegrates.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.15: One of the Black Channel I spells may be used (see Section 10.7); the Gamemaster must decide which one(s) may be used on the basis of culture and world system.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.16: Target’s skin splits and begins to bleed. One bleeding wound opens for every 10 by which the target’s RR was failed, with each bleeding 1-5 hits per round (roll randomly for each wound).
FLESH DESTRUCTION.17: As one of Sprain Limb, Limb Pain, Lock Joint, Break Limb, Skin Death, or Ear Disruption, except with a range of 300'.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.18: Target must be dead; the spell causes a body to decompose almost instantaneously, including the bones or other hard parts, crumbling into a fertile dust.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.19: As any lower level spell other than Disruption or Black Channel spells, except that it may be cast upon as many targets as the caster has levels.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.20: The target’s bones will gradually petrify; a loss of activity (as in Limb Death) occurs due to loss of blood production. Treat the loss of use as a penalty to all physical maneuvers and all Endurance rolls. At 100%, this effect becomes fatal.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.25: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 100'.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.30: As Black Channel I, except one of the Black Channel II spells may be used.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.35: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 300' and delivers an E Impact critical if the target fails to resist.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.40: As Black Channel I , except that it may be cast upon as many targets as the caster has levels.
FLESH DESTRUCTION.50: As Black Channel I, except one of the Black Channel III spells may be used.

FLUID DESTRUCTION.3: Causes 1000 cubic feet of liquid to rapidly evaporate over the course of 1 hour.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.4: Instantly disintegrates 100 cubic feet of water.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.5: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.6: A bolt of water is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.7: No precipitation will occur in the area of effect for the duration of the spell.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.8: Removes all the liquid (usually water) from 1 cubic foot of inanimate material.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.9: As Evaporation I, except 10,000 cubic feet are affected.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.10: As Unwater I, except 1,000 cubic feet are affected.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.11: As Unrain, except affects a 500'/level radius for 1 day/level.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.12: As Dehydrate, except affects 10 cubic feet.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.13: As Evaporation I , except affects 100,000 cubic feet.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.14: As Unwater I, except affects 10,000 cubic feet.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.15: As Calm Water, except radius is 100'/lvl and waves are cut by 50' in the center and caster need not concentrate.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.16: A bolt of acid is shot from the palm of the caster; results determined on the Fire Bolt Table, but replacing Heat criticals with Acid criticals and also doing Impact criticals one severity level less than the primary critical.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.17: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300', or a 75' range increment in the case of Water Bolt.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.18: [RR Mod.: -25] This spell takes advantage of any bleeding wounds on a target to draw out and destroy additional blood. Target suffers the equivalent of ten rounds bleeding from his wounds as a result of this spell (e.g., if the target were bleeding at a rate of 3 hits per round, this spell would inflict 30 hits of damage).
FLUID DESTRUCTION.19: The target immediately begins to dehydrate and wither as if in a wind-blown sandy desert. Target is immediately at a -10 penalty to all actions and loses 1% of his total hits. For each round that the caster concentrates, the target takes another -5 penalty and 1% of total hits. If the caster maintains concentration for 10 consecutive rounds, animate targets will be reduced to dried out husks, dying. If the target gets out of range or the caster stops concentrating, there is no further damage, but the penalty and hits loss remain. The hits can heal normally, but the penalty recovers at a rate of 10 per day, assuming regular hydration. A Cure Disease spell can remove the penalty, once the hit loss is healed.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.20: For every minute that the caster concentrates, the humidity drops 1% of the original humidity (up to 50%). The humidity will gradually increase at a rate of 5% (of original humidity) per day until the original humidity is reached. Note that this change in humidity levels could affect an area’s climate (in turn affecting the area’s vegetation).
FLUID DESTRUCTION.25: As Evaporation I, except 1,000,000 cubic feet can be affected.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.30: As Calm Water True , except radius is 1000'R/level.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.35: As Acidic Bolt I , but with a range increment of 125'.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.40: As Desiccation, except caster may affect up to one target/level.
FLUID DESTRUCTION.50: Every minute that the caster concentrates, the humidity drops 5% of the original humidity (up to 95%). The humidity later increases as per the Dehumidify spell. Note that this change in humidity levels could affect an area’s climate (in turn affecting the area’s vegetation).

FLUID ELEMENTS.1: Caster can whisper and the air will carry his words to one person within range.
FLUID ELEMENTS.2: A missile within 10' of the caster is given calm air around it throughout its flight, allowing it to ignore all penalties due to wind, including magical air movement (e.g., an Airwall).
FLUID ELEMENTS.3: Creates a dense fog within the area of effect. Treat as a heavy fog as described in RMCL Section 15.7.
FLUID ELEMENTS.4: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty.
FLUID ELEMENTS.5: Creates a wall of water up to 10'x10'x1'. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Run going in would be a Walk pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty.
FLUID ELEMENTS.6: A missile launched from within 10' of the caster is aided by a wind that leads it towards its intended target. This provides a +10 bonus to the missile attack. This cannot be combined with Path of Calm.
FLUID ELEMENTS.7: Disperses fog within the area of effect. If not all of a fog is dispersed (i.e., the fog covers a greater area than the area of effect for this spell), the fog will refill the area of effect, reducing the cleared radius by 10' every 1-5 minutes (varying depending on the heaviness of the fog and ambient humidity).
FLUID ELEMENTS.8: A bolt of water is shot from the caster’s palm; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table.
FLUID ELEMENTS.9: Target’s fluids are immediately replenished, eliminating any dehydration that the target may have been suffering. This spell also meets the target’s water needs for the next 24 hours, not including losses directly due to magic (i.e., it will not defend against a Dessication spell, but will cure its effects if cast after).
FLUID ELEMENTS.10: Creates a 5' radius of near vacuum; all within the radius take a B Impact critical as the air leaves and rushes back in (if they fail an RR).
FLUID ELEMENTS.11: As Guiding Wind I, except bonus is +20.
FLUID ELEMENTS.12: As Waterwall, except caster need not concentrate and the duration is 1 minute per level.
FLUID ELEMENTS.13: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
FLUID ELEMENTS.14: By concentrating, the caster can convert a liquid that is mostly water into pure water or a gas that is mainly breathable air into fresh air. If purifying a liquid, the caster can purity a cubic foot per round of concentration. If purifying a gas, the caster can purify 10' cubed (i.e., 1000 cubic feet) each round. The purification has a permanent effect; the duration of Concentration is purely a matter of continuing purification.
FLUID ELEMENTS.15: As Water Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
FLUID ELEMENTS.16: As Vacuum I, except radius is 10'.
FLUID ELEMENTS.17: As Guiding Wind I, except bonus is +30.
FLUID ELEMENTS.18: Caster can control the speed and direction of the wind. Caster can increase or decrease wind speed by 1 mph per caster level (e.g., if the wind speed is normally 20 mph, a 15th level caster could alter the wind speed to between 5 and 35 mph). By directing the wind against missile attacks the caster can cause a -1 modification for each mph of wind speed. Note that caster can also control the direction of the flow of gases and/or clouds.
FLUID ELEMENTS.19: As Water Bolt I , except range increment is 125'.
FLUID ELEMENTS.20: As Vacuum I, except radius is 15'.
FLUID ELEMENTS.25: As Guiding Wind I, except bonus is +50 and any range penalties are halved.
FLUID ELEMENTS.30: The area of effect for this spell is the water immediately surrounding the boat that the caster is on (where “boat” should be interpreted loosely; a log is a “boat” for purposes of this spell if the caster is riding it on water). For as long as the caster concentrates, the speed of the boat will be increased by 25' per round (approximately 3 miles per hour).
FLUID ELEMENTS.35: Creates a whirlpool with a 50 foot radius in any liquid body that will draw in any objects in the liquid up to 500' away. Objects capable of moving under their own power may make a Very Hard Swimming maneuver to resist the pull of the whirlpool.
FLUID ELEMENTS.40: As Vacuum I, except radius is 30'.
FLUID ELEMENTS.50: Summons forces of nature in a fierce thunderstorm which includes heavy rain, 25-60 mph winds, lightning (random) and a blizzard (if correct climate). Can be delayed up to 1 hour/level after casting.

FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.1: Caster’s eyes enlarge. Caster gains a +20 to Perception rolls involving vision exclusively and a +10 to Perception rolls involving vision along with other senses. Any vision enhancement spells cast on the caster while this spell is active will last until this spell expires or their normal duration, whichever is longer.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.2: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 2. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.3: Caster grows a prehensile tail strong enough to support his own weight.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.4: Caster grows several tentacles, which can be used to make an attack on the Grapple Attack Table without occupying the caster’s hands. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.5: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 3. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.6: Caster grows wings. Caster can now fly at a rate equal to his normal walking speed. He can fly at faster paces, but must make Endurance rolls as if moving one pace faster than actual, with movement at Dash pace requiring an Endurance roll every round at a -20 penalty.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.7: Caster grows horns, which can be used to make an attack on the Horn Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.8: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 4.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.9: Caster grows clawed hands, which can be used to make an attack on the Claw Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.10: As Wings I , except Fatigue checks are as normal for the pace of movement and any maneuver rolls related to flight are made with a +20 bonus.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.11: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 5. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.12: Caster grows fangs or teeth suitable for attacks, which can be used to make an attack on the Bite Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.13: Caster grows spiny projections. Any time the caster is attacked unarmed or with a weapon less than onehalf the caster’s height in length, the attacker will suffer an attack resolved on the Stinger Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is half the caster’s skill in this list. This attack is automatic and does not require an action by the caster.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.14: As Wings II, except base speed is twice the caster’s normal walking pace.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.15: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 6. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.16: When using the attacks provided by spells on this list, the caster can substitute his skill in this list for any one of the following Combat Expertise skills: Disarm, Multiple Attacks, Restricted Quarters, or Reverse Strike. The skill to which this applies may be different each round.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.17: The attack provided by Horns, Claws, Bite, or Spines will deliver a poison upon doing a critical. The level of the poison is the caster’s level. Only one attack becomes poisonous, but this spell can be cast multiple times if the caster has cast more than one of the listed spells active. Caster determines the type of poison. This spell lasts as long as the spell whose attack it is enhancing.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.18: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 7. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.19: Caster grows a group of spinnerets somewhere on his body, which can be used to make webs. This can be used to create up to 100' of silk rope per round, capable of withstanding a thousand pounds of weight. Webs can be sticky in some parts and not in others, allowing the creation of anchored bridges made of webbing. The webs can also be launched with the ranges of a javelin, with the attack resolved on the Grapple Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.20: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 8. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.25: Caster begins to exhale a toxic gas (caster is immune to the effects). Any living thing coming within 10' of the caster must make an RR versus poison each round or suffer a cumulative -5 penalty and take 1d10 damage from the effects of the gas. Treat the penalty as organ damage (lungs) for treatment/healing purposes and the final penalty as a single injury. Level of the poison is the level of the caster. Caster can also exhale a 50' long cone, reaching a width of 25' at its furthest extent, but this requires such effort that an Endurance roll is called for.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.30: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 9.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.35: Caster’s body adapts to survive in environmental conditions. This can involve submersion in freezing water at depths with crushing pressure, traversing molten lava, enduring exposure to a raging sandstorm, or withstanding the bizarre conditions of an alien dimension. This does not cover any form of deliberate attack (even while comfortable naked in a blizzard, the caster will still suffer normal damage from a Cold Ball).
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.40: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 10.
FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS.50: Caster may use any lower level spell for this list once per round.

GAS ALTERATION.1: Condenses 1 cubic foot of water from the surrounding air. Condensation can be channeled into any suitable receptacle, including caster’s cupped hands.
GAS ALTERATION.2: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
GAS ALTERATION.3: Creates very dense fog in a 20' radius; normal visibility is 1'.
GAS ALTERATION.4: Creates a near vacuum with a five foot radius; all within this radius take an A Impact critical (if they fail an RR), as air leaves and rushes back in.
GAS ALTERATION.5: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' radius.
GAS ALTERATION.6: As Thick Fog II, except radius is 100'.
GAS ALTERATION.7: As Airwall, except has a set duration of 1 min/level and does not require concentration.
GAS ALTERATION.8: A bolt of fire is shot from a fire source within 20' of the caster; results are determined on the Fire Bolt Table. Range modifier is based on the distance from the fire source to the bolt’s target.
GAS ALTERATION.9: As Vacuum A, except results in a B Impact critical.
GAS ALTERATION.10: As Air Stop I, except radius is 50'.
GAS ALTERATION.11: As Thick Fog II, except radius is 300'.
GAS ALTERATION.12: All gas within 10' of caster is converted to breathable air.
GAS ALTERATION.13: As Vacuum A, except results in a C Impact critical.
GAS ALTERATION.14: Enriches a 50' radius of air with extra oxygen; within this radius all animal life receives a +20 bonus to their OBs and fire attacks deliver double the normal number of hits.
GAS ALTERATION.15: Creates a whirlwind about caster; it moves with the caster and has a 10'R. No missile attacks can penetrate it and any movement (except for caster’s) into the whirlwind costs
GAS ALTERATION.16: As Bolt of Fire I , except range increment is 75'.
GAS ALTERATION.17: As Vacuum A, except causes a D Impact critical.
GAS ALTERATION.18: As Vacuum B, except radius is 10'.
GAS ALTERATION.19: Allows the caster to change the direction of the wind within the radius.
GAS ALTERATION.20: As Thick Fog II, except duration is 1 hour per level and the radius is 100' per level.
GAS ALTERATION.25: As Vacuum A, except causes an E Impact critical.
GAS ALTERATION.30: Caster has complete control of clouds within area of effect (includes fog); storm clouds can be gathered and made to rain (or snow or hail in suitable conditions), but they cannot be created.
GAS ALTERATION.35: As Vacuum E, except radius is 10'.
GAS ALTERATION.40: Creates a cloud of toxic gas. Any living thing coming within the cloud must make an RR versus poison each round or suffer a cumulative -10 penalty and take 2d10 damage from the effects of the gas. Treat the penalty as organ damage (lungs) for treatment/healing purposes and the final penalty as a single injury. Level of the poison is the level of the caster. The cloud takes one round to form, but the gas is barely visible (Very Hard Perception maneuver to notice by sight). Those in the cloud during the formation round will have their breathing irritated but suffer no damage if they leave the cloud before the next round.
GAS ALTERATION.50: May transmute 1,000 cubic feet of gas (no part can be more than 20' away) into another non-magical gas that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work.

GAS DESTRUCTION.1: Disperses all fog in a 10'/level radius and prevents new fog from forming within that radius for 10 minutes per level. If a fog-creating spell makes its RR against this spell, it can create fog within the area of effect.
GAS DESTRUCTION.2: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' radius.
GAS DESTRUCTION.3: Creates a 5' radius of near vacuum; all within the radius take a B Impact critical as the air leaves and rushes back in (if they fail an RR).
GAS DESTRUCTION.4: Caster can remove the oxygen in a 10' radius at the rate of 2% of the original oxygen each round.
GAS DESTRUCTION.5: As Air Stop I, except affects a radius of 20'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.6: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 10'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.7: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.8: Changes any gas to normal, breathable air at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round as long as the caster concentrates.
GAS DESTRUCTION.9: As Air Stop I, except affects a radius of 100'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.10: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 20'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.11: As Vacuum I, except delivers a D Impact critical in a 5' radius.
GAS DESTRUCTION.12: As Deoxygenation I , except removal rate is 5% per round.
GAS DESTRUCTION.13: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 50'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.14: As Great Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 10'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.15: As Fast Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.16: As Air Stop I , except affects a radius of 10'/level.
GAS DESTRUCTION.17: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.18: As Great Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 15'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.19: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20' and removal rate is 10% per round.
GAS DESTRUCTION.20: [RR Mod.: -10] As Vacuum I, except delivers an E Impact critical in a 5' radius.
GAS DESTRUCTION.25: As Deoxygenation I , except affects a radius of 20' and removal rate is 20% per round.
GAS DESTRUCTION.30: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Vacuum I, except affects a 10' radius.
GAS DESTRUCTION.35: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 50'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.40: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 20'.
GAS DESTRUCTION.50: Destroys gases in a single enclosure (usually a building) up to a size of 100'x100'x100' (or 1,000,000 cubic feet). The building has a chance of collapsing (dependent on construction) and everyone inside takes an E Impact critical from falling debris. The implosion will make a very loud noise. If the building collapses everyone within a distance from the building equal to the building’s height takes a C Impact critical from flying debris.

GAS MANIPULATION.1: Condenses 1 cubic foot of water from the surrounding air. Condensation can be channeled into any suitable receptacle, including caster’s cupped hands.
GAS MANIPULATION.2: Warms 1 cubic foot per level of air (any inanimate gas) up to 100°F (56°C) above ambient temperature.
GAS MANIPULATION.3: Creates very dense fog in a 10' radius; normal visibility is 1'.
GAS MANIPULATION.4: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
GAS MANIPULATION.5: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' radius.
GAS MANIPULATION.6: Creates a near vacuum with a five foot radius; all within this radius take an A Impact critical, as air leaves and rushes back in.
GAS MANIPULATION.7: Creates very dense fog in a 50' radius; normal visibility is 1'.
GAS MANIPULATION.8: Disperses fog within the area of effect. If not all of a fog is dispersed (i.e., the fog covers a greater area than the area of effect for this spell), the fog will refill the area of effect, reducing the cleared radius by 10' every 1-5 minutes (varying depending on the heaviness of the fog and ambient humidity).
GAS MANIPULATION.9: As Airwall, except wall is 10'x20'x3' and can be curved up to a semicircle.
GAS MANIPULATION.10: As Airwall, except has a set duration of 1 min/level and does not require concentration.
GAS MANIPULATION.11: Creates very dense fog in a 100' radius; normal visibility is 1'.
GAS MANIPULATION.12: As Air Stop I, except radius is 50'.
GAS MANIPULATION.13: As Unfog V, except radius is 100'.
GAS MANIPULATION.14: As Soft Vacuum I, except radius is 10'.
GAS MANIPULATION.15: All gas within 10' of caster is converted to breathable air.
GAS MANIPULATION.16: As Soft Vacuum I, except it does a C Impact critical.
GAS MANIPULATION.17: Creates a whirlwind about caster; it moves with the caster and has a 10'R. No missile attacks can penetrate it and any movement (except for caster’s) into the whirlwind costs 2 AP and slows movement to a Walk pace, melee or elemental attacks on the caster through the whirlwind suffer a -50 penalty to OB.
GAS MANIPULATION.18: As Soft Vacuum I, except radius is 20'.
GAS MANIPULATION.19: Controls the wind within the radius of effect, from completely still air to up to 1 mph/level of wind in a constant direction.
GAS MANIPULATION.20: Enriches a 50' radius of air with extra oxygen; within this radius all animal life receives a +20 bonus to their OBs and fire attacks deliver double the normal number of hits.
GAS MANIPULATION.25: As Thick Fog I, except duration is 1 hour/level and the radius is 100'/level.
GAS MANIPULATION.30: As Unfog V, except radius is 100'/level.
GAS MANIPULATION.35: As Soft Vacuum I, except radius is 30'.
GAS MANIPULATION.40: As Hard Vacuum I, except radius is 20'.
GAS MANIPULATION.50: Caster has complete control of clouds within area of effect (includes fog); storm clouds can be gathered and made to rain (or snow or hail in suitable conditions), but they cannot be created.

GATE MASTERY.1: The caster can attune himself to a small creature of animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw) to serve as his familiar. The caster must obtain the creature (which can be no more than 10% of caster’s own mass) through normal means and cast the spell on the animal daily for 1 week (associating with the animal for 2 hours each day). The caster can then control the familiar and view the world through its senses by concentrating on it (must be within 50' per caster level). If the animal is killed, the caster will have a -25 modification to all actions for 2 weeks.
GATE MASTERY.2: Caster can instantly summon a first level non-intelligent (i.e., possessing the Animalistic or Mindless flaw) living creature, which appears somewhere within the spell’s range. The caster can specify the general type of creature, but exactly what the creature is should be determined randomly (e.g., the caster could specify “four-legged, hoofed” and could get a zebra, horse, camel, etc.). If the creature can understand the caster’s communication, the creature can be controlled by normal communication. Otherwise, control is maintained through concentration of the caster. If the creature summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in its own perception), the duration for this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the creature is ever put into a dangerous situa -
GATE MASTERY.3: While this spell is active, any spell targeting the caster (including “self” spells) for which the caster is a willing target will also affect the familiar. Note that a familiar may not benefit from every possible spell (e.g., even though familiars tend to be smarter than normal for their kind, a familiar would not know what to do with the benefits of Fire Mastery).
GATE MASTERY.4: [RR Mod.: Special, see text] By concentrating, the caster can control one Grade I elemental. If this spell fails, the elemental will act normally unless it is a spell-caster, in which case it will become hostile (i.e., only a spell-casting elemental will recognize a control attempt as a hostile act). If the caster of this spell knows the Magician Base list corresponding to the elemental’s element, then the caster’s ranks in that list are subtracted from the RR. If the caster knows the Closed Essence elemental list corresponding to the elemental’s element (e.g. Fluid Elements for Water or Air elements), then the subtraction is half the number of the caster’s ranks in that list. Once free of control, the elemental will be hostile to the caster if the elemental was placed in danger. A controlled elemental may be commanded to return to its origin; this will take the elemental two rounds. Thus, this spell doubles as a means of banishing elementals.
GATE MASTERY.5: As Summons I, except the caster chooses one of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options to the higher level Summons spells, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).
GATE MASTERY.6: Allows the caster to summon a Grade I elemental. This elemental will appear over the course of two rounds. If uncontrolled after those two rounds are complete, it will act normally according to its nature. Uncontrolled elementals can return to their place of origin at any time, taking two rounds to do so. If the elemental is in a region that is particularly suitable for it (e.g., an Ice elemental summoned to a glacier), it will not return for 1d10 days. If the region is not particularly suitable for it (e.g., an Ice elemental summoned to a temperate forest), it will stay for 1d10 hours. If the region is unsuitable for the elemental (e.g., an Ice elemental summoned to a desert), it will stay for 1d10 minutes. This is normal behavior and if a method of communication exists, or if there is an obvious motive to act otherwise, behavior may vary.
GATE MASTERY.7: As Summons II , except two options can be chosen.
GATE MASTERY.8: As Elemental Control I, except that a Grade I or II elemental can be controlled.
GATE MASTERY.9: As Summons III , except four options can be chosen.
GATE MASTERY.10: As Elemental Summons I, except a Grade I or II elemental can be summoned.
GATE MASTERY.11: As Summons III, except 9 options can be chosen.
GATE MASTERY.12: As Elemental Control I, except that a Grade I-III elemental can be controlled.
GATE MASTERY.13: As Familiar, except that there is no limit on the mass of the familiar, and the caster suffers a -50 penalty for two weeks if the familiar is killed.
GATE MASTERY.14: As Elemental Summons I, except an elemental of Grade III or lower can be summoned.
GATE MASTERY.15: As Summons X, except it can be delayed up to 1 day/level or until triggered by one of the following (decided by the caster at the time of casting): specified movements, sounds, touch, or actions. Trigger must be such as the caster would be able to sense it if still at the location of the spell effect. At the time of casting, the caster may specify a single task for the creature(s) to perform when it/they arrive(s).
GATE MASTERY.16: As Summons III, except 19 options can be chosen.
GATE MASTERY.17: As Elemental Control I, except that a Grade I-IV elemental can be controlled.
GATE MASTERY.18: Cast on a Grade I, II, or III elemental that the caster currently controls (through an Elemental Control spell), this spell places the elemental under the caster’s permanent control. By concentrating, the caster can issue orders to mastered elementals, which will continue to follow an order without the caster’s concentration. A mastered elemental receives a +40 to all RRs against attempts to control it by a different caster. However, any elemental will become hostile if this spell is cast on it and the spell does not succeed (and the elemental is no longer under the caster’s control and has a +40 bonus to resist new controls). A mastered elemental can be sent away by its master in the same way as a controlled elemental; this banishment terminates the Mastery spell. A caster can never have more levels of elementals mastered than his number of ranks in this list (e.g., a caster with 20 ranks in Gate Mastery may be Master of 10 level 2 elementals or 5 level 4 elementals or 2 level 6 elementals and 4 level 2 elementals or some other combination whose total levels do not exceed 20). The range given is only for initial mastery; once mastery is achieved, the caster and the mastered elemental may part without breaking the bond.
GATE MASTERY.19: As Elemental Summons I, except an elemental of Grade IV or lower can be summoned.
GATE MASTERY.20: As Summons III , except one option per caster level is chosen.
GATE MASTERY.25: As Elemental Control I, except that a Grade I-V elemental can be controlled.
GATE MASTERY.30: As Elemental Summons I, except an elemental of Grade V or lower can be summoned.
GATE MASTERY.35: As Elemental Control I, except that a Grade I-VI elemental can be controlled.
GATE MASTERY.40: As Elemental Summons I, except an elemental of Grade VI or lower can be summoned.
GATE MASTERY.50: As Lesser Elemental Mastery, except Grade I through VI elementals can be mastered.

GATHERING SECRETS.1: This spell copies a document that the caster is examining. The caster merely concentrates and touches the document with one hand and a blank sheet with his other hand. The copy is not exact (i.e., not a forgery). Note that the caster does not have to be able to read the document. Alternatively, this spell can transcribe (in monochromatic black ink) one scene that he is viewing onto a page, creating the equivalent of a well-drawn, detailed realistic drawing. The process occurs at the rate of 1 minute per page or scene.
GATHERING SECRETS.2: Caster can pick a point up to 10'/level away and will hear as if physically at that point (there can be intervening objects such as walls). Note that this means that he cannot hear where his ears are.
GATHERING SECRETS.3: This spell makes a hardened clay copy of a small, 3-dimensional object (up to 6"x6"x6"). This spell requires that the caster have enough clay to make the copy (at a one to one scale). The clay must start off soft (the spell will harden it). This process requires one minute of concentration.
GATHERING SECRETS.4: One location is “marked” so that the caster can find it again later with a Far Sense spell. The marker is automatically located when the caster casts a Far Sense spell if the marker is within range of the Far Sense spell. A marker can be placed on any inanimate object. The caster can only have as many markers as he has ranks in this list; a Sensory Marker spell can be cancelled even if the caster is no longer within range of the marker.
GATHERING SECRETS.5: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene every four rounds.
GATHERING SECRETS.6: Caster may designate any one of his senses to be used at a “marked” location within range (see Sensory Marker).
