The caster can use any perception abilities he has at the time (e.g., if he is currently under the effects of Nightvision, he will have Nightvision at the marked location). The remote sensory point may be rotated to face any direction. While he is sensing at the marked location, the caster must remain immobile and concentrate (and does not have that sense available at his own location).
GATHERING SECRETS.7: After casting this spell, the caster will know what (if any) evidence exists within the range which would implicate the currently studied patsy if found at the scene of the crime (see Study Patsy on the Assassination Mastery list). The evidence (if any exists) will be circumstantial, but will imply that the patsy was at the scene when a crime occurred. This spell provides only the direction, distance, and description of the piece of evidence. It does not provide any details as to why the patsy will be implicated by the evidence.
GATHERING SECRETS.8: As Listen, except caster sees from the fixed point (it can rotate).
GATHERING SECRETS.9: As Duplicate I, except will work on an object up to 3' x 3' x 3' in dimensions.
GATHERING SECRETS.10: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene per 2 rounds.
GATHERING SECRETS.11: As Far Sense I, except caster may designate up to two senses for use at the marked location.
GATHERING SECRETS.12: Caster can see through wood (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).
GATHERING SECRETS.13: As Locate Evidence I, except two pieces of evidence are located (providing such exist) and the evidence will be incriminating (not just circumstantial).
GATHERING SECRETS.14: As Duplicate I, except will work on an object up to 6' x 6' x 6' in dimensions.
GATHERING SECRETS.15: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene per round.
GATHERING SECRETS.16: As Far Sense I, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).
GATHERING SECRETS.17: Caster can see through stone (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).
GATHERING SECRETS.18: As Locate Evidence I , except three pieces of evidence are located and the evidence will “prove” that the patsy is guilty.
GATHERING SECRETS.19: As Far Sense II , except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).
GATHERING SECRETS.20: Caster can see through iron or steel (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).
GATHERING SECRETS.25: As Far Sense I , except the range is 500 feet/level.
GATHERING SECRETS.30: As Copy I, except copies 2 pages/scenes per round.
GATHERING SECRETS.35: Caster touches two similar writing surfaces (e.g., two parchments, two bound volumes of like dimensions, or two clay tablets) and so long as the spell remains in effect, anything written on one of these documents will also appear written on the other.
GATHERING SECRETS.40: As Far Sense II, except the range is 500 feet/level.
GATHERING SECRETS.50: As Copy I, except will copy any bound volume onto an equal number of pages (not necessarily bound). Also, the caster need not examine the material or maintain touch contact after the spell begins (he simply casts the spell and then he can leave the spell running). The spell will copy pages at the rate of 5 pages per round (and will even copy color images).

GUISES.1: Causes target to appear blurred to attackers, granting a +10 to the target’s DB.
GUISES.2: Target and objects on the target’s person appear to be in shadow and thus are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus of between +25 and +75).
GUISES.3: Target has a limited illusion on himself that allows him to look like any humanoid race within 20% of his own size. The illusion moves as the target moves.
GUISES.4: [RR Mod: -50] Caster can communicate simple ideas to the target through sign language (yes, no, hungry, good-bye, etc.); to the target it will seem as if the caster were using the gestures appropriate to the target’s culture.
GUISES.5: Target’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.
GUISES.6: As Façade I, except one of the other senses can be added.
GUISES.7: As Façade I, except that a specific person can be impersonated with regards to looks. The person must have been observed for at least 1 minute of concentration.
GUISES.8: As Façade I, except two of the other senses can be added.
GUISES.9: As Impersonation Façade I, except that one other aspect (or trait) of the person is impersonated. Additionally, the caster must study the target for at least two minutes of concentration. For example, the target’s voice can be impersonated.
GUISES.10: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 20%.
GUISES.11: Creates a duplicate of the caster, which the caster may concentrate to control and otherwise does exactly what the caster does. The image includes visual and audio components, but no other senses.
GUISES.12: As Impersonation Façade I , except that two other aspects (or traits) of the person are impersonated. Additionally, the caster must study the target for at least three minutes of concentration. For example, the target’s voice and scent can be impersonated.
GUISES.13: As Blur, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
GUISES.14: As Façade I, except all of the other senses may be added.
GUISES.15: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 30%.
GUISES.16: As Impersonation Façade I, except that three other aspects (or traits) of the person are impersonated. Additionally, the caster must study the target for at least four minutes of concentration. For example, the target’s voice, scent, and subtle mannerisms can be impersonated.
GUISES.17: The caster may cast this spell and instantly know everything necessary for Impersonation Façade spells (i.e., this spell replaces time spent studying the subject to be impersonated).
GUISES.18: As Shadow, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
GUISES.19: As Impersonation Façade I , except that all aspects of the person are impersonated. Additionally, the caster must study the target for at least five minutes of concentration. With this spell, even the closest friends and relatives of the individual impersonated will have difficulty noticing the façade.
GUISES.20: As Displacement III , except chance of missing is 40%.
GUISES.25: As Façade II, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
GUISES.30: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 50%.
GUISES.35: As Displacement II , except caster can protect one target per level.
GUISES.40: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 60%.
GUISES.50: As Façade V , except the caster can change the façade to a different façade each round.

HERB MASTERY.1: Caster can understand the nature, origin, value, and uses of any magical herb. If a plant is not a magical herb, no information can be gained other than that fact.
HERB MASTERY.2: Allows caster to enhance the efficacy of any one herb (growing or dead) with healing or durational effects. For healing herbs, recovery rolls receive an extra +25 bonus. For herbs with a durational effect, the duration is doubled (i.e., x2). This spell may be employed only once per herb.
HERB MASTERY.3: Allows caster to locate any one specific variety of herb that is sought, regardless of physical obstacles. This spell gives exact direction, distance, and quantity. If multiple patches of the herb are within range, the caster is aware of all of them.
HERB MASTERY.4: Allows caster to prepare one dose of any herb so that it will be instantly effective. When the herb is subsequently used, its benefits/dangers will be immediately felt. The herb will keep for 1 day per level of the caster.
HERB MASTERY.5: Caster can double the potency of any one herb (growing or dead). This has the effect of halving the quantity of herb needed for a full dose, i.e. if a normal dose requires one full leaf to be effective, following this spell, only half a leaf would be required. This spell may be employed only once per herb. May not be combined with Herb Enhancement.
HERB MASTERY.6: As Herb Finding I , except that the range is 300'.
HERB MASTERY.7: Caster can grow an herb to sudden maturity by planting the proper seed; the herb is sterile and the growth takes 1-10 rnds.
HERB MASTERY.8: As Herb Enhancement II , except healing herbs receive a +50 bonus and herbs with a durational effect have the duration trebled (x3). This spell may be employed only once per herb and may not be combined with Herb Enhancement II.
HERB MASTERY.9: As Herb Finding I, except range is 500'.
HERB MASTERY.10: Allows caster to remove any harmful poisons, by-products, or side effects from all herbs within range. This includes eliminating any addictive properties.
HERB MASTERY.11: As Herb Mastery II, except potency is 3x.
HERB MASTERY.12: As Herb Finding I, except locates all herbs in area of effect.
HERB MASTERY.13: As Herb Finding I , except range is 1 mile. Location is known within 10'R.
HERB MASTERY.14: Preserves an herb indefinitely. If another spell is then cast on the herb, this spell will be cancelled and normal deterioration of the herb will begin.
HERB MASTERY.15: As Herb Mastery II, except potency is 4x
HERB MASTERY.16: As Herb Finding III, except locates all herbs in area of effect.
HERB MASTERY.17: As Instant Herbal Cures, except herb will keep for 1 week per level of the caster.
HERB MASTERY.18: As Herb Finding I, except range is 5 miles. Location is known within 25'R.
HERB MASTERY.19: As Herb Finding V, except locates all herbs in area of effect.
HERB MASTERY.20: As Herb Finding I, except range is 20 miles; location is known within 100'R.
HERB MASTERY.25: As Herb Enhancement III , except that 1 herb per caster level can be affected. All herbs must be of the same type.
HERB MASTERY.30: As Herb Mastery II , except that the potency of 1 herb, growing or dead, can be increased by 100% x lvl/5 of caster (e.g., a 30th Ivl caster could increase potency of any herb by 600%, i.e., 6x, so one normal “dose” of a herb could be split into six effective portions).
HERB MASTERY.35: As Instant Herbal Cures, except herb will keep until used.
HERB MASTERY.40: As Preserved Instant Herbal Cures, except affects up to 1 herb/level.
HERB MASTERY.50: As Herb Finding I , except that caster can locate any 1 herb variety within a range of 1 mile/lvl of caster (e.g., 50 miles for a 50th level caster); location is known within 100'R. Note: See Treasure Law Section 2.3 for details on herbs.   

HIDING.1: Causes caster to appear blurred to attackers, granting a +10 to the caster’s DB.
HIDING.2: A single object is made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or target is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or target makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or target casts a spell of type E or F.
HIDING.3: Caster and objects on the caster’s person appear to be in shadow and thus are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus of between +25 and +75).
HIDING.4: Creates a 1'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As explained in Section 10.17, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Quiet - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Quiet radius, however).
HIDING.5: As Unseen I, except everything within 1' of the target is invisible as long as it is within the 1'R and none of the Unseen I termination conditions occur. The first time an Unseen I termination condition occurs, the target will become visible for one round and then become invisible again. If an Unseen I condition happens again, the spell ends. A target rendered invisible by this spell therefore can make one violent move (an attack) or cast one spell of type E or F or be struck once by a violent blow without losing the invisibility.
HIDING.6: As Resilient Invisibility I , except the caster can vary the radius up to 1'. This makes the invisible target harder to see by reducing the “fringe effect”.
HIDING.7: Creates a 1,000 square foot screen on which the caster can place any static scene; this scene seems 3-dimensional and normal.
HIDING.8: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.
HIDING.9: As Resilient Invisibility I, except target is also undetectable by smell and sound.
HIDING.10: Creates a duplicate of the caster; if the caster concentrates, it will move as willed; otherwise, it does exactly as the caster does. The duplicate cannot move more than 100’ away from the caster. If the caster concentrates, he can “merge” the duplicate with himself and then split back apart again (making it difficult to discern which one is the “real” caster).
HIDING.11: As Resilient Invisibility I, except the constant radius is 10'. Note that anything that exits the radius will become visible again.
HIDING.12: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 20%.
HIDING.13: Caster is flattened until becoming virtually two-dimensional. This allows the caster to slide through cracks and makes the caster effectively invisible when viewed from the side.
HIDING.14: Caster can merge into any solid, inanimate material. While merged, the caster cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the caster emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the caster’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The caster can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the caster automatically emerges at the entrance point).
HIDING.15: As Resilient Invisibility II, except the invisibility can survive (level/5) termination actions. For example, if cast by a 15th level caster, the invisibility will survive three (15/5) termination events, e.g. a total of three violent moves, spell casting attempts or being struck violently, thus the caster could make an attack, be hit by an opponent, and cast a spell without losing the invisibility, but if the caster made another attack or cast another E or F spell or suffered another hit, the invisibility would end .
HIDING.16: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 30%.
HIDING.17: Caster may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.
HIDING.18: Caster’s actions are extremely subtle for the duration of this spell. Any form of non-magical detection or observation regarding the Mystic and his activities suffers a -50 penalty. The caster also may hide spell-casting without the normal Subtlety penalty to his spell-casting roll (and the -50 is applied to attempts to detect this casting as well).
HIDING.19: As Merging, except caster can turn within the material and can perceive the outside world if within 6" of the surface.
HIDING.20: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 40%.
HIDING.25: Caster and objects on his person cannot be detected by any “Detect...” spells.
HIDING.30: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 50%.
HIDING.35: Caster disappears into a private world. This place is largely featureless, but by concentrating, the caster can view (and listen) from the point of departure. The caster may also create any material objects desired, although anything not consumed by the caster will disappear upon leaving the dimensional closet (so the caster may eat, drink, take an antidote to a poison, etc., but cannot arm himself or emerge with great wealth). The caster will reappear in the same place when the duration of the spell expires or when the caster chooses to cancel the spell. If the point of departure is now occupied, the caster will be teleported D10x100 feet in a random direction.
HIDING.40: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 60%.
HIDING.50: Caster can “Merge” into a material and may observe surrounding activity, and is affected by Unpresence, Nondetect, and Greater Resilient Invisibility all with this one spell.

HOLY ARMS.1: Adds +5 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.2: After casting this spell, the caster’s next melee or missile attack will, if made against a foe who receives a critical reduction due to size or other factors, have the critical reduction reduced by one. For example, a Medium combatant attacking a Large foe would normally have a C critical done against that foe reduced to an A critical. If that Medium combatant benefited from this spell, that critical would only be reduced to a B.
HOLY ARMS.3: Caster gains a +5 to his Strength stat bonus.
HOLY ARMS.4: Adds +10 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.5: As Righteous Blow I, except two steps of critical reduction are negated.
HOLY ARMS.6: Weapon becomes indestructable for spell duration. This includes not only normal breakage, but also magical effects that would break or otherwise damage the weapon. It is still possible to disarm the wielder of the weapon. Once the weapon has spent three consecutive rounds outside the caster’s possession, it can be broken normally.
HOLY ARMS.7: Adds +15 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.8: As Righteous Blow I, except three steps of critical reduction are negated.
HOLY ARMS.9: A bright, shimmering aura surrounds the caster. All creatures “of darkness” who are in the area of effect must make an RR each round or receive an A severity Holy critical.
HOLY ARMS.10: Adds +20 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.11: As Righteous Blow I, except four steps of critical reduction are negated.
HOLY ARMS.12: Caster gains a +10 to his Strength stat bonus.
HOLY ARMS.13: Adds +25 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.14: Caster will not fumble. Caster cannot be disarmed nor his weapon(s) broken while the spell lasts.
HOLY ARMS.15: A bright, shimmering aura surrounds the caster. All creatures “of darkness” who are in the area of effect must make an RR each round or receive a B severity Holy critical.
HOLY ARMS.16: Adds +30 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.17: Caster gains a +15 to his Strength stat bonus.
HOLY ARMS.18: Caster immediately hurls a (melee) weapon, which attacks using his normal throwing OB with the weapon or his melee OB with the weapon, whichever is higher. This spell allows this attack to use the ranges for a short bow.
HOLY ARMS.19: Adds +35 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.20: A bright, shimmering aura surrounds the caster. All creatures “of darkness” who are in the area of effect must make an RR each round or receive a C severity Holy critical.
HOLY ARMS.25: Adds +40 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.30: A bright, shimmering aura surrounds the caster. All creatures “of darkness” who are in the area of effect must make an RR each round or receive a D severity Holy critical.
HOLY ARMS.35: Adds +50 to the caster’s melee or missile OB for the current round.
HOLY ARMS.40: A bright, shimmering aura surrounds the caster. All creatures “of darkness” who are in the area of effect must make an RR each round or receive an E severity Holy critical.
HOLY ARMS.50: Caster exposes all within the area of effect to the raw power of his deity. This has an effect equal to a Fire Ball attack with an OB equal to twice the caster’s level (i.e., a 50th level caster results in a +100 OB), with concussion damage doubled, Fire criticals replaced with Holy criticals, and all criticals being treated as Slaying III. The caster acts as the center-point for the blast and is not immune to any of the damage. For the caster, no objects, spells, etc. will protect him from the blast.

HOLY HEALING.1: Reduces target’s blood loss by up to 1 hit per round. Until the injury is actually treated by some means (e.g., Medicine maneuver or appropriate Relief spell), the target cannot move or be moved without the bleeding resuming. Multiple Flowstop spells may not be cast upon the same wound.
HOLY HEALING.3: Removes up to -30 in penalties from fatigue.
HOLY HEALING.4: An injury of any type other than nerve or brain injury incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
HOLY HEALING.6: Target is relieved of 1 round worth of accumulated stun effects.
HOLY HEALING.7: As Flowstop I, except up to 5 hits per round.
HOLY HEALING.8: Instantly stops bleeding from one wound that bleeds at a rate of 1 hit/round.
HOLY HEALING.9: As Light Wound Relief, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
HOLY HEALING.12: As Light Wound Relief, except an injury incurring a penalty of any amount.
HOLY HEALING.13: Instantly stops bleeding from one wound that bleeds at a rate of up to 3 hits/round.
HOLY HEALING.14: Target is relieved of 3 rounds worth of accumulated stun effects.
HOLY HEALING.15: Caster can cure any paralysis problem. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as -100 injury for progress of recovery.
HOLY HEALING.17: Target regains function in one sensory organ, so long as that sensory organ has not been completely destroyed (in which case Organ Regeneration is required). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
HOLY HEALING.18: Target comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure. Process takes 1-10 hours and target is at -50 to Endurance rolls for 1-10 days.
HOLY HEALING.19: Stops bleeding on a wound that is bleeding at any rate. For one hour, the target can engage in no activity more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume at the prior rate.
HOLY HEALING.20: Target is relieved of all accumulated stun effects.
HOLY HEALING.25: Target is healed of all concussion hit damage.
HOLY HEALING.30: As Light Wound Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
HOLY HEALING.35: As Medium Wound Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
HOLY HEALING.40: As Severe Wound Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
HOLY HEALING.50: Caster can use one of the lower level spells on this list of 25th level or below once per round.

HOLY SHIELDS.1: Everyone in the area of effect who is aligned with the caster (see above) subtracts 5 from all elemental attacks against them. They may also add 5 to all RRs versus spells. These bonuses are not conferred to the caster.
HOLY SHIELDS.2: Causes a bright aura about the caster, providing a more powerful appearance and subtracting 10 from all attacks on the caster.
HOLY SHIELDS.3: Creates a golden full shield emblazoned with the holy symbol of the caster’s faith. If the caster has a free arm, it functions in all ways as a physical shield, but it does not count for encumbrance, and lasts for 1 minute per level. If the caster does not have a free arm, it moves of its own accord to defend the caster, providing +25 DB against up to 3 frontal attacks per round, but cannot benefit from Shield skill and cannot be com-
HOLY SHIELDS.4: Caster may employ the Protecting Others rules (see RMCL Section 9.7) to defend two other individuals at the same time with only the normal penalty for protecting one individual.
HOLY SHIELDS.5: Caster is able to sustain an additional 25% of his total concussion hits (round off fractional hits) before passing out. Hits are still taken and remain when the spell lapses.
HOLY SHIELDS.6: As Protection Prayer I, except bonuses are 10.
HOLY SHIELDS.7: Caster can deflect any one missile that the caster can see and which passes within the 100' range of this spell; this causes 50 to be subtracted from the missile’s attack.
HOLY SHIELDS.8: As Aura, except it produces an extremely powerful appearance and subtracts 15 from all attacks.
HOLY SHIELDS.9: As Deflections I, except its effect is against 1 melee attack.
HOLY SHIELDS.10: As Unpain I, except an additional 50% of hits can be sustained.
HOLY SHIELDS.11: As Protection Prayer I , except bonuses are 15.
HOLY SHIELDS.12: As Deflections I, except 2 missiles may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.13: As Deflections I, except missile automatically misses.
HOLY SHIELDS.14: As Bladeturn I, except 2 melee attacks may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.15: As Unpain I, except an additional 75% of hits can be sustained.
HOLY SHIELDS.16: As Protection Prayer I , except bonuses are 20.
HOLY SHIELDS.17: As Deflections I, except 3 missiles may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.18: As Aim Untrue I , except 2 missiles may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.19: As Bladeturn I, except 3 melee attacks may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.20: As Unpain I, except an additional 100% of hits can be sustained.
HOLY SHIELDS.25: As Protection Prayer I, except bonuses are 25.
HOLY SHIELDS.30: As Deflections I, except as many missiles as the caster’s level may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.35: As Bladeturn I, except as many melee attacks as the caster’s level may be affected.
HOLY SHIELDS.40: Creates a golden suit of plate armor around the caster. This armor does not interfere with spell-casting, but otherwise acts as a normal suit of armor with Armor Type 10 including arm and leg armor and helmet.
HOLY SHIELDS.50: As Protection Prayer V , except radius of effect is 100' and duration is 10 minutes per level. Note: None of the “Aura” spells (Aura, True Aura, Mass Aura, and Alkar) are cumulative with each other or Blur. The Gamemaster may wish to allow the bonus from these spells to apply to Leadership as well as defense. When under the effects of an Unpain spell, there are no penalties for hits lost. Full effects of the lost hits occur when the spell duration ends. However, if the hits are healed before the duration ends, there is no effect. So, a character could be reduced to -38 hits while sustained by Unpain, but restored to 2 hits before the duration of the spell ended. He would never pass out. Likewise, if Unpain allows fighting beyond lethal hit loss, this has no effect until the spell expires. The GM should be careful and clear when defining “alignment”. Depending on the state of theology in the game world, this might mean that one needs to worship the caster’s deity, or it might merely require that a deity of the same mythos be followed (e.g., in a world using Greek, Norse, Indian, Chinese, and Celtic gods, a devotee of Zeus might be considered of the same “alignment” as a priest of Poseidon).   

HOLY WAR.1: Detects the presence of Undead in the area of effect. Caster can concentrate on a different area each round. (See Section 10.4 for more about detection spells.)
HOLY WAR.2: Detects the total number of “enemies” (giving approximate direction and distance). This spell also reveals the most heavily represented type of enemy (by total levels). Caster can concentrate on a different 50'R each round.
HOLY WAR.3: Caster’s next melee attack (within one minute of casting this spell) which scores a critical on an undead will do an additional A Holy critical.
HOLY WAR.4: Caster’s next melee attack (within one minute of casting this spell) which scores a critical on a demon will do an additional A Holy critical.
HOLY WAR.5: The caster gains a +10 bonus to his RRs against Life Drain by the undead.
HOLY WAR.6: For the duration of this spell, the caster can read and speak one “enemy” language with skill rank 8.
HOLY WAR.7: As Smite Undead I, except the additional Holy critical is severity B.
HOLY WAR.8: As Smite Demon I, except the additional Holy critical is severity B.
HOLY WAR.9: Caster’s next melee attack (within one minute of casting this spell) which scores a critical on an ‘enemy’ will do an additional A Holy critical.
HOLY WAR.10: As Resist Undead I, except bonus is +30.
HOLY WAR.11: As Smite Undead I , except the additional Holy critical is severity C.
HOLY WAR.12: As Smite Demon I, except the additional Holy critical is severity C.
HOLY WAR.13: As Resist Undead I, except protects everyone within 20' of the caster.
HOLY WAR.14: As Smite Enemy I, except the additional Holy critical is severity B.
HOLY WAR.15: Detects the presence of Undead in the area of affect. Caster can examine a different 50'R each round. The caster gains a general notion of the most prominent undead type in the radius (by total levels). “General” means that the caster could learn that the undead were mainly skeletons, but not which type(s) of skeletons.
HOLY WAR.16: As Smite Undead I , except the additional Holy critical is severity D.
HOLY WAR.17: As Smite Demon I , except the additional Holy critical is severity D.
HOLY WAR.18: As Smite Undead I, except effect applies to every melee attack while duration lasts.
HOLY WAR.19: As Smite Enemy I, except the additional Holy critical is severity C.
HOLY WAR.20: As Reveal Enemies, except for range and area of effect.
HOLY WAR.25: Caster’s next melee attack (within one minute of casting this spell) which scores a critical on an undead or a demon will do an additional E Holy critical.
HOLY WAR.30: As Smite Demon II, except effect applies to every melee attack while duration lasts.
HOLY WAR.35: Caster’s next melee attack (within one minute of casting this spell) which scores a critical on an ‘enemy’ will do an additional E Holy critical.
HOLY WAR.40: As Smite Undead III, except effect applies to every melee attack while duration lasts.
HOLY WAR.50: As Smite Abomination, except effect applies to every melee attack while duration lasts.

ICE LAW.1: The caster may decrease the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by up to 100°F (56°C) per round. Once the liquid begins to freeze it will not continue to cool, and will begin to warm naturally if the caster stops concentrating. (Temp. cannot be lower then -20°F[-29°C]).
ICE LAW.2: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -20°F (-29°C), at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round (cooling spreads evenly from initial point of cooling unless caster concentrates to direct spread).
ICE LAW.3: Creates a wall of intense cold (up to 10'x10'x1'), normally visible as mist condenses out of the air. Anyone passing through takes an A Cold critical (no RR).
ICE LAW.4: While this spell lasts, the caster can walk through ice as if it were air. The caster will not drop through ice if he enters it in the middle of a tall ice formation, as under his feet, the ice will still be solid for him and provide good footing.
ICE LAW.5: As Cool Solid, except material can be cooled to -200°F (-129°C), at a rate of cooling one cubic foot of material by 100°F (56°C) each round. The caster must concentrate to decrease the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).
ICE LAW.6: A bolt of ice is shot from the palm of the caster. Results are resolved on the Ice Bolt Table.
ICE LAW.7: A 1' ball of cold is shot from the palm of the caster. Upon reaching the selected target/location, it expands to affect a 20'R area. Resolve attacks on the Cold Ball Table.
ICE LAW.8: Creates a wall of ice up to 10'x10'x (2' at base, 1' at top); it can be melted through or chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
ICE LAW.9: Creates a cube of cold (up to 10'x10'x10') similar to a Wall of Cold; it takes 1 complete round for the cube to form and be effective. Anyone passing through (or caught within) the cube takes an A Cold critical each round spent in the cube.
ICE LAW.10: As Wall of Cold, except the “wall” is 10' high and forms a 20'R circle (6" thick) with the caster at the center. The wall will not move with the caster.
ICE LAW.11: As Ice Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
ICE LAW.12: Changes 10 cubic feet per level of water to ice, instantly.
ICE LAW.13: Chills metal to the point that it becomes extremely brittle (1 object only), giving it a -50 penalty to its strength and the object will be subject to a breakage check if it strikes another object or is struck. Anyone in direct skin-to-metal contact with the chilled metal will take a B Cold Critical each round contact is maintained.
ICE LAW.14: As Call Cold I , except the size is up to a 20' cube and the range is 20'.
ICE LAW.15: As Ice Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
ICE LAW.16: Three bolts of ice are shot from the palm of the caster. They can strike up to three different targets as long as they are all within 60° of each other and are within the caster’s field of vision. Caster may apply his Directed Spell skill by dividing it as he chooses among the three bolts (e.g., a +120 OB could be split evenly into three +40 bolts or one +120 bolt and two +0 bolts or one +60, one +40, and one +20 bolt).
ICE LAW.17: As Wall of Cold, except it lasts for 1 minute per level and does a D Cold Critical to anyone who passes through it.
ICE LAW.18: As Call Cold I, except size is up to a 50' cube and the range is 50'.
ICE LAW.19: As Ice Bolt I, except range increment is 250'.
ICE LAW.20: As Cold Ball II, except area of effect is 40'R and the range is 300'.
ICE LAW.25: For as long as it continues to rain, this spell changes all rain within 1 mile of the caster to snow; radius decreases 500' for every 10°F (5.5°C) the ambient air temperature is above freezing (32°F) (0°C).
ICE LAW.30: Causes the ambient temperature in the area of effect to drop 1°F (0.6°C) every ten minutes with a maximum reduction equal to the caster’s level in degrees Fahrenheit (within a 1000'/lvl R).
ICE LAW.35: As Call Cold I, except size is up to a 100' cube and the range is 100'.
ICE LAW.40: As Cold Ball II, except area of effect is 60'R and the range is 500'.
ICE LAW.50: Caster can use any one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

ILLUSION MASTERY.1: Creates a simple, immobile image or scene within the area of effect. One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (the corresponding Mirage spell must be known); b) the duration can be doubled; c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, or e) the illusion can cast light. All parts of the area of effect must be in the caster’s field of vision at the time of casting.
ILLUSION MASTERY.2: Creates the image of one object or being that will move however the caster wants (as long as the caster concentrates). When the caster stops concentrating, the image remains but stops moving. The caster may resume concentration again later and make the image move again (presuming that the duration has not expired). The image can be of any size that would fit within the area of effect.
ILLUSION MASTERY.3: As Illusion II, except any two of the options may be chosen. See notes above for guidelines on how to combine the options.
ILLUSION MASTERY.4: As Illusion II, except it can be delayed by up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster chooses the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following: a) time period, b) specified movements, c) specified sounds, d) specified smells, e) specified tastes, f) specified touch, or g) a Presence spell cast in the area of effect. If a sense is used to trigger the spell, that sense must be included in the illusion.
ILLUSION MASTERY.5: As Phantasms I, except one of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (the corresponding Mirage spell must be known); b) the duration can be doubled; c) the range can be doubled; d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled; e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within area of effect of the spell); f) the phantasm can cast light; or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion). See notes for guidelines on how to combine the options.
ILLUSION MASTERY.6: As Illusion II, except any four of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.7: As Phantasms II, except it can be delayed as in Waiting Illusion II. The Phantasm will move and act if given a simple command (e.g., attack, run, etc.) or it can deliver a short speech if sound was one of the senses.
ILLUSION MASTERY.8: As Waiting Illusion II, except any two of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.9: As Phantasms II, except any two of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.10: As Illusion II, except any six of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.11: As Waiting Phantasm II, except any two of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.12: As Phantasms II , except any three of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.13: As Waiting Illusion II, except any four of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.14: As Phantasm II, except any four of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.15: As Illusion II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.16: As Waiting Phantasm II, except any four of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.17: As Phantasms II, except any six of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.18: As Waiting Illusion II, except any six of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.19: All of the terrain in the area of effect will appear differently than it actually is (the corresponding Terrain spell for each sense incorporated must be known). The caster may designate in what way the terrain is different. For example, a clearing in the woods could be disguised as a heavily wooded area of the woods.
ILLUSION MASTERY.20: As Phantasms II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.25: As Waiting Illusion II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.30: As Illusion X, except its duration is permanent (until dispelled).
ILLUSION MASTERY.35: As Illusion II, except any nineteen of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.40: As Phantasms II, except any nineteen of the options may be chosen.
ILLUSION MASTERY.50: As Phantasms X , except its duration is permanent (until dispelled); Phantasm can move only if caster concentrates, except for simple programmed repetitions, if that option is chosen.

INFLUENCES.1: [RR Mod.: -30] Target that is already at least napping falls into a very deep sleep, taking a -50 penalty to Perception rolls to wake up.
INFLUENCES.2: Target animal(s) will believe the caster is a good friend.
INFLUENCES.3: Caster gains a +10 bonus to all Social Awareness skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.4: Anyone making a Social Awareness maneuver with respect to the caster becomes a target of this spell. If affected, the target will suffer a -25 penalty to all such Social Awareness maneuvers while the spell lasts.
INFLUENCES.5: Caster gains a +10 bonus to all Influence skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.6: Caster can read the surface thoughts of the target.
INFLUENCES.7: Caster gains a +20 bonus to all Social Awareness skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.8: One humanoid target for every five levels (or fraction thereof) of the caster will believe the caster is a very good friend.
INFLUENCES.9: Caster becomes highly fluent (equivalent of rank 10) in any spoken or signed language in which he has at least one rank.
INFLUENCES.10: Caster gains a +30 bonus to all Social Awareness skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.11: Humanoid targets (up to one for every five levels, or fraction thereof, of the caster) will perform a single suggested act that is not self-destructive or diametrically opposed to a target’s personality (e.g., no suicide suggestions, no self-mutilation suggestions, no suggestions that a priest pray to an enemy god, etc.). Suggestion must be the same for all targets.
INFLUENCES.12: Caster gains a +20 bonus to all Influence skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.13: Humanoid targets have their Action Points per round reduced by 3 (i.e., normally, the targets will have 1 AP each round). Up to one target for every five levels (or fraction thereof) of the caster may be affected.
INFLUENCES.14: Caster has a +25 bonus to any Performance Art skill maneuver using his voice.
INFLUENCES.15: As Mind Reading I, except for range.
INFLUENCES.16: Caster gains a +40 bonus to all Social Awareness skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.17: Caster gains a +30 bonus to all Influence skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.18: As Group Suggestion, except group will obey throughout duration and different commands can be issued to different members of the group.
INFLUENCES.19: The caster must record in writing a single suggested act that is not self-destructive or diametrically opposed to the ultimate target’s personality (e.g., no suicide suggestions, no blinding himself suggestions, no suggestions that a priest pray to an enemy god, etc.). This creates a minor magic item, and at this time no RR is made. When the text is read by the intended recipient (caster may specify an individual, a specific type, or simply aim for the first reader), the spell is triggered and if the triggering reader fails his RR, he will act as suggested. Once triggered, the text loses its enchantment.
INFLUENCES.20: Caster gains a +50 bonus to all Social Awareness skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.25: As Mind Reading I, except for range.
INFLUENCES.30: [RR: -25] The caster presents any paper or badgelike item or whatever item is roughly similar in type to the proper identification, credential, pass, or other document granting permission and/or authority that is being claimed by the caster. If the spell succeeds, the targets perceive the object presented as the genuine article. Caster must initially touch the item, but then the item may be taken for inspection and even handed from one individual to another out of sight of the caster without interruption of the effect.
INFLUENCES.35: Caster gains a +50 bonus to all Influence skill maneuvers.
INFLUENCES.40: [RR Mod.: -10] Humanoid targets (up to one per level of the caster) will perform a single suggested act that is not self-destructive or diametrically opposed to a target’s personality (e.g., no suicide suggestions, no self-mutilation suggestions, no suggestions that a priest pray to an enemy god, etc.). Suggestion must be the same for all targets.
INFLUENCES.50: Targets are given one task; failure results in a penalty determined by the Gamemaster (task must be within capabilities of the targets). If a target ignores the quest, or fails to cooperate with the other targets towards the success of the quest, this incurs the same effects as for failure.

INNER WALLS.1: Target is protected from natural heat up to 80°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures above 80°C, subtract 60°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. heat spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental fire/heat attacks. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes per level if cast on a non-mobile target. If cast on a mobile target, the spell will last as long as the caster concentrates. If cast on a non-mobile target that is moved, the spell is dispelled. If the caster is the target, duration is one minute/level and concentration is not required.
INNER WALLS.2: Target is protected from natural cold down to -10°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures below 20°C, add 30°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. cold spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental cold/ice attacks. Duration is as for Heat Resistance.
INNER WALLS.3: Target is protected from natural light and light changes (instantly adjusting vision to changing light intensity to never be blinded by glare, sudden light, or sudden darkness. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. light or electricity spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental light/electricity attacks. Duration is as for Heat Resistance.
INNER WALLS.4: Caster can ignore a penalty due to injury of up to -10 or subtract that amount from a more severe penalty (e.g., if the caster was suffering wounds causing a -45 penalty, he would only experience a -35 penalty). Movement while this spell is active will not aggravate the injury.
INNER WALLS.5: Caster gains a +10 bonus to all RRs.
INNER WALLS.6: Caster can sterilize 1 cubic foot per level of any solid or liquid. This will not affect inert poisons or harm creatures larger than 1/4" in length.
INNER WALLS.7: As Ignore Wounds I, except caster ignores up to -30 in penalties.
INNER WALLS.8: Caster gains a +10 bonus to his DB.
INNER WALLS.9: As Resistance Wall I, except bonus is +20.
INNER WALLS.10: As Ignore Wounds I, except caster ignores up to -50 in penalties.
INNER WALLS.11: Caster is protected from any natural cold, heat, light, or electricity (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). Caster also receives a +40 to all RRs vs. heat, fire, cold, ice, light, and electricity; +40 DB vs. elemental cold/ice, fire/heat, and electricity attacks.
INNER WALLS.12: If the caster receives any form of Stun result, the caster may make a Hard Body Development maneuver (which does not require any AP). If this maneuver is successful, the caster does not suffer the Stun result.
INNER WALLS.13: As Martial Wall I, except bonus is +20.
INNER WALLS.14: As Ignore Wounds I , except caster ignores up to -80 in penalties.
INNER WALLS.15: As Resistance Wall I, except bonus is +30.
INNER WALLS.16: Allows caster to attempt to throw off the effects of any poison or disease by making an RR with a special +25 modification. This spell may only be cast once per incident of poisoning or disease until the caster gains another level.
INNER WALLS.17: As Ignore Wounds I, except caster ignores up to -100 in penalties.
INNER WALLS.18: As Martial Wall I , except bonus is +30.
INNER WALLS.19: As Resistance Wall I, except bonus is +40.
INNER WALLS.20: Allows target to attempt to throw off any 1 disease by making an RR with a special +50 modification. This spell may only be cast once per target per disease until the caster gains another level.
INNER WALLS.25: An RR is made for the curse (using the curse’s level as the defending level). If the RR fails, the curse is removed. This spell may only be cast once per target per curse until the caster gains another level.
INNER WALLS.30: Allows target to attempt to throw off the effects of any poison by making an RR with a special +50 modification. This spell may only be cast once per target per poison until the caster gains another level.
INNER WALLS.35: As Heat Resistance, Cold Resistance, and Light Resistance, plus caster is immune to natural heat, cold, and light/electricity, and is treated as one Size larger when taking damage from spells using these elements.
INNER WALLS.40: As Ignore Wounds I, except caster ignores all penalties from wounds and duration is 10 minutes per level.
INNER WALLS.50: Caster gets +25 to his DB, RRs, and maneuver rolls.

INSPIRING SONGS.1: Target becomes an “Ally” for purposes of this list. The caster is always considered an Ally for purposes of this list. The caster is limited to a number of Allies (other than the caster) equal to his ranks in this list, but may remove any one individual from the status of Ally with a round of concentration at any time.
INSPIRING SONGS.2: All Allies make Endurance rolls with a +50 bonus.
INSPIRING SONGS.3: When Allies are working together on some shared task, this spell provides a bonus to that task equal to +3 per number of Allies involved (includes the caster, although the caster obviously cannot be providing “hands on” help).
INSPIRING SONGS.4: Allies gain a +10 bonus to all RRs against fear or magic that produces fear or calm. They also gain +4 OB.
INSPIRING SONGS.5: This spell casts another spell known to the caster, but changes the nature of that spell. The spell to be shared must have “1 target” or a larger number of targets as its normal area of effect. The Song of Sharing I will cause the shared spell to affect all Allies within a 5'R as if the shared spell had been directly cast on each of them. The PP cost of casting is the cost of this spell (5 PP) plus the cost of the spell to be shared. For example, if a Bard knew the spell Leaping from the Lofty Bridge list, every Ally within 5' could be given the ability to Leap by casting Song of Sharing I and expending
INSPIRING SONGS.6: Caster creates a defensive song that will protect anyone (not just Allies) from sound-based attacks (including the spells on Controlling Songs). Any such attack must make a successful RR against the caster’s level or fail within the bounds of the Countersong.
INSPIRING SONGS.7: All Allies gain a +20 bonus to all Lore skill maneuvers and any other memory-based tasks.
INSPIRING SONGS.8: All Allies within area of effect are telepathically linked. They may communicate mentally with each other freely. Essentially this is silent, largely secure communication.
INSPIRING SONGS.9: As Battle Hymn I, except the RR bonus is +20 and the OB bonus is +8.
INSPIRING SONGS.10: As Song of Sharing I , except for radius of effect.
INSPIRING SONGS.11: Allies may add the caster’s level to their spell-casting maneuver rolls.
INSPIRING SONGS.12: As Countersong I, except radius is 20'.
INSPIRING SONGS.13: As Battle Hymn I, except the RR bonus is +30 and the OB bonus is +12.
INSPIRING SONGS.14: Allies reduce all stun rounds taken by 1 and gain a +30 bonus to RRs against spells which cause confusion.
INSPIRING SONGS.15: As Song of Sharing I , except for radius of effect.
INSPIRING SONGS.16: Allies recover 2 concussion hits per round.
INSPIRING SONGS.17: As Countersong I, except radius is 30'.
INSPIRING SONGS.18: As Battle Hymn I, except the RR bonus is +40 and the OB bonus is +16.
INSPIRING SONGS.19: Allies gain +10 OB and do double hits and an additional Impact critical (one severity level less than the primary critical) in melee combat.
INSPIRING SONGS.20: As Song of Sharing I , except for radius of effect.
INSPIRING SONGS.25: As Battle Hymn I, except the RR bonus is twice the caster’s level and the OB bonus is +20.
INSPIRING SONGS.30: As Song of Sharing I , except for radius of effect.
INSPIRING SONGS.35: As Countersong I, except radius is 50'.
INSPIRING SONGS.40: As Song of Sharing I, except that radius is equal to the caster’s level in feet.
INSPIRING SONGS.50: As Countersong I, except radius is 100'.

INSPIRING WAYS.1: For the duration of this spell, all within the area of effect (including the caster) may make a second RR versus any fear or panic effect. This second RR is made with an additional +10 modifier.
INSPIRING WAYS.2: Caster inspires all allies who are within the area of effect (they must be aware of the caster). All allies gain +5 to melee attacks and Endurance rolls for the duration of the spell. This spell will only work during combat or highly religious moments (e.g., an oratory presentation prior to a large battle). These bonuses do not apply to the caster.
INSPIRING WAYS.3: For as long as they speak together, the target of this spell will believe that the caster is a good friend and is acting in the target’s best interests.
INSPIRING WAYS.4: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +10.
INSPIRING WAYS.5: For as long as the caster speaks, the target will believe that the reasoning and ideas that the caster expresses are correct and valid.
INSPIRING WAYS.6: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +15.
INSPIRING WAYS.7: For as long as the caster speaks, the target will become more cautious (less rash) and will tend to obey the caster’s warnings without hesitation or doubt.
INSPIRING WAYS.8: Target must obey the caster. However, he will not perform any act that is completely alien to him or is self-destructive.
INSPIRING WAYS.9: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +20.
INSPIRING WAYS.10: As Voice of Friendship, except spell will affect all who can hear the caster.
INSPIRING WAYS.11: The caster gains a +25 bonus to his Leadership skill and +5 to any Influence maneuvers.
INSPIRING WAYS.12: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +25.
INSPIRING WAYS.13: As Command I, except spell affects two targets.
INSPIRING WAYS.14: As Voice of Reason, except spell will affect all who can hear the caster.
INSPIRING WAYS.15: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +30.
INSPIRING WAYS.16: As Courage, except the second RR automatically succeeds.
INSPIRING WAYS.17: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +35.
INSPIRING WAYS.18: As Voice of Caution, except spell will affect all who can hear the caster.
INSPIRING WAYS.19: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +40.
INSPIRING WAYS.20: [RR Mod.: -50] After casting this spell, the caster must tell the truth (as he knows it). The target will believe that the caster is speaking the absolute truth.
INSPIRING WAYS.25: As Inspirations I, except bonuses are +50.
INSPIRING WAYS.30: As Voice of Conviction, except spell will affect all who can hear the caster.
INSPIRING WAYS.35: The caster gains a +50 bonus to his Leadership skill and +15 to any Influence maneuvers.
INSPIRING WAYS.40: As Inspirations True, except radius of effect is 100'.
INSPIRING WAYS.50: Caster can use any lower level spell from this list once per round.

INVISIBLE WAYS.1: A single inanimate object is made nearly invisible, allowing it to be seen through. Object cannot be larger than 10'x10'x3'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.2: A single object is made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or the object is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or the object makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or casts a spell of type E or F.
INVISIBLE WAYS.3: As Transparency, except duration is one minute/level.
INVISIBLE WAYS.4: As Unseen I, except everything within 1' of the target is invisible as long as it is within the 1'R and none of the Unseen I termination conditions occur.
INVISIBLE WAYS.5: As Unseen I, except range is 100'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.6: As Invisibility I, except the caster can vary the radius up to 1'. This makes the invisible target harder to see by reducing the “fringe effect”.
INVISIBLE WAYS.7: One melee attack (armed or unarmed) is covered by a moment of invisibility. Unless the target of that attack has the power to see the invisible, the target’s parry is subject to a -100 penalty (down to a minimum of 0 parry DB).
INVISIBLE WAYS.8: As Invisibility I, except the constant radius is 10'. Note that anything that exits the radius will become visible again.
INVISIBLE WAYS.9: Caster can see all invisible things anywhere that he can normally see. In addition, he suffers no penalties against invisible targets (as they are not invisible to him).
INVISIBLE WAYS.10: As Unseen I, except 3 objects can be affected.
INVISIBLE WAYS.11: As Invisibility Sphere I, except caster can vary the radius up to 10', determined at time of casting.
INVISIBLE WAYS.12: As Invisibility I, except range is 100'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.13: As Unseen I, except 5 objects can be affected.
INVISIBLE WAYS.14: As Unseen III, except range is 100'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.15: As Invisibility I except two targets can be the center of two separate radii of invisibility.
INVISIBLE WAYS.16: As See Invisible I, except it allows a target to see invisible things.
INVISIBLE WAYS.17: As Unseen I, except 10 objects can be affected.
INVISIBLE WAYS.18: As Split Invisibility, except the caster can vary the two radii by up to 10'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.19: As Unseen Blow, except affects all melee attacks by the target during the spell’s duration.
INVISIBLE WAYS.20: As Invisibility Sphere II , except caster can vary the radius by up to 20'.
INVISIBLE WAYS.25: As Unseen I, except as many objects as the caster’s level may be affected.
INVISIBLE WAYS.30: As Invisibility I, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be the centers of constant 1' radii of invisibility.
INVISIBLE WAYS.35: As Invisibility II, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be the centers of variable radii of invisibility.
INVISIBLE WAYS.40: As See Invisible II, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be given the power to see invisible objects.
INVISIBLE WAYS.50: As Invisibility I, except the caster can vary the radius of invisibility up to 1' and if the caster attacks he is only visible for the 5 seconds immediately following the attack (two instances). Likewise, he may cast a type E or F spell and become visible for the following 5 seconds. Violent blows do not affect this spell.

ITEM LORE.1: Caster can assess the value of a musical instrument to within 10% of its actual value. This spell allows the caster to calculate different values for the different cultures he is familiar with based on the general value of the instrument, the raw materials used to make it, and the quality of craftsmanship and sound. Additional value (e.g., from being made by a famous maker) is not revealed. The caster also gains a general knowledge of the instrument’s origins (the culture of origin and the instrument’s age within 10% of its actual age). Finally, he understands how the instrument is to be played, although actually playing competently requires the development of skill with the instrument through practice.
ITEM LORE.2: The caster has a +50 bonus to any attempt to appraise the market value of the target item. Item may be appraised for any culture/location of which the caster has knowledge.
ITEM LORE.3: Detects magical power in an item, but not the realm or how much. A different item can be examined each round by concentrating.
ITEM LORE.4: The caster can examine any visual symbol of an individual or group, be it a company logo, shield design, tattoo, flag, or other device. The caster learns the name of the individual or group so represented, a rough idea of the nature and (usual) location of that which is represented (e.g., “a knight of Glakony” or “a criminal gang in the southern islands”), and the purpose of the emblem (e.g., “to mark his works”, “to identify church grounds, property, or servants”, or “battlefield identification”).
ITEM LORE.5: Any Runes maneuver made by the caster has a +15 bonus.
ITEM LORE.6: The caster learns the area of origin of the item, the race of the being who made it, and when it was made (within 100 years). If the creator gave the item a name, that is also revealed.
ITEM LORE.7: Any Attunement maneuver made by the caster has a +15 bonus.
ITEM LORE.8: The caster learns the primary power of a magical item. This knowledge comes in the form a short narrative illustrating this power or an explanatory bit of verse.
ITEM LORE.9: The caster learns a narrative account of the most significant event in the item’s recent past. The event cannot have occurred more than one hour per level of the caster in the past.
ITEM LORE.10: The caster learns who made the item and when. He also learns of any craftsmanship bonus the item possesses. He learns whom the item was made for, if it was crafted for a specific person or group, and he learns for what purpose the item was made.
ITEM LORE.11: The caster learns if there is a curse on the item.
ITEM LORE.12: As Rune Literacy III, but bonus is +35.
ITEM LORE.13: The caster learns the cultural and historical significance of one item. He also learns its personal significance, if any, to its current owner. This knowledge comes in the form of an illustrative narrative.
ITEM LORE.14: As Attuning III, but bonus is +35.
ITEM LORE.15: The caster gains complete information on the item’s construction, purpose, magical abilities, and any non-magical bonuses.
ITEM LORE.16: The caster receives a narrative account of how someone died. Since this is not a vision, the caster has only a description of the killer (and perhaps the name, if it is revealed in dialog), but vision-centric defenses against retrosurveillance will not stymie this spell. When this spell is cast, the caster must be touching an object that had significance in the death (i.e., the murder weapon, the body, etc.) The narrative will be “from the point of view” of the object in question, so casting on multiple objects may provide a more complete account of events.
ITEM LORE.17: As Item’ s Tale, except without a time limit.
ITEM LORE.18: As Rune Literacy II, but bonus is +50.
ITEM LORE.19: The next touch-ranged spell on this list cast by the caster of this spell will have its range improved to 20'.
ITEM LORE.20: As Attuning III, but bonus is +50.
ITEM LORE.25: As Item’ s Tale, except caster learns the whole history of the item. This does not include every detail, but does include all significant events.
ITEM LORE.30: As Item Analysis and Item Origins combined, except that the Bard may study one item each round during the duration of the spell.
ITEM LORE.35: As Rune Literacy III, but bonus is +75.
ITEM LORE.40: Caster immediately knows the location of the most valuable (financially) object within the area of effect.
ITEM LORE.50: As Attuning III, but bonus is +75.

LESSER ILLUSIONS.1: Caster can speak and the caster’s voice will appear to come from any point desired within 100' (point must be in the caster’s field of vision).
LESSER ILLUSIONS.2: Creates any simple, immobile image/scene in an area of up to 10'R, or creates a set of immobile sounds in an area of up to 10'R.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.3: As Sound/Light Mirage, except it creates a set of immobile smells/scents in the area of effect, or a set of immobile tastes in the area of effect.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.4: Creates a simple, immobile image or scene up to an area of 10'R. One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, or e) the illusion can cast light. All parts of the area of effect must be in the caster’s field of vision at the time of casting.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.5: Creates the image of one object or being that will move however the caster wants (as long as the caster concentrates). When the caster stops concentrating, the image remains but stops moving. The caster may resume concentration again later, and make the image move again (presuming that the duration has not expired). The image can be of any size that would fit in a 10'R.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.6: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its visual appearance altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes. This spell cannot make an object cast light (although it may alter the appearance of light given off, whether naturally or from another spell).
LESSER ILLUSIONS.7: As Illusion II, except it can be delayed by up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster must decide upon the trigger for the spell. The caster can chose one of the following: a) time period, b) specified movements, c) specified sounds, d) specified smells, or e) specified tastes. If a sense is used to trigger the spell, that sense must be included in the illusion.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.8: As Illusion II, except two options can be chosen. See notes above for guidelines on how to combine the options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.9: As Phantasms I, except one of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within range of the spell), f) the phantasm can cast light, or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion).
LESSER ILLUSIONS.10: As Phantasms II, except it can be delayed as in Waiting Illusion II. The phantasm will move and act if given a simple command (at the time of casting) or it can deliver a short speech (if sound was one of the included senses).
LESSER ILLUSIONS.11: As Waiting Illusion II , except two options can be chosen. See notes above for guidelines on how to combine the options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.12: As Phantasms II, except two options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.13: As Illusion III, except four options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.14: As Waiting Illusion III, except three options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.15: As Phantasms III, except it can be delayed as in Waiting Phantasms II.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.16: As Waiting Illusion III , except four options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.17: As Phantasms II, except three options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.18: As Phantasms IV, except it can be delayed as in Waiting Phantasms II.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.19: As Illusion III, except six options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.20: As Waiting Illusion III , except five options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.25: As Phantasms II, except four options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.30: As Illusion III, except nine options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.35: As Waiting Illusion III , except nine options can be chosen.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.40: As Illusion III, except fourteen options.
LESSER ILLUSIONS.50: As Phantasms II, except nine options.

LIFE MASTERY.1: Caster can detect presence and number of living entities (Tiny animals or larger) within the area of effect and can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round. Caster will be aware of mundane life, such as insects, very small animals and plants, but not of their exact number, only general types.
LIFE MASTERY.2: Caster can preserve a dead body, preventing decomposition. Preservation can only be used on bodies of living beings that are or were capable of life, it cannot be used to preserve other organic matter.
LIFE MASTERY.3: The cast can reduce a stat penalty due to Life Drain by 1. A target can only receive one Heal Life Essence spell per day, and can only have a specific stat affected once per week.
LIFE MASTERY.4: Caster prevents the soul of a target from leaving its body (thus preventing actual death), although the body will continue to take damage (from bleeding or other effects) for the duration. If the body is not capable of sustaining life at the end of the duration, then the soul will depart. This will prevent Absolution of any kind, unless the Absolution makes a successful RR against this spell.
LIFE MASTERY.5: Target is infused with a part of the caster’s life force. Caster takes a -2 to a stat bonus of his choice, and the target gets a +2 to any stat bonus (which does not have to be the same stat bonus as the caster reduces). The target receives the benefits for the duration of the spell, but the caster’s penalty can only be removed via Heal Life Essence. Each stat can only be under the benefit of a single Infuse Life at a time, although the caster could have several penalties due to multiple Infuse Life spells being cast.
LIFE MASTERY.6: Target is temporarily revived by returning the soul to the body for a brief time, regardless of the condition of the body (if body is terribly damaged the target may not be able to take advantage of his brief return to the living).
LIFE MASTERY.7: As Preservation I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.8: Caster can analyze a life form he can see or has previously detected with Life Awareness. He understands the nature of the life form (race), its approximate level, and if its life is currently in danger. Caster can analyze one being each round for the duration of the spell (requires Concentration).
LIFE MASTERY.9: Caster can restore life to a recently dead body (within 1 rnd/lvl) by giving up his own life. The target’s soul is returned to his body with no ill effects (although he is still subject to any wounds he had). The caster’s soul departs, however this spell also acts as a Preservation II spell on the caster’s body. Duration is for the Preservation effect; the other effects are permanent.
LIFE MASTERY.10: As Heal Life Essence I except that a stat penalty can be reduced by 2.
LIFE MASTERY.11: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.12: As Infuse Life I , except the caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 4 and the target’s stat bonus is increased by 4.
LIFE MASTERY.13: As Preservation I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.14: Caster can return the soul of a being (that has not been dead for more than 1 min/lvl) to its body. If the body is not capable of sustaining life, then the soul will depart again. The target gets a -15 to all stats (which can be restored via Heal Life Essence).
LIFE MASTERY.15: As Heal Life Essence I , except penalty can be reduced by 3.
LIFE MASTERY.16: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.17: As Infuse Life I, except caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 6 and the target’s stat bonus increased by 6.
LIFE MASTERY.18: As Preservation I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.19: As Lifegiving I, except body can have been dead for up to 1 day/level.
LIFE MASTERY.20: As Heal Life Essence I except penalty can be reduced by 5.
LIFE MASTERY.25: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.
LIFE MASTERY.30: As Infuse Life I, except caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 10 and the target’s stat bonus increased by 10.
LIFE MASTERY.35: As Lifegiving I, except body can have been dead for up to 1 month/level.
LIFE MASTERY.40: As Heal Life Essence I, except the entire stat penalty is removed.
LIFE MASTERY.50: As Lifegiving I, except there is no time limit.

LIGHT LAW.1: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.
LIGHT LAW.2: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
LIGHT LAW.3: Lights a 10'R area about the point touched.
LIGHT LAW.4: All shadows and darkness within the area of effect deepen, giving all Perception maneuvers involving seeing anything hidden/hiding in the area a penalty of -20. Area is not mobile.
LIGHT LAW.5: Causes a 10'R burst of intense light, all within are stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.
LIGHT LAW.6: Reduces illumination to a dark night in a 10'R area about the point touched.
LIGHT LAW.7: As Light I, except radius can be up to 50' (can be changed by concentrating 1 round).
LIGHT LAW.8: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
LIGHT LAW.9: As Darkness I, except radius is 50'.
LIGHT LAW.10: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; resolve attack on the Lightning Bolt Attack Table as a Medium-sized attack.
LIGHT LAW.11: In conjunction with any Light or Darkness spell (i.e., this spell is cast and then the Light/Darkness spell is cast) it can delay the action of that spell up to 24 hours; it can be triggered by one selected trigger (decided by the caster): time period, certain movements, certain sounds, touch, violent actions in the area, or any other trigger so long as the caster would be able to sense it if the caster remained at the area of effect.
LIGHT LAW.12: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
LIGHT LAW.13: Ray of light of any color springs from caster’s palm; it can be up to 5 miles long.
LIGHT LAW.14: As Light I, except radius is 30', duration is 1 hour per level, and spell must be cast upon a non-living organic object, like a wooden staff or leather cap.
LIGHT LAW.15: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
LIGHT LAW.16: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 250'.
LIGHT LAW.17: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 100'R, and lights that area as full daylight.
LIGHT LAW.18: Darkens a 100'R area, no light (except Utterlight) can exist within this radius.
LIGHT LAW.19: As Beacon V, except beacon can be up to 10 miles long.
LIGHT LAW.20: As Lightning Bolt I , except range increment is 125'.
LIGHT LAW.25: As Lightning Bolt III, except caster can make the bolt turn once by up to 90° before striking the target (the caster must know the target’s location). The caster’s OB is modified by -25 when using this spell. However, this spell can be used to bypass a positional modifier – any DB bonus gained through the use of cover (partial/half/full soft cover; partial/half/full hard cover) is reduced to zero; a target that otherwise would be unable to be hit can be attacked this spell.
LIGHT LAW.30: As Corner Lightning Bolt, except bolt can make as many turns as necessary, with an OB penalty of -25 per turn, and go through openings (must be larger than 6" radius) to strike the target.
LIGHT LAW.35: Caster is surrounded by a blazing electrical aura. This illuminates a 30' radius, but more importantly, it also provides a defensive (or rather, counter-offensive) effect. Anyone attacking the caster in melee receives an electrical critical of the same severity as that suffered by the caster. This is not scaled by target size. If a massive troll does an E+A critical to a Magician surrounded by a Corona, the troll takes an E+A Electricity critical in return.
LIGHT LAW.40: As Utterlight, except that duration is 10 minutes per level and the radius illuminated is 10' per level.
LIGHT LAW.50: Caster can use one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

LIGHT MOLDING.1: Creates any simple immobile image or scene up to an area of 10'R. The visual aspects of the scene are really created (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally be accomplished only through spells or the use of a sense other than sight.
LIGHT MOLDING.2: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.
LIGHT MOLDING.3: Caster can control the intensity of light within the area of effect; the intensity can vary from daylight to darkness (natural), and it can be different in different parts of the radius.
LIGHT MOLDING.4: Causes a 10'R burst of intense light, all within are stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.
LIGHT MOLDING.5: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
LIGHT MOLDING.6: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its visual appearance altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes. This spell cannot make an object cast light (although it may alter the appearance of light given off, whether naturally or from another spell).
LIGHT MOLDING.7: Lights a 50'R area about the point touched.
LIGHT MOLDING.8: Reduces illumination to a dark night in a 50'R area about the point touched.
LIGHT MOLDING.9: Creates an area of darkness about the target’s head that blinds him unless dispelled or canceled by Utterlight.
LIGHT MOLDING.10: As Light Control I, except area affected is 50'R.
LIGHT MOLDING.11: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 100'R and lights that area as full daylight.
LIGHT MOLDING.12: All of the terrain in the area of effect will look differently than it actually is. The caster may designate in what way the terrain appears different. For example, a group of small trees could be made into a group of tall trees. Note that only the visual aspect of the terrain changes.
LIGHT MOLDING.13: A ray of light (any color) springs from caster’s palm; it can be up to 1 mile long.
LIGHT MOLDING.14: Darkens a 100'R area, no light (except Utterlight) can exist within this radius.
LIGHT MOLDING.15: As Light Control I, except area affected is 100'R.
LIGHT MOLDING.16: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
LIGHT MOLDING.17: As Light Glamour, except that it affects up to one item per caster level, with the duration and range listed.
LIGHT MOLDING.18: As Beacon I, except the ray of light can be up to 5 miles long.
LIGHT MOLDING.19: As Light Control I , except area affected is 200'R.
LIGHT MOLDING.20: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; resolve attack on the Lightning Bolt Attack Table as a Medium-sized attack.
LIGHT MOLDING.25: As Light Control I , except area affected is 500'R.
LIGHT MOLDING.30: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
LIGHT MOLDING.35: As Lightning Bolt I , except range increment is 125'.
LIGHT MOLDING.40: As Shock Bolt V, except the caster can make one Shock Bolt attack each round the spell remains in effect.
LIGHT MOLDING.50: As Light Control I, except area affected is 100'R/level.

LIGHT'S WAY.1: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.
LIGHT'S WAY.2: Lights a 10'R area about the point touched. This spell may be cast onto a target as explained in Section 10.17.
LIGHT'S WAY.3: Causes a bright aura about the target, providing a more powerful appearance and subtracting 10 from all attacks on the target.
LIGHT'S WAY.4: As Light I, except 1 or 2 areas can be created with a total radius of 20' (e.g., one area of 20'R or two areas of 10’R) and the location(s) for the light(s) need not be touched.
LIGHT'S WAY.5: Causes a 10'R burst of intense light, all within are stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.
LIGHT'S WAY.6: As Aura, except it produces an extremely powerful appearance and subtracts 15 from all attacks.
LIGHT'S WAY.7: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
LIGHT'S WAY.8: As Lights II, except up to three areas can be created with a total radius of 30'.
LIGHT'S WAY.9: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 10'R, and lights that area as full daylight.
LIGHT'S WAY.10: As Light I , except caster can delay the spell up to 24 hours; it can be triggered by one of the following (decided by caster): elapsed time, specific movements, specific sounds, touch, etc.
LIGHT'S WAY.11: A ball of light one foot in diameter is shot from the palm of the caster: it will travel to the limit of Long range, explode (as a flare), burn with a bright light (as a flare), drift slowly to earth, and go out. An area equal to the range is lit if the flare can rise to its full range; drops 10'/round. It can be fired at a target as a Small Fire Bolt.
LIGHT'S WAY.12: As Lights II, except duration is one hour per level.
LIGHT'S WAY.13: As Lights II, except up to five areas can be created with a total radius of 50'.
LIGHT'S WAY.14: As Utterlight I, except up to three areas can be created with a total radius of 30'
LIGHT'S WAY.15: As Lights II, except up to ten areas can be created with a total radius of 100'.
LIGHT'S WAY.16: As Lights III, except duration is one hour per level.
LIGHT'S WAY.17: As Utterlight I, except up to five areas can be created with a total radius of 50'
LIGHT'S WAY.18: As Lights II, except total radius is 10' per level and this may be split between a number of areas equal to the caster’s level.
LIGHT'S WAY.19: As Lights V, except duration is one hour per level.
LIGHT'S WAY.20: As Aura, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
LIGHT'S WAY.25: Caster extends a finger and a 1" globe of blue-white light shoots out to the target location, then erupts in arcs of electricity in a 40' sphere. Attack is resolved on the Lightning Ball table as a Miniscule attack.
