LIGHT'S WAY.30: As Aura, except target seems like a minor god and the subtraction is 25.
LIGHT'S WAY.35: As True Aura, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
LIGHT'S WAY.40: As Mass Lights, except duration is one hour per level.
LIGHT'S WAY.50: As Utterlight I, except total radius is 10' per level and this may be split between a number of areas equal to the caster’s level.

LIQUID ALTERATION.1: The caster may alter the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by 50°F [28°C] per round of concentration. Once the liquid begins to boil or freeze, it will not continue to change temperature, but will begin to warm or cool naturally if the caster stops concentrating. Caster is immune to the heat/cold thus generated.
LIQUID ALTERATION.2: Removes all sediment and dissolved substances from a liquid.
LIQUID ALTERATION.3: All targeted liquid (up to a maximum of 1000 cubic feet per level) is evaporated (i.e., boiled away and turned into heated gas) at a rate of one hundred cubic feet of liquid per round of concentration.
LIQUID ALTERATION.4: Creates a wall of water up to 10'x10'x1'. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Run going in would be a Walk pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
LIQUID ALTERATION.5: A bolt of water is shot from a water source within 20' of the caster; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table. Range modifier is based on the distance from the water source to the bolt’s target.
LIQUID ALTERATION.6: Creates a corridor through liquid up to 100' long, 3' wide, and 10' deep (must be open at the top).
LIQUID ALTERATION.7: If there are clouds in the sky, it rains outdoors for the duration of the spell.
LIQUID ALTERATION.8: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
LIQUID ALTERATION.9: Creates a wave moving away from the caster; wave is 1' high per level in the center and 10' per level wide.
LIQUID ALTERATION.10: As Waterwall, except caster need not concentrate and the duration is 1 minute per level.
LIQUID ALTERATION.11: As Water Corridor I , except limit is 300'x4'x50' deep.
LIQUID ALTERATION.12: Changes any liquid into fresh water.
LIQUID ALTERATION.13: Creates a whirlpool with a 20 foot radius in any liquid body that will draw in any objects in the liquid up to 200' away. Objects capable of moving under their own power may make a Very Hard maneuver to resist the pull of the whirlpool.
LIQUID ALTERATION.14: Creates a tunnel through liquid that is 5' in diameter and 100' long.
LIQUID ALTERATION.15: As Calm Water, except radius is 100'/lvl and waves are cut by 50' in the center and caster need not concentrate.
LIQUID ALTERATION.16: The area of effect for this spell is the water immediately surrounding the boat that the caster is on (where “boat” should be interpreted loosely; a log is a “boat” for purposes of this spell if the caster is riding it on water). For as long as the caster concentrates, the speed of the boat will be increased by 25' per round (approximately 3 miles per hour).
LIQUID ALTERATION.17: Creates a bubble of air (10'R) around the caster and others, which will carry them in up to 100' depth of water (air is replenished). Caster can direct the motion of the bubble at a rate of 30'/round (may be adjusted by the presence of (un) favorable water currents. If/when the caster stops concentrating, the bubble will rise to the surface at the same rate (30'/round). If the caster resumes concentrating before the bubble surfaces, the bubble may be controlled again. All objects inside the bubble will rise with it. After reaching the surface, the bubble will burst.
LIQUID ALTERATION.18: As Bolt of Water I, but range increment is 125'.
LIQUID ALTERATION.19: Caster can cause up to one cubic foot per level of liquid into a magical acid strong enough to corrode an equal volume of metal or cause an A Acid critical to someone splashed with the acid or a D Acid critical each round immersed in the acid (with a C, a B, and an A critical coming in that order during the three rounds after getting out).
LIQUID ALTERATION.20: As Water Corridor I, except limit is 100' per level long, 6' wide and 100' deep and the caster need not concentrate.
LIQUID ALTERATION.25: As Water Tunnel, except limit is 6' in diameter and 100' per level long and the caster need not concentrate.
LIQUID ALTERATION.30: Caster can divert a water source or part of a water source into a stream which can be up to 15'x6' and as long as the range of this spell.
LIQUID ALTERATION.35: As Water Bubble, except that caster need only concentrate on the desired route for one round and the bubble will “pilot” itself until the destination is reached and the radius of the bubble may be up to 20' in radius and the maximum speed of the bubble is 50'/round and there is no depth limit.
LIQUID ALTERATION.40: As Call Rain, except for area of effect and duration.
LIQUID ALTERATION.50: May transmute 1 cubic foot of liquid into another non-magical liquid that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work.

LIQUID MANIPULATION.1: The caster may increase the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by 50°F (28°C) per round of concentration. Once the liquid begins to boil, it will not continue to heat up, but will begin to cool naturally if the caster stops concentrating. Caster is immune to the heat thus generated.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.2: The caster may decrease the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by 50°F [28°C] per round of concentration. Once the liquid freezes, it will not continue to grow colder, but will begin to warm naturally if the caster stops concentrating. Caster is immune to the cold thus generated.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.3: Clears target liquid of all sediment.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.4: Gives nature and origin of natural liquid, and when and how a worked liquid was obtained and worked.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.5: Removes all dissolved substances from the target liquid.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.6: Creates a wall of water up to 10’x10’x1’. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Sprint going in would be a Jog pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.7: As Boil Liquid, except all the liquid is evaporated (i.e., boiled away and turned into heated gas) at a rate of one cubic foot of liquid per round of concentration. Liquid and vapor are subject to natural cooling once caster ceases concentration.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.8: Creates a corridor through liquid up to 100’ long, 3’ wide, and 10’ deep (must be open at the top).
LIQUID MANIPULATION.9: A bolt of water is shot from a water source within 20’ of the caster; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table. Range modifier is based on the distance from the water source to the bolt’s target.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.10: As Waterwall, except wall is 10’x20’x1’ and can be curved up to a semicircle.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.11: All water within a 100’R is calmed; waves are cut by 20’ in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.12: Transforms up to one cubic foot of acid per level to plain water.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.13: As Waterwall, but has a set duration of 1 minute/level and does not require concentration.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.14: Caster can be exposed to liquid, even complete submersion, and come out completely dry, protecting his person and (probably more importantly) his possessions. While this does not prevent drowning, any penalties for heavy clothing should be significantly reduced, as they will not become water-logged and thus retain air and a certain degree of bouyancy.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.15: As Liquid Corridor I , except limit is 300’x4’x50’ deep.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.16: As Bolt of Water I , but range increment is 75’.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.17: Creates a whirlpool with a 20 foot radius in any liquid body that will draw in any objects in the liquid up to 200’ away. Objects capable of moving under their own power may make a Very Hard maneuver to resist the pull of the whirlpool.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.18: Creates a wave moving away from the caster; wave is 1’ high/level in the center and 10’/level wide.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.19: Creates a bubble of air (10’R) around the caster and others, which will carry them in up to 100’ depth of water (air is replenished). Caster can direct the motion of the bubble at a rate of 30’/round (may be adjusted by the presence of (un) favorable water currents). If/when the caster stops concentrating, the bubble will rise to the surface at the same rate (30’/round). If the caster resumes concentrating before the bubble surfaces, the bubble may be controlled again. All objects inside the bubble will rise with it. After reaching the surface, the bubble will burst.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.20: As Calm Water, except radius is 100’/lvl and waves are cut by 50’ in the center and caster need not concentrate.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.25: As Liquid Corridor, except limit is 100’/lvl x 6’x100’ deep.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.30: As Water Bubble, except radius is 20’ and there is no depth limit.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.35: Caster creates a waterspout over a body of water (minimally 1’ water depth), causing it to arise anywhere within range. The waterspout moves as the caster wills within the range of the spell (only a 10’ radius may be effectively attacked each round, and a Spell Mastery roll may be required to attack an actively evasive target). Anything caught within the waterspout is subjected to a battering vortex of water (use the Water Bolt table with a +50 OB, but treat the attack size as Large). Waterspout takes one round to form.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.40: The area of effect for this spell is the water immediately surrounding the boat that the caster is on (where “boat” should be interpreted loosely; a log is a “boat” for purposes of this spell if the caster is riding it on water). For the duration of this spell, the speed of the boat will be modified by 25’ per round (approximately 3 miles per hour) in a given direction (which may be “upstream” or “downstream” if following a river). One round of concentration will allow the caster to change the direction of this magical current.
LIQUID MANIPULATION.50: Caster can use one of the lower spells on this list each round, but with lesser ranges extended to 100’.

LIVING CHANGE.1: Allows caster to analyze the form of another being for future use with Exact Change or Change Self Exact. The caster can only have as many forms analyzed as he has ranks in this list. Caster may choose to forget a form at any time to make room for replacements.
LIVING CHANGE.2: Caster enhances the target’s appearance. The target still looks like himself or herself, but in an idealized way, such as seen in a flattering portrait.
LIVING CHANGE.3: Allows caster to grow or shrink within the limits of his Size category.
LIVING CHANGE.4: Alters the caster’s entire body to the form of another humanoid race. The form cannot be that of a particular individual form. For example, a human caster could take the form of a Dwarf, but not the Dwarven High Chancellor. Only the physical form is gained (e.g., a target who becomes a Dwarf would not receive the Dwarves’ ability to see in the dark). This spell does not allow the caster to change his Size category, but size may be changed as desired within that limit.
LIVING CHANGE.5: As Resize Self, except that non-magical organic objects held or carried by the caster will change size in the same proportion as does the caster.
LIVING CHANGE.6: As Changing to Kind , except target to be changed need not be the caster.
LIVING CHANGE.7: As Changing to Kind , except alteration can be to any organic form within the caster’s Size category. No special abilities of the form are gained.
LIVING CHANGE.8: Allows caster to grow or shrink the target within the limits of the target’s Size category.
LIVING CHANGE.9: As Change Self, except that non-magical organic objects held or carried by the caster will refit to the caster’s new form or merge into his body for the duration, depend on whether the item can readily be adapted to the new form.
LIVING CHANGE.10: As Change Self, except altered form can be that of a specific being analyzed by Change Lore.
LIVING CHANGE.11: As Change to Kind, except alteration can be to any organic form within the target’s Size category. No special abilities of the form are gained.
LIVING CHANGE.12: Caster can shrink down to within the limits of one Size category smaller than his own.
LIVING CHANGE.13: As Change, except altered form can be that of a specific being analyzed by Change Lore.
LIVING CHANGE.14: Caster can grow to within the limits of one Size category larger than his own.
LIVING CHANGE.15: As Change Self Exact , except caster may assume a different form anytime during the spell, by concentrating for one round/change.
LIVING CHANGE.16: Target can shrink down to within the limits of one Size category smaller than his own.
LIVING CHANGE.17: Target can merge into any solid, inanimate material. While merged, target cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the target emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the target’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The target can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the target automatically emerges at the entrance point).
LIVING CHANGE.18: As Exact Change, except target may assume a different form anytime during the spell, by concentrating for one round/change.
LIVING CHANGE.19: Target can grow to within the limits of one Size category larger than his own.
LIVING CHANGE.20: Target may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.
LIVING CHANGE.25: As Resize, except it simultaneously enlarges or shrinks as many targets (can be beings or objects) as the caster’s level. All targets must change size by the same percentage amount.
LIVING CHANGE.30: As Change, except affects up to the caster’s level in targets (all targets must take the same type of form).
LIVING CHANGE.35: Target can shrink or grow within the limits of two Size categories above or below his own.
LIVING CHANGE.40: As Merging, except affects up to the caster’s level in targets; all targets exit after a set time or with caster.
LIVING CHANGE.50: As Passing, except affects up to the caster’s level in targets.

LOCATING WAYS.1: Caster knows which way is up and where the cardinal directions (north, east, south, and west) lie.
LOCATING WAYS.2: When faced with a choice about which the caster has little or no information (e.g., which corridor leads to the wine cellar), the caster may cast this spell and the Gamemaster will determine which choice the caster actually makes, biasing the choice favorably by 25% (e.g., if there are two choices, roll 1-100: correct on 26-100, incorrect on 01-25). The caster must have a limited number of options (i.e., finite and countable). After the spell is cast, the caster may not cast another Guess for the same information until his experience level has increased.
LOCATING WAYS.3: Caster learns the location(s) of any “path(s)” within 100'; gives the nearest point on each path, but not the path’s course.
LOCATING WAYS.4: Caster is subtly directed (by hints and clues) towards a specific destination (within 100'/lvl) by guidance from the caster’s deity. The exact form of the guidance may vary from deity to deity (e.g., a deity of water might leave hints in the rocks of a stream, etc.). For the duration of this spell, the caster must concentrate on following the guidance to perceive the clues.
LOCATING WAYS.5: Gives the direction and distance to any specific object or place with which the caster is familiar or has had described in detail. The caster must have the dimensions of the object exact within 10% of actual size and know at least two other attributes that make the item unique (e.g., color, texture, weight, etc.) within range of the spell. Note that sometimes the caster must know more than two other attributes to make the item unique, although the Tag spells allow one to shortcut this process.
LOCATING WAYS.6: As Pathfinding I, except range is 300'.
LOCATING WAYS.7: Caster places a distinctive “tag” on the aura of a person or item, which will qualify as a unique descriptive marker for purposes of Location (e.g., the caster could cast Tag I on a common coin and Location would be able to pick it out amongst all the various similar coins).
LOCATING WAYS.8: As Location I, except range is 300'.
LOCATING WAYS.9: As Pathfinding I, except range is 500'.
LOCATING WAYS.10: As Location I, except range is 500'.
LOCATING WAYS.11: Caster can remember an exact route that the caster has previously traveled regardless of whether or not the original journey was made with the caster’s normal sensory abilities active (e.g., a 12th level caster could remember the course of 12 hours of a trip even if blindfolded during that time). To be effective, this spell must be cast within one month of the journey.
LOCATING WAYS.12: As Pathfinding I, except range is 1 mile.
LOCATING WAYS.13: As Guidance, except the form of the guidance is that of an animal companion sent by the deity. The caster can follow the animal to a specific destination.
LOCATING WAYS.14: As Tag I, except for duration.
LOCATING WAYS.15: Caster can locate any 1 object the caster can describe, if indeed it exists and is in range (e.g., caster could describe a type of mace he had never seen or heard of). Description does not need to be unique. If item description is not unique, the spell locates the nearest item matching the description.
LOCATING WAYS.16: As Location I, except range is 1 mile.
LOCATING WAYS.17: As Pathfinding I, except range is 10 miles.
LOCATING WAYS.18: As Find Item I, except range is 300'.
LOCATING WAYS.19: As Tag I, except for duration.
LOCATING WAYS.20: As Location I, except range is 20 miles.
LOCATING WAYS.25: As Pathfinding I, except range is 1 mile/level and exact routes within the range are ascertained.
LOCATING WAYS.30: As Location I , except range is 1 mile/level.
LOCATING WAYS.35: As Tag I, except for duration.
LOCATING WAYS.40: As Location True, except it locates a person. Caster must still be able to provide a unique description.
LOCATING WAYS.50: As Find Item I , except range is 100'/level.

LOFTY BRIDGE.1: Allows the target to make one leap up to 50' laterally or 20' vertically (as a separate 1 AP action, with no Pace penalty for the leaping movement) in the round that the spell was cast.
LOFTY BRIDGE.2: This spell is cast on a target who is falling and takes effect immediately, allowing the target to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall.
LOFTY BRIDGE.3: Caster teleports target to a point up to 100' away, but there can be no intervening “barriers” in a direct line between the target and the point. A “barrier” is anything the target could not physically go through; a closed door is a barrier, a pit is not.
LOFTY BRIDGE.4: Allows target to move up and down vertically 10' per round. This spell does not confer any ability to control horizontal movement, which must be accomplished by other means.
LOFTY BRIDGE.5: Allows target to fly at a rate of up to 35'/round (approximately 5 mph). This spell allows free movement in all three dimensions and allows hovering.
LOFTY BRIDGE.6: Opens a portal 3'x6'x3' in any solid surface, through which anyone can pass. At the end of the duration, the surface is returned to its previous state. Anyone in the area of effect when the spell ends will be forced back out to the point of entry.
LOFTY BRIDGE.7: As Fly I, except maximum rate is 75'/round (approximately 10 mph).
LOFTY BRIDGE.8: As Leaving I, except target can pass through barriers by specifying exact distances. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the target does not move, instead being stunned (-50) 1-10 rounds, and the PP for this spell are expended.
LOFTY BRIDGE.9: As Leaving I, except target can be moved 300'.
LOFTY BRIDGE.10: As Long Door I , except movement range is 10 miles per level. Making a precise appearance is difficult, especially if unfamiliar with the destination. The following chances for error exist: Never seen place (only described) = 50%; been briefly (1 hr) = 25%; Studied (24 hr) = 10%; Studied carefully (1 week) = 1%; Lived in (1 yr) = .01 %. In case of failure: first determine direction of error (randomly), then determine amount of error (make an open-ended d100 roll for the number of feet).
LOFTY BRIDGE.11: As Fly I, except rate is 150'/round (approximately 20 mph).
LOFTY BRIDGE.12: As Long Door I, except movement range is 300'.
LOFTY BRIDGE.13: As Teleport I, except up to 3 targets may be moved to same place.
LOFTY BRIDGE.14: As Portal, except portal is 3'x6' and up to 5'/level deep.
LOFTY BRIDGE.15: As Long Door I , except movement range is 500'.
LOFTY BRIDGE.16: As Teleport III, except 5 targets may be moved.
LOFTY BRIDGE.17: As Fly I, except rate is 225'/round (approximately 30 mph).
LOFTY BRIDGE.18: As Teleport III, except 10 targets may be moved.
LOFTY BRIDGE.19: As Leaving I, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be moved.
LOFTY BRIDGE.20: As Teleport III, except 20 targets can be moved.
LOFTY BRIDGE.25: As Long Door III, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be moved up to 300'.
LOFTY BRIDGE.30: As Teleport III, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be moved.
LOFTY BRIDGE.35: As Fly III, except as many targets as the caster’s level may fly at 150' per round.
LOFTY BRIDGE.40: As Fly True, except as many targets as the caster’s level may fly at 225' per round.
LOFTY BRIDGE.50: As Teleport I, except with no movement range limit.

LOFTY MOVEMENTS.1: Target gains a bonus of +30 for any Endurance rolls due to movement.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.2: Target can walk along narrow edges (down to 2" wide) that can support his weight. Maneuver rolls are not normally required.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.3: Target can walk along slick and/or wet surfaces as if on dry, secure footing.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.4: Target can walk along tree limbs as if on level ground.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.5: Target can walk on stone surfaces angled up to 60° as if on level ground.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.6: Target can walk on water as if on level dry ground. Water need not be perfectly still, but must be fairly calm.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.7: Target can merge into any organic material (alive or dead). While merged, target cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the target emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the target’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The target can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the target automatically emerges at the entrance point).
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.8: Caster “stores” a location for use with spells later on this list. While casting this spell, the caster must meditate for one hour in the location that will be stored. The caster can only have one sanctuary stored at a time. As long as this location remains “stored”, it will detect as a magical (i.e., “holy”) place.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.9: As Limbwalking, except target can move at up to a running pace (three times walking speed), without it being any more fatiguing than walking.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.10: Target can walk or run on stone surfaces angled up to 60° as if on level ground.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.11: As Waterwalking, except target can move at up to a running pace (three times walking speed), without it being any more fatiguing than walking.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.12: Target can walk on calm air. This spell only allows for horizontal movement. Vertical movement must be achieved by other means.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.13: Caster may return (as a teleport) to the caster’s stored sanctuary (from Store Sanctuary) so long as the sanctuary is no more than 1 mile away.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.14: Target can walk or run along slick and/or wet surfaces as if on dry, secure footing.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.15: As Merging Organic, except target can turn within the material and can perceive the outside world if within 6" of the surface.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.16: As Returning I, except the sanctuary can be up to 5 miles away.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.17: As Airwalking, except target can walk when winds do not exceed twice the caster’s level in miles per hour (i.e., when cast by a 20th level character, the target can walk in winds up to 40 mph). Wind does affect the target’s movement.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.18: As Airwalking, except target can run.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.19: As Returning I, except the sanctuary can be up to 10 miles away.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.20: As Great Merging Organic, except target may use self-targeted spells while merged.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.25: As Airrunning, except target can run up to thrice the normal speed (Running pace) on calm air without expending energy. This pace may be retained without any danger of Endurance checks related to movement during the duration.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.30: As Returning, except spell is instantaneous and the sanctuary can be up to 10 miles per level away.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.35: As Skywalking, except target can move at up to a running pace (three times walking speed), without it being any more fatiguing than walking.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.40: As Returning, except there is no distance limit.
LOFTY MOVEMENTS.50: Caster may return to the caster’s stored sanctuary (from Store Sanctuary) , stay up to 1 round per level, and then return to the point where he cast his spell (thus making it a round trip). Distance limit is 10 miles per level.

LORE.1: Allows caster virtually total recall of any conversation or writing encountered within a period of days equal to caster’s level.
LORE.2: Detects any evil being or any item created by evil or long used by a very evil person; caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round.
LORE.3: As Detect Evil, except detects curses on people or things.
LORE.4: As Detect Evil, except caster can detect the emotion of hatred, in the living mind or in an object forged with great malice. Any object or being “Of Slaying” will show as being filled with hate.
LORE.5: Caster can ascertain the origin point and nature of any one “holy item” that is not “of darkness” or “of evil”. This will not give specific capabilities.
LORE.6: Caster may analyze a poison to determine the exact effects and what antidotes are necessary. Poison may be on an item or in a being.
LORE.7: Caster can detect any extreme emotion (e.g., hatred, love, sadness, fear, etc.). In addition, the caster learns which emotion(s) the target is feeling. If there are no extremes of emotion (i.e., only normal feelings), nothing is detected. Caster may change the spell’s target each round.
LORE.8: Caster can determine the nature and type of any one living target. This will not ascertain the target’s personal traits or capabilities, but the caster will know the normal abilities associated with the target’s species.
LORE.9: Caster can recall appropriate quotations (from the caster’s own memory) which will both help make the caster’s point and be relevant to the target audience. This adds +15 to any maneuver involving persuasive speaking or writing and +10 to any maneuver involving artistic speaking or writing, but only applies if the caster has at least one rank in a Lore skill relevant to the audience.
LORE.10: Caster can determine nature and origin of any one curse, including the name of the one who bequeathed it.
LORE.11: As Light Lore I, except caster can ascertain the origin point and nature of any one item “of darkness” or “of evil.”
LORE.12: As Light Lore I, except that caster can ascertain the origin point and nature of any two “holy items” or caster can determine the nature, origin point, and the exact significance of any one “holy item.”
LORE.13: Caster can determine the nature and origin of active hatred found within any 1 target (inanimate or animate); the degree and other details of such hatred can also be acquired. Any objects or beings “Of Slaying” will show as hating their target type.
LORE.14: As Light Lore I, except caster can ascertain the origin point and nature of any one item that is neither holy nor unholy in nature.
LORE.15: As Hate Analysis, except caster can determine the nature and origin of any extreme emotion(s) in the target.
LORE.16: Caster can read any text (as if possessing eight ranks in the appropriate written language), although this spell does not assist in understanding cultural context, cultural references, implications, etc.
LORE.17: As Light Lore I , except that caster can ascertain the origin point and nature of any three “holy items” or caster can determine the age, origin point, name of the creator, and nature of any one “holy item.” Caster also learns item’s specific capabilities.
LORE.18: As Light Lore II , except that it can only be used on items “of darkness” or “of evil.”
LORE.19: Caster can ascertain exact nature, origin, purpose, history, and specific capabilities of any one “holy item”; this allows caster to visualize all who have possessed the item.
LORE.20: As Light Lore III, except that it can be used only on items “of evil” or “of darkness.”
LORE.25: As Life Lore, except caster can determine personal traits and capabilities of any one target.
LORE.30: As White Lore, except it can only be used on an item “of darkness” or “of evil.”
LORE.35: As Light Lore III, except it can only be used on items that are neither holy nor unholy in nature.
LORE.40: As Life Lore True, except caster determines the personal traits and capabilities of one target per caster level.
LORE.50: As White Lore, except caster can acquire the information on all “holy items” within 100'.

LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.1: Gives nature and origin of natural light, and when and how magical light (including visual illusions, if the caster knows it is an illusion) was created (list and specific spell).
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.2: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.3: Causes any wood to ignite and burn. All wood ignited must be within 1' of caster’s palm.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.4: Lights a 10'R area about the point touched. This spell may be cast onto a target as explained in Section 10.17.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.5: Causes all non-magical flames in a 5'R to die down and cools the area to where it will not reignite on its own.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.6: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.7: Target weapon, which must remain in the caster’s possession or the spell is cancelled, bursts into magical flame. Neither weapon nor wielder is harmed, but any time the weapon delivers a critical an additional Heat critical with a severity one less than the regular critical (e.g., a ‘C’ crit produces an additional ‘B’ Heat crit) is also inflicted.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.8: Creates an opaque wall of fire (up to 10'x10'x6"). Anyone passing through it takes an A Heat critical (no RR).
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.9: As Light I, except radius can be up to 50' (can be changed by concentrating 1 round).
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.10: Target weapon, which must remain in the caster’s possession or the spell is cancelled, crackles with electical arcs. Neither weapon nor wielder is harmed, but any time the weapon delivers a critical an additional Electricity critical of equal severity is also inflicted.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.11: As Shock Bolt I , except range increment is 75'.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.12: A bolt of fire is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Fire Bolt Table.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.13: As Light I, except radius can be up to 100' (can be changed by concentrating 1 round).
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.14: As Wall of Fire, except it is up to 30' long and does a B Heat critical.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.15: A 1' diameter ball of fire is shot from the palm of the caster. Upon reaching the selected target/location, it expands to affect a 10'R area. Resolve attack on the Fire Ball Table.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.16: As Shock Bolt I , except range increment is 125'.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.17: As Light I, except radius can be up to 150' (can be changed by concentrating 1 round).
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.18: Ray of light of any color springs from caster’s palm; can be up to 3 miles long.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.19: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 100'R, and lights that area as full daylight. This spell may be cast onto a target, as explained in Section 10.17.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.20: As Fire Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.25: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; the results are determined on the Lighting Bolt Table as a Medium-sized attack.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.30: As Flaming Blade, except can affect 1 target/level of caster and each weapon functions while held by the person holding it when the spell is cast.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.35: As Shocking Blade, except can affect 1 target/level of caster and each weapon functions while held by the person holding it when the spell is cast.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.40: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
LUMINOUS ELEMENTS.50: As Utterlight, except that duration is 10 minutes per level and the radius illuminated is 10' per level.

MIND ATTACK.2: Target hesitates in any non-defensive action, suffering a -20 to initiative, and must parry with at least half OB.
MIND ATTACK.3: Target takes 25% of current concussion hits (i.e., those not already taken).
MIND ATTACK.4: Target takes an A Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.6: Target drops anything currently held and takes a -20 to any maneuver attempted this round.
MIND ATTACK.7: Target is paralyzed, unable to move or take any action.
MIND ATTACK.8: Target takes a B Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.9: Target loses 50% of remaining concussion hits.
MIND ATTACK.10: Everyone except the caster within the radius is a target, all failing to resist are stunned (-50).
MIND ATTACK.12: Target is in a stunned (-75) condition.
MIND ATTACK.13: Target takes a C Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.14: As Paralyze I , except duration is 3 rounds/10 failure.
MIND ATTACK.15: As Mind Shout I, except the area of effect is a circle with a radius of 50'.
MIND ATTACK.16: Target takes a D Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.17: As Paralyze I , except duration is 5 rounds/10 failure.
MIND ATTACK.19: As Mind Shout I, except the area of effect is a circle with a radius of 100'.
MIND ATTACK.20: Target takes an E Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.25: As Mind Shout I, except area of effect is a circle with a radius of 50' and any target failing by more than 50 is unconscious for eight hours.
MIND ATTACK.30: As Mind Shout I, except the area of effect is circle with a radius of 300'.
MIND ATTACK.35: Target takes an F (E+A) Electricity critical strike.
MIND ATTACK.40: As Jolts I, except affects 1 target/level and duration varies by degree of RR failure.
MIND ATTACK.50: Targets failing by 1-50 are unconscious, and targets failing by more than 50 are dead; RRs for spell casters of Mentalism are modified by -20. Affects everyone else within 50' of the caster.

MIND CONTROL.1: Target must answer a single-concept question truthfully (assuming target can understand the question).
MIND CONTROL.2: Target falls into natural sleep. The first round of sleep is magical (the target cannot be awakened through normal means). Falling asleep is rapid, but not so instantaneous that the victim cannot slump gently to the ground (no injuries under normal circumstances from falling to the ground).
MIND CONTROL.3: Humanoid target believes caster is a good friend.
MIND CONTROL.4: Target will take no offensive action and will fight only in self-defense.
MIND CONTROL.5: Target is incapable of making decisions or initiating action; he may continue to fight current foes or in self-defense. The target may also follow orders from the target’s leader (if any), but will only be able to follow them so long as no ambiguities arise.
MIND CONTROL.6: Target fears caster and attempts to flee. (This does not involve a Fear RR or the usual fear mechanics. Use a normal Mentalism RR and the result is flight for the specified duration.)
MIND CONTROL.7: Target will perform a single suggested act that is not self-destructive or diametrically opposed to the target’s personality (e.g., no suicide suggestions, no blinding himself suggestions, no suggestions that a priest pray to an enemy god, etc.).
MIND CONTROL.8: Humanoid target has its Action Points per round reduced by 3 (i.e., normally, the target will have 1 AP each round).
MIND CONTROL.10: Humanoid target must obey the caster as specified in Suggestion.
MIND CONTROL.11: [RR Mod: +20] Target falls into a coma.
MIND CONTROL.12: As Charm Kind, except works on any sentient creature.
MIND CONTROL.13: Upon being told a story (or related series of statements) the target will completely believe it to be true for the duration of the spell, defending it as truth.
MIND CONTROL.14: As Hold Kind, except works on any sentient creature.
MIND CONTROL.15: Target is given one task by the caster. Until the task is completed, the target has a -10 penalty to all actions not related to completing the task, so long as the target is making an effort to accomplish the task. If the target stops pursuing the task, all actions are at a -25 penalty.
MIND CONTROL.16: [RR Mod: -20] Humanoid target is paralyzed and can do nothing.
MIND CONTROL.17: As Master of Kind , except works on any sentient creature.
MIND CONTROL.18: Target is a blathering idiot. The target may be led around, but can take no action.
MIND CONTROL.19: [RR Mod: -20] As Hold Kind True, except works on any sentient creature.
MIND CONTROL.20: [RR Mod: -20] Target is unconscious and cannot be woken except by magic; even awakening the victim with a spell requires that the waking spell make a successful RR against this spell.
MIND CONTROL.25: As Geas, except the target suffers a -20 penalty for actions not related to completing the task or a -50 penalty to all actions if the task is ignored. Maneuvers (not attack rolls or SCRs) directly related to the task receive a +10 bonus.
MIND CONTROL.30: Caster has total control of target’s mind (when concentrating). After the initial casting, the range for control is 1 mile/level.
MIND CONTROL.35: As Fear, except target will always flee when confronted by the caster.
MIND CONTROL.40: Target is completely enthralled with the caster. The caster cannot control the target directly, but the target will treat the caster as his best friend, believe him to be of the utmost character and will believe anything the caster tells him unless confronted with definitive proof of its falsity.
MIND CONTROL.50: As Mind Control True , except duration is permanent (until dispelled); caster need only concentrate to give commands. Only one target can be so controlled at a time.

MIND DEATH.1: The target’s memory of a 20 minute period is temporarily eliminated. After a period of up to one hour (specified by the caster at the time of casting), the target will remember all that transpired during the period.
MIND DEATH.2: Target forgets 10 contiguous minutes totally. The caster determines which ten minute period is forgotten.
MIND DEATH.3: Target’s mind is temporarily blank and he can do nothing. The target will remember nothing that occurs during the period. His body stops (all voluntary actions cease).
MIND DEATH.4: Any time the target attempts to use (roll a maneuver using) a skill with a strong mental component (e.g. one for which Memory or Reasoning stat bonus is used), there is a 20% chance that the victim will not be able to recollect the skill at all and cannot use it again until having slept.
MIND DEATH.5: As Forget I, except 100 minutes can be erased.
MIND DEATH.6: As Mental Block I, except up to 1 hour of memories can be delayed for up to 24 hours.
MIND DEATH.7: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 3 rounds.
MIND DEATH.8: As Skill Amnesia I, except duration is 1 week per 10 failure.
MIND DEATH.9: As Forget I, except 200 minutes can be erased.
MIND DEATH.10: Target mind blanks out for a period of 10 minutes each day at random. Target is incapable of activity or thought during this period (as in Mind Blank).
MIND DEATH.11: Target loses 5% of his collected experience (i.e., experience points).
MIND DEATH.12: As Mental Block I, except memories of up to one hour/level can be delayed for up to one day per level.
MIND DEATH.13: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 5 rounds.
MIND DEATH.14: As Forget I, except 1 hour per level of the caster can be erased.
MIND DEATH.15: As Mind Death I , except the target blanks out twice per day.
MIND DEATH.16: As Skill Amnesia I, except duration is 1 month per 10 failure.
MIND DEATH.17: As Mind Death I , except the target blanks out three times per day.
MIND DEATH.18: As Mind Blank I , except duration is 10 rounds.
MIND DEATH.19: As Lost Experience I, except percentage of experience lost is 10%.
MIND DEATH.20: As Mind Death I , except the target blanks out five times per day.
MIND DEATH.25: As Lost Experience I , except percentage of experience lost is 15%.
MIND DEATH.30: As Mind Death I , except the target blanks out ten times per day.
MIND DEATH.35: As Lost Experience I , except percentage of experience lost is 20%.
MIND DEATH.40: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 20 rounds.
MIND DEATH.50: As Mind Blank I, except the target has a 5% chance of blanking out each round.

MIND DESTRUCTION.1: Causes loss of 25% of target’s remaining concussion hits (i.e., those not already taken); these hits are healed when the duration is up, provided target has not died.
MIND DESTRUCTION.3: Target must subtract 25 from all Perception maneuvers and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.
MIND DESTRUCTION.4: Target must subtract 25 from all maneuvers involving movement and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.
MIND DESTRUCTION.5: Target must subtract 25 from all combat rolls and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.
MIND DESTRUCTION.7: As Minor Pain, except 50% of remaining hits are taken.
MIND DESTRUCTION.8: Target forgets a period (of up to one minute per level) of the past, as desired by the caster.
MIND DESTRUCTION.9: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Pain, except hits must be cured or heal normally.
MIND DESTRUCTION.10: As As Disorientation, Unbalance, and Falter (all at once), except initiative penalty is 10.
MIND DESTRUCTION.11: Target mind blanks out for a period of 10 minutes each day at random. Target is incapable of activity or thought during this period.
MIND DESTRUCTION.13: Target’s mind is completely blank for the duration. The target cannot perceive events, time, or activity; when the target’s mind does come back, this produces one round of stun (-50) due to reorientation.
MIND DESTRUCTION.14: As Disorientation, except for range, duration, and that it affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
MIND DESTRUCTION.15: Target is a blathering idiot. The target can be led around, but can take no action.
MIND DESTRUCTION.16: As Unbalance, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
MIND DESTRUCTION.17: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.
MIND DESTRUCTION.18: As Falter, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
MIND DESTRUCTION.19: [RR Mod: -20] Target hallucinates an attack by a creature drawn from the target’s worst fears. The target will be attacked each round by the creature (doing real damage with a +100 Martial Arts Strikes attack). Regardless of the damage done by the target (to the creature), the creature will only be defeated when the target either loses consciousness or makes an RR (one RR each 6 rounds).
MIND DESTRUCTION.20: Target loses 5% of collected experience (usually experience points).
MIND DESTRUCTION.25: As Minor Pain, except as many targets as the caster’s level are affected, the duration is until healed, and the range is 300'.
MIND DESTRUCTION.30: Target forgets completely about any one particular subject (e.g., the caster, the Fire Ruby of Romju, the Battle of Xon’s Crossing).
MIND DESTRUCTION.35: As Mind Shock, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected and the range is 300'.
MIND DESTRUCTION.40: As Major Pain, except as many targets as the caster’s level are affected, the duration is until healed, and the range is 300'.
MIND DESTRUCTION.50: As Unminding, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected and the range is 300'. Note: When a target loses experience points, this has nothing to do with experience levels. The lost experience points simply means that the character will have to gain that many more before rising to the next level. The experience loss may be reversed by healing the brain. Initiative penalties from Disorientation, Unbalance, and Falter are not cumulative.   

MIND DISEASE.1: Target has trouble sleeping. Treat any sleep time as half as effective (e.g., count eight hours “sleep” as four hours). This hinders hit and PP recovery. See RMCL Section 5.5 for effects of inadequate sleep on fatigue. Drugs/herbs or spells may allow for temporary relief by forcing sleep.
MIND DISEASE.2: Target has an extreme dislike for any specific thing the caster chooses. Target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of overcoming the neurosis when given a choice (e.g., neurosis concerning horses would mean that every time the target tried to get on or near a horse the target would need to make a roll in order to do so).
MIND DISEASE.3: Target experiences chronic guilt over some action in his past. The GM should choose some significant event in the target’s past. The target will not perform the same or similar actions again and must take steps to alleviate the guilt (acting to rectify the past wrong or, if that is impossible, similar wrongs).
MIND DISEASE.4: Target believes everyone other than his close associates is out to get him.
MIND DISEASE.5: Target must make an RR against Fear (use the highest level foe, or GM’s judgment for other perils, as level of Fear) at -10 in any personally dangerous situation.
MIND DISEASE.6: Target has a fear of a specific type of thing the caster chooses. The target has a 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)% chance of maintaining control around the feared thing. If the target fails to control himself, he has a 25% chance of fleeing from the object of fear (moving at maximum pace to get away from it), otherwise (75% of the time) he will avoid the object of fear at all costs.
MIND DISEASE.7: Target develops a tendency to hallucinate and see things as they are not. For each major event in a day, the target has a 10% chance of hallucinating and seeing major threats as nothing major and minor things as major threats (similar to the syndrome that Don Quixote suffered from).
MIND DISEASE.8: Target develops a second personality with different values. There is a 10% chance per day that the second personality will become active (at a random time during the day). The GM should select d10 character traits that must be extremely different from the target’s.
MIND DISEASE.9: Target has a psychosis about a specific type of thing of the caster’s choosing. The target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of maintaining control around the object of his psychosis. If the target fails to control himself, he will blindly attack the object (attempting to destroy it at all costs) 20% of the time, avoiding it the rest of the time. If avoidance is not possible, the reaction will be violent.
MIND DISEASE.10: Target drifts in and out of a catatonic state. Every hour there is a 25% chance he will become catatonic for one hour.
MIND DISEASE.11: As Insomnia, except target can only sleep if he uses drugs or spells to induce sleep. He adds +100 to his RRs against all sleep spells. If he gets no sleep, he will slowly go mad (GM’s discretion on the specifics).
MIND DISEASE.12: As Neurosis, except that the base chance of overcoming the neurosis is 25%.
MIND DISEASE.13: As Guilt, except every day that he fails an RR (against the original level of this spell), the target must take actions to atone for his guilt (or sink into a deep depression).
MIND DISEASE.14: Target becomes tempted to steal things at every opportunity. It does not matter if the target even actually wants the things. The target has a chance of maintaining control equal to 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)%. If the target does not maintain control, he will steal something every time there is an opportunity. (Check no more often than once per hour.)
MIND DISEASE.15: Target will trust no one absolutely. When in a life threatening/ dangerous situation, there is a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) that the target will not trust the protection of others and will act unilaterally for self-preservation.
MIND DISEASE.16: Target becomes tempted to set fires at every opportunity. He will also take great pleasure in watching things burn, so this may tip off his companions. The target has a chance of maintaining control equal to 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)%. If the target does not maintain control, he will set something on fire. Check once per day; additional checks may be necessary if exceptional fire-setting opportunities arise.
MIND DISEASE.17: As Paranoia, except target will not associate with anyone for longer than one hour per day.
MIND DISEASE.19: As Psychosis, except that the base chance of overcoming the psychosis is 10%.
MIND DISEASE.20: As Split Personality, except the second personality is active 50% of the time.
MIND DISEASE.25: As Split Personality, except target develops d10+1 extra personalities (Multiple Personality Disorder). There is a 10% chance per day that a non-active personality will become active.
MIND DISEASE.30: As Catatonia, except every hour there is a 95% chance target will be catatonic.
MIND DISEASE.40: Target suffers from one of the lower level spells on this list, but which one changes each day. This makes any attempt to treat the target through non-magical means suffer a -50 penalty.
MIND DISEASE.50: Target may be given any mental disease that the caster chooses.

MIND DOMINATION.1: Target is distracted by an imaginary object. For the duration of this spell, the target wastes 1 AP on this distraction each round.
MIND DOMINATION.2: Caster and target are locked in mental contact until: the caster cancels the spell or the spell is dispelled or either the caster or the target has an RR failure of 30 or greater (both the caster and the target must make RRs against each other every round). The one failing by 30 becomes unconscious. While this spell is active, neither the caster nor the target can take any action.
MIND DOMINATION.3: As Mind Lock, except while target cannot take any action during the mental struggle, the caster may act as normal, within the restrictions of maintaining concentration.
MIND DOMINATION.4: The next Type “Fm” spell cast by the caster of this spell (cast within one minute of completing this spell) will have a special RR penalty of -10.
MIND DOMINATION.5: Target is controlled by a Type I Demon. The target may make an additional RR once per day (attack level equals Demon level) to regain control. The Demon is random, maniacal, and homicidal.
MIND DOMINATION.6: Target’s “soul” and the caster’s “soul” are interchanged; the caster can operate at half of his normal activity (double all AP costs as with concentration) while in the body of the target. The target (in the caster’s body) is inactive. The caster can cancel the spell at any time (takes one round). The target can cancel the spell if he makes a successful RR (he gets one every 10 minutes). If either body is killed, both “souls” are destroyed.
MIND DOMINATION.7: As Mind Invasion, except for range and the caster gains a +10 to his RRs.
MIND DOMINATION.8: Target must obey the caster. If the caster gives an order to do something completely alien to the target, he may make another RR with a +25 modification.
MIND DOMINATION.9: An intelligent magic item becomes submissive to the caster’s will and will obey the caster completely. If the item successfully resists, another attempt to dominate the item cannot be made until the caster gains a rank in this spell list.
MIND DOMINATION.10: As Demonic Possession I , except Demon is Type II.
MIND DOMINATION.11: As Domination, except target is a zombie until he succeeds in his once per day RR. On any round that the caster concentrates, the target must obey him.
MIND DOMINATION.12: The next Type “Fm” spell cast by the caster of this spell (cast within one minute of completing this spell) will have a special RR penalty of -25.
MIND DOMINATION.13: As Mind Slave , except target’s “mind/essence/soul” is in agony and any round that the caster does not concentrate, the target will obey anyone’s verbal commands. Conflicting commands mean the target does nothing.
MIND DOMINATION.14: As Lesser Object Domination , except for duration.
MIND DOMINATION.15: As Demonic Possession I , except Demon is Type III.
MIND DOMINATION.16: As Domination, except the target must perform a single, straightforward task. The task may take the target out of range of the caster and the target must still attempt to complete the task.
MIND DOMINATION.17: As Lesser Object Domination, except for duration.
MIND DOMINATION.18: As Mind Slave, except the target only gets an RR once per week.
MIND DOMINATION.19: As Demonic Possession I, except Demon is Type IV .
MIND DOMINATION.20: As Mind Slave, except caster need not concentrate to command the target.
MIND DOMINATION.25: As Demonic Possession I , except Demon is Type V .
MIND DOMINATION.30: As Transferral, except target only gets his additional RRs once per month and every time he fails his chance to successfully resist decreases by 1 and the caster may operate normally in the target’s body.
MIND DOMINATION.35: The next Type “Fm” spell cast by the caster of this spell (cast within one minute of completing this spell) will have a special RR penalty of -50.
MIND DOMINATION.40: As Demonic Possession I, except Demon is Type VI.
MIND DOMINATION.50: As Oppression, except target only gets his RRs once per month and every time he fails his chance to successfully resist decreases by 1.

MIND EROSION.1: Target suffers a -1 penalty to one of his mental stats. The penalty applies to the stat bonus, not the stat itself. The mental stats are: Presence, Empathy, Intuition, Memory, Reasoning, and Self Discipline. The stat that suffers the penalty is determined randomly.
MIND EROSION.2: Target is mentally bewildered. Each round, he has only a 50% chance of making a decision. He will always defend himself from direct attack.
MIND EROSION.3: Target immediately loses 10% of his remaining Power Points (PP). They may be recovered normally.
MIND EROSION.4: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -2.
MIND EROSION.5: As Dull Mind I , except stat influenced is determined by the caster.
MIND EROSION.6: As Power Leak I , except 20% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.7: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -3.
MIND EROSION.8: As Power Leak I, except 30% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.9: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -4.
MIND EROSION.10: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -2.
MIND EROSION.11: As Power Leak I, except 40% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.12: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -5.
MIND EROSION.13: As Power Leak I, except 50% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.14: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -6.
MIND EROSION.15: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -3.
MIND EROSION.16: As Power Leak I, except 60% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.17: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -7.
MIND EROSION.18: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -4.
MIND EROSION.19: As Power Leak I, except 70% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.20: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -8.
MIND EROSION.25: As Power Leak I, except 90% of remaining PP are lost.
MIND EROSION.30: As Dull Mind I, except penalty is -10.
MIND EROSION.35: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -6.
MIND EROSION.40: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -8.
MIND EROSION.50: As Mind Erosion I, except penalty is -10.

MIND ILLUSIONS.1: All text that the target attempts to read will appear jumbled (and is indecipherable).
MIND ILLUSIONS.2: The target believes that he is very ill (or sicker than he actually is). The GM should base the severity of the “imaginary” illness upon the amount the target fails his RR by (e.g., if he fails his RR by more than 50, he might think he is terminally ill).
MIND ILLUSIONS.3: Target will perceive something incorrectly through one of his senses. For example, he might see something out of the corner of his eye, or hear something that is not there. The sensory effect cannot last longer than one round.
MIND ILLUSIONS.4: Target’s perception is skewed. All Awareness maneuvers are modified by -10.
MIND ILLUSIONS.5: The target is attacked by a creature of shadow. The creature is actually a hallucination and only the target sees the creature. The creature will immediately attack the target (in melee). The creature has the same DB, OB, and Hits as the target (use the target’s highest melee OB as the creature’s OB), but AT is always 1. If the caster casts spells upon himself, the creature acts as if affected by the same spell. The creature is immune to spell attacks. If the creature is slain (by killing or incapacitating it), the spell is cancelled. Creature uses same attack table as target, but damage is not real. However, if damage would render target dead or unconscious if real, target falls unconscious for the remainder of the spell’s duration.
MIND ILLUSIONS.6: Target will view things as if he were in a dream. His reactions are slowed (he suffers a -10 to all actions). In addition, he will sometimes perceive things differently than they actually are. The GM should roll for each event that occurs with a 10% chance that the event is mistaken. For example, the target might see an approaching horse as a large wild animal; or he might see the charging Orc as a messenger bringing news. Note that the caster has no control over what the target perceives or how he perceives it.
MIND ILLUSIONS.7: Target will believe that the next person he sees is someone else. At the time of casting, the caster chooses what type of person the target sees. For example, he might see a servant entering the palace gate instead of the skulking figure. If the next person attacks the target, the effects of this spell are cancelled.
MIND ILLUSIONS.8: As Misleading I, except penalty is -30.
MIND ILLUSIONS.9: As False Sense I, except affects up to 2 senses and the effect can last for up to 2 rounds.
MIND ILLUSIONS.10: As Lesser Phantom , except the creature has an AT of 3.
MIND ILLUSIONS.11: As Waking Dream I, except actions suffer a -20 penalty, and the chance of perceiving things incorrectly is 20%.
MIND ILLUSIONS.12: As Misleading I, except penalty is -50.
MIND ILLUSIONS.13: As False Sense I, except affects up to 3 senses and the effect can last for up to 3 rounds.
MIND ILLUSIONS.14: As Mistaken Identity I , except target mistakes the next person he sees for a specific person. For example, he might see the Chamberlain entering the throne room, instead of a burglar.
MIND ILLUSIONS.15: As Lesser Phantom, except the creature has an AT of 5.
MIND ILLUSIONS.16: As False Sense I, except affects up to 4 senses and the effect can last for up to 4 rounds.
MIND ILLUSIONS.17: As Misleading I, except penalty is -70.
MIND ILLUSIONS.18: As Waking Dream I, except actions suffer a -30 penalty, and the chance of perceiving things incorrectly is 30%.
MIND ILLUSIONS.19: As False Sense I, except affects up to 5 senses and the effect can last for up to 5 rounds.
MIND ILLUSIONS.20: As Lesser Phantom , except the creature has an AT of 7.
MIND ILLUSIONS.25: As Misleading I, except penalty is -100.
MIND ILLUSIONS.30: As Mistaken Identity I , except the target will permanently confuse the next person he sees with someone else. Each time he sees that person, there is a 50% chance that he will mistake that person for someone else.
MIND ILLUSIONS.35: As False Sense I, except affects as many senses as the target has and the effect can last for up to 20 rounds.
MIND ILLUSIONS.40: As Lesser Phantom, except the creature has an AT of 10.
MIND ILLUSIONS.50: As Waking Dream I, except actions suffer a -40 penalty, the chance of perceiving things incorrectly is 40%, and the effects are permanent.

MIND MASTERY.1: Caster may cast this spell with another spell in order to store that second spell. The spell to be stored must be cast within one minute after the Spell Store spell is cast. The stored spell may then be cast later as an instantaneous spell. The Spell Store spell costs the same number of PP as the spell that is stored. No other spell may be cast while a spell is stored.
MIND MASTERY.2: Caster is aware of the presence of all sentient/thinking beings within 20'.
MIND MASTERY.3: Caster gets +5 bonus to RRs versus mind attacks.
MIND MASTERY.4: Caster’s next sleep period will require 25% less time to complete, with the caster awaking as fully refreshed as if the complete normal time had been spent (e.g., if the caster normally required eight hours of sleep in a night, this spell would allow the caster to sleep only six hours instead).
MIND MASTERY.5: Caster’s recall of relevant past experience and education are enhanced to assist in the current situation; this increases the caster’s Memory stat bonus by +25 for the duration of one maneuver involving Memory (e.g., the use of a “lore” skill).
MIND MASTERY.6: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, caster appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.
MIND MASTERY.7: Caster gains insight or facts by observing a person or thing with intense concentration (e.g., room, item); increases caster’s Perception skill bonus by +50 for one “observation”.
MIND MASTERY.8: Caster gets +10 bonus to RRs versus mind attacks.
MIND MASTERY.9: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s profession may be misrepresented.
MIND MASTERY.10: Caster is able to correlate known facts to obtain conclusions; increases the caster’s Reasoning stat bonus by +25 for the duration of one maneuver involving Reasoning (e.g., the use of a “lore” skill, picking a lock, one “navigation” attempt, etc.).
MIND MASTERY.11: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s level may be misrepresented (to an upper limit of twice the caster’s actual level).
MIND MASTERY.12: This spell enables the caster to have unusually intense focus and self-control for one situation or maneuver; increasing the caster’s Self Discipline stat bonus by +25 for the duration of one maneuver involving Self Discipline (e.g., stalking a foe, a Perception maneuver, one “silent kill” attempt, etc.).
MIND MASTERY.13: Caster gets +15 bonus to RRs versus mind attacks.
MIND MASTERY.14: This spell assigns a compartmentalized portion of the caster’s mind to maintain a spell with a duration of “C”. Effectively, it allows that part of the caster’s mind to concentrate on the spell, freeing the rest of the caster’s mind to do other things (i.e., the caster does not take the normal penalty for Concentration). The caster can have only one Pseudoconcentration active at one time.
MIND MASTERY.15: Target becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.
MIND MASTERY.16: Caster gets +25 bonus to RRs versus mind attacks.
MIND MASTERY.17: As Recall, except recall is nearly automatic and in effect gives the caster an eidetic memory; Memory bonus is +50.
MIND MASTERY.18: Allows caster to use all of the Misfeel spells at once.
MIND MASTERY.19: Caster gets +50 bonus to RRs versus mind attacks.
MIND MASTERY.20: As Observation, except virtually every detail in even a large situation is noticed; Perception bonus is +100.
MIND MASTERY.25: As Correlation, except obtaining conclusions is nearly automatic; the Reasoning bonus is +50.
MIND MASTERY.30: As Resolve, except focus/control is nearly total; the Self Discipline bonus is +50.
MIND MASTERY.35: As Inner Wall True, except protects not only the caster, but anyone with whom the caster is in telepathic contact.
MIND MASTERY.40: As Misfeel, except it has a ten minute/level duration and does not require concentration.
MIND MASTERY.50: Caster reflects all mind attacks back on the attacker who must then make an RR against his own spell.

MIND MERGE.1: Caster learns target’s basic feelings; caster can concentrate on a new target each round.
MIND MERGE.2: Caster learns target’s emotions in detail; caster can concentrate on a new target each round.
MIND MERGE.3: Allows two Mentalism spell users to communicate mentally and exchange Power Points.
MIND MERGE.4: When cast within one minute before a Mind Merge or Ready Merge or Hivemind spell, this spell allows the caster to focus on a specific person as the target. This target must be Mind Stored (see the Presence spell list).
MIND MERGE.5: Caster receives surface thoughts from target.
MIND MERGE.6: Allows caster and target to interchange thoughts; if both are Mentalism spell users, they can interchange Power Points. They must be able to see each other or the caster must know the exact location of the target or the caster must use Focus Merge to locate the target. In all cases, the target must also be within range.
MIND MERGE.7: The caster prepares himself to be the target of a Mind Merge or Ready Merge spell, thus, increasing the range of the “incoming” spell. The increased range is equal to the original range times twenty.
MIND MERGE.8: As Mind Merge I, except range is 100'.
MIND MERGE.9: Some minds may be sufficiently different from the caster’s own as to be almost incomprehensible, whereas others may be so alien as to create terror and psychic pain. When cast, this spell acts as a filter and interpreter which allows a caster to understand and tolerate these mental differences. This can be as mundane as catching cultural implications in someone’s Inner Thoughts or as exotic as understanding interdimensional fish monsters which directly sense forces of gravitation, magnetism, essence, and luck.
MIND MERGE.10: As Thoughts, except caster also learns reasoning and thought patterns behind surface thoughts.
MIND MERGE.11: As in Mind Merge II, except for range and instantaneous nature of spell. Caster and target must have Merged before.
MIND MERGE.13: Target and caster switch minds and spell-casting abilities; target (in caster’s body) is in a coma if not also a Mentalist. An unwilling target may make a new RR every hour.
MIND MERGE.14: Breaks any contacts made with the caster’s mind through various spells or other effects, regardless of how they were made. Connections may make an RR, but at a -50 penalty. Also expels any mental intruders in residence, such as through demonic possesion. Intruders only have a -25 penalty.
MIND MERGE.15: As in Inner Thoughts , except affects a single target and caster can also scan target’s conscious memories; at a rate of 1 scene/ round.
MIND MERGE.16: As Inner Thoughts, except for range.
MIND MERGE.17: Spell creates a mental bond between the caster and one target which allows each to use one skill possessed by the other. This gives the borrower the skill ranks of the other so long as the spell lasts; in all respects, it is as if the borrower actually had developed those ranks himself. The following are not allowed as shared skills: Body Development, Power Point Development, Spell Lists. The skill ranks are not lost to the individual who originally had them. If the caster and target are ever more than 100' apart, the spell terminates immediately.
MIND MERGE.18: As Mind Merge I , except range is 50'/level.
MIND MERGE.19: As Mind Scan, except for range.
MIND MERGE.20: As Mind Scan, except caster can probe target’s subconscious memories (i.e., all experiences).
MIND MERGE.25: As in Mind Switch, except lasts as long as the caster likes (or until an unwilling target makes an RR).
MIND MERGE.30: Caster can remove one thought or memory from the target’s mind each round.
MIND MERGE.35: As Mind Merge I, except range is 50'/level, and multiple minds may be merged. The location of the various minds must be known to the caster as in Mind Merge and the caster must have Merged with each of them before and there is a limit of one mind for every five caster levels. Any of the merged minds may interchange Power Points if they share a realm.
MIND MERGE.40: Two targets switch minds and spell casting abilities; both parties must be willing to experience the transfer.
MIND MERGE.50: As in Ready Merge, except range is unlimited.

MIND OVER MATTER.1: Caster gets a +5 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.2: Caster gets a +20 bonus to his next Body Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.3: Caster gets a +20 bonus to his next Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.4: For the duration of this spell, the caster’s natural AT is increased by one (e.g., most humanoids would go from AT 1 to AT 2, but if the caster’s normal skin was AT 3 it would become AT 4).
MIND OVER MATTER.5: Caster can strike an inanimate object and deliver damage to it (taking no damage himself). Caster makes a normal Unarmed attack against the object. In addition, the object must make a normal Breakage Check.
MIND OVER MATTER.6: Caster gets a +30 bonus to his next Body Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.7: Caster gets a +10 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.8: Caster gets a +30 bonus to his next Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.9: As Body Armor I, but caster’s natural AT is increased by two.
MIND OVER MATTER.10: The caster may ignore the effects of the first critical strike made against him. At the end of this spell’s duration, all effects apply normally. Only one wound at a time can be ignored in this fashion (i.e., the caster cannot have more than one Lion’ s Heart spell active at any given time).
MIND OVER MATTER.11: Caster gets a +40 bonus to his next Body Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.12: Caster gets a +40 bonus to his next Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.13: Caster gets a +15 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.14: As Shattering Blow I, except object receives a -30 penalty to its Breakage Check.
MIND OVER MATTER.15: Caster gets a +50 bonus to his next Body Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.16: Caster gets a +50 bonus to his next Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.17: As Body Armor I, but caster’s natural AT is increased by three.
MIND OVER MATTER.18: As Shattering Blow I , except object receives a -60 penalty to its Breakage Check.
MIND OVER MATTER.19: Caster gets a +20 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.20: Caster gets a +60 bonus to his next Body Discipline or Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.25: Caster gets a +30 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.30: As Shattering Blow I, except object receives a -100 penalty to its Breakage Check.
MIND OVER MATTER.35: Caster gets a +40 bonus to all Unarmed attacks.
MIND OVER MATTER.40: Caster gets a +80 bonus to his next Body Discipline or Mental Discipline maneuver within 1 minute per level.
MIND OVER MATTER.50: Caster may use any one of the lower level spells on this list, once per round.

MIND SENSE MOLDING.1: Caster can check one object or place (up to a 5'R) and tell if it is an illusion or has an illusion on it.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.2: Detects any invisible objects or beings; one 5'R area can be checked each round. All attacks against detected targets are modified by -50.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.3: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, target appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.4: As Misfeel Kind, except target’s level may be misrepresented by 1 level (up or down).
MIND SENSE MOLDING.5: Creates a false “presence” of a being for the purposes of magical detection.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.6: As Misfeel Kind, except target’s profession may be misrepresented.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.7: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level may be misrepresented by up to 3 levels.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.8: Caster becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.9: Conceals an item’s magical power. Any attempt to detect its magical power (an appropriate Detect spell while it is active, an Attunement attempt, or other means) is hindered. If the item has a single power, then if the detection attempt fails an RR against this spell, it does not register the item’s magic. In the case of multiple powers, a magical detection attempt gains a +5 to its RR for each power beyond the first, while someone attempting Attunement (and failing the RR) will be unable to discover one ability (chosen at the time this spell is cast), but may discover and use the remaining powers normally.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.10: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level may be misrepresented by up to 5 levels.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.11: While this spell is in effect, one illusion within the caster’s range ceases to exist for the caster only. The caster must specify the illusion to be negated (unless caster knows something is an illusion, this spell would be gambling PP).
MIND SENSE MOLDING.12: As Detect Illusion, except caster can concentrate on one object or place per round.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.13: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level can be misrepresented by up to 10 levels.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.14: As Disillusion I, except up to three illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.15: Applies all the lower level Misfeels to a target at once (only one Misfeel Power can be applied).
MIND SENSE MOLDING.16: Target becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.17: As Disillusion I, except up to five illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.18: As Reduce Power Emanations, except affects up to some combination of five powers/targets, i.e. the caster could use this to conceal up to five powers of a single item, three powers of one item and two powers of another item, one power each of five items, etc. .
MIND SENSE MOLDING.19: As Disillusion I, except all illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.20: As Unpresence I, except affects up to five targets.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.25: As Misfeel Multiple, except duration is 1 hour/level.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.30: As Unpresence, except 20 targets may be affected.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.35: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level may be misrepresented by any number of levels.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.40: Creates one false “presence” of a being for the purposes of magical detection for each level of the caster and these will be located as the caster desires within the area of effect. Caster may move these presence mirages by concentrating at a rate of 10'/round each.
MIND SENSE MOLDING.50: As Misfeel Multiple, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.

MIND SPEECH.1: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster may mentally speak with another spell caster of the Mentalism realm.
MIND SPEECH.2: Caster enters a trance-like state to mentally prepare to be the recipient for a Mind Tongue spell. While he is under the effects of this spell, any “incoming” Mind Tongue spell has its range increased to 10 times normal.
MIND SPEECH.3: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster may mentally speak with any one thinking being. Caster must be able to see the target or the caster must know the exact location of the target or the caster must use Focus Mind to locate the target. In all cases, the target must also be within range.
MIND SPEECH.4: When this spell is cast, the next Mind Tongue spell will be able to focus in on a particular person as its target. The person must be Mind Stored (see Presence spell list).
MIND SPEECH.5: As Prepare Mind I, except range is increased to 20x normal.
MIND SPEECH.6: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 500'.
MIND SPEECH.7: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mentalist Tongue, except range extends as far as the caster can see.
MIND SPEECH.8: As Prepare Mind I , except range is increased to 50x normal.
MIND SPEECH.9: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster can broadcast thoughts to minds of all within range. This is a one-way broadcast.
MIND SPEECH.10: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 1,000'.
MIND SPEECH.11: As Prepare Mind I, except range is increased to 100x normal.
MIND SPEECH.12: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Speech I, except area of effect is a radius of 100'.
MIND SPEECH.13: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Speech I, except caster can limit broadcast to desired beings.
MIND SPEECH.14: As Prepare Mind I , except range is increased to 500x normal.
MIND SPEECH.15: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Speech I, except area of effect is a radius of 300'.
MIND SPEECH.16: [RR Mod.: -50] A 25-word mental message can be delayed and then later triggered by the passage of a fixed time period of up to 24 hours or by certain movements, certain sounds, touch, reading, etc. within the 10' radius of effect or by a specific Mind Stored person entering the area.
MIND SPEECH.17: As Prepare Mind I, except range is increased to 1000x normal.
MIND SPEECH.18: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 1 mile.
MIND SPEECH.19: [RR Mod.: -50] As Friend Speech I, except range is limited to any desired minds within a radius of 100'.
MIND SPEECH.20: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Speech I , except range is all minds within a radius of 2,000'.
MIND SPEECH.25: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 1 mile/level.
MIND SPEECH.30: [RR Mod.: -50] As Waiting Tongue, except it is broadcast to all within a 300'R.
MIND SPEECH.35: [RR Mod.: -50] As Friend Speech I, except range is limited to any desired minds within a radius of 2,000'.
MIND SPEECH.40: [RR Mod.: -50] As Waiting Tongue, except it is broadcast to all within a radius of up to 1000' as determined by the caster and message may be up to 100 words long (or substitute one visual image for 25 words) and it may wait as long as one year.
MIND SPEECH.50: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Speech I, except radius can be up to 1 mile/level from caster.

MIND SUBVERSION.1: Target suspects the actions of his associates (GM may give descriptions that arouse the player’s own suspicions).
MIND SUBVERSION.2: One of the target’s personality traits is perverted (that trait is taken to an extreme). If the target already has a tendency in one direction, it will be taken to an extreme: if spending habits were affected, a thrifty fellow would become a miser, while a generous spender would become a spendthrift.
