However, someone may still have a middle of the road trait affected, in which case it might go to either extreme (e.g., someone not particularly brave or cautious could become cowardly or reckless).
MIND SUBVERSION.3: Target has a 20% chance of lying anytime he makes a statement.
MIND SUBVERSION.4: As Trait Subversion I, except 2 traits may be perverted.
MIND SUBVERSION.5: Target will attempt to cheat on all matters (e.g., splitting loot, card games, etc.).
MIND SUBVERSION.6: Target has urges to commit minor property damage. When such an opportunity arives, there is a 60% chance the target must attempt to commit such damage.
MIND SUBVERSION.7: Target becomes a sneak thief and has a 10% chance of attempting to steal anything that catches his fancy (if it does not involve violence).
MIND SUBVERSION.8: As Trait Subversion I , except 3 traits may be perverted.
MIND SUBVERSION.9: Target becomes fascinated with subtle damage that endangers others. Every time he is alone with someone else’s things, he has a 50% chance of cutting ropes partway through, punching small holes in waterskins, loosening saddles, etc.
MIND SUBVERSION.10: As Stealing, except target will use violence to steal.
MIND SUBVERSION.11: As Trait Subversion I , except 5 traits may be perverted.
MIND SUBVERSION.12: As Trait Subversion I, except a different trait will be affected each day (with the previous day’s trait reverting back to its normal state).
MIND SUBVERSION.13: There is a 5% chance the target will assault any given person when he initially encounters him (the target must actually meet the person, not merely pass him on the street).
MIND SUBVERSION.14: Target will become bent on avenging even minor disputes with the setting of fires. There is a 40% chance that he will attempt to set on fire goods or a building belonging to anyone with whom he has even the slightest quarrel.
MIND SUBVERSION.15: As Trait Subversion I, except 7 traits may be perverted.
MIND SUBVERSION.16: As Random Trait Subversion I, except two traits are affected at one time.
MIND SUBVERSION.17: As Assault, except there is a 10% chance that the target will attempt to maim any given person.
MIND SUBVERSION.18: As Trait Subversion I, except 10 traits may be perverted.
MIND SUBVERSION.19: As Random Trait Subversion I, except three traits are affected at one time.
MIND SUBVERSION.20: As Assault, except target attempts to kill.
MIND SUBVERSION.25: As Assault, except target will take note of a person and plan to assassinate him later. This spell will last until the target has made an assassination attempt on a target.
MIND SUBVERSION.30: As Trait Subversion I, except duration is permanent.
MIND SUBVERSION.35: As Assassination, except each assassination attempt will only allow a new RR against this spell. If the RR fails, a new victim will be selected and another assassination attempt will be made, continuing until an RR is successfully made.
MIND SUBVERSION.40: As any spell on this list of up to 25th level, except effects may be delayed until some trigger occurs (a word is spoken in the target’s presence, the target visits some location, the next full moon rises, etc.). The effects may wait years until being triggered; the triggered spell’s duration is based on the RR failure to this spell, where applicable.
MIND SUBVERSION.50: Whenever the target is injured, suffers humiliation, or fails in something, he will attempt suicide in some imaginative way.

MIND'S DOOR.1: Opens a door. Any bars/bolts that the caster can see will be displaced to allow opening, while locks (magical or mundane) have but a 10% chance of being unlocked. See Section 10.14 for guidelines on handling locks.
MIND'S DOOR.2: Caster “teleports” a single inanimate object to a point up to 100' away from its current location. There can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes, a barrier is anything the object could not physically go through). The object has a maximum weight limit of 1 pound per level of the caster.
MIND'S DOOR.3: Caster “teleports” to a point up to 50' away, but there can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes a barrier is anything the caster could not physically go through; a closed door is a barrier, a pit is not).
MIND'S DOOR.4: As Leave Item I, except the object can pass through barriers by specifying exact direction and distance. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the object does not move (though the caster still loses the PP).
MIND'S DOOR.5: As Minor Leaving , except caster can move 100'.
MIND'S DOOR.6: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster may mentally speak with any one thinking being. Caster must be able to see the target or the caster must know the exact location of the target. In all cases, the target must also be within range.
MIND'S DOOR.7: As Long Door Item I, except object can be moved up to 200'.
MIND'S DOOR.8: As Leaving I, except caster can move 300'.
MIND'S DOOR.9: As Leave Item I , except object can be moved up to 500'.
MIND'S DOOR.10: As Minor Leaving, except caster can pass through barriers by specifying exact direction and distance. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the caster does not move, instead being stunned (-50) 1-10 rounds, and the PP for this spell are still expended.
MIND'S DOOR.11: As Leaving I, except caster can move 500'.
MIND'S DOOR.12: As Minor Long Door, except caster can move 100'.
MIND'S DOOR.13: As Long Door Item I, except object can be moved up to 500'.
MIND'S DOOR.14: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 1,000'.
MIND'S DOOR.15: As Leaving I, except caster can move 100'/level up to 2000'.
MIND'S DOOR.16: As Long Door I , except caster can move 300'.
MIND'S DOOR.17: As Long Door Item I , except object may weigh up to 10 pounds/level and may be moved up to 300'.
MIND'S DOOR.18: As Long Door I, except caster can move 500'.
MIND'S DOOR.19: [RR Mod.: -50] As Mind Tongue I, except range is 1 mile.
MIND'S DOOR.20: If caster has established mental contact with a target (an intelligent being) that will cooperate, the caster can teleport to the target’s location or the target can teleport to the caster’s location. There is no chance of error and the movement limit is 1 mile.
MIND'S DOOR.25: As Leaving I, except movement is limited only by the barrier restriction (on a flat plain this would be the horizon).
MIND'S DOOR.30: As Mind’ s Door I, except limit is 10 miles/level.
MIND'S DOOR.35: As Long Door I, except caster can move 2000'.
MIND'S DOOR.40: As Mind’ s Door I, except that there is no distance limit and instead of being able to change their own locations, the caster may hand an item up to 1 pound/level to the target, or the target may hand such an item to the caster.
MIND'S DOOR.50: As Mind’ s Door I, except that there is no distance limit.

MISDIRECTIONS.1: [RR Mod = -20] Target will think that the caster has the proper credentials for the current situation (this could include passwords or documents).
MISDIRECTIONS.2: A single object is made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or the object is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or the object makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or casts a spell of type E or F.
MISDIRECTIONS.3: [RR Mod = -20] Up to three targets will incorrectly identify the caster as someone else (all three will describe the caster in the same fashion).
MISDIRECTIONS.4: [RR Mod = -50] Affects up to three targets. Each target makes an RR. Failure results in the caster being invisible to the target; others see the caster normally. If the caster makes an obvious action (e.g., talking, moving an object, etc.), any target that would normally sense that action may make another RR (the GM may deem it appropriate to apply modifiers to this RR based upon the action the caster takes). If the caster attacks a target, that target may make another RR with a +50 modifier. If the caster begins to concentrate, he can extend the duration of this spell indefinitely (as long as he can concentrate).
MISDIRECTIONS.5: Target’s perception is skewed. All Awareness maneuvers are modified by -10.
MISDIRECTIONS.6: As Unseen I, except affects two objects.
MISDIRECTIONS.7: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to five targets.
MISDIRECTIONS.8: As Eyewitness III, except affects five targets.
MISDIRECTIONS.9: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except every target in the area of effect must make an RR. New targets entering the sphere while the spell is still in effect will need to make an RR or be affected as well. Once a target makes a successful RR, he need not make another RR against the spell, however once affected, leaving the sphere does not cancel the effect.
MISDIRECTIONS.10: As Misleading I, except penalties are -30.
MISDIRECTIONS.11: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to ten targets.
MISDIRECTIONS.12: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.
MISDIRECTIONS.13: As Eyewitness III, except affects ten targets.
MISDIRECTIONS.14: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a twenty foot radius.
MISDIRECTIONS.15: As Misleading I, except penalties are -50.
MISDIRECTIONS.16: As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to twenty targets.
MISDIRECTIONS.17: Creates a false trail with the same physical markings (typically footprints) and scent(s) as the actual trail left by the caster, but Routine (+30) to follow (without appearing to be so). The trail can be laid out as the caster wishes, within the range of the spell. This can be especially effective when combined with some form of teleportation to break the real trail.
MISDIRECTIONS.18: As Eyewitness III, except affects one target per level of the caster.
MISDIRECTIONS.19: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a thirty foot radius.
MISDIRECTIONS.20: As Misleading I, except penalties are -100.
MISDIRECTIONS.25: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 30%.
MISDIRECTIONS.30: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except the caster can affect as many targets as he has levels and the range is only limited to the caster’s field of vision.
MISDIRECTIONS.35: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a hundred foot radius.
MISDIRECTIONS.40: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 40%.
MISDIRECTIONS.50: [RR Mod.: -50] As Eyewitness III, except affects everyone who sees the caster.

MONK'S BRIDGE.1: Allows caster to make one leap up to 50' laterally or 20' vertically (as a separate 1 AP action, with no Pace penalty for the leaping movement) in the round that the spell was cast.
MONK'S BRIDGE.2: This spell may be cast when the caster begins to fall, taking effect immediately, allowing the caster to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall.
MONK'S BRIDGE.3: Caster can walk or run on even, unstable surfaces (sand, ice, etc.) as if on a hard, stable surface.
MONK'S BRIDGE.4: Caster can walk or run on even, narrow (at least 2" wide) surfaces as if on normal ground.
MONK'S BRIDGE.5: As Leaping I, except the caster can execute 3 “leaps” in succession. Each leap must be within 90° of the last leap’s direction. Each leap is a 1 AP action.
MONK'S BRIDGE.6: Allows caster to execute a turn up to 180° with no deceleration or unbalancing (can be used with any “running” spells).
MONK'S BRIDGE.7: Allows caster to move up and down vertically 10' per round. This spell does not confer any ability to control horizontal movement, which must be accomplished by other means.
MONK'S BRIDGE.8: As Landing, except caster can land safely from any fall 99% of the time, and still takes the normal 20'/level off any fall for the 1% of the time that safety is not complete.
MONK'S BRIDGE.9: Caster can walk on solid surfaces up to 90° as if on normal ground.
MONK'S BRIDGE.10: As Leaping I, except limit is 10'/level laterally and 5'/level vertically.
MONK'S BRIDGE.11: As Cornering I, except caster may execute 3 such turns in one round.
MONK'S BRIDGE.12: The caster can leap up to a wall (no more than 10' away), bounce off and land up to 50' (horizontally and/or vertically) from the wall (facing any direction).
MONK'S BRIDGE.13: As Wallwalking, except caster may run.
MONK'S BRIDGE.14: Caster “teleports” to a point up to 100' away, but there can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes a barrier is anything the caster could not physically go through; a closed door is a barrier, a pit is not).
MONK'S BRIDGE.15: Caster can walk or run on air if there is a wind blowing. However, running directly into the wind is not possible, and when running in any other direction, the wind’s speed will modify the caster’s movement rate (i.e., like the effects of wind on a sail boat).
MONK'S BRIDGE.16: Caster can walk on any liquid surface as if on level ground.
MONK'S BRIDGE.17: As Leaping I, except caster may make one leap per round for the duration of the spell.
MONK'S BRIDGE.18: As Leaving I, except range is 300'.
MONK'S BRIDGE.19: As Windrunning, except the caster may run against the wind with a movement rate unaffected by the wind speed.
MONK'S BRIDGE.20: Caster can walk on any solid surface as if on normal ground (includes ceilings).
MONK'S BRIDGE.25: As Ceilingwalking, except caster may run.
MONK'S BRIDGE.30: As Edgerunning, Windrunning True, Liquidrunning, and Ceilingrunning all at once.
MONK'S BRIDGE.35: As Leaving I, except range is 1000'.
MONK'S BRIDGE.40: As Run True, except that the caster may move at any movement pace.
MONK'S BRIDGE.50: Caster can use any one of the lower level spells on this list each round.

MONK'S SENSE.1: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of hearing. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only hearing, +25 to Perception involving hearing and other senses.
MONK'S SENSE.2: Caster can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.
MONK'S SENSE.3: Caster has a 300° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the caster is lowered to +5, and the rear bonus is lowered to +15.
MONK'S SENSE.4: Caster gains extremely acute sense of smell (e.g., +50 to Perception involving only smell, +25 to Perception involving smell and other senses).
MONK'S SENSE.5: Caster can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.
MONK'S SENSE.6: As Nightvision, plus caster sees 100' in any precipitation (including thick fog) as if the air were clear.
MONK'S SENSE.7: Caster gains extreme tactile sensitivity. This provides a bonus to any action requiring a sense of touch. For example, caster might receive +25 to picking locks, disarming traps, opening secret doors, etc. and +50 to Perception involving only touch, +25 to Perception involving touch and other senses).
MONK'S SENSE.8: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.
MONK'S SENSE.9: Detects any invisible objects or beings; one 5'R area can be checked each round. All attacks against detected targets are modified by -50.
MONK'S SENSE.10: Caster can check one object or place (up to a 5'R) and tell if it is an illusion or has an illusion on it.
MONK'S SENSE.11: As Scent, except caster can detect extremely faint scents (e.g., up to 48 hours old, after rainstorms, through snow, etc.).
MONK'S SENSE.12: While this spell is in effect, one illusion within the caster’s range ceases to exist for the caster only. The caster must specify the illusion to be negated (unless caster knows something is an illusion, this spell would be gambling PP).
MONK'S SENSE.13: As Touch, except bonus is +50, or +75 for pure touch Perception.
MONK'S SENSE.14: Caster can see through wood (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.15: Caster can see all invisible things anywhere that would normally be visible to the caster. In addition, the caster suffers no penalties against invisible targets (as they are not invisible to the caster).
MONK'S SENSE.16: Caster can see through any woven organic substance (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.17: As any of the lower level Vision spells on this list but without a range limit (i.e., it will function out to normal limits of sight).
MONK'S SENSE.18: Caster cannot see any visual illusions. This does not affect any of the other senses.
MONK'S SENSE.19: Caster can see through stone (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.20: As all lower level Vision spells functioning at the same time.
MONK'S SENSE.25: Caster can see through iron or steel (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.30: Caster can see through any metal (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.35: Caster gains a +100 bonus to any Perception roll to detect danger.
MONK'S SENSE.40: Caster can see through any one chosen substance (up to 1" per level).
MONK'S SENSE.50: Caster can use any one of the lower level spells (on this list) each round.

MOUNTED WAYS.1: Creates a bond between the caster and an animal, making the animal the caster’s “mount” for the purposes of other spells on this list. It is only possible to have one “mount” at a time. The target of this spell must be a typical mount of the caster’s culture (usually a horse or camel).
MOUNTED WAYS.2: Caster telepathically summons his mount, which will come to him as quickly as possible.
MOUNTED WAYS.3: Caster gains a +5 bonus to his Riding skill.
MOUNTED WAYS.4: The next maneuver by the mount receives a +20 bonus.
MOUNTED WAYS.5: Caster and mount make RRs using the higher of their two levels and the higher of their RR bonuses (e.g., if the mount has the higher level and the caster has the better RR bonus, they get the benefit of both).
MOUNTED WAYS.6: As Riding I, except bonus is +10.
MOUNTED WAYS.7: Mount gains the benefits of AT 4, but without any encumbrance or armor penalties since no armor is actually worn. This provides full suit coverage (i.e., treat the mount as having limb armor and a helm).
MOUNTED WAYS.8: As Select Mount, except that the target may be any rideable animal or other creature that the GM considers a suitable mount (e.g., the GM may wish to allow use of this list with a hippogriff mount).
MOUNTED WAYS.9: As Riding I, except bonus is +15.
MOUNTED WAYS.10: As Call Mount , except for range.
MOUNTED WAYS.11: As Augment Mount I, except bonus is +40.
MOUNTED WAYS.12: As Riding I, except bonus is +20.
MOUNTED WAYS.13: Restores all lost hits and treats any Light injuries (penalties up to -20) suffered by the mount. Recovery time for injuries takes normal time as described in RMCL Chapter 13.
MOUNTED WAYS.14: As Armor Mount I, except AT is 7.
MOUNTED WAYS.15: As One Spirit, except it also has the effect that any spells the caster casts during the duration of this spell that are “self” spells also affect the mount.
MOUNTED WAYS.16: As Riding I, except bonus is +25.
MOUNTED WAYS.17: As Augment Mount I, except bonus is +60.
MOUNTED WAYS.18: As Heal Mount I , except treats Medium injuries (penalties up to -40) as well. Recovery time is as described in RMCL, but convert days to hours.
MOUNTED WAYS.19: As Armor Mount I , except AT is 10.
MOUNTED WAYS.20: As Riding I, except bonus is +30.
MOUNTED WAYS.25: As One Soul, except it also has the effect that any Utility spells cast on the caster of this spell also affect the mount.
MOUNTED WAYS.30: As Augment Mount I , except bonus is +100.
MOUNTED WAYS.35: As Heal Mount I, except treats all injury short of death (will heal fatal damage so long as mount is still alive). Recovery time is as described in RMCL, but convert days to minutes.
MOUNTED WAYS.40: Caster and mount move as one, giving them both a +25 OB to all physical attacks (i.e., not spell) and +10 DB to attacks of which they are aware. This bonus does not apply if one of the duo is no longer fully aware and active (e.g., when one of them is stunned).
MOUNTED WAYS.50: During the duration of this spell, the mount needs no rest, sleep, food, or water. Its hunger and thirst do not grow, nor can it experience fatigue (i.e., it automatically makes any Endurance rolls). Hunger, thirst, and tiredness will begin to increase normally after the end of this spell.

MOVEMENT.1: Allows caster to make one leap up to 50' laterally or 20' vertically (as a separate 1 AP action, with no Pace penalty for the leaping movement) in the round that the spell was cast.
MOVEMENT.2: This spell may be cast when the caster begins to fall, taking effect immediately, allowing the caster to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall.
MOVEMENT.3: Allows caster to move up and down vertically 10' per round. This spell does not confer any ability to control horizontal movement, which must be accomplished by other means.
MOVEMENT.4: Caster becomes nearly weightless (lighter than air), but has no magical movement control; weight gradually returns at the end of the duration.
MOVEMENT.5: Caster can take action underwater as if on land.
MOVEMENT.6: Caster can fly at a rate of 8'/round (approximately 1 mph). ). This spell allows free movement in all three dimensions and allows hovering.
MOVEMENT.7: As Levitation I, except rate is 50'/round.
MOVEMENT.8: Caster can safely dive through the air up to 50' per level so long as there is deep enough water present at the end of the dive (at least 1' of water per 25' of dive).
MOVEMENT.9: As both Leaping and Landing, once each round.
MOVEMENT.10: Caster can merge into any solid, inanimate material. While merged, caster cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the caster emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the caster’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The caster can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the caster automatically emerges at the entrance point).
MOVEMENT.11: As Slow Fly, except rate is 35'/round (approximately 5 mph).
MOVEMENT.12: As Landing, except caster can land safely from any fall 99% of the time, and still takes the normal 20'/level off any fall for the 1% of the time that safety is not complete.
MOVEMENT.13: As Levitation I, except rate is 200'/round.
MOVEMENT.14: Caster may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.
MOVEMENT.15: As Fly I, except rate is 75'/round (approximately 10 mph).
MOVEMENT.16: As Fly I , except duration is 10 minutes/level.
MOVEMENT.17: As Merging, except caster can turn within the material and can perceive the outside world if within 6" of the surface.
MOVEMENT.18: As Levitation I, except rate is 100'/round and caster may remain levitated for an entire day.
MOVEMENT.19: When the caster casts another spell from this list (within one minute of completing this spell), the caster will be able to move three times the normal weight with that spell.
MOVEMENT.20: As Great Merge, except caster can cast spells on himself while merged.
MOVEMENT.25: As Fly I, except rate is 150'/round (approximately 20 mph).
MOVEMENT.30: As Passing, except caster can pass through as much material as the duration allows at a rate of 10'/rnd.
MOVEMENT.35: As Fly I, except rate is 225'/round (approximately 30 mph).
MOVEMENT.40: As Fly True, except duration is 10 minutes/level.
MOVEMENT.50: Caster may cast any of the spells up to 30th level on this list each round.

MOVEMENT MASTERY.1: Caster may move at two times normal walking pace (i.e., Jog pace) as easily as walking. Fatigue and difficulty of maneuvers are as if at Walk pace; spells requiring a “walking pace” may be used (e.g. Shadow Steps), but not those requiring slower than walking pace (e.g., Spider’ s Step). Once the caster stops moving, the spell is canceled.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.2: Caster may jump (from any pace) up to 10' plus 5' per level of the caster horizontally or 5' plus 2' per level of the caster vertically. This spell will allow the caster to always land safely from jumps of this distance or less. The jump itself is a 1 AP action or it may be incorporated into movement at no cost if the caster is moving at a Jog (x2) or greater pace.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.3: This spell may be cast when the caster begins to fall, taking effect immediately, allowing the caster to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall. This landing will also be noiseless.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.4: Allows the caster to cloak all of his movements in complete silence, so long as he moves no faster than a walking pace. This provides a +25 bonus to Stalking maneuvers and negates any penalties for especially noisy surfaces.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.5: Caster can take action underwater as if on land.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.6: Adds +50 to any rolls for any moving maneuvers performed at a walking pace or less (e.g., walking a 3" beam).
MOVEMENT MASTERY.7: Caster can move (at Creep pace) along any solid surface angled up to 90° so long as at least three point contact (e.g., two feet and a hand) is maintained with the surface.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.8: Caster can safely dive through the air up to 50' per level so long as there is deep enough water present at the end of the dive (at least 1' of water per 25' of dive).
MOVEMENT MASTERY.9: Caster can move at a walking pace without leaving tracks or other visible signs of his passing.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.10: As Spider’ s Step, except caster can move along any surface (including ceilings).
MOVEMENT MASTERY.11: Caster can float upon the air. While floating, the caster is at the mercy of the prevailing winds and cannot control any aspect of his flight without access to some other motive force (i.e., pulling along a rope or wall, being towed, etc.).
MOVEMENT MASTERY.12: As Spider’ s Step, except caster may move at a normal walking pace and he needs only to maintain a 2-point contact with the wall.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.13: As Jog, except pace is three times walking pace.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.14: As Soft Landing, except caster can land safely from any fall 99% of the time, and still takes the normal 20'/level off any fall for the 1% of the time that safety is not complete. While falling, the caster gets his full Stalking bonus and adds +25 to that if not in direct sunlight.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.15: Caster can “glide” through the air. The product of caster’s altitude and horizontal speed cannot exceed 100 (with altitude in feet and speed in feet per round). For example, a caster can glide along at a rate of 5' per round if he were 20' off the ground, or he could glide at the rate of 50' per round if he were 2' off the ground. Note that the product must equal 100 (i.e., the caster cannot choose to have a result less than or more than 100). Elevation may be increased up to 5' or decreased up to 10' each round, with the highest elevation being used to calculate the horizontal speed. There is a minimum elevation of 2'.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.16: Caster can walk on calm air. This spell only allows for horizontal movement. Vertical movement must be achieved by other means.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.17: Caster can move at any pace or using any means (e.g., swimming, which would normally be less stealthy due to splashing water) without penalty to any Stalking rolls.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.18: As Jog, except pace is four times walking pace.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.19: As Glide I, except product is 200.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.20: As Spider’ s Walk, except caster can move along any surface (including ceilings).
MOVEMENT MASTERY.25: As Glide I, except product is 300.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.35: As Jog, except pace is five times walking pace.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.40: As Glide I, except product is 500.
MOVEMENT MASTERY.50: Caster may use any lower level spell from this list once per round.

MOVING WAYS.1: Caster can walk or run on any nearly horizontal rocky or earthen surfaces as if on level ground. Running using this spell is no more fatiguing than walking.
MOVING WAYS.2: Caster can walk along tree limbs as if on level ground.
MOVING WAYS.3: Target may swim at a “walking” pace without any chance of fatigue, using the normal swimming rules (RMCL Sections 5.3 & 3.17). This will allow someone without Swimming skill to tread water in place and avoid drowning without the need for a maneuver.
MOVING WAYS.4: Caster can walk on water as if on level dry ground. Water need not be perfectly still, but must be fairly calm.
MOVING WAYS.5: Caster can walk or run on any sandy surfaces as if on level solid ground. Running using this spell is no more fatiguing than walking.
MOVING WAYS.6: Caster can merge into any organic material (alive or dead). While merged caster cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the caster emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the caster’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The caster can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the caster automatically emerges at the entrance point).
MOVING WAYS.7: Caster can walk without leaving tracks or other visible signs of his passing.
MOVING WAYS.8: As Limbwalking, except caster can move at a running pace (three times walking speed), without it being any more fatiguing than walking.
MOVING WAYS.9: As Waterwalking, except caster can move at a running pace (three times walking speed), without it being any more fatiguing than walking.
MOVING WAYS.10: As Traceless Passing, except caster can hide the tracks of any 2 beings (not necessarily including the caster).
MOVING WAYS.11: As Swimming, except caster may swim at up to a “Dash” pace with no chance of fatigue.
MOVING WAYS.12: As Merging Organic, except caster can turn within the material and can perceive the outside world if within 6" of the surface.
MOVING WAYS.13: As Hide Tracks I , except it hides 5 beings’ tracks.
MOVING WAYS.14: As the Running spells above, except any one of the spell effects can be used as needed (e.g., the caster could run on sand, then on water, then on limbs, etc.).
MOVING WAYS.15: As Traceless Passing, except the duration is 10 minutes per level.
MOVING WAYS.16: As Hide Tracks I, except it hides 10 beings’ tracks.
MOVING WAYS.17: As Traceless Passing, except caster can move at three times normal pace (i.e., at a Run pace) with no more fatigue than walking.
MOVING WAYS.18: As Great Merging Organic, except caster may use self-targeted spells while merged.
MOVING WAYS.19: As Traceless Passing True, except caster can move at three times normal pace (i.e., at a Run pace) with no more fatigue than walking.
MOVING WAYS.20: As Hide Tracks I, except it hides 20 beings’ tracks.
MOVING WAYS.25: As Running True, except caster can move at Sprint speed (four times walking pace) with it being no more fatiguing than walking.
MOVING WAYS.30: As Running True, except caster can affect one target per level (one target must be the caster).
MOVING WAYS.35: As Running True, except caster can run with no chance of fatigue.
MOVING WAYS.40: As Hide Tracks I, except caster can either absolutely hide his own tracks (preventing discovery via any spell, etc.) or he can hide the tracks of a number of beings equal to his level as long as they remain within 100'.
MOVING WAYS.50: Caster can swim a mile per minute without fatigue and has a +100 bonus to any Swimming maneuvers.

MUSCLE LAW.1: Target receives a +10 bonus to all Jumping and Climbing maneuvers.
MUSCLE LAW.2: An injury to muscle or tendons incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
MUSCLE LAW.3: Target is not fatigued by moving at a walk or jog pace.
MUSCLE LAW.4: As Light Muscle Relief , except injuries incurring a penalty up to -40.
MUSCLE LAW.5: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Blood, Bone, and Nerve Limb Preservation spells as well.
MUSCLE LAW.6: Target gains a +5 Strength bonus, granting +5 to most skills involving strength (+10 melee OB).
MUSCLE LAW.7: As Light Muscle Relief, except injuries incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE LAW.8: Target is not fatigued by moving on the ground or in water up to a run pace.
MUSCLE LAW.9: An injury to muscle or tendons incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
MUSCLE LAW.10: Target drops anything currently held and takes a -20 to any maneuver attempted this round.
MUSCLE LAW.11: As Severe Muscle relief, except it treats every muscle and tendon injury in the target.
MUSCLE LAW.12: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spell from the Bone, Blood, and Nerve spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
MUSCLE LAW.13: A random limb is in intense pain and the joint locks, rendering the limb unusable. A moving bipedal target afflicted with a leg lock will fall down; a target with an arm lock will drop anything in that hand.
MUSCLE LAW.14: As Light Muscle Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
MUSCLE LAW.15: Target regenerates any one tendon. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE LAW.16: Target will not accumulate any additional fatigue penalties for the duration.
MUSCLE LAW.17: As Light Muscle Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE LAW.18: Target regenerates any one muscle (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE LAW.19: As Strength I, except the bonus is +15 (resulting in +30 for melee OB).
MUSCLE LAW.20: As Light Muscle Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE LAW.25: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
MUSCLE LAW.30: As Strength I, except the bonus is +20 (resulting in +40 for melee OB).
MUSCLE LAW.35: As Light Muscle Healing True, except an injury incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE LAW.40: Target regenerates all tendons and muscles in his body (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE LAW.50: All injuries to muscle or tendons are cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to rounds rather than days.

MUSCLE MASTERY.1: Target receives a +10 bonus to all Jumping and Climbing maneuvers.
MUSCLE MASTERY.2: An injury to muscle or tendons incurring a penalty up to -40 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
MUSCLE MASTERY.3: As Medium Muscle Relief, except injuries incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE MASTERY.4: Target gains a +5 Strength bonus, granting +5 to most skills involving strength (+10 melee OB).
MUSCLE MASTERY.5: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Bone Mastery Limb Preservation spell as well.
MUSCLE MASTERY.6: Target is not fatigued by moving on the ground or in water at a walk or run pace.
MUSCLE MASTERY.7: An injury to muscle or tendons incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
MUSCLE MASTERY.8: Target drops anything currently held and takes a -20 to any maneuver attempted this round.
MUSCLE MASTERY.9: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spells from the Bone, Blood, and Nerve and Organ Mastery spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
MUSCLE MASTERY.10: As L ight Muscle Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
MUSCLE MASTERY.11: A random limb is in intense pain and the joint locks, rendering the limb unusable. A moving target afflicted with a leg lock will fall down; a target with an arm lock will drop anything in that hand.
MUSCLE MASTERY.12: As Light Muscle Healing , except an injury incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE MASTERY.13: As Strength I, except the bonus is +10 (resulting in +20 for melee OB).
MUSCLE MASTERY.14: As Light Muscle Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE MASTERY.15: Target will not accumulate any additional fatigue penalties for the duration.
MUSCLE MASTERY.16: As Light Muscle Healing True, except injuries incurring penalties up to -40.
MUSCLE MASTERY.17: Target regenerates any one tendon or muscle (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE MASTERY.18: As Strength I, except the bonus is +15 (resulting in +30 for melee OB).
MUSCLE MASTERY.19: As Light Muscle Healing True, except an injury incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE MASTERY.20: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
MUSCLE MASTERY.25: As Strength I, except the bonus is +20 (resulting in +40 for melee OB).
MUSCLE MASTERY.30: As Severe Muscle Healing , except all of the muscle and tendon injuries sustained by the target.
MUSCLE MASTERY.35: Target regenerates all tendons and muscles (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE MASTERY.40: All injuries to muscle or tendons are cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to rounds rather than days.
MUSCLE MASTERY.50: Caster can regrow 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the Regrow Limb spells from Bone, Blood, and Nerve and Organ Mastery for the limb to become fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

MUSCLE WAYS.1: An injury to tendons incurring any penalty is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
MUSCLE WAYS.2: As Severe Tendon Relief, except an injury to a muscle.
MUSCLE WAYS.3: An injury to tendons incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
MUSCLE WAYS.4: As Light Tendon Healing , except an injury to a muscle incurring a penalty up to -20.
MUSCLE WAYS.5: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a tendon incurring a penalty up to -40.
MUSCLE WAYS.6: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a muscle incurring a penalty up to -40.
MUSCLE WAYS.7: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
MUSCLE WAYS.8: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a tendon incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE WAYS.9: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a muscle incurring any penalty.
MUSCLE WAYS.10: As Light Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.11: As Light Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.12: Caster can regenerate any 1 tendon. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE WAYS.13: As Medium Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.14: As Medium Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.15: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.
MUSCLE WAYS.16: Caster can regenerate any one muscle (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE WAYS.17: As Severe Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.18: As Severe Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.19: As Severe Tendon Healing , except it affects all tendon injuries.
MUSCLE WAYS.20: As Severe Muscular Healing, except it affects all muscle injuries.
MUSCLE WAYS.25: Caster can regenerate all of the muscles and/or tendons in his body (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
MUSCLE WAYS.30: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
MUSCLE WAYS.35: As Mass Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.40: As Mass Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
MUSCLE WAYS.50: Caster can repair or regenerate a number of damaged muscles and/or tendons equal to his level. Recovery is instantaneous.

MYSTICAL CHANGE.1: Caster studies and memorizes the appearance and mannerisms of a being, for later use with higher level spells on this list. The caster must observe the target for five minutes after casting the spell in order to complete the study. The caster can only have as many forms studied as he has ranks in this list. Caster may choose to forget a studied form at any time to make room for replacements.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.2: Allows caster to alter the form of his face. He can take on the exact face of another being if he has used Study Individual on the being he wishes to copy.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.3: Caster can alter his own form to the form of any desired humanoid race within his own Size category. The form can be that of a particular individual that has been the target of Study Individual; this does not include mannerisms, but only appearance. Note that only the physical form is gained (i.e., a target that becomes a Dwarf would not receive a Dwarf’s ability to see in the dark). Non-magical clothing will refit itself to the new form, but other equipment will not change, with the exception of prosthetics added by means of the Lay Healer Base list Prosthetics.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.4: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, caster appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.5: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster fools a magic item into reacting to the caster as if the caster were of another race, sex, Profession, religion, etc. in order to make use (or better use) of the item. This does not allow the caster to use a PP multiplier or Spell Adder for a realm he does not actually use, although it will allow use of a PP multiplier that works for only one of his realms.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.6: As Minor Changing, except form need not be humanoid, but must be a living organic thing.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.7: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s profession may be misrepresented.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.8: Caster can breathe water, air, or any gas at will.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.9: As Minor Changing, except form may be one Size category smaller than the caster’s.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.10: [RR Mod: -50] Caster can speak mentally with any sentient target, and to the target, it will seem as if the caster was speaking in the target’s primary language.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.11: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s level may be misrepresented (to an upper limit of twice the caster’s actual level).
MYSTICAL CHANGE.12: As Major Changing, except a specific being who has been the target of Study Individual can be duplicated in both appearance and mannerisms.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.13: Caster becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.14: Allows caster to use all of the “Misfeel” spells at once.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.15: As Major Changing, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.16: As Minor Changing, except form may be one Size category larger than the caster’s.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.17: As Impersonation, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.18: As Major Changing , except for duration.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.20: As Unpresence Self, except caster does not have to concentrate.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.25: As Misfeel, except it has a ten minute/level duration and does not require concentration.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.30: As Misfeel, except caster can misrepresent his presence and power, so that he seems to be a minor deity. Even those viewing with normal senses will be given an impression of superhuman power and majesty.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.35: Caster can alter his own form to the form of any desired living thing. The form can be that of a particular individual that has been the target of Study Individual, in which case the caster may have the mannerisms of that individual as well. The caster gains all natural abilities of the form taken, but not magical abilities. Non-magical clothing will change form to fit the caster’s new body, or may join the rest of items worn or carried, which merge into the caster’s body for the duration of the spell. If the caster dies, the spell immediately ends.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.40: As Shapechanging, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.
MYSTICAL CHANGE.50: Caster develops an entire false persona and places his own persona deep in his subconscious. To all detection attempts (except, perhaps, Mind Probe), the caster seems to be the false persona. The caster has no control over his actions (except for having designed the persona now in control), until the spell ends. The false persona can be a specific individual if Study Individual has been cast once/day (for 30 days) on that individual. The caster must determine a set period after which the spell will end and may also specify one triggering event that can cancel the spell before that period expires. The caster will remember everything that happened while the false persona was in control.

NATURE'S GUISES.1: Allows caster to take on the physical coloration of any 1 organic object. The caster must be in contact with the object when the spell is cast. In many situations, this will give a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking attempts.
NATURE'S GUISES.2: All shadows and darkness within the area of effect deepen, giving all Perception maneuvers involving seeing anything hidden/hiding in the area a penalty of -20. Area is not mobile.
NATURE'S GUISES.3: Caster’s odor becomes greatly reduced, providing a -50 penalty for the caster to be detected or tracked using the sense of smell.
NATURE'S GUISES.4: Caster can move silently, so long as he does not create a sound originating more than 1' from his body. This typically provides a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking maneuvers.
NATURE'S GUISES.5: Caster blends into surrounding terrain, providing a +75 bonus to Stalking attempts. Caster may not move (appreciably) without destroying this effect. This bonus is not cumulative with Hues or Shadow.
NATURE'S GUISES.6: Caster and objects on his person appear to be a shadow and are almost invisible in dark areas (e.g., in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus between +25 and +75).
NATURE'S GUISES.7: Caster studies and memorizes the form of one type of animal or plant for use with spells later on this list. The caster can only have as many forms of each kingdom studied as he has ranks in this list. Thus, a Ranger with 8 ranks in Nature’s Guises may have studied 8 types of animal and 8 types of plant. Caster may choose to forget a studied form at any time to make room for replacements.
NATURE'S GUISES.8: Allows caster to appear as any one type of plant that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes no physical change.
NATURE'S GUISES.9: For the duration of this spell, the caster’s mental patterns will appear to be those of any animal he has studied. The caster cannot move while this spell is active.
NATURE'S GUISES.10: As Study Form, except a particular animal or plant form may be studied. For example, the woodsman’s dog (as opposed to just a dog) may be studied. The caster may only have as many of these particular forms studied as he has ranks in this list. These are in addition to the general forms learned by Study Form.
NATURE'S GUISES.11: Allows caster to appear as any one type of animal that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes no physical change.
NATURE'S GUISES.12: Caster momentarily takes on a fearsome appearance that requires all within a 20' radius to resist versus Fear (attack level is that of the caster) or flee for 1 round per 5 fail. Targets must be able to see the caster in order to be affected.
NATURE'S GUISES.13: As Animal Thoughts I, except caster can move (though he must still maintain concentration).
NATURE'S GUISES.14: Caster appears to be friendly and pleasant so long as he does not make any overtly hostile action. This adds +20 to his social interactions with all living things.
NATURE'S GUISES.15: Allows caster to take the form of any one plant he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the plant. The caster’s size can change by up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the plant other than Size category.
NATURE'S GUISES.17: Allows caster to take the form of any one animal he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the animal. The caster’s size can be varied up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the animal, other than Size category.
NATURE'S GUISES.18: As Terrible Form, except dreadful appearance lasts for 1 round per level, during which any person in the radius of effect must make an RR against Fear each round (with a +10 bonus for each RR after the first) or flee for 1 round per 5 fail. Targets must be able to see the caster in order to be affected. Those who flee in terror will afterwards not be able to recollect clearly what the caster looked like.
NATURE'S GUISES.19: As Plant Form I, except caster may also assume the scent and physical abilities of the plant.
NATURE'S GUISES.20: As Animal Form I , except caster may also assume the movement abilities of the animal.
NATURE'S GUISES.25: As Animal Form II, except caster also gains the physical attacks of the animal. Caster can vary his size up to two Size increments. Caster gains normal OB of the animal with its natural attacks, gaining the instincts to use them properly.
NATURE'S GUISES.30: As Plant Form II, except caster can change his form to any other plant he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and he can vary his size by up to 2 size increments.
NATURE'S GUISES.35: As Animal Thoughts II, except caster does not need to concentrate, and duration is 1 minute per level.
NATURE'S GUISES.40: As Animal Form III , except caster can change his form to any other animal he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and caster has all physical abilities of the animal.
NATURE'S GUISES.50: As Plant Form True and Animal Form True operating at the same time.

NATURE'S LAW.1: Caster learns nature, value, and history of one animal.
NATURE'S LAW.2: Caster learns nature, value, and history of one plant (that is not a magical herb).
NATURE'S LAW.3: Caster can understand the nature, origin, value, and use of any magical herb. If the plant is not a magical herb, no information will be related.
NATURE'S LAW.4: Caster learns nature, value, and history of one fungus.
NATURE'S LAW.5: Caster learns the nature, history, and origin of any one stone.
NATURE'S LAW.6: Caster can increase speed of growth for any one species of plant within a 10' radius by ten times normal growth rate.
NATURE'S LAW.7: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any one animal species.
NATURE'S LAW.8: Caster learns the nature, value, origin, and eventual destination or outflow of one body of fresh water.
NATURE'S LAW.9: Allows caster to control the actions of any 1 animal.
NATURE'S LAW.10: Allows caster to monitor animate activity in the area (e.g., the caster will be aware of movements, subtle and overt). Caster cannot move while concentrating. Caster does not receive a picture of what is moving, just an awareness of the movement. This will allow distinguishing general size and shape of a moving object. Someone staying still, but breathing, will be detected, but only a very general idea of size would be determinable.
NATURE'S LAW.11: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of one plant species.
NATURE'S LAW.12: As Animal Mastery I , except caster can control up to three animals.
NATURE'S LAW.13: Caster can understand and/or visualize the thoughts and emotions of any one animal.
NATURE'S LAW.14: Caster can control the automatic and/or mental processes of any one plant. Caster can also control the plant’s movements; this does not allow movement beyond normal allowances. If cast daily, this can be used to direct a plant’s growth into specific unusual shapes, so long as these would be naturally possible.
NATURE'S LAW.15: Caster can communicate with any one stone if it possesses any mental qualities (e.g., caster could speak to a holy stone which possesses some sort of intelligence).
NATURE'S LAW.16: Caster can grow an herb to sudden maturity by planting the proper seed; the herb is sterile and the growth takes 1-10 rnds.
NATURE'S LAW.17: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of one species of fungus.
NATURE'S LAW.18: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control up to five animals.
NATURE'S LAW.19: As Plant Control I , except caster can control any three plants.
NATURE'S LAW.20: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except that the radius is 500'.
NATURE'S LAW.25: Caster can understand and/or visualize the thoughts and emotions of any one animal, plant, fungus, stone, or dead organic object. The caster can change target each round.
NATURE'S LAW.30: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control all animals of a given species within 10'R per level.
NATURE'S LAW.35: Caster can control the actions of up to five fungal life forms.
NATURE'S LAW.40: Caster is made aware of the ecological health of the area and anything that is causing significant ecological impact on the area (e.g., the water from the magic spring makes everything here grow fast, poison is leaching out from the pile of mine tailings, or the unicorns prevent disease from spreading through the wildlife).
NATURE'S LAW.50: As Animal Mastery I , except duration is permanent and concentration is not required. At a given time, the total number of levels of animals controlled by caster through use of this spell cannot exceed the caster’s own level.

NATURE'S LORE.1: Caster has a 75% chance of detecting each individual trap in a 5'R. Caster can concentrate on a 5'R area each round. There must be plants or animals in the area of effect or no information will be gained. Spiders and insects are animals, so many indoor environments will still allow the use of this spell. If a trap is missed, the spell will not find it no matter how many times the caster scans the same area.
NATURE'S LORE.2: Allows caster to monitor animate activity in the area (e.g., the caster will be aware of movements, subtle and overt). Caster cannot move while concentrating. Caster does not receive a picture of what is moving, just an awareness of the movement. This will allow distinguishing general size and shape of a moving object. Someone staying still, but breathing, will be detected, but only a very general idea of size would be determinable.
NATURE'S LORE.3: Caster learns the location(s) of any “path(s)” within 200'. The spell gives the nearest point on the path, but not the path’s course. (See Section 10.16 for information on interpretation of “path”.)
NATURE'S LORE.4: Detects any active spell or active magical item. Caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round. (See Section 10.4 for more about detection spells.)
NATURE'S LORE.5: Caster has a 95% chance of correctly predicting time, type, and severity of weather over the next 24 hr period.
NATURE'S LORE.6: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except that the radius is 200'.
NATURE'S LORE.7: As Weather Prediction I, except period is 3 days.
NATURE'S LORE.8: For the duration of this spell, the caster will know the direction and distance to any unique natural feature that the caster is familiar with. The feature must be within range or no information is gained.
NATURE'S LORE.9: Allows caster to set up a natural “alarm system” in the area of effect; any disturbing activity will cause caster to be alerted. This alert will wake the caster, if he is in a natural sleep.
NATURE'S LORE.10: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except that radius is 300'.
NATURE'S LORE.11: As Weather Prediction I, except period is 5 days.
NATURE'S LORE.12: Caster can remember an exact route that the caster has previously traveled regardless of whether or not the original journey was made with the caster’s normal sensory abilities active (e.g., a 12th level caster could remember the course of 12 hours of a trip even if blindfolded during that time). To be effective, this spell must be cast within one month of the journey.
NATURE'S LORE.13: As Waiting Awareness I, except radius is 100'.
NATURE'S LORE.14: As Locate Landmark I, except for range.
NATURE'S LORE.15: Allows caster to visualize and sense activity in a known fixed area (100'R).
NATURE'S LORE.16: As Nature Awareness I, except radius is 500'.
NATURE'S LORE.17: Caster can communicate with any elemental force within range. The elemental force must be able to communicate (i.e., this spell does not confer communication abilities upon the force, just translation of those it does have). Elemental forces include all elementals, water spirits, forest spirits, etc.
NATURE'S LORE.18: The target of this spell is a small pool of water. The water must be calm (and remain calm for the duration of the spell). The caster may designate a topic and a vision will appear in the pool that relates to the topic. The vision will take 1-50 rounds.
NATURE'S LORE.19: As Locate Landmark I , except for range.
NATURE'S LORE.20: As Nature’ s Eye I, except the range is 10 miles and caster can monitor an area of 500'R.
NATURE'S LORE.25: As Weather Prediction I , except period is 1 day/level.
NATURE'S LORE.30: As Nature’ s Eye I, except range is 100 miles and caster can monitor an area of 500'R.
NATURE'S LORE.35: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except radius is 100'/level.
NATURE'S LORE.40: Caster is made aware of the ecological health of the area and anything that is causing significant ecological impact on the area (e.g., the water from the magic spring makes everything here grow fast, poison is leaching out from the pile of mine tailings, or the unicorns prevent disease from spreading through the wildlife).
NATURE'S LORE.50: Allows caster to monitor an area of 100'R that the caster has both been to and become acquainted with (i.e., spent at least 1 day meditating in or 30 days living in).

NATURE'S PROTECTION.1: Caster studies and memorizes the form of one type of animal or plant for use with spells later on this list. The caster can only have as many forms of each kingdom studied as he has ranks in this list. Thus, a Druid with 8 ranks in Nature’s Protection may have studied 8 types of animal and 8 types of plant. Caster may choose to forget a studied form at any time to make room for replacements.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.2: Protects caster from natural heat up to 176°F (80°C) and natural cold down to 14°F (-10°C), experiencing either as a 68°F (20°C) environment. For temperatures beyond the protected ranges, treat the caster as being in an environment of 20°C modified by one degree per degree beyond the protection limit (e.g., if the temperature were 180°F, the caster would experience it as 72°F). Against cold/ice or heat/fire attacks, the caster gains +20 to DB and RRs. Duration is 1 hour per level if the caster does not move, but if the caster moves, then duration becomes Concentration.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.3: Allows caster to take on the physical coloration of any 1 organic object. The caster must be in contact with the object when the spell is cast. In many situations, this will give a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking attempts.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.4: Allows caster to appear as any one type of plant that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes no physical change.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.5: Caster can move silently, so long as he does not create a sound originating more than 1' from his body. This typically provides a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking maneuvers when moving.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.6: Caster can breathe normally under water. Water may be salt or fresh, but no other liquid is breathable by means of this spell.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.7: Allows caster to appear as any one type of animal that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes no physical change.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.8: Caster and objects on his person appear to be a shadow and are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus between +25 and +75).
NATURE'S PROTECTION.9: Any single attack made against the caster this round suffers a -50 modifier. The weapon or object defended against must be from an organic source (e.g., an animal) or at least a partially organic weapon (e.g., an arrow) and the caster must be able to see the attack.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.10: Allows caster to take the form of any one plant he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the plant. The caster’s size can change by up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the plant other than Size category.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.11: As Resist Elements, except it protects caster from any natural heat or natural cold (treat as if caster were in 68°F [20°C] environment), and DB/RR bonus is +40.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.12: For the duration of this spell, the caster’s mental patterns will appear to be those of any animal he has studied. The caster cannot move while this spell is active.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.13: As Organic Defense I, except the penalty is -100.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.14: Allows caster to take the form of any one animal he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the animal. The caster’s size can be varied up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the animal, other than Size category.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.15: As Animal Thoughts I, except caster can move (though he must still maintain concentration).
NATURE'S PROTECTION.16: As Plant Form I, except caster may also assume the scent and physical abilities of the plant.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.17: As Animal Form I , except caster may also assume the movement abilities of the animal.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.18: As Organic Defense I , except attack automatically misses.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.19: As Animal Form II, except caster also gains the physical attacks of the animal. Caster can vary his size up to two Size increments. Caster gains normal OB of the animal with its natural attacks, gaining the instincts to use them properly.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.20: As Plant Form II, except caster can change his form to any other plant he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and he can vary his size by up to 2 size increments.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.25: As Animal Form III , except caster can change his form to any other animal he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and caster has all physical abilities of the animal.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.30: As Plant Form True and Animal Form True operating at the same time.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.35: As Animal Thoughts II, except caster does not need to concentrate, and duration is 1 minute per level.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.40: As Nature’ s Form True, except for duration.
NATURE'S PROTECTION.50: As Organic Defense III, except one attack may be defended against each round the spell is in effect.

NATURE'S WRATH.1: The caster’s next attack gains a +5 to its OB. If the caster is attacking in animal form, this bonus is doubled.
NATURE'S WRATH.2: Target will find that animals, both wild and domestic have become wary of him. All maneuvers dealing with handling, influencing, or working with animals suffer a penalty of -25.
NATURE'S WRATH.3: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Small Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (grasses, flowers, or larger plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +0.
NATURE'S WRATH.4: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.
NATURE'S WRATH.5: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +10.
NATURE'S WRATH.6: Creates an opaque wall of fire (up to 10'x10'x6"). Anyone passing through it takes an A Heat critical (no RR). See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
NATURE'S WRATH.7: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +15.
NATURE'S WRATH.8: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Medium Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (vines, tall stalks such as corn/maize, small bushes such as roses, or larger plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +5.
NATURE'S WRATH.9: The caster’s next attack (including spells) does double concussion hit damage.
NATURE'S WRATH.10: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
NATURE'S WRATH.11: Winds gust about the target, always blowing from the direction in which he tries to move and anyone else in the radius will share this effect (no RR). This will prevent a sailed vessel from moving, reduce normal movement by two Paces (e.g., target may no longer Walk, but must at least use a Jog pace, which will produce a Creep). In addition, the target (but not others in the radius) will have a special -20 penalty to Endurance rolls due to moving. Any loose items will be blown away. Any missile fire into or out of the area affected will suffer a -80 modification to OB.
NATURE'S WRATH.12: As Wall of Fire, except “wall” is 10' high and forms a 10'R circle (6" thick) with the caster at the center (it is non-mobile).
NATURE'S WRATH.13: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +25.
NATURE'S WRATH.14: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Big Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (large bushes, mature ivy, or larger plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +10.
NATURE'S WRATH.15: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
NATURE'S WRATH.16: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +30.
NATURE'S WRATH.17: The area of effect is pelted with heavy hail. Treat as an Ice Bolt attack on everyone in the area of effect with an OB equal to half the caster’s skill in this list and with no range modification. Fumble results on the Ice Bolt attack are ignored.
NATURE'S WRATH.18: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Large Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (generally trees or plentiful vines). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +15.
NATURE'S WRATH.19: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +35.
NATURE'S WRATH.20: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; the results are determined on the Lighting Bolt Table as a Medium sized attack.
NATURE'S WRATH.25: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Huge Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (thick growth including large plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +25.
NATURE'S WRATH.30: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.
NATURE'S WRATH.35: As Nature’ s Ire I, except bonus is +50 and attack does double concussion hit damage.
NATURE'S WRATH.40: As Hailstorm, except for range, radius, and the OB is equal to the caster’s skill in this list.
NATURE'S WRATH.50: Caster can cause an earthquake; with the caster’s location at the time of casting as the epicenter. The quake can be delayed up to 1 round/level. The severity of the quake on the Richter scale is determined by a roll: (01-20) = 5.5, (21-45) = 6, (46-65) = 6.5, (66-80) = 7.0, (81-90) = 7.5, (91-95) = 8.0, (96-98) = 8.5, (99-100) = 9.0.

NECROMANCY MASTERY.1: Allows the caster to understand and speak the language(s) of any undead beings.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.2: Caster is not subject to automatic harmful effects caused by the proximity of the undead. This provides no protection against deliberate attack.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.3: Caster becomes aware of all undead within the area of effect. He knows their general locations (within 10'), number, and general nature (whether corporeal or incorporeal, mindless or intelligent, controlled or uncontrolled, and what Class of undead).
NECROMANCY MASTERY.4: Caster can control one Class I undead. If this spell is resisted, the target will immediately attack the caster, preferring him over any other available targets.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.5: Caster has a +10 bonus to RRs and DB against attacks by the undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.6: Using a suitable corpse or spirit, the caster creates a Class I undead. Corpses must be a condition suitable for the type of undead to be created and in any case, the being to be converted into one of the undead may not have been dead any longer than one day per level of the caster. If the caster succeeds in a Hard Spell Mastery maneuver with an additional -10 penalty times the Class number of the undead created, then the caster may select what type of undead is created by this spell. If this maneuver fails, the GM selects an appropriate undead creature. Upon creation, the undead must make an RR against its creator. If it fails, it will be non-hostile to its creator until some circumstance (such as a failed attempt to Control it) should change things. If it succeeds, it is free to attack its maker if uncontrolled.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.7: Target undead will obey a command by the caster for a period of one day. At the end of that time, the undead returns to its normal behavior. If this spell is resisted, the target will immediately attack the caster, preferring him over any other available targets.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.8: As Control Undead I, except caster can control a Class II undead or control a Class I undead with a -10 to its RR.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.9: This spell must be cast within 100' of the place of death of the being whose spirit is to be turned into an incorporeal form of undead. This spell will fail if the body is protected against being turned into one of the undead (e.g., under the protection of a Restful Spirit spell). The spirit summoned by this spell is suitable for creating an undead being of up to Class III. A Create Undead spell must be successful before the end of this spell’s duration, or the spirit will depart once more. Spirit may not have been dead for more than 1 day/level of caster.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.10: As Create Undead I, except creates a Class II or lesser undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.11: Caster has a +20 bonus to RRs and DB against attacks by the undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.12: As Control Undead I , except caster can control a Class III undead or control a Class II undead with a -10 to its RR or control a Class I undead with a -20 to its RR.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.13: As Command Undead I , except duration is 1 month.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.14: As Create Undead I, except creates a Class III or lesser undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.15: As Summon Lesser Spirit , except the spirit summoned is suitable for the creation of undead of up to Class VI and may have been dead up to 1 week/level of caster.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.16: As Control Undead I , except caster can control a Class IV undead or control a Class III undead with a -10 to its RR or control a Class II undead with a -20 to its RR or control a Class I undead with a -30 to its RR.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.17: Caster has a +30 bonus to RRs and DB against attacks by the undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.18: As Command Undead I, except duration is 1 year.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.19: As Create Undead I, except creates a Class IV or lesser undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.20: As Control Undead I , except caster can control a Class V undead or control a Class IV undead with a -10 to its RR or control a Class III undead with a -20 to its RR or control a Class II undead with a -30 to its RR or control a Class I undead with a -40 to its RR.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.25: As Control Undead I , except caster can control a Class VI undead or control a Class V undead with a -10 to its RR or control a Class IV undead with a -20 to its RR or control a Class III undead with a -30 to its RR or control a Class II undead with a -40 to its RR or control a Class I undead with a -50 to its RR.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.30: As Create Undead I, except creates a Class V or lesser undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.35: Caster can control undead up to his level in total of the undeads’ Class numbers. The RR of an undead may be reduced by -10 by assigning extra levels to its control up to a maximum penalty of -50. For example, a 38th level caster really wants to get control of the Class VI Greater Vampire leading the undead marauders, so he assigns an extra five levels on top of the six minimally required in order to create a -50 RR penalty. This leaves the caster with 27 levels left to assign to the control of other undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.40: As Create Undead I, except creates a Class VI or lesser undead.
NECROMANCY MASTERY.50: Places target undead under the caster’s lasting control. The caster need but command the mastered undead, and it will obey to the best of its ability. Undead created by the caster get a -25 to their RRs. The caster can never have more levels of undead mastered than he has levels, although the undead mastered by means of the Necromantic Ways list do not count against the limit mastered by this list and vice versa (i.e., a 60th level caster could have 60 levels of mindless undead mastered by means of Master Mindless Undead and another 60 levels of undead mastered by means of Undead Mastery).

NECROMANTIC WAYS.1: Caster can cause any dead body (within 50% of his own mass), to rise up and move. The caster must concentrate for the body to perform an action; if he stops concentrating, the body will stand motionless. The body can be maneuvered with a -25 modifier and can melee with a -20 OB. The body cannot have been dead more than 1 day. Use the stats from Creature Law for Class I Standard Created Undead except that the OB is -20 and it cannot move faster than a walk.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.2: Caster can control one Class I mindless undead. When the caster does not concentrate, the undead will stay in place and attack only if a living target is within 10'.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.3: [RR Mod.: -25] Caster may master 1 “Class” worth of mindless undead per level of the caster. This spell must be cast separately on each undead. The target undead must already be controlled by the caster and if the target resists this spell, it will attack the caster immediately and may not be subject to the caster’s control again until the caster gains another level. A mastered undead allows the caster to control this specific undead without concentration (it will continue to follow the last order it was given).
NECROMANTIC WAYS.4: As Animate Dead I, except caster can control 2 bodies at once or one body with a -15 modifier to maneuvers and an OB of -10. Additionally, bodies may have been dead for up to 2 days.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.5: Given a body that has been dead less than 1 week, the caster can turn the body into a Class I undead. The newly-created undead will attempt to attack the closest living being (if uncontrolled), but can take no other activity than moving to that being and attacking. If controlled, the undead will do as commanded by the controller.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.6: As Control Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class II mindless undead or 2 Class I undead can be controlled.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.7: As Animate Dead I, except caster can control 3 bodies at once or one body with a -5 modifier to maneuvers and an OB of +0. Additionally, bodies may have been dead for up to 3 days.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.8: As Create Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class II undead or 2 Class I undead can be created.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.9: As Control Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class III mindless undead or 1 Class II and 1 Class I undead or 3 Class I undead can be controlled.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.10: As Animate Dead I , except caster can control 4 bodies at once or one body with a +5 modifier to maneuvers and an OB of +10. Additionally, bodies may have been dead for up to 4 days.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.11: As Create Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class III undead or 1 Class II and 1 Class I or 3 Class I undead can be created.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.12: As Animate Dead I, except caster can control 5 bodies at once or one body with a +10 modifier to maneuvers and an OB of +20. Additionally, bodies may have been dead for up to 5 days.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.13: As Control Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class IV mindless undead or 1 Class III and 1 Class I undead or 2 Class II undead or 1 Class II and 2 Class I undead or 4 Class I undead can be controlled.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.14: As Animate Dead I , except caster can control 6 bodies at once or one body with a +15 modifier to maneuvers and an OB of +30. Additionally, bodies may have been dead for up to 6 days.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.15: As Create Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class IV undead or 1 Class III and 1 Class I or 2 Class II or 1 Class II and 2 Class I or 4 Class I undead can be created.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.16: As Control Mindless Undead I , except 1 Class V mindless undead or 5 Class I undead or any other combination where the total Class numbers of the undead is five can be controlled.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.17: Caster fills a corpse with extra Essence. If the corpse becomes an undead creature within the duration of the spell, its level is determined using the Level Spread Table (Table 3-2) in Creature Law normally, but with a bonus to the roll equal to the caster’s ranks in this list. A caster may also cast this spell on himself prior to using Cheat Death — normally the conversion into a Lich results in a reduction in level, so every bit helps.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.18: As Create Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class V mindless undead or 5 Class I undead or any other combination where the total Class numbers of the undead is five can be created.
