NECROMANTIC WAYS.19: As Control Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class VI mindless undead or 6 Class I undead or any other combination where the total Class numbers of the undead is six can be controlled.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.20: As Create Mindless Undead I, except 1 Class VI mindless undead or 6 Class I undead or any other combination where the total Class numbers of the undead is six can be created.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.25: As Control Mindless Undead I, except caster can control any group of undead whose Class numbers total no more than the caster’s level.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.30: This spell is used only once, if ever. Using it, the caster transforms himself into a Lich, one of the most feared of the undead. If the caster fails when attempting this spell, he will simply die. With success, the caster becomes one of the undead without ever actually dying. At this point, the caster is sustained by magic and dwells in a place between life and death.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.35: As Animate Dead VI , except the caster can raise as many bodies as he has levels or improve individual corpses as in the Animate Dead spells by assigning one level per number of the spell above one (e.g. a 37th level caster decides that the first corpse in his army will have +5 to maneuvers and an OB of +10 as in Animate Dead IV; to do this he must assign one level for the corpse, plus three more for the improvement to Animate Dead IV quality, and has 33 levels left to assign to animating further dead). These corpses will remain animated for a full day, during which the caster can control the entire army whenever he concentrates. Additionally, corpses may be as old as one day dead per level of caster.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.40: As Create Mindless Undead VI , except any combination where the total Class numbers of the undead is no more than the caster’s level can be created. At the time the army is raised, the caster can give it an order that it will relentlessly follow for 1 day. At the end of that time, the undead will become uncontrolled and begin attacking the nearest living targets. Additionally, corpses may be as old as one day dead per level of caster.
NECROMANTIC WAYS.50: As Cheat Death, except caster’s organs are transferred into a ready container (jar, box, chest, etc.). The caster then becomes a somewhat more powerful Classic Lich and can only be truly destroyed if the container with his organs is destroyed.

NERVE & ORGAN LAW.1: Diagnoses target’s wounds, injuries, and diseases allowing a +20 bonus to all Medicine maneuvers made by the caster to treat the target’s diagnosed condition(s). Caster can take a round to advise someone else on treating the target, providing a +10 bonus to treat the target.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.2: Target becomes completely infertile for the spell’s duration.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.3: An injury to an organ (e.g. eye, ear, liver but not the brain or nerves) incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.4: Target is at -30 for all actions.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.5: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Blood, Bone, and Muscle Limb Preservation spells as well.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.6: As Light Organ Relief , but an injury incurring a penalty up to -40. This can be used to cure most causes of infertility (if the requisite organs are still present).
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.7: One of target’s natural senses (e.g. vision, hearing, touch) is honed and perfected for the duration. Perception maneuvers based exclusively on that sense gain a +20 bonus, while Perception that involves that sense and others gain a +10 bonus.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.8: Target’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.9: An injury to the brain or nerves incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.10: Target can see twice as far through fog, mist, water, or other semi-transparent substances that would otherwise reduce their range of vision.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.11: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring any penalty.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.12: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spell from the Bone, Blood, and Muscle spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.13: An injury to an organ incurring a penalty up to -20 is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.14: Target comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure. Process takes 1-10 hours and target is at -50 to Endurance rolls for 1-10 days.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.15: As Light Organ Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.16: An injury to the brain or nerves incurring a penalty up to -20 is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.17: Target regains function in one sensory organ, so long as that sensory organ has not been completely destroyed (in which case Organ Regeneration is required). Recovery time is instantaneous.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.18: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.19: Caster can cure any paralysis problem. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as -100 injury for progress of recovery.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.20: As Light Nerve Healing , except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.25: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.30: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.35: Target will regenerate any one organ (external or internal) other than the brain or nerves. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.40: As Paralysis Cures, except recovery is instantaneous.
NERVE & ORGAN LAW.50: Target will regenerate his brain. Spell must be cast before target dies. Target will be in a “healing coma” while brain grows back, and memories may be lost depending on the severity of the damage. Recovery time is 1-10 days. Note: See Section 10.9 for the application of wound-healing spells (various Relief, Healing, and Healing True spells). While medical treatment of the wound is miraculously immediate, the body will still need a period of recovery before the injury has healed, depending on several factors, including which type of wound-healing spell is used.   

NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.1: Diagnoses target’s wounds, injuries, and diseases allowing a +20 bonus to all Medicine maneuvers made by the caster to treat the target’s diagnosed condition(s). Caster can take a round to advise someone else on treating the target, providing a +10 bonus to treat the target.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.2: An injury to an organ (e.g. eye, ear, liver but not the brain or nerves) incurring a penalty up to 20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.3: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring a penalty up to -40. This can be used to cure most causes of infertility (if the requisite organs are still present).
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.4: Prevents deteriorization of any specific organ, even if it is outside of its body.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.5: Target’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.6: One of target’s natural senses (e.g. vision, hearing, touch) is honed and perfected for the duration. Perception maneuvers based exclusively on that sense gain a +20 bonus, while Perception that involves that sense and others gain a +10 bonus.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.7: As Light Organ Relief , but an injury incurring any penalty.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.8: Target comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure. Process takes 1-10 hours and target is at -50 to Endurance rolls for 1-10 days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.9: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spells from the Bone, Blood, and Muscle Mastery spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.10: Target experiences all sense impressions as distorted and confused. Target is at -25 for most actions, suffers double penalties if attempting to move faster than a walk, and has a -40 to Perception rolls.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.11: Target is kept in a state of suspended animation and prevented from dying for the duration of the spell (i.e., the body is “preserved” and the “soul” will not leave the body). Also requires the use of the other Suspend Life I spell (on the Blood Mastery list).
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.12: An injury to an organ, brain, or nerves incurring a penalty up to -40 is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.13: Allows caster to transplant a healthy organ (not including brain or nervous system) into the target. Requires a one-hour operation. Recovery time is 1-10 days. Chance of rejection is 1% if organ comes from a relative of the target, 10% if it comes from a member of the same species, 30% if it comes from a similar species (e.g., one humanoid to another), and 60% if it comes from a vaguely similar species (e.g., ape to humanoid).
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.14: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.15: Target’s senses are impaired. All Perception maneuvers are at -40 and target cannot sense anything further than 20' away.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.16: Caster can cure any paralysis problem. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as a -100 injury for progress of recovery.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.17: As Suspend Life I, except for duration and the requirement of Suspend Life II from Blood Mastery.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.18: As Organ Transplant, except recovery time is only 1-10 hours.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.19: An injury to the brain or nerves incurring any penalty is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.20: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.25: Target will regenerate any one organ (external or internal) or the brain or nerves. The recovery time is 1-10 hours for most organs or 1-10 days for the brain, during which the target is unconscious. This spell will restore memories and knowledge which might otherwise have been lost due to brain damage.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.30: As Severe Nerve Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.35: As Organ Transplant, except a brain may be transplanted and the chance of rejection becomes a chance of the brain going insane because it cannot adapt to its new body.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.40: Caster can regrow one severed limb; it requires the use of the Regrow Limb spells for Bone, Blood, and Muscle Mastery for the limb to become fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
NERVE AND ORGAN MASTERY.50: As Nerve/Organ Regeneration, except the recovery time for brain regeneration is 1-10 hours.

ORGAN WAYS.1: An injury to an organ (e.g. eye, ear, liver, but not the brain or nerves) incurring a penalty up to 20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.
ORGAN WAYS.2: As Light Organ Relief , but an injury incurring a penalty up to -40. This can be used to cure most causes of infertility (if the requisite organs are still present).
ORGAN WAYS.3: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring any penalty.
ORGAN WAYS.4: Caster’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.
ORGAN WAYS.5: Caster regains function in one sensory organ, so long as that sensory organ has not been completely destroyed (in which case Organ Regeneration is required). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
ORGAN WAYS.6: As Light Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.7: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
ORGAN WAYS.8: Caster comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure.
ORGAN WAYS.9: As Medium Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.10: As Sense Restoration, except recovery is instantaneous.
ORGAN WAYS.11: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.12: Removes any paralysis problem suffered by the caster. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as -100 injury for progress of recovery.
ORGAN WAYS.13: As Light Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.14: As Light Organ Relief, except an injury to nerves incurring a penalty up to -20, and the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.15: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.
ORGAN WAYS.16: As Medium Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.17: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury to nerves incurring a penalty up to -40.
ORGAN WAYS.18: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.
ORGAN WAYS.19: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury to nerves incurring any penalty.
ORGAN WAYS.20: Caster can repair brain damage (such as that caused by a skull fracture, etc. at the discretion of the GM). Recovery time is 1-10 hours. This includes damage from the Mind Death spell list.
ORGAN WAYS.25: Caster can regenerate any 1 organ (external or internal), except the brain. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.
ORGAN WAYS.30: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
ORGAN WAYS.35: As Paralysis Cures, except recovery is instantaneous.
ORGAN WAYS.40: As Brain Repair, except instantly provides complete recovery, taking data stored in caster’s or original victim’s aura to reconstruct the brain exactly.
ORGAN WAYS.50: Caster may reunite his soul with his body, provided the latter is functional. To restore a dead target to life, a Healer would first have to heal all of the latter’s wounds (without “killing” himself) and then restore the target’s soul by giving up his own (for the target this process operates like the 19th level Lifegiving II spell on the Cleric Base list, Life Mastery). While technically alive, but without his soul, the Healer would be affected as if he had been “absolved” (see Cleric Base list, Channels); thus, he could take no conscious actions and he would be at -75 for all subconscious actions. The caster may regain his own soul through the use of this spell (which does not suffer the -75 penalty) or through someone else casting an appropriate spell.

PATHMASTERY.1: Caster learns the origin and nearest “destination” of any path within the area of effect.
PATHMASTERY.2: Caster knows which way is north, which way is up, and his distance above or below the ground’s surface.
PATHMASTERY.3: Caster gets a +50 bonus to all Tracking maneuvers.
PATHMASTERY.4: Caster acquires a visual image of any user(s) of a given path, up to 1 hour in the past per level of the caster.
PATHMASTERY.5: Caster learns the location(s) of any path(s) within 1 mile. This spell reveals the nearest point of each path, but not the path’s course.
PATHMASTERY.6: Caster learns the origin of one set of tracks, and acquires a visual image of the one who left them. Caster will gain a +25 bonus when tracking this particular being or animal.
PATHMASTERY.7: Caster is aware of any being within the area of effect that has hostile intentions towards him (i.e., he knows their number and locations). The hostility must be directed specifically at the caster (or the group he is in) or the spell reveals nothing. The being’s hostility must be active (i.e., driving the being to perform aggressive actions), not passive (i.e., hate exists, but no action is planned).
PATHMASTERY.8: Caster acquires visual and aural (sound) images of any being or animal that has passed within a 50'R of a specific object. The being or animal can have passed up to 1 hour per level earlier. If more than one being or animal has passed, the caster will see them one image at a time, one image per round (starting with the oldest image).
PATHMASTERY.9: As Far Pathfinding I, except range is 5 miles.
PATHMASTERY.10: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any one animal or plant species.
PATHMASTERY.11: Caster acquires a visual image of all beings or animals who left tracks in the area. This provides a +50 bonus when later tracking any of these beings or animals. If more than one being or animal has left tracks, the caster will see them one image at a time, one image per round (starting with the oldest image).
PATHMASTERY.12: Caster can communicate with any one stone if it possesses any mental qualities (e.g., caster could speak to a holy stone which possesses some sort of intelligence).
PATHMASTERY.13: As Far Pathfinding I, except range is 20 miles.
PATHMASTERY.14: As Sense Ambush I, except area of effect is 100'R.
PATHMASTERY.15: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any one species of “natural” monster. This excludes the undead, artificial creations (e.g., constructs), and entities from another plane (e.g., demons).
PATHMASTERY.16: Caster gets a +75 bonus to all Tracking maneuvers.
PATHMASTERY.17: As Sense Ambush I, except area of effect is 150'R.
PATHMASTERY.18: Caster can track individuals through an underwater environment without penalty.
PATHMASTERY.19: As Far Pathfinding I, except range is 1 mile per level and exact routes within the range are ascertained.
PATHMASTERY.20: As Sense Ambush I , except area of effect is 10'R/lvl.
PATHMASTERY.25: Allows caster to ascertain the proper path to what he seeks (includes the course along that path). For example, he could know the quickest way to the city or the safest way to the city, depending on his goals.
PATHMASTERY.30: Negates any Hide Tracks spell (except for Hide Tracks True) and provides a +100 Tracking bonus.
PATHMASTERY.35: Caster can track individuals through the air (i.e., track a flying being, object) without penalty.
PATHMASTERY.40: Caster learns of one being: where it originated (was born/created), where it began its current journey, and where it is currently bound, both its next destination and ultimate destination, if these are not the same. This will detect the largest, most important journey that the target is on. For example, if cast on two individuals going out to fetch water from the local well, that is what the spell would reveal if cast on the innkeeper’s daughter who is not planning on going anywhere in particular other than back to the inn with the water, but if the other person was a pilgrim traveling to a holy city, just getting some water at a stopover point, then that pilgrimage would be the journey revealed.
PATHMASTERY.50: Allows caster to know entire course of the tracks in the area of effect (i.e., exactly where and how they go). This will not negate Hide Tracks True. Note: See Section 10.16 for information on interpretation of “path”.   

PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.1: Caster can tell the exact mass and/or weight of one object per caster level.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.2: Target gains an extremely acute sense of hearing. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only hearing, +25 to Perception involving hearing and other senses.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.3: Adds +50 to any rolls for any moving maneuvers performed at a walking pace or less (e.g., walking a 3" beam).
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.4: Target can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.5: Target has a 300° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the target is lowered to +5 and the rear bonus is lowered to +15.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.6: Target’s voice carries three times as far as normal.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.7: Target can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.8: Target can breathe water but not air.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.9: Target can see varying degrees of heat on the surfaces of objects. This spell does not allow the caster to see “through” things. While this vision is sufficient for moving around, it is not sufficient at all for reading or mapping.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.10: Target can breathe any gas as normal air.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.11: Delays the effect of a poison on a target. If the poison is not eliminated before the spell expires, the poison will affect the target at that time.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.12: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.13: Target can hear normally underwater or in any other medium that distorts sound.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.14: Target can dig through earth at a rate of 10'/round. Target cannot dig through solid stone or metal.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.15: Target can breathe water, air, and any gas at will.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.16: As Balance, except can affect one target/level of caster.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.17: Target has a 360° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the target is lowered to zero and the rear bonus is lowered to +5.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.18: As Nightvision, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.19: As Watervision, except can affect one target/level of caster.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.20: As Nightvision, Sidevision, Watervision, Heatvision, and Darkvision all functioning at the same time.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.25: As Waterlungs, except can affect 1 target/level of caster.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.30: As Gaslungs, except can affect 1 target/level of caster.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.35: As Darkvision, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.40: As Changing Lungs, except can affect 1 target/level of caster.
PHYSICAL ENHANCEMENT.50: As Vision, except can affect 1 target/level of caster.

PLANT MASTERY.1: Caster learns nature, value, and history of one plant (that is not a magical herb).
PLANT MASTERY.2: Allows caster to increase speed of growth for any 1 species of plant within the area of effect to ten times the normal rate.
PLANT MASTERY.3: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any 1 plant species.
PLANT MASTERY.4: Instantly heals damage to one plant that is not a tree.
PLANT MASTERY.5: Allows caster to increase speed of growth for any 1 species of plant within the area of effect to a hundred times the normal rate.
PLANT MASTERY.6: Caster can locate members of any 1 species of plant or find out what species are in the area.
PLANT MASTERY.7: Allows caster to double the size of any 1 plant. This requires 1 day’s growth. When fully mature the plant will achieve twice its normal size.
PLANT MASTERY.8: Caster can control the automatic and/or mental processes of any one plant. Caster can also control the plant’s movements, although this does not allow movement beyond normal allowances. If cast daily, this can be used to direct a plant’s growth into specific unusual shapes, so long as these would be naturally possible.
PLANT MASTERY.9: As Speed Growth I, except the range is 100' and the radius is 100'.
PLANT MASTERY.10: As Plant Growth II, except size increase is 3 times.
PLANT MASTERY.11: As Plant Control I, except caster can control any three plants.
PLANT MASTERY.12: As Plant Restore, except it can affect any plant (including trees).
PLANT MASTERY.13: As Plant Growth II , except size increase is 5 times.
PLANT MASTERY.14: As Rapid Growth I , except the range is 100' and the radius is 100'R.
PLANT MASTERY.15: As Plant Control I, except caster can control any 5 plants.
PLANT MASTERY.16: Summons a wall of very tough (but pliable) thorny growth. The wall is up to 10'x10'x5'. Anyone moving through the wall receives d10 dagger attacks (each with a +50 OB) per foot of wall moved through. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
PLANT MASTERY.17: This spell will put out any fires upon living materials that are in the area of effect. Fumble results on the Ice Bolt attack are ignored.
PLANT MASTERY.18: As Plant Growth II , except size increase is 10 times.
PLANT MASTERY.19: This spell requires a tree at least 80 years old as its target. If this spell is cast on a tree every day for a year (not necessarily by the same person each time), then the tree will awaken, becoming a Slowroot (See Creature Law). The newly awakened tree will not automatically be under any control of the caster(s) who wakened it.
PLANT MASTERY.20: As Plant Growth II, except caster can increase size of all plants of 1 species within a 100'R by 5x.
PLANT MASTERY.25: As Rapid Growth I, except for range and area of effect.
PLANT MASTERY.30: As Plant Control I , except caster can control all plants within range (100'R).
PLANT MASTERY.35: As Wall of Thorns, but dimensions are up to 20'x10'x5' and the duration is permanent (the wall is rooted and living).
PLANT MASTERY.40: Caster can animate any 1 plant; the plant can then move slowly (10'/rnd for plants under 10' tall, 20'/rnd if taller), carrying any root system it may possess along.
PLANT MASTERY.50: As Plant Animation, except caster can animate up to 1 plant/level.

POISON MASTERY.1: The caster knows what type of poison(s) are needed to achieve a particular effect. The GM may deem it appropriate to give the caster a bonus of +25 to any maneuver directly involving poison(s).
POISON MASTERY.2: The caster receives an additional RR versus poisons (i.e., if the caster is poisoned, the caster has two chances to roll a successful RR).
POISON MASTERY.3: This spell causes one dose of a poison to adhere to an inanimate, solid surface. The poison will wear off normally.
POISON MASTERY.4: This spell magically prepares a poison (up to 5th level in potency). This spell normally requires d100 minutes before it is completed. Note that the caster must have the poison in its raw form before casting this spell (i.e., the spell does not create the poison; it merely prepares it).
POISON MASTERY.5: Caster may analyze a poison to determine the exact effects and what antidotes are necessary. Poison may be on an item or in a being.
POISON MASTERY.6: For the duration of this spell, all effects from a single poison are suspended. Note that the poison will still be in the caster and takes effect normally after the duration (though the spell may be recast to continue delaying the effects, and some poisons may leave the caster’s system before the spell expires).
POISON MASTERY.7: Caster can transfer a single dose of a poison from one target to the caster (who can then use the self-only poison negation spells on this list to deal with it).
POISON MASTERY.8: As Apply Poison I , except affects two doses (which can be applied to different surfaces).
POISON MASTERY.9: Any poison inside the caster must make an RR with a -20 modifier or be neutralized. In any case, it delays the effects of the poison for the duration of the spell.
POISON MASTERY.10: As Work Poison V, except affects poisons up to a potency of 10th level.
POISON MASTERY.11: The caster can convert any poison that he is touching into a venom. The poison will now be effective when introduced into the victim’s blood stream (regardless of its normal method of affecting a target).
POISON MASTERY.12: As Apply Poison II , except affects three doses.
POISON MASTERY.13: As Neutralize Poison, except poison has a -70 modifier to its RR.
POISON MASTERY.14: Caster can transfer a single dose of a poison from one target to a second target. Caster must be touching both targets simultaneously.
POISON MASTERY.15: Caster can coat the target’s hands with a poison. The target is unaffected by the poison.
POISON MASTERY.16: As Work Poison V, except affects any poison.
POISON MASTERY.17: As Apply Poison II, except affects four doses.
POISON MASTERY.18: As Venom, except converts the poison into a contact poison (a poison that affects its victim upon contact with the victim’s skin) regardless of its normal method of affecting a target.
POISON MASTERY.19: As Transfer Poison II, except caster need only touch the target from which the poison is transferred; the target to receive the poison need merely be within the spell’s range.
POISON MASTERY.20: The caster can ingest a single dose of a poison. For the duration of this spell, he will be unaffected by the poison. Anytime before the duration expires, the caster can attempt to transfer the poison to a single target. The target must be no further away than 5' and the caster’s mouth must be capable of delivering the poison (either through a kiss, a whisper, spitting, etc.). The target gets an RR to avoid the effects of this spell (in addition to the RR to resist the poison).
POISON MASTERY.25: As Venom’ s Breath, except the caster can transfer the poison to any single target within the range. The caster must be able to cast an obvious, evil glare at the target.
POISON MASTERY.30: The caster can disperse a poison (which becomes an inhaled poison) into a misty cloud. The size of the mist is one foot in radius per dose of the poison used. The poison will linger in the area normally until the duration of the spell expires (i.e., the mist will be subject to the wind, etc.). When the duration expires, the poison disperses.
POISON MASTERY.35: As Transfer Poison III, except caster need not touch the target from which the poison is transferred, both targets need merely be within the spell’s range.
POISON MASTERY.40: Improves the potency of a poison such that any victim of the poison must make two RRs and take the worse of the two results. If in or on an object (e.g., goblet of a poisoned drink, poison on a blade, but not a person), then the poison will linger for 24 hours, even if normally effective washing procedures are done. This residual poison will continue to affect anyone who would normally be affected by the poison there, but it is diluted from the original potency and will allow a normal single RR, with a +10 bonus.
POISON MASTERY.50: As Venom’ s Glare, except for the range.

PRESENCE.1: Caster is aware of the presence of all sentient/thinking beings within range.
PRESENCE.2: Caster gets general idea of the race and level of one being. This being must first be located by Presence, Presence True, or Awareness.
PRESENCE.3: As Feel I, except caster stores mental pattern of target for later use with other spells (on this list and others).
PRESENCE.4: The caster can transfer a copy of any one Mind Stored person to another caster from the Mentalism realm (i.e., both casters now have the person stored). The target of this spell must also know the Mind Store spell.
PRESENCE.5: As Feel I, except three targets are analyzed at once.
PRESENCE.6: As Feel I, except caster learns race, profession, sex, age, and level of target.
PRESENCE.7: Caster gets direction and distance to any unshielded mind for which caster has a Mind Stored reference pattern.
PRESENCE.8: As Mind Finding I , but with an extended range and caster learns direction only.
PRESENCE.9: Can detect presences in area with a 50' radius; caster can concentrate on one such area each round.
PRESENCE.10: As Presence, plus a general knowledge of the actions of each being (e.g., being is casting a spell, but not what spell is being cast).
PRESENCE.11: As Direction I, except range is 1 mile/level.
PRESENCE.12: As Feel I, except that range is 100'/level.
PRESENCE.13: As Feel I, except five targets are analyzed at once.
PRESENCE.14: As Feel I, except caster may “feel” one being per round.
PRESENCE.15: As Mind Typing, except range is 100'/level.
PRESENCE.16: As Mind Typing, except caster also learns what spell lists and skills the target has learned, the target’s general place/culture of origin, and any current mental contacts with the target mind (including those target may be unaware of; nature of these contacts is revealed, but not identity on other end).
PRESENCE.17: Caster is aware anytime that a spell with a mental subtype targets the caster. If the targeting spell fails an RR against the caster’s level, then the caster of this spell learns what spell it was.
PRESENCE.18: As Mind Finding I, except range is 1 mile/level.
PRESENCE.19: As Feel I, except ten targets are analyzed at once.
PRESENCE.20: As Awareness, except that very detailed knowledge of actions is given.
PRESENCE.25: As Mind Finding I, except range is 5 miles/level.
PRESENCE.30: As Direction I, except with no range limitations.
PRESENCE.35: As Feel I, except up to the caster’s level number of targets are analyzed at once.
PRESENCE.40: As Mind Typing, except simultaneously “types” a number of targets up the caster’s level.
PRESENCE.50: As Mind Finding I, except range is unlimited.

PROSTHETICS.1: Caster learns the proper dimension needed to make a false limb or other prosthetic for the target.
PROSTHETICS.2: Allows the caster to create a perfectly formed artificial limb (or other body part) out of non-magical wood. This takes 8 hours. Suitability for use is guaranteed by this spell. Any special features or enhanced appearance depends on the appropriate Crafting skill(s) of the caster. Requires that Measurements has been cast to determine necessary form of the prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.3: Allows caster to attach an artifical wooden limb or other prosthetic to a body. Process takes 1 hour. This spell must be cast before an Animation spell can be cast.
PROSTHETICS.4: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 8 hours.
PROSTHETICS.5: As Fashion Wood, except the material worked is non-magical glass, and it requires two castings (16 hours of work) to finish the prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.6: As Fit Wood; except allows caster to attach a glass prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.7: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 24 hours.
PROSTHETICS.8: As Fashion Wood, except the material worked is metal, and it requires three castings (24 hours of work) to finish the prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.9: As Fit Wood; allows caster to attach a metal prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.10: This spell enables the caster to convert clay into artificial flesh which can be used to cover an artificial limb (which must be done at the time of casting). The flesh is only semi-realistic (similar to plastic). Time required to cover a full limb is 4 hours if the limb is for a Small recipient, 8 hours for a Medium limb, and 16 hours for a Big limb. If the prosthetic is not a full limb (e.g., a hand or foot), the GM should decrease the time.
PROSTHETICS.11: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 week.
PROSTHETICS.12: As Fashion Wood , except the material worked may be of any type, including magical materials, and it requires four castings (32 hours of work) to finish the prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.13: As Fit Wood; allows caster to attach a “special” (of enchanted or exotic material) prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.14: Allows incorporation of simple mechanisms into a prosthetic (e.g., a small retractable knife blade hidden in a finger), which can then be operated by the will of the wearer when an Animation spell is active on the prosthetic. The mechanism must be constructed normally.
PROSTHETICS.15: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 month.
PROSTHETICS.16: Repairs all damage to a single prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.17: Allows an artificial organ (created with one of the Mold spells on this list and/or through normal crafting) to be incorporated into a living target. Typically, this will be a glass eye that can provide sight when an Animation spell is cast on it, but theoretically any organ could be replaced with a suitable artificial organ. If an internal organ is replaced, surgery will be required for the insertion of the artificial organ.
PROSTHETICS.18: As Mechanical Contrivance, except that a complex mechanism (e.g., a dart gun) can be included in a prosthetic.
PROSTHETICS.19: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 3 months.
PROSTHETICS.20: As Artificial Flesh, except perfectly realistic synthetic flesh can be made and worked. The process will require days instead of hours, with the same numbers as in Artificial Flesh.
PROSTHETICS.25: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 year.
PROSTHETICS.30: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 decade.
PROSTHETICS.35: Caster can emplace one spell of any level into a prosthetic. Within one minute of casting this spell, the spell to be emplaced must also be cast (within 10'). Once the spell is implanted, it will remain waiting in the prosthetic until used. When the prosthetic is attached and an Animation spell is in effect, the prosthetic wearer may trigger the spell at any time (treat as an innate power).
PROSTHETICS.40: This spell allows the caster to attach an artificial part that is not a replacement. The recipient of the part could be given wings (two spells and two prosthetics required to actually fly), an extra arm, or the like. An Animation spell is required for the added part to actually function.
PROSTHETICS.50: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will permanently.

PROTECTIONS.1: Target gets a +5 bonus to RRs and maneuver rolls. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes per level if cast on a non-mobile target. If cast on a mobile target, the spell will last as long as the caster concentrates. If cast on a non-mobile target that is moved, the spell is dispelled. If the caster is the target, the duration is one minute per level and concentration is not required.
PROTECTIONS.2: As Prayer I, except bonus applies to DB (Defensive Bonus) and maneuver rolls.
PROTECTIONS.3: As Prayer I, except the bonus applies to RRs and DB.
PROTECTIONS.4: Target is protected from natural heat up to 80°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures above 80°C, subtract 60°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. heat spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental fire/heat attacks. If the caster is not the target, concentration is required. Duration is as for Prayer I.
PROTECTIONS.5: Target is protected from natural cold down to -10°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures below 20°C, add 30°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. cold spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental cold/ice attacks. If the caster is not the target, concentration is required. Duration is as for Prayer I.
PROTECTIONS.6: Target is protected from physical attacks as if armored. Target is granted the benefits of AT 4, but is not encumbered in any way, as he is not actually armored.
PROTECTIONS.7: Target gains a +30 bonus to his RRs against a specific spell (spells whose name differs only by Roman numeral are considered the same spell for this purpose, but a “Lord” or “True” version is not).
PROTECTIONS.8: All who are within the sphere are allowed the benefits of either a Heat Resistance or a Cold Resistance spell (must be the same for all targets). The sphere is not mobile.
PROTECTIONS.9: As Sacred Protection I, except AT is 6.
PROTECTIONS.10: All within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer I or Bless I or Resistance I (must be the same for all targets). The sphere is not mobile.
PROTECTIONS.11: As Prayer I, except total bonus is +15 and the spell may be divided among 1-3 targets (1 target would get a +15 bonus, 3 targets could each get +5 bonuses, etc.).
PROTECTIONS.12: As Bless I, except total bonus is +15 and spell may be divided among 1-3 targets as in Prayer III.
PROTECTIONS.13: As Resistance I, except total bonus is +15 and the spell may be divided among 1-3 targets as in Prayer III.
PROTECTIONS.14: As Sacred Protection I, except AT is 8.
PROTECTIONS.15: As Temperature Sphere I, except radius is 20'.
PROTECTIONS.16: As Protective Sphere I, except radius is 30'.
PROTECTIONS.17: As Protective Sphere I, except all within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer III or Bless III or Resistance III (i.e., each protected individual gains a +15 bonus).
PROTECTIONS.18: As Prayer I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets (1 target would get a +25 bonus, two targets could get a +13 bonus for one and a +12 bonus for the other, etc.)
PROTECTIONS.19: As Sacred Protection I , except AT is 10.
PROTECTIONS.20: As Bless I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets as in Prayer V.
PROTECTIONS.25: As Resistance I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets as in Prayer V.
PROTECTIONS.30: As Protective Sphere I , except all within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer V or Bless V or Resistance V (i.e., each protected individual gains a +25 bonus).
PROTECTIONS.35: As Temperature Sphere I , except radius is 10' per level and the effect is as both Heat Resistance and Cold Resistance, depending on what temperatures occur to protect against.
PROTECTIONS.40: As Sacred Protection I, except multiple targets may be given the equivalent of any armor from AT 2 to AT 10, with the restriction that the total ATs provided must be no greater than the level of the caster (e.g., a 40th level caster could provide AT 10 to four targets or AT 5 to eight targets or AT 8 to three targets and AT 4 to four targets).
PROTECTIONS.50: Target gets a +30 bonus to RRs, maneuver rolls, and DB.

PURIFICATIONS.1: This spell neutralizes abnormal diseases, poison, and other similar substances in one day’s worth of food and/or water. It will not neutralize a poison or similar substance that is a natural part of the food (e.g., it will not neutralize the natural poison in poisonous mushrooms).
PURIFICATIONS.2: Stops infection and/or spread of a non-magical disease in one target that has acquired the disease. Infected areas may continue to worsen. Thus, no further damage will occur in uninfected areas of the body, and healing may begin.
PURIFICATIONS.3: This spell perfectly preserves one day’s worth of food (by the caster’s dietary requirements) for one week, after which it will spoil normally.
PURIFICATIONS.4: Neutralizes any one non-magical poison in the system of one target. Damage already sustained is not cured.
PURIFICATIONS.5: Target gets an additional RR against any disease(s).
PURIFICATIONS.6: Target gets an additional RR against any poison(s).
PURIFICATIONS.7: As Food Preservation, except preserves one day’s worth of food per level of the caster.
PURIFICATIONS.8: As Disease Resistance I , except target gets 2 additional RRs.
PURIFICATIONS.9: As Poison Resistance I, except target gets 2 additional RRs.
PURIFICATIONS.10: Target is cured of one mental illness or malady. This will require a 1-10 day recovery period to take effect.
PURIFICATIONS.11: As Disease Resistance I, except target gets 3 additional RRs.
PURIFICATIONS.12: As Poison Resistance I , except target gets 3 additional RRs.
PURIFICATIONS.13: As Food Preservation, except preserves one day’s worth of food for one month.
PURIFICATIONS.14: Caster can remove any one disease from a target.
PURIFICATIONS.15: Caster can remove any one poison from a target.
PURIFICATIONS.16: As Mind Disease Cures, except cures one mental illness or malady in a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
PURIFICATIONS.17: As Food Preservation, except preserves one day’s worth of food for a year.
PURIFICATIONS.18: Caster can remove any one disease from a number of targets up to the caster’s level (e.g., an 18th level caster could remove leprosy from 18 targets).
PURIFICATIONS.19: Caster can remove any one poison from the systems of a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
PURIFICATIONS.20: Target is instantly cured of one mental illness or malady.
PURIFICATIONS.25: Any disease in the area is eliminated.
PURIFICATIONS.30: Any poison in the area is neutralized.
PURIFICATIONS.35: As Food Preservation, except preserves one day’s worth of food per level of the caster for a period of one year/level.
PURIFICATIONS.40: As Mind Disease Cures True, except cures one mental illness or malady in a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
PURIFICATIONS.50: Eliminates all poisons and/or diseases in the area.

RAPID WAYS.1: Caster draws or changes weapon “instantly” (i.e., without expending AP; the action is included as part of this spell). This includes readying ammunition, but not long actions like readying a crossbow.
RAPID WAYS.2: Target gains a +20 bonus to any Movement maneuver done at a Jog (x2) pace or faster. If no Movement maneuver is required, the pace penalty can be reduced by 20 for any simultaneous action.
RAPID WAYS.3: Target gains an additional 1 AP to use each round this spell is in effect (i.e., target normally gets 5 AP to use during the round).
RAPID WAYS.4: Target gains a +5 bonus to initiative rolls.
RAPID WAYS.5: The round in which this spell is cast counts as a round of spell preparation for the caster.
RAPID WAYS.6: Target gains an additional 1 AP to use each round this spell is in effect (i.e., target normally gets 5 AP to use during the round).
RAPID WAYS.7: Target gains a +10 bonus to initiative rolls.
RAPID WAYS.8: Caster requires 2-3 AP to cast a spell (i.e., he may cast a spell taking 3 AP at no penalty or taking
RAPID WAYS.9: As Speed I, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.10: Target gains a +15 bonus to initiative rolls.
RAPID WAYS.11: As Speed I, except target gains an additional
RAPID WAYS.12: As Swiftness I , except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.13: Target gains a +20 bonus to initiative rolls.
RAPID WAYS.14: Caster requires 2 AP to cast a spell (i.e., he casts taking 2 AP at no penalty).
RAPID WAYS.15: As Haste I, except target gains an additional
RAPID WAYS.16: As Speed II, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.17: As Speed I, except target gains an additional
RAPID WAYS.18: As Haste I, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.19: As Swiftness II, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.20: As Haste I, except target gains an additional
RAPID WAYS.25: As Swiftness III, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.30: As Speed I, except target gains an additional
RAPID WAYS.35: As Haste II, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.40: As Speed IV, except affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level.
RAPID WAYS.50: As Haste I, except target gains an additional

REPULSIONS.1: Causes up to 5 Undead to flee or disintegrate. Up to five “points” of Undead may be affected: a “Class I” Undead counts as 1 point, a “Class II” Undead counts as 2 points, etc. “Animated Dead” are treated as “Class I” Undead but are not entitled to an RR (they automatically disintegrate if the spell does not fail). Otherwise, each target is entitled to an RR: if it fails by 1-50, it will flee (targets that cannot flee remain motionless) for 1 round per 1 failure. If it fails by more than 50, it disintegrates. The RR of any undead creature can be modified by concentrating the effects of the spell on that target: for each additional point of the spell allocated to a target (beyond those normally needed to affect it), that target’s RR is modified by -5. Thus Repel Undead V could affect 5 Class I Undead with no RR modifier or 1 Class 1 Undead with an RR modifier of -20 or 1 Class II Undead with an RR modifier of -5 and 2 Class I Undead with no RR modifier or any other combination of targets providing no more than 5 points were allocated. Once an undead has made an RR against a given caster’s Repel Undead spell, it cannot be affected by a Repel Undead spell from that caster again until one of the following happens: the caster gains a level, the undead is “killed” and has to reconstitute itself, or the caster converts to a new power source (i.e., changes religion).
REPULSIONS.2: When the caster is a target of a spell from the Realm of Channeling, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR, using the level of this spell as the attacking level and the level of the incoming spell as the defending level. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
REPULSIONS.3: Cancels a Channels I spell being transmitted or received within range. Note that the Channels I spell gets no RR.
REPULSIONS.4: [RR Mod: -20] Nullifies a curse for the duration of this spell. The curse is not dispelled and takes effect again once the duration of this spell expires.
REPULSIONS.5: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels III.
REPULSIONS.6: As Repel Undead V, except 9 points of undead may be affected.
REPULSIONS.7: Dispels a curse if the curse fails an RR. The curse’s level is the level of the caster of the curse. If the curse is not dispelled, the caster cannot try again until he has gone up another level of experience.
REPULSIONS.8: Any active spell from the realm of Channeling that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
REPULSIONS.9: [RR Mod: -20] As Neutralize Curse I, except curse is nullified for one hour per level.
REPULSIONS.10: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels V.
REPULSIONS.11: As Repel Undead V , except 12 points of Undead may be affected.
REPULSIONS.12: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels VIII.
REPULSIONS.13: An entity from another plane (typically a demon) is sent back to whence it came. This spell will affect entities of up to Type (or Grade for elementals) II or up to 10th level if they do not have a “Type”.
REPULSIONS.14: As Dispel Channeling I, except affects up to five targets.
REPULSIONS.15: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels X.
REPULSIONS.16: As Repel Undead V , except 15 points of Undead may be affected.
REPULSIONS.17: As Lesser Banishment , except affects entities up to Type (or Grade) IV or 20th level if they do not have a “Type”.
REPULSIONS.18: [RR Mod: -20] As Neutralize Curse I , except curse is nullified for 1 day/level.
REPULSIONS.19: As Dispel Channeling I, except affects up to ten targets.
REPULSIONS.20: As Repel Undead V , except this spell disintegrates any one Undead of Class V or lower automatically or automatically sends a Class VI fleeing for 1 round per caster level.
REPULSIONS.25: [RR Mod: -50] As Uncurse, except for the RR modification.
REPULSIONS.30: As Anti-Channels I except any Channeled spell can be canceled. Spells channeled with the aid of a Holy Bridge (Cleric Base list, Channels) are not affected.
REPULSIONS.35: As Repel Undead V , except a number of points of Undead equal to the caster’s level may be affected and if 20 points are applied to any one Undead, the effect is that of Lord Repel Undead on that target.
REPULSIONS.40: As Lesser Banishment , except affects any entity short of a god.
REPULSIONS.50: Once per round, the caster can intercept one channeled spell that is being transmitted or received within this spell’s range and cast that spell himself. The person sending the channeled spell gets an RR (the defending level is that of the caster sending the channeled spell). Alternatively, the caster can intercept channeled PP for his own use. Interception takes no activity. Casting an intercepted spell takes the normal time.

RUNE MASTERY.1: Caster may cast this spell with another spell in order to store that second spell. The spell to be stored must be cast within one minute after the Spell Store spell is cast. The stored spell may then be cast later as an instantaneous spell. The Spell Store spell costs the same number of PP as the spell that is stored. No other spell may be cast while a spell is stored.
RUNE MASTERY.2: This spell attempts to remove an existing rune. The rune will resist at its own level, but if the caster knows what the rune is, the rune suffers a -20 penalty to its RR.
RUNE MASTERY.3: This spell inscribes another spell on a specially prepared piece of paper. The rune can then be used to cast the inscribed spell once (although the paper can be reused). The spell to be inscribed must be cast within one minute after the Rune spell is cast. Rune I can only inscribe 1st level spells. The rune can be set to affect the reader. The spell to be inscribed in the rune must be cast with that intent, but the caster of the Rune spell need not be the caster of the spell to be inscribed. The caster of the Rune spell and the caster of the inscribed spell must both touch the Rune Paper, that is to hold the rune.
RUNE MASTERY.4: Any Runes maneuver made by the caster has a +10 bonus.
RUNE MASTERY.5: Any attempt to read the affected rune by someone other than the caster will suffer a penalty of -50 to the Runes maneuver.
RUNE MASTERY.6: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-2nd level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.7: [RR Mod.: -30] Attempts to remove a Runic Lock from a rune. If this spell fails, the caster must gain a level before he can attempt to unlock this rune again.
RUNE MASTERY.8: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-3rd level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.9: Any Runes maneuver made by the caster has a +25 bonus.
RUNE MASTERY.10: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-5th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.11: [RR Mod: -20] A Sign can be inscribed on any “non-mobile” surface and targets the triggering being. A Sign can be triggered by one of the following (decided by caster): a) certain movements within 20’, b) certain sounds within 20’, c) touch, or d) reading. The Sign is erased when a being fails an RR against it. Sign of Shock puts the target in a state of shock for 1 min/1 failure – treat as if the target is stunned (-50).
RUNE MASTERY.12: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-6th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.13: [RR Mod: -20] As Sign of Shock, except target will flee the sign’s location for 1 min/5 failure. Note that the target’s RR is made against Essence, not against Fear.
RUNE MASTERY.14: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-7th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.15: [RR Mod: -20] As Sign of Shock, except target falls into a sleep from which he cannot be awakened for 10 min/10 failure.
RUNE MASTERY.16: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st8th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.17: [RR Mod: -20] As Sign of Shock , except target is blind for 1 hour/10 failure.
RUNE MASTERY.18: As Rune I, except caster may inscribe 1st-9th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.19: [RR Mod: -20] As Sign of Shock , except target is paralyzed and can take no action for 1 hour/10 failure.
RUNE MASTERY.20: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st-10th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.25: This spell allows for the random creation of magic items. See Treasure Law Section 5.3.
RUNE MASTERY.30: As Rune I, except caster can inscribe 1st20th level spells.
RUNE MASTERY.35: [RR Mod: -20] As Sign of Shock, except target takes one C critical of any type specified by the caster per 10 failure.
RUNE MASTERY.40: As Rune I, except it may be used to inscribe a spell up to the caster’s level.
RUNE MASTERY.50: [RR Mod: -20] As any Sign spell from this list, except that it can affect a number of targets up to the level of the caster before disappearing.

SEEMINGS.1: The caster can alter the visual appearance of an item, being, or place as perceived in the mind of one target for the duration of this spell. The other characteristics of the item, being, or place do not change. The nature of the item, place, or being does not change (e.g., a book will remain a book, a bridge will remain a bridge, etc.). The caster can set up to one conditional action per level of the caster in the Overlay spell (this action requires no concentration from the caster). The action is triggered on a given action by the target. By concentrating, the caster can exercise dynamic control over what is perceived in the target’s mind with regard to the affected item, being, or place (though the target must still be within range of the caster).
SEEMINGS.2: The caster can create a visual (static or dynamic) image (e.g., of an item, being, or place) which is perceived in the mind of one target for the duration of this spell. The caster can set up to one conditional action per level of the caster in the Seeming spell (this action requires no concentration from the caster). This action is triggered on a given action by the target. By concentrating, the caster can exercise dynamic control over what is perceived in the target’s mind with regard to the affected item, being, or place (though the target must still be within range of the caster).
SEEMINGS.3: As Overlay I, but the caster may choose one of the following options: a) an extra sense can be added to the Overlay, b) an additional target may be affected, c) the duration can be doubled, d) the range can be doubled, or e) an additional item, being, or place may be altered.
SEEMINGS.4: As Seeming I, but the caster may choose one of the following options: a) an extra sense can be added to the Seeming, b) an additional target may be affected, c) the duration can be doubled, d) the range can be doubled, or e) an additional image (of an item, being, or place) can be created.
SEEMINGS.5: As Overlay II, except the caster may choose two of the options.
SEEMINGS.6: As Seeming II, except the caster may choose two of the options.
SEEMINGS.7: This spell will allow the next Overlay or Seeming spell the caster casts to be able to affect animal targets (though the spell must be cast within one minute of completing this spell).
SEEMINGS.8: As Overlay II, except it can be delayed up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster must decide upon the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following as a trigger for the spell: a) time period, b) specified movement, c) specified sound, d) specified touch, e) specified smell, f) specified taste, or g) other specified sensation perceived by the target.
SEEMINGS.9: As Seeming II, except it can be delayed up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster must decide upon the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following as a trigger for the spell: a) time period, b) specified movement, c) specified sound, d) specified touch, e) specified smell, f) specified taste, or g) other specified sensation perceived by the target.
SEEMINGS.10: As Overlay II, except the caster may choose four of the options.
SEEMINGS.11: As Seeming II, except the caster may choose four of the options.
SEEMINGS.12: As Waiting Overlay II, except two options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.13: As Waiting Seeming II , except two options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.14: As Overlay II, except the caster may choose nine of the options.
SEEMINGS.15: As Seeming II, except the caster may choose nine of the options.
SEEMINGS.16: As Waiting Overlay II, except four options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.17: As Waiting Seeming II, except four options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.18: As Overlay II, except the caster may choose fourteen of the options.
SEEMINGS.19: As Seeming II, except the caster may choose fourteen of the options.
SEEMINGS.20: As Waiting Overlay II, except nine options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.25: As Waiting Seeming II , except nine options may be chosen.
SEEMINGS.30: As Overlay II, except the caster may choose nineteen of the options.
SEEMINGS.35: As Seeming II, except the caster may choose nineteen of the options.
SEEMINGS.40: As Waiting Overlay II, except the caster may choose nineteen of the options.
SEEMINGS.50: As Waiting Seeming II, except the caster may choose nineteen of the options. Note: For a full explanation of the operations of overlays and seemings, see Section 10.10.   

SELF HEALING.1: Caster is healed of 5 concussion hits damage.
SELF HEALING.2: Allows caster to stop bleeding on a wound that is bleeding at rate of 1 hit/round. For one hour, the caster can engage in no activity more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume at the prior rate.
SELF HEALING.3: Removes up to -30 in penalties from fatigue.
SELF HEALING.4: Allows caster to stop bleeding on a wound that is bleeding at rate of up to 3 hits/round. For one hour, the caster can engage in no activity more vigorous than walking or bleeding will resume at the prior rate.
SELF HEALING.5: Caster is relieved of 1 round’s worth of accumulated stun effects.
SELF HEALING.6: An injury incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the caster receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll. Cuts (wounds that involve both bleeding and injury penalties) must have the bleeding stopped (e.g., with Clotting) before the injury penalty may be treated (e.g., by this spell).
SELF HEALING.7: Instantly stops bleeding from 1 wound that bleeds at rate of 1 hit/round or permanently reduces bleeding from a more severe cut by 1 hit/round. This spell may be used repeatedly on the same wound and the effects are cumulative.
SELF HEALING.8: Reduces damage by 2 hits every round as long as caster concentrates; if caster is unconscious, this spell will operate without concentration.
SELF HEALING.9: As Light Wound Relie f, except an injury incurring a penalty up to 40.
SELF HEALING.10: Caster is relieved of 3 rounds of accumulated stun effects.
SELF HEALING.11: This spell will awaken the caster from sleep the round following casting (caster can preset conditions under which the spell will activate).
SELF HEALING.12: As Light Wound Relief, except an injury incurring any penalty.
SELF HEALING.13: Caster comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure.
SELF HEALING.14: As Clotting I, except caster can heal bleeding in multiple wounds up to a total hits/round of bleeding equal to his level.
SELF HEALING.15: Upon receiving a death blow, the caster goes into a state of suspended animation, until cured or killed by brain destruction.
SELF HEALING.16: This spell allows the caster to spend ten minutes in renewing his body’s blood. This allows him to gain a new RR with a +10 bonus to any blood-borne disease or poison which might be in his system. It will also stop any form of conversion process that entered through a wound (e.g., lycanthropy, vampirism). Caster remains automatically Concentrating throughout this process.
SELF HEALING.17: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb. The recovery time is 1-10 days.
SELF HEALING.18: As Regeneration I , except 6 hits are healed every round.
SELF HEALING.19: Caster’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.
SELF HEALING.20: Caster is relieved of 5 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
SELF HEALING.25: An injury incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
SELF HEALING.30: As Light Wound Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.
SELF HEALING.35: As Light Wound Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
SELF HEALING.40: The caster instantly heals all concussion hit damage, hits/round damage, accumulated stun effects, and fatigue.
SELF HEALING.50: Caster can cast one lower level spell on this list each round, so long as he concentrates. He can also entirely regenerate body parts: a muscle, tendon, or bone takes two rounds of concentration, while an organ other than the brain requires ten rounds of concentration, and a limb would take 1-10 hours, depending on how much is missing (10 hours for a full arm or leg). This does not allow regeneration of brain damage or nerve damage, except to grow new nerves in a regenerated limb. Concentration on this healing is not tiring and is maintained automatically.

SENSE CONTROL.1: Target is at -30 for all actions.
SENSE CONTROL.2: Random limb of target is numb and useless.
SENSE CONTROL.3: Target has a -100 OB modification for missile attacks and a -50 penalty for all other actions involving sight.
SENSE CONTROL.4: Causes minor false sensations in any one of smell, taste, or touch (e.g., an itch, a stinky smell, or a salty taste).
SENSE CONTROL.6: Causes target to hear any sound(s) caster desires.
SENSE CONTROL.7: Target experiences all sense impressions as distorted and confused. Target is at -25 for most actions, suffers double penalties if attempting to move faster than a walk, and has a -40 to Perception rolls.
SENSE CONTROL.8: Causes target to see whatever the caster desires.
SENSE CONTROL.9: The sensation of bright light blinds target.
SENSE CONTROL.10: Target is totally numbed; target is at -75 for all actions.
SENSE CONTROL.11: Target sees a nonexistent foe and must fight this foe until it is “defeated” (i.e., takes damage that would drop the target). Foe has same capabilities as target but does no damage (i.e., always misses).
SENSE CONTROL.12: As Hallucination, except foe will never attack. Instead, the foe will taunt the target, using full parry against any attacks by the target.
SENSE CONTROL.13: Causes false sensations in the target’s sight and hearing.
SENSE CONTROL.14: Target is troubled by sense of being watched by an unseen, sinister presence and seems to hear mysterious and disturbing whispers. These feelings distract the target, causing a -50 to all actions.
SENSE CONTROL.15: This spell overloads the target’s senses. RR failure by 50 or less results in stunning (-50) for the duration; failure by more than 50 results in unconsciousness.
SENSE CONTROL.16: Same as Sense Control II, except affects any three senses.
SENSE CONTROL.17: As Hallucination, except foe actually seems to the target to attack and do damage normally. The only actual effects are stun results and general penalties, due to the pain and disorientation that the target actually feels. However, no physical harm is done. The target does not lose hits, does not bleed or suffer broken bones, though the target will experience these things as being so while the spell lasts. The pain and confusion causing the penalties and/or stun vanish when the spell ends (target defeats the hallucination or caster stops concentrating).
SENSE CONTROL.18: Same as Sense Control II, except affects any four senses.
SENSE CONTROL.19: Causes total sensory deprivation, i.e., no sensory input. Every day of deprivation gives a 5% chance (cumulative) of a temporary insanity.
SENSE CONTROL.20: As Sense Control II, except affects five senses.
SENSE CONTROL.25: As Sense Control V, except initial range is 300' and then once the spell is in effect, the caster can control the target’s sensations at up to 1 mile/level.
SENSE CONTROL.30: As Far Control, except caster can “program” sensory input (i.e., sense X will experience sensation Y under condition Z) and concentration is only required to “program” sensory input or change current sensations and the duration is 10 min/level and all senses are controlled.
SENSE CONTROL.35: As Sense Control V, except for duration. False sense impressions fade away if the caster stops concentrating, but the caster can renew (or change them) at any time by concentrating, so long as the spell lasts and the target is within range.
SENSE CONTROL.40: As Far Control, except duration is as Long Control.
SENSE CONTROL.50: Target lives in own fantasy world, which may be totally controlled by caster when caster concentrates. While in the fantasy, the target receives no sensory input from the real world.

SENSE MASTERY.1: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of hearing. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only hearing, +25 to Perception involving hearing and other senses.
SENSE MASTERY.2: Caster can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.
SENSE MASTERY.3: Caster has a 300° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the caster is lowered to +5 and the rear bonus is lowered to +15.
SENSE MASTERY.4: Caster can check one object or place (up to a 5'R) and tell if it is an illusion or has an illusion on it.
SENSE MASTERY.5: Caster can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.
SENSE MASTERY.6: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of smell (e.g., +50 to Perception involving only smell, +25 to Perception involving smell and other senses).
SENSE MASTERY.7: As Nightvision, plus caster sees 100' in any precipitation (including thick fog) as if the air were clear.
SENSE MASTERY.8: Caster gains extreme tactile sensitivity. This provides a bonus to any action requiring a sense of touch. For example, caster might receive +25 to picking locks, disarming traps, opening secret doors, etc. and +50 to Perception involving only touch, +25 to Perception involving touch and other senses).
SENSE MASTERY.9: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.
SENSE MASTERY.10: Caster can pick a point up to 100'/ level away and hear as if at that point (there can be intervening objects such as walls); the caster must have visited the point before or have the location in sight. Note that this means that the caster does not have hearing at his own location.
SENSE MASTERY.11: As Mentalist Ear, except caster can see instead of listening.
SENSE MASTERY.12: While this spell is in effect, one illusion within the caster’s range ceases to exist for the caster only. The caster must specify the illusion to be negated (unless caster knows something is an illusion, this spell would be gambling PP).
SENSE MASTERY.13: As Detect Illusion, except caster can concentrate on one object or place per round.
SENSE MASTERY.14: As all lower level Vision spells functioning at the same time.
SENSE MASTERY.15: As Nightvision, except range is the normal daylight vision range.
SENSE MASTERY.16: As Mentalist Ear, except caster can use all senses at the remote point.
SENSE MASTERY.17: As Disillusion, except all illusions within range cease to exist for the caster.
SENSE MASTERY.18: As Watervision, except range is the normal daylight vision range.
SENSE MASTERY.19: As Fogvision, except range is the normal daylight vision range.
SENSE MASTERY.20: As all lower level Vision spells functioning at the same time.
SENSE MASTERY.25: As Mentalist Ear, except range is 1 mile/level.
SENSE MASTERY.30: As Mentalist Eye, except range is 1 mile/level.
SENSE MASTERY.35: As Mentalist Senses, except range is 1 mile/level.
SENSE MASTERY.40: Caster can view objects within 2' at up to 40x magnification.
SENSE MASTERY.50: Caster can “merge” senses with those of a subject whose location is known by spells or by prior arrangement. Both caster and target will have a full awareness of both locations; the spell provides for this not being terribly confusing. If target is not at the specified location, the PP for this spell are wasted.

SENSE MOLDING.1: Fills the area of effect with a set of immobile smells. The smells are real (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally only be accomplished through spells or the use of a sense other than smell.
SENSE MOLDING.2: Target is struck with the equivalent of a (Medium-sized) bare fist (Martial Arts Striking attack table). The attack is invisible unless combined with the visual sense in an Illusion or Phantasms. Directed Spells: Illusionary Attacks skill may be developed for this attack.
SENSE MOLDING.3: As Smell Mirage, except a set of tastes can be created in a 10'R.
SENSE MOLDING.4: Creates a 10'R cloud of noxious gas that will stun anyone failing to resist (all targets must make RRs each round they are in the cloud). Cloud drifts with the wind. Targets are stunned (-50) 1 round per 10 failure.
SENSE MOLDING.5: As Smell Mirage, except all the objects and surfaces in a 10'R can be made to feel differently than they really are. Striking an object or surface will cancel the spell for that object or surface only.
SENSE MOLDING.6: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has either its taste, its smell, or the way its surface feels altered for the duration of the spell. Note that the visual aspects of the object do not change.
SENSE MOLDING.7: As Strike I, except range increment is 75'.
SENSE MOLDING.8: Creates a dulling of tactile sensations in the target, especially the hands. Fumble ranges of weapons are increased by 2, applied after any reduction for number of ranks, and all tasks requiring tactile feedback (e.g., picking a lock, dancing with a partner, playing the piano) are at -25, while Perception rolls for the sense of touch are at -50.
SENSE MOLDING.9: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 20'.
SENSE MOLDING.10: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck three times before the feeling is cancelled.
SENSE MOLDING.11: As Strike I, except range increment is 125'.
SENSE MOLDING.12: All of the terrain in the area of effect will feel differently than it normally would. The caster may designate in what way the terrain feels differently. For example, flat ground could be made to feel rocky or rocky ground could be made to feel flat.
SENSE MOLDING.13: As many objects as the caster’s level can be given individual smells and tastes.
SENSE MOLDING.14: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 50'.
SENSE MOLDING.15: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck five times before the feeling is cancelled.
SENSE MOLDING.16: As many objects as the caster’s level can be given individual feelings to the touch. Striking an object or surface will cancel the spell for that object only.
SENSE MOLDING.17: As Strike I, except one attack can be made each round. Target may be changed from one round to another.
SENSE MOLDING.18: As Feel Mirage I , except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck eight times before the feeling is cancelled.
SENSE MOLDING.19: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 100'.
SENSE MOLDING.20: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck ten times before the feeling is cancelled.
SENSE MOLDING.25: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent scent.
SENSE MOLDING.30: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent taste.
SENSE MOLDING.35: As Tear Cloud I, except radius and range are as listed.
SENSE MOLDING.40: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent feel to its surface.
SENSE MOLDING.50: As Smell Mirage, Taste Mirage, and Feel Mirage I, except all changes are permanent on every affected object and surface unless it is moved beyond the area of effect.

SENSES.1: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of hearing. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only hearing, +25 to Perception involving hearing and other senses.
SENSES.2: Caster can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.
SENSES.3: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of smell. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only smell, +25 to Perception involving smell and other senses.
SENSES.4: One location is “marked” so that the caster can find it again later with a Far Sense spell. The marker is automatically located when the caster casts a Far Sense spell if the marker is within range of the Far Sense spell. A marker can be placed on any inanimate object. The caster can only have as many markers as he has ranks in this list; a Sensory Marker spell can be cancelled even if the caster is no longer within range of the marker.
SENSES.5: Caster may designate any one of his senses to be used at any “marked” location within range (see Sensory Marker). The caster can use any perception abilities he has at the time (e.g., if he is currently under the effects of Nightvision, he will have Nightvision at the marked location). The remote sensory point may be rotated to face any direction. While he is sensing at the marked location, the caster must remain immobile and concentrate (and does not have that sense available at his own location).
SENSES.6: Caster has a 300° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the caster is lowered to +5 and the rear bonus is lowered to +15.
SENSES.7: Caster gains extreme tactile sensitivity. This provides a bonus to any action requiring a sense of touch. For example, caster might receive +25 to picking locks, disarming traps, opening secret doors, etc. and +50 to Perception involving only touch, +25 to Perception involving touch and other senses).
SENSES.8: Caster can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.
SENSES.9: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.
SENSES.10: As Far Sense I, except caster may designate up to two senses for use at the marked location.
SENSES.11: Caster can see through wood (up to 1" per level).
SENSES.12: Caster can see in any brightness of light (including that which would be blinding or glaring). This spell does not work in the absence of light.
SENSES.13: Caster can see through any woven organic substance (up to 1" per level).
SENSES.14: As Far Sense I , except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).
SENSES.15: As Far Sense I, except for range.
SENSES.16: Caster can see through stone (up to 1" per level).
SENSES.17: Caster knows the location (distance and direction) of one chosen Sensory Marker relative to the caster’s current location.
SENSES.18: As Far Sense II, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).
SENSES.19: Caster can see through metal (up to 1" per level).
SENSES.20: As Far Sense II, except for range.
SENSES.25: As all Vision spells on this list operating at the same time.
SENSES.30: Caster has the effects of Sly Ears, Scent, Touch, and all Vision spells simultaneously.
SENSES.35: Caster has a warning of any impending danger.
