This will give the caster a second chance to notice any trap he has missed before actually triggering it, prevent any attacker from gaining the bonus for Surprise or succeeding in using Ambush skill against the caster, and should provide similar protection against other sudden danger (GM’s discretion).
SENSES.40: As Greater Far Sense I, except caster can use all senses and the range is improved.
SENSES.50: Caster may use any lower level spell on this list once per round.

SHIELD MASTERY.1: Gives a +10 magical bonus to a non-magical shield, enhancing the wielder’s Shield skill.
SHIELD MASTERY.2: Creates an invisible force shield in front of the caster; it subtracts 25 from up to 3 attacks per round from the caster’s front. This does not occupy a hand, but is not usable with a physical shield (nor does it benefit from the Shield skill).
SHIELD MASTERY.3: Causes target to appear blurred to attackers, granting a +10 to the target’s DB.
SHIELD MASTERY.4: Caster temporarily enchants one non-magical set of clothes (not armor) to confer a +10 DB. Wearer must be at his natural AT to gain the bonus.
SHIELD MASTERY.5: Caster can deflect any one missile that the caster can see and which passes within the 100' range of this spell; this causes 50 to be subtracted from the missile’s attack.
SHIELD MASTERY.6: Caster temporarily enchants one non-magical organic armor to confer a +10 DB. Wearer’s AT must come from this armor to gain the bonus.
SHIELD MASTERY.7: As Deflections I, except its effect is against 1 melee attack.
SHIELD MASTERY.8: As Enchanted Shield, accept range is 50' and one shield per caster’s level can be enchanted.
SHIELD MASTERY.9: As Deflections I, except 2 missiles may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.10: As Deflections I, except missile automatically misses.
SHIELD MASTERY.11: As Bladeturn I, except 2 melee attacks may
SHIELD MASTERY.12: Caster temporarily enchants one non-magical inorganic (usually metal) armor to confer a +10 DB. Wearer’s AT must come from this armor to gain the bonus.
SHIELD MASTERY.13: As Deflections I, except 3 missiles may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.14: As Shield, except bonus is 40 against up to 4 attacks per round and duration is 10 minutes per level.
SHIELD MASTERY.15: As Aim Untrue I, except 2 missiles may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.16: As Blur, except as many targets as the caster’s level may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.17: As Bladeturn I, except 3 melee attacks may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.18: One missile passing within 100' of caster is reversed and makes an attack on its source (use caster’s ranks in this list as OB, no DB except from magic or armor material bonus).
SHIELD MASTERY.19: As Aim Untrue I, except 3 missiles may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.20: As Deflections I, except as many missiles as the caster’s level may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.25: As Bladeturn I, except as many melee attacks as the caster’s level may be affected.
SHIELD MASTERY.30: As Mass Deflections, except missiles automatically miss.
SHIELD MASTERY.35: As Mass Deflections or Mass Bladeturn, except caster may mix defending against melee or missile attacks, to a combined total up to the caster’s level.
SHIELD MASTERY.40: As Enchanted Shield, Enchanted Clothes, Enchanted Organic, and/or Enchanted Inorganic except as many targets as the caster’s level may be affected. An individual may have both clothing/armor and shield enchanted for double protection, although this counts as two targets.
SHIELD MASTERY.50: As Re-aiming, except the caster may reverse any number of missiles passing within 100'. Note: When casting the Bladeturn/Deflections/Aim Untrue spells, the caster can concentrate in order to hold the spell until it can be applied to the allowed number of targets (See “Delaying a Spell” in Section 4.8).   

SHIFTING.1: Adds +50 to any rolls for any moving maneuvers performed at a walking pace or less (e.g., walking a 3” beam).
SHIFTING.2: Allows caster to disjoint limbs, contract muscles, etc.; in order to escape manacles and ropes and slip through small places (+50 to +100 to escape maneuvers).
SHIFTING.3: Alters the caster’s face to resemble someone else, but not so closely as to be confused on a close inspection.
SHIFTING.4: Allows caster to analyze the form of another being for future use with True Change. The caster can only have as many forms analyzed as he has ranks in this list. Caster may choose to forget a form at any time to make room for replacements.
SHIFTING.5: Caster can breathe water but not air.
SHIFTING.6: Alters the caster’s flesh and bones to a more elastic form, reducing by one the severity of all “Krush” and “Impact” criticals taken.
SHIFTING.7: Alters the caster’s entire body to the form of another humanoid race. The form cannot be that of a particular individual form. For example, a human caster could take the form of a Dwarf, but not the Dwarven High Chancellor. Only the physical form is gained (e.g., a target who becomes a Dwarf would not receive the Dwarves’ ability to see in the dark). This spell does not allow the caster to change his Size category, but size may be changed as desired within that limit. This does not change anything the caster carries or wears, only his own body, except that prosthetics added by the Lay Healer Base list Prosthetics will change with the caster.
SHIFTING.8: Caster can breathe any gas as normal air.
SHIFTING.9: Caster’s body is able to adapt to extremely high or low pressure or sudden changes in pressure, without suffering any harm. This does not apply to crushing by solids, only pressures from environmental gasses or liquids.
SHIFTING.10: As Face Shifting, except duration is 1 hour/level.
SHIFTING.11: Caster can breathe water, air, and any gas at will.
SHIFTING.12: Alters the caster’s Size by one category. This does not change anything the caster carries or wears, only his own body, except that prosthetics added by the Lay Healer Base list Prosthetics will change with the caster.
SHIFTING.13: As Changing to Kind , except alteration can be to any organic form within the caster’s own Size category; caster does not obtain any special abilities.
SHIFTING.14: As Waterlungs, except duration is one hour/level.
SHIFTING.15: Caster becomes as if made of very dense stone (cannot move or cast spells).
SHIFTING.16: Caster does not need to breathe at all.
SHIFTING.17: As Change, except altered form can be that of a specific being analyzed by Change Lore.
SHIFTING.18: As True Change, except caster may assume a different form anytime during the spell; each change of shape takes one round of concentration.
SHIFTING.19: As Waterlungs plus Depth Mastery, except duration is one hour/level.
SHIFTING.20: Caster takes the form of a liquid mass, able to seep through cracks as well as water. In this form, the caster can move through water at 10 mph, but is unable to cast spells.
SHIFTING.25: Caster takes the form of a mist, able to fly 20 mph (modified by ambient winds, of course), seep through cracks, extend self to become virtually invisible, etc. (may not cast spells in this form).
SHIFTING.30: As Waterform and Mistform, except the two forms are interchangeable at will (concentrate for 1 round to change).
SHIFTING.35: As Waterform, but caster can cast spells with a -10 penalty.
SHIFTING.40: As Mistform, but caster can cast spells with a -10 penalty.
SHIFTING.50: As Changing and Form Master, except all forms are interchangeable at will (concentrate for 1 round to change). The caster’s Size may be changed by one category.

SMOKE AND MIRRORS.1: Makes one surface of an inanimate object up to 10'x10' perfectly reflective in the orientation desired by the caster, e.g. to permit viewing around a corner.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.2: Creates a cloud of obscuring smoke (caster’s choice of color) which obscures as heavy fog (-50 Perception, visibility reduced to 50').
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.3: Creates any simple, immobile visual image/scene in an area of up to 10'R, or creates a set of immobile sounds in an area of up to 10'R.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.4: Caster gains a +25 Perception bonus to notice that which is hidden by any means (including noticing illusions).
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.5: Creates a simple, immobile image or scene up to an area of 10'R. Two of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, or e) the illusion can cast light. All parts of the area of effect must be in the caster’s field of vision at the time of casting.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.6: Creates an area of darkness up to 2'R/lvl about the point touched. The darkness is equal to the darkest night. (See RMCL Section 15.7) If the point is on a mobile object or being, it will move with the object/being (but any target gets an RR to avoid having it “stick” to them). One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within range of the spell), f) the phantasm can cast light, or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion).
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.7: Creates the image of one object or being that will move however the caster wants (as long as the caster concentrates). When the caster stops concentrating, the image remains but stops moving. The caster may resume concentration again later, and make the image move again (presuming that the duration has not expired). The image can be of any size that would fit in a 10'R. One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within range of the spell), f) the phantasm can cast light, or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion).
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.8: Instantly extinguishes a fire up to the size of a campfire.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.9: The next spell cast from this list (must be within 1 minute) will have its effect delayed up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster must decide upon the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following: a) time period, b) specified movements, or c) specified sounds.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.10: Instantly ignites any one thing intended to burn, e.g. it will light a lantern, torch, firewood, but not other items such as furniture or clothing.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.11: As Subtle Eye I, except bonus is +50.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.12: As Illusion II, except four options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.13: As Phantasms II, except two options may be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.14: As Extinguish Minor, except fires up to 10' in diameter.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.15: As Extinguish Minor, except all minor fires in the area of effect.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.16: As Illusion II, except seven options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.17: As Smoke Cloud, but area of effect is 10'/lvl R.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.18: As Phantasms II, except three options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.19: Immediately extinguishes all fire within 5’/lvl radius.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.20: As Ignite Fuel, except it will ignite all suitable things of the specified type (or all suitable types) within the radius.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.25: As Phantasms II, except four options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.30: As Illusion II, except nine options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.35: As Subtle Eye I, except bonus is +100.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.40: As Illusions II, except 14 options can be chosen.
SMOKE AND MIRRORS.50: As Phantasms II, except nine options can be chosen.

SOLID ALTERATION.1: If the caster holds one end of a rope, he can cause the rope to move up to its length in any direction and to tie itself in knots (it cannot attack or tie up a living being).
SOLID ALTERATION.2: Warms 1 cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material per level up to 500°F (260°C) at a rate of 1 cubic foot heated by 100°F (56°C) per round of concentration (without concentration, the material will remain at its current temperature for the remainder of the duration). The caster is immune to this heat.
SOLID ALTERATION.3: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -20°F (-29°C), at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round (cooling spreads evenly from initial point of cooling unless caster concentrates to direct spread).
SOLID ALTERATION.4: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.
SOLID ALTERATION.5: Can cause a door to expand or contract due to subtle temperature changes; door can be jammed or loosened (a special modification of +/- 50 to maneuvers to force the door can be granted).
SOLID ALTERATION.6: Causes any wood to ignite and burn. All wood ignited must be within 1' of caster’s palm.
SOLID ALTERATION.7: Causes any one object of up to 1 cubic foot per level of the caster to be much more resistant to damage. It will resist destructive spells as if the RR was being made by the caster and it will have a +50 bonus to any normal rolls against breakage.
SOLID ALTERATION.8: As Heat Solid I, except the range is 50'.
SOLID ALTERATION.9: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -200°F (-129°C), at a rate of (1 cubic foot and -100°F/56°C)/round. The caster must concentrate
SOLID ALTERATION.10: Creates a wall of ice up to 10'x10'x (2' at base, 1' at top); it can be melted through or chipped through or toppled if not against a wall. Requires a source of water or ice within 10'. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.
SOLID ALTERATION.11: Creates a doorway through any solid, inanimate material; it can be up to 3'x6'x1'.
SOLID ALTERATION.12: Will turn 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth, 100 cubic feet of earth to mud, 100 cubic feet of mud to earth, or 100 cubic feet of earth to stone. The transformation takes 3 rounds to complete.
SOLID ALTERATION.13: Causes a non-metal, inanimate object to shatter (up to 1 cubic foot in size). All within 5' radius take an A Impact critical; anyone holding the object takes a C Impact critical.
SOLID ALTERATION.14: As Solid Door I, except size is 4'x8'x5'
SOLID ALTERATION.15: Caster’s hands can mold up to one cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material as if it were putty or clay. The material hardens to normal consistency when the caster is finished. Quality of results depends on caster’s skill and time invested.
SOLID ALTERATION.16: One cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material becomes very brittle. This causes the object to break if struck (the GM may rule that certain objects get an RR to resist breakage).
SOLID ALTERATION.17: Disintegrates (i.e., destroys without a residue) a target of up to 100 cubic feet of stone.
SOLID ALTERATION.18: As Strengthen Material, except object can be up to 100 cubic feet per level in size.
SOLID ALTERATION.20: Creates a tunnel through solid, inanimate material that is 4' in diameter and 5'/level long.
SOLID ALTERATION.25: As Solid Door , except size is 6'x12'x10'.
SOLID ALTERATION.30: As Solid Tunnel, except that it is permanent and size is 3'x6'x (1'/level).
SOLID ALTERATION.35: As Mold Solid, but working on a larger scale, suitable for monuments, architectural components, etc.
SOLID ALTERATION.40: Caster becomes able to work non-magical metals with his bare hands, needing no other tools to give it the shape and qualities desired (gaining a +20 to any appropriate maneuvers rolled). This includes being able to create alloys if the necessary components are available. If this spell is used in conjunction with proper equipment, a +35 bonus may be gained to appropriate maneuvers.
SOLID ALTERATION.50: Transmutes up to 1 ounce of any solid material into another non-magical solid material that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work.

SOLID DESTRUCTION.1: Melts up to 10 cubic feet/level of ice.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.2: Loosens 100 cubic feet of earth to the consistency of plowed ground.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.3: Causes a 100 cubic feet/level section of inorganic material to erode and deteriorate at 1000 x normal rate.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.4: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.5: Will vaporize a non-magical door up to 6" thick, 10' high, and 10' wide (if the door is thicker than 6" it will vaporize the closest 6 inches).
SOLID DESTRUCTION.6: Changes 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth. This change is gradual, taking three rounds to complete.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.7: Causes one non-living wooden object to burst apart in a spray of splinters. All within a 5' radius take an A Puncture critical and holder takes a C Puncture critical.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.8: As Stone/Earth, except changes earth to soft mud.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.9: Loosens 43,560 cubic feet of earth (1 acre-foot) to the consistency of plowed ground.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.10: Creates a 3'x6' corridor that is up to 1'/level long; it can be cut through any non-metal, inorganic material. The corridor can be cut at a rate of 1' (length) per round as the caster concentrates.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.11: Causes a non-metal, inanimate object to shatter (up to 1 cubic foot in size). All within 5' radius take an A Impact critical; anyone holding the object takes a C Impact critical.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.14: As Undoor I, except any single door is vaporized.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.16: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot of any inanimate solid.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.17: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.18: As Corridor, except that it can cut through non-magical metal.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.19: Causes a large crack to open in the ground. The size of the crack will be up to 10' wide, up to 100' deep, and up to 200' long. The crack takes 2 rounds to open.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.20: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot per level of any inanimate solid.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.25: Causes a very minor earthquake which could cause shoddy construction to collapse (and can be very terrifying). This is approximately 5.5 on the Richter scale.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.30: Causes a large crevasse to open in the ground (up to 10' per level deep, 1' per level wide and 20' per level long). The crevasse takes 2 rounds to open. The caster can concentrate for 3 rounds to close the crevasse.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.35: As Shatter, but affects up to 1 target per level (each individual target restricted in size as per Shatter). If someone is within attack radius of more than one shattered object, multiple criticals are possible.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.40: As Metal Corridor, except that it can cut through even magical metal. Note that this is a Force spell so the magical metal will normally get an RR.
SOLID DESTRUCTION.50: Caster can cause an earthquake; with the caster’s location at the time of casting as the epicenter. The quake can be delayed up to 1 round/level. The severity of the quake on the Richter scale is determined by a roll: (0120) = 5.5, (21-45) = 6, (46-65) = 6.5, (66-80) = 7.0, (81-90) = 7.5, (91-95) = 8.0, (96-98) = 8.5, (99-100) = 9.0.

SOLID ELEMENTS.1: Caster transforms up to one cubic foot per level of clay into baked pottery. Firing clay in this manner will always be successful. This can also cook glazes onto earthenware. Quality of product depends on the skill of whomever shapes the clay.
SOLID ELEMENTS.2: Creates a cube of ice, 1' along each edge. It can be formed around an inanimate object, so long as that object cannot resist or fails its RR.
SOLID ELEMENTS.3: Caster may add +15 to all maneuvers to work ice or stone.
SOLID ELEMENTS.4: The caster may decrease the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by 50°F [28°C] per round of concentration. Once the liquid freezes, it will not continue to grow colder, but will begin to warm naturally if the caster stops concentrating. Caster is immune to the cold thus generated.
SOLID ELEMENTS.5: A single inanimate solid object of no more than 1 pound/level weight is made stronger. It will take only half as many hits if damaged and if checked for breakage, it receives a +50 bonus to its roll.
SOLID ELEMENTS.6: Creates a wall of packed earth up to 10'x10'x (3' at base, 1' at top); it can be dug through only (20 manrounds at top).
SOLID ELEMENTS.7: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.
SOLID ELEMENTS.8: Caster may add +30 to all maneuvers to work ice or stone.
SOLID ELEMENTS.9: Creates a wall of non-precious stone up to 10'x10'x1'; it can be chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
SOLID ELEMENTS.10: A bolt of ice is shot from the palm of the caster. Results are resolved on the Ice Bolt Table.
SOLID ELEMENTS.11: Target weapon, which must remain in the caster’s possession or the spell is cancelled, glitters with frost. Neither weapon nor wielder is harmed, but any time the weapon delivers a critical an additional Cold critical of equal severity is also inflicted.
SOLID ELEMENTS.12: Creates a wall of ice up to 10'x10'x (2' at base, 1' at top); it can be melted through or chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.
SOLID ELEMENTS.13: As Earthwall, except duration is permanent.
SOLID ELEMENTS.14: Caster may add +45 to all maneuvers to work ice or stone.
SOLID ELEMENTS.15: As Icewall I, except size is up to 20'x20'x (4' at base, 2' at top).
SOLID ELEMENTS.16: As Ice Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
SOLID ELEMENTS.17: As Stonewall, except duration is permanent and dimensions may be up to 20'x20'x1'.
SOLID ELEMENTS.18: Chills metal to the point that it becomes extremely brittle (1 object only), giving it a -50 penalty to its strength and the object will be subject to a breakage check if it strikes another object or is struck. Anyone in direct skin-to-metal contact with the chilled metal will take a B Cold Critical each round contact is maintained.
SOLID ELEMENTS.19: Caster may add +60 to all maneuvers to work ice or stone.
SOLID ELEMENTS.20: As Ice Bolt I , except range increment is 125'.
SOLID ELEMENTS.25: Caster may add +75 to all maneuvers to work ice or stone.
SOLID ELEMENTS.40: Creates a wall of bronze up to 20'x10'x (4' at base, 2' at top).
SOLID ELEMENTS.50: Caster can hurl an Ice Bolt (as Ice Bolt V) each round (as an instantaneous action) for up to one round for every five levels of the caster.

SOLID MANIPULATION.1: Warms 1 cubic foot of stone per level up to 100°F (38°C) at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round of concentration (without concentration, the material will remain at its current temperature for the remainder of the duration).
SOLID MANIPULATION.2: As Warm Stone, except 6 cubic inches of metal/level can be warmed at a rate of 6 cubic inches per round of concentration.
SOLID MANIPULATION.3: As Warm Stone, except can affect any solid, inanimate, non-metal material.
SOLID MANIPULATION.4: As Warm Stone except temperature limit is 500°F (260°C) and it takes 1 round per 100°F (56°C). Caster need only touch the target when the spell is cast. The caster is immune to this heat. The caster must concentrate to increase the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).
SOLID MANIPULATION.5: As Heat Stone, except 6 cubic inches of metal/level can be heated.
SOLID MANIPULATION.6: As Heat Stone, except affects any solid, inanimate, non-metal material.
SOLID MANIPULATION.7: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -20°F (-29°C), at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round (cooling spreads evenly from initial point of cooling unless caster concentrates to direct spread).
SOLID MANIPULATION.8: As Cool Solid, except 6 cubic inches of metal/level can be cooled.
SOLID MANIPULATION.9: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -200°F (-129°C), at a rate of (1 cu' and -100°F[-56°C])/round. The caster must concentrate to decrease the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).
SOLID MANIPULATION.10: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to a 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.
SOLID MANIPULATION.11: As Heat Other Solid, except the only limit to the temperature is the melting point of the solid (i.e., until the solid melts) or sublimation point if the solid converts directly to a gas when heated. Will affect 6 cu"/level of metal or 1 cu'/level of any other material.
SOLID MANIPULATION.12: As Chill Solid, except affects metal to a limit of six cubic inches per level and a like rate of chilling.
SOLID MANIPULATION.13: As Cracks Call, except material with cracks will crumble to dust.
SOLID MANIPULATION.14: Will break a piece of wood up to one foot in diameter (6" radius).
SOLID MANIPULATION.16: As Stone Door, except doorway can be through metal.
SOLID MANIPULATION.17: As Bowbreak, except a piece of metal can be broken, up to 3" in diameter.
SOLID MANIPULATION.18: Causes a lock to break; it can be broken in a locked or unlocked state.
SOLID MANIPULATION.19: As Stone Door, except doorway can be in any inanimate, solid material and it can be 3'x6' with a depth of 1'/level for stone and 3"/level for all other materials.
SOLID MANIPULATION.20: Caster’s hands can mold up to one cubic foot of stone as if it were putty or clay. Stone hardens to normal consistency when the caster is finished. Quality of results depends on caster’s skill and time invested.
SOLID MANIPULATION.25: As Mold Stone, except caster may mold metal.
SOLID MANIPULATION.30: As Mold Stone, except caster may mold any inanimate, solid material.
SOLID MANIPULATION.35: Caster can repair any flaws in a piece of stone, whether cracks, blemishes, or other imperfections, including the flaws that reduce the value of precious stones. The stone affected cannot be larger than one cubic foot per level.
SOLID MANIPULATION.40: As Mold Solid, but working on a larger scale, suitable for monuments, architectural components, etc.
SOLID MANIPULATION.50: Transmutes up to 1 ounce of any solid material into another non-magical solid material that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work.

SOUL DESTRUCTION.1: Target must answer a single concept question (presuming that the target can understand the question).
SOUL DESTRUCTION.2: Target is possessed by a Type I Demon; caster has no control over the target or Demon. The Demon will force the target to do random (not necessarily destructive) things. Target gets an RR every other round to attempt to get rid of the Demon.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.3: Target has an extreme dislike for any specific thing the caster chooses. Target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of overcoming the neurosis when given a choice (e.g., neurosis concerning horses would mean that every time the target tried to get on or near a horse the target would need to make a roll in order to do so).
SOUL DESTRUCTION.4: Target experiences chronic guilt over some action in his past. The GM should choose some significant event in the target’s past. The target will not perform the same or similar actions again and must take steps to alleviate the guilt (acting to rectify the past wrong or, if that is impossible, similar wrongs).
SOUL DESTRUCTION.5: Target will trust no one absolutely. When in a life threatening/ dangerous situation, there is a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) that the target will not trust the protection of others and will act unilaterally for self-preservation.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.6: As Possession I, except Demon is Type II and the possessed only gets an RR once every minute.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.7: Target flees in total panic from caster. Fleeing equates to movement away at maximum pace for 1 round per 5 failure of the target’s RR.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.8: Target’s “soul” and the caster’s “soul” are interchanged; the caster can operate at half of his normal activity (double all AP costs as with concentration) while in the body of the target. The target (in the caster’s body) is inactive. The caster can cancel the spell at any time (takes one round). The target can cancel the spell if he makes a suc -
SOUL DESTRUCTION.9: This spell torments a spirit, typically a conjured being like an elemental or demon. These spirits may be targeted even if in an incorporeal form or if possessing another’s body. The GM may wish to include some undead among potential targets. The pain caused by this spell reduces the target to 2 AP per round and the target takes 1 hit per 1 failure on the RR as well. These hits do not return at the end of the spell duration; that aspect of the spell is “permanent”.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.10: As Possession I, except Demon is Type III and the possessed target only gets an RR once every 10 minutes.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.11: Part of the target’s “soul” is taken and placed in an organic object on the caster’s person. The body of the target will act as the caster directs (caster must concentrate).The spell ends when: the caster cancels it or the object is destroyed or the body is more than 100' from caster or the object leaves the caster’s person. If the caster is not concentrating the target may act freely, though at -30 to all activities. While being commanded, the target is aware of all the actions performed, but is helpless to stop them.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.12: Part of the target’s soul is transferred to a random object somewhere within 100 miles; the target is at -30 to all activities until the spell is dispelled or the target touches the object (which returns the missing piece of soul). The target will always know the direction of the lost “soul”.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.13: As Possession I, except Demon is Type IV and the possessed target only gets an RR once every hour.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.14: As Word of Panic, except affects everyone within 20’ except the caster.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.15: [RR Mod.: -10] Target must make an RR against Fear (use the highest level foe or GM’s judgment for other perils as level of Fear) at -25 in any personally dangerous situation.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.16: [RR Mod.: +10] As Spirit Burn, except if target fails RR it must retreat to its own plane of existence, if applicable, or it will be transported instantly to its “home” location on this plane and is reduced to zero hits.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.17: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.18: As Possession I, except Demon is Type V and the possessed target only gets an RR once every day.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.19: Target is put into a state of deep sleep and will neither awaken nor age. The target will only die if killed (does not need food, water, etc.). This sleep will continue until dispelled or until a single, non-magical event (specified by the caster at the time of casting) is accomplished. Note that the caster must specify the non-magical condition (i.e., it is not optional).
SOUL DESTRUCTION.20: As Subjugation, except the “soul” is only released if the caster cancels the spell or the object is destroyed. The target is always aware of the direction of the object holding the stolen “soul”.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.25: As Transferal, except target only gets his additional RRs once per month and every time he fails his chance to successfully resist decreases by 1 and the caster may operate normally in the target’s body.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.30: [RR Mod: -20] Target’s “soul” is torn from the target’s body (or other physical location if the soul is otherwise housed); it remains in whatever place “souls” go for 30 days per 10 failure. The soul can be returned early only through Lifegiving or Reuniting. Target is unconscious until soul is restored; even subconscious activity (i.e., healing, etc.) suffers a -75 penalty.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.35: As Possession I, except Demon is Type VI and the possessed target only gets an RR once every week.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.40: [RR Mod: -20] As Long Absolution , except “soul” can only be brought back through Lifegiving.
SOUL DESTRUCTION.50: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution Pure , except “soul” is utterly destroyed. Note: Neurosis, Guilt, Paranoia, Unsoul Curse and Chronic Panic are curses, with mental effects. See Section 10.2 for treatment options. Absolution does not kill the body, so Dark Absolution leaves a living, but soulless body.   

SOUND CONTROL.1: Creates a 1'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As discussed in Section 10.17, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Quiet - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Quiet radius, however).
SOUND CONTROL.2: Caster may manipulate sound within the area of effect, creating any single sound desired. This sound may be heard outside the area of effect, if the caster wishes. Sound cannot be damaging.
SOUND CONTROL.3: For the duration of this spell, all sounds in the area of effect are no louder than a whisper. The caster is immune to the effects of this spell. In addition, the caster can designate up to one extra target per level to be immune to the effects of the spell. By concentrating for one round, the caster may make one target immune to the effect or remove one target’s immunity.
SOUND CONTROL.4: A fanfare of music plays. The caster may designate one instrument (that will make the sounds of the fanfare) for every five levels of experience (or portion thereof). The volume of the fanfare cannot exceed that which would be normally made by the designated instrument(s).
SOUND CONTROL.5: As Quiet I, except radius is 10'.
SOUND CONTROL.6: As Sonic Law I, except radius is 10'.
SOUND CONTROL.7: Causes a very loud, sudden sound next to the target’s ears; target is stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.
SOUND CONTROL.8: Target cannot hear sounds occurring more than 6" from the target’s ears.
SOUND CONTROL.9: Certain sound waves cause any cracks or flaws in solid, inanimate material (up to 1,000 cu') to expand to their limit.
SOUND CONTROL.10: Caster may designate any music to be played as if the caster were playing it (and the caster must actually have played the music at least once). The music will play continuously for the duration of the spell. If the musical score finishes before the duration is over, the music will repeat as many times as needed. The caster can designate singing instead of an instrument.
SOUND CONTROL.11: As Sphere of Silence I , except radius is 50'.
SOUND CONTROL.12: As Sonic Law I, except radius is 100'.
SOUND CONTROL.13: Causes a nonmetal, rigid, inanimate object to shatter (up to 1 cubic foot in size). All within a 5'R take an A Impact critical, anyone holding the object takes a C critical.
SOUND CONTROL.14: A set series of sounds can be concentrated upon and then delayed up to 24 hours or until triggered by a specified sound. The sounds to be delayed may last up to 1 minute per level.
SOUND CONTROL.15: As Music, except caster may designate one instrument per five levels of experience (or portion thereof) that will play simultaneously. All instruments (including vocals) will play as if the caster were playing them.
SOUND CONTROL.16: As Sonic Law I, except radius is 10' per level of the caster.
SOUND CONTROL.17: As Sudden Sound, except for range.
SOUND CONTROL.18: All beings (except the caster) within range capable of hearing ultrasonic sounds are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; an RR must be made once per round while in the radius. Even if they are not stunned, animals capable of hearing ultrasonics will have to make a second, non-magical RR against Fear (level of Fear is half the caster’s level).
SOUND CONTROL.19: First, this spell will clean and tune an instrument instantly; this effect is “permanent”. This spell cannot repair actually broken or missing parts; if new strings are supplied, it can use them to replace broken ones, for example. Second, the sound quality of the instrument is improved to the equivalent of a +10 quality instrument if it was below that value. Third, if the caster spends a round in concentration, the instrument can be made to sound like any other instrument with which the caster is familiar.
SOUND CONTROL.20: As Cracks, except material with cracks will crumble to dust.
SOUND CONTROL.25: As Sphere of Silence I , except radius is 100'.
SOUND CONTROL.30: As Minor Ultrasonics, except for radius.
SOUND CONTROL.35: Caster launches a concentrated burst of sound at the target, doing a D Impact critical.
SOUND CONTROL.40: As Sudden Sound, except affects up to one target per caster level.
SOUND CONTROL.50: As Sonic Law I, except parameters are as listed and sounds can be programmed, the sounds may be delayed (up to 24 hr), there can be more than one sound at a time, and sounds are instantly changeable.

SOUND MOLDING.1: Creates a set of immobile sounds in an area of up to 10'R. The sounds are real (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally only be accomplished through spells or the use of a sense other than hearing.
SOUND MOLDING.2: Creates a 10'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As noted above, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Sphere of Silence - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Sphere of Silence radius, however).
SOUND MOLDING.3: Caster can control the intensity of sounds within the range; the intensity can vary from none to a very loud shout, and it can be different in different parts of the radius.
SOUND MOLDING.4: Causes a very loud, sudden sound next to the target’s ears; target is stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.
SOUND MOLDING.5: As Sound Control I, except radius is 50'.
SOUND MOLDING.6: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its sound altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes.
SOUND MOLDING.7: Target cannot hear sounds occurring more than 6" from the target’s ears (or other organs of hearing), and the target’s own voice cannot be heard from more than 6" away from the target’s mouth (or other organ of speech).
SOUND MOLDING.8: As Sphere of Silence I, except radius is 50'.
SOUND MOLDING.9: All beings (except the caster) within range capable of hearing ultrasonic sounds are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; an RR must be made once per round while in the radius. Even if they are not stunned, animals capable of hearing ultrasonics will have to make a second, non-magical RR against Fear (level of Fear is half the caster’s level).
SOUND MOLDING.10: As Sound Control I, except radius is 100'.
SOUND MOLDING.11: As Sudden Sound, except everyone in a 20'R is affected.
SOUND MOLDING.12: All of the terrain in the area of effect will have sounds that are different than the terrain’s normal sounds. The caster may designate in what way the terrain sounds different. For example, an open plain could be made to sound like a rustling forest. Note that only the audio aspect of the terrain changes. This can provide between a -30 to a +30 modification to all Stalking attempts within the terrain, depending on how “noisy” the illusionary environment is.
SOUND MOLDING.13: As Sphere of Silence I , except radius is 100'.
SOUND MOLDING.14: As Muffle, except everyone in a 10'R is affected.
SOUND MOLDING.15: As Sound Control I, except radius is 10'R/lvl.
SOUND MOLDING.16: As Sphere of Silence I , except range is 10' per level.
SOUND MOLDING.17: As Sound Contol I , except range is 10' per level.
SOUND MOLDING.18: As Sudden Sound, except for range.
SOUND MOLDING.19: As Muffle, except range is 10' per level.
SOUND MOLDING.20: As Muffle, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
SOUND MOLDING.25: As many targets as the caster’s level can have a 1'R silence on them. Except for the radius hugging closer to the body of the target, this functions as described in Sphere of Silence I.
SOUND MOLDING.30: As Sound Control I, except radius is 20'/lvl and the caster does not have to concentrate except to make changes.
SOUND MOLDING.35: As Sudden Sound Sphere II , except everyone in a 50'R is affected.
SOUND MOLDING.40: As Sudden Sound Sphere II , except everyone in a 100'R is affected and the range is 500'.
SOUND MOLDING.50: All beings (except the caster) within range capable of hearing ultrasonic sounds are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; an RR must be made once per round while in the radius. Anyone failing to resist by 40-75 falls unconscious, while anyone failing the RR by more than 75 dies. Even if they are not stunned, animals capable of hearing ultrasonics will have to make a second, non-magical RR against Fear (level of Fear is equal to the caster’s level).

SOUND PROJECTION.1: Caster can whisper and the whisper can be heard at any point he chooses within the range.
SOUND PROJECTION.2: Both the caster’s voice and the sound of any musical instrument(s) he is playing carry twice as far as normal. This allows for speaking to or performing for larger crowds.
SOUND PROJECTION.3: The caster has a +15 bonus to Performance Art maneuver rolls made when casting a spell on the Controlling Songs or Inspiring Songs spell lists. The “Song” must be cast within 1 minute after completing this spell. This bonus applies both to the initial roll and to any continuation rolls.
SOUND PROJECTION.4: The center of the radius of a spell on the Controlling Songs or Inspiring Songs spell lists may be placed at a distance of up to 50' from the caster. The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell. Silent performance techniques (e.g. juggling) cannot be used with Distant Drums.
SOUND PROJECTION.5: As Long Whisper I, except range is 300'.
SOUND PROJECTION.6: As Sounding II, except increase is five times.
SOUND PROJECTION.7: As Distant Drums V, but maximum distance is 100'.
SOUND PROJECTION.8: A spell on the Controlling Songs or Inspiring Songs spell lists will continue to stay in effect after the caster is done performing. The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell. This extended effect is as long as the initial performance (including any extension by making continuation rolls). For example, if the caster plays a Stunning Song for four rounds, then runs away, the victims will suffer a total of eight rounds of stun (-50).
SOUND PROJECTION.9: As Long Whisper I, except range is 500'.
SOUND PROJECTION.10: A spell on the Controlling Songs spell list can affect only those individuals the caster chooses. He may either select them individually or by choosing one or more categories of being to affect (e.g., “rats”, “children”). The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell. This spell allows the caster to make himself one of the targets of his own Controlling Song.
SOUND PROJECTION.11: As Sweet Music III, but bonus is +30.
SOUND PROJECTION.12: As Distant Drums V , but maximum distance is 200'.
SOUND PROJECTION.13: As Long Whisper I, except range is 1000'.
SOUND PROJECTION.14: As Sounding II , except sound carries out to a chosen distance up to one mile radius without diminution of sound.
SOUND PROJECTION.15: As Distant Drums V, but maximum distance is 300'.
SOUND PROJECTION.16: As Echoes, but the extended effect lasts twice as long as the performance.
SOUND PROJECTION.17: As Long Whisper I, except range is 100' per level of the caster.
SOUND PROJECTION.18: As Sweet Music III, but bonus is +50.
SOUND PROJECTION.19: As Distant Drums V, but maximum distance is 500'.
SOUND PROJECTION.20: A spell on the Controlling Songs or Inspiring Songs spell list will not interfere with the caster’s normal movement. The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell. The caster may continue his performance while doing full movement at up to a jogging pace with no pace penalties.
SOUND PROJECTION.25: As Distant Drums V, but maximum distance is one mile, and the caster must be able to see the place where he wants to center his Controlling Song.
SOUND PROJECTION.30: As Echoes, but the extended effect lasts three times as long as the performance.
SOUND PROJECTION.35: A spell on the Controlling Songs spell list will influence even beings normally immune to mental spells, be they mindless undead, strange demons, or animated stone. The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell.
SOUND PROJECTION.40: As Sweet Music III, but bonus is +75.
SOUND PROJECTION.50: A spell on the Controlling Songs spell list can be made to repeat itself at a later time in the same place. The “Song” must be cast within one minute after completing this spell. The repetition can be triggered any time in the next 48 hours after completion of the initial performance. The caster can choose one of the following triggers: a) a set time, b) specified movement/action, c) specified sound, d) specified species, or e) specified realm of magic actively cast. For options b through e, they trigger the Repeat Performance if they occur/exist in the area affected by the Controlling Song. For those options, the response to the trigger may occur instantly or there may be a set delay in order, e.g., to let more orcs enter the area of effect before the spell goes off.

SOUND'S WAY.1: Target is able to communicate basic ideas in a specific language (e.g., hungry, thirsty, bathroom, peace, etc.); equivalent to skill rank 2.
SOUND'S WAY.2: Creates a 1'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As discussed in Section 10.17, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Quiet - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Quiet radius, however).
SOUND'S WAY.3: Caster is able to communicate basic ideas in a specific language (e.g., hungry, thirsty, bathroom, peace, etc.); equivalent to skill rank 2.
SOUND'S WAY.4: Caster learns the origin and significance of any one sound that the caster has heard in the last minute.
SOUND'S WAY.5: Creates a plane (up to 20'x20') through which sound will not pass.
SOUND'S WAY.6: As Speech I, except target can communicate more complex concepts, although there is a good chance of misinterpretations; equivalent to skill rank 4.
SOUND'S WAY.7: As Quiet I, except radius is 10'.
SOUND'S WAY.8: As Quiet I, except 3 targets can be affected.
SOUND'S WAY.9: As Tongues I, except caster can communicate more complex concepts, although there is a good chance of misinterpretations; equivalent to skill rank 4.
SOUND'S WAY.10: As Soundwall I, except six such walls are created; each Soundwall must be connected to at least one other Soundwall.
SOUND'S WAY.11: As Quiet I, except 5 targets may be affected.
SOUND'S WAY.12: Creates a wordless sound (may resemble a human voice, it just cannot transmit words) that can be heard only by members of the caster’s faith. Sound will not have any direct effects, but may serve as a signal and as the spell name implies, it is often used as a signal to assemble for religious services.
SOUND'S WAY.13: As Sphere of Silence I , except radius is 50'.
SOUND'S WAY.14: Caster’s voice is made more pleasant and suitable for whatever task it is put to. The caster gains a +20 bonus to any vocal tasks (singing, oratory, etc.).
SOUND'S WAY.15: As Speech I, except target is reasonably fluent in the language. Target’s accent is imperfect, but there is only a slight chance of misinterpretation; equivalent to skill rank 6.
SOUND'S WAY.16: Caster can speak a language he already knows in any regional or subcultural dialect, accent, and/or slang as if that form was his normal way of speaking.
SOUND'S WAY.17: Target’s voice carries five times as far as normal.
SOUND'S WAY.18: Anyone speaking within the area of effect has his words scrabbled into nonsense; anyone hearing speech within the area of effect hears the words as nonsense. The only way two individuals within the area of effect can communicate through speech is if they both make their RRs.
SOUND'S WAY.19: As Tongues I, except caster is reasonably fluent in the language. Caster’s accent is imperfect, but there is only a slight chance of misinterpretation; equivalent to skill rank 6.
SOUND'S WAY.20: As Sphere of Silence I , except radius is 100'.
SOUND'S WAY.25: As Soundwall V, except as many Soundwalls as the caster’s level can be created.
SOUND'S WAY.30: As Quiet I, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.
SOUND'S WAY.35: As Speech III, except target speaks the specified language like a native and the caster does not need to concentrate (duration is 1 min/level). Skill is equivalent to skill rank 8.
SOUND'S WAY.40: No sound can exist within the area of effect. It may be targeted on one being as its center, and if that being fails an RR, the radius of effect will travel with that being.
SOUND'S WAY.50: As Tongues I, except caster speaks the specified language like a native (equivalent to skill rank 8) and the duration is 10 minutes/level.

SPEED.1: Caster can change stance instantaneously at any time during the spell duration. Stance is standing, sitting, crouching, prone, etc.
SPEED.2: Caster mounts or dismounts a riding beast “instantly” (i.e., the action is considered part of this instantaneous spell action for 0 or 1 AP).
SPEED.3: Caster makes a Perception maneuver at no penalty to assess the current situation instantly (i.e., the Perception maneuver is considered part of this instantaneous spell action for 0 or 1 AP).
SPEED.4: Caster gains a +5 bonus to initiative rolls.
SPEED.5: Caster gains an additional 1 AP to use each round this spell is in effect (i.e., caster gets 5 AP to use during the round), and he may maintain the spell so long as he wishes. However, caster incurs a -2 fatigue penalty at the end of each round (adding to any existing penalty including from previous rounds). It is not possible to rest while this spell remains in effect.
SPEED.6: Caster has a 10% increase in his base movement rate (BMR), rounding up to the nearest foot/round. For a BMR of 20'/round, this results in a 2'/round increase.
SPEED.7: Caster gains an additional 1 AP to use each round this spell is in effect (i.e., caster gets 5 AP to use during the round).
SPEED.8: Caster gains a +10 bonus to initiative rolls.
SPEED.9: When he would otherwise be surprised, the caster’s subconscious casts this spell and this negates surprise effects.
SPEED.10: Caster gains a +15 bonus to initiative rolls.
SPEED.11: As Rush I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.12: As Long Stride I, except increase is 30%.
SPEED.13: Caster gains a +20 bonus to initiative rolls.
SPEED.14: Caster’s mind works at incredible speed, allowing time to carefully consider the situation as if the world paused while he makes up his mind. No physical action may be taken in this moment, nor may any spells be cast, but much mental activity may be done. In addition to being able to take time to consider when otherwise faced with a need for an instant decision, the character’s Lore and Science skills (and the knowledge aspects of other skills) may be used to make maneuvers (although no skill may be consulted more than three times). If the caster is in mental contact with someone, he may send a message of any length, though the recipient will need the normal time to process it. Anything mental sensed may be examined as carefully as might be done in a full round.
SPEED.15: As Haste I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.16: While this spell is active, the caster casts all spells as instantaneous spells.
SPEED.17: As Rush I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.18: Caster’s Quickness bonus is increased by +5.
SPEED.19: Allows caster to complete an entire sleep cycle in one hour (allows quick PP and hit recovery).
SPEED.20: As Long Stride I , except increase is 50%.
SPEED.25: As Haste I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.30: As Rush I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.35: As Rush I, except caster gains an additional
SPEED.40: Caster’s Quickness bonus is increased by +15.
SPEED.50: As Haste I, except caster gains an additional

SPELL DEFENSE.1: Target gains a bonus of +5 to RRs against spells and a bonus of +5 to DB against elemental attacks.
SPELL DEFENSE.2: When the caster is a target of a spell from the Realm of Channeling, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
SPELL DEFENSE.3: As Protection I, except all beings within 10'R of target (sphere moves with target) get the benefits.
SPELL DEFENSE.4: As Cancel Channeling, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
SPELL DEFENSE.5: As Protection I , except bonuses are +10.
SPELL DEFENSE.6: As Cancel Channeling, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
SPELL DEFENSE.7: As Protection Sphere I, except bonuses are +10.
SPELL DEFENSE.8: Any active spell from the realm of Channeling that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.
SPELL DEFENSE.9: Creates a shimmering shield in front of the caster: it provides a bonus to the caster’s RR or DB of +10 against all frontal Channeling spell attacks on the caster (force and elemental). If the caster takes an instantaneous action to do so, he can “deflect” one spell attack. This results in adding the caster’s ranks in this list to his RR or, for spells that use an attack table, to his DB (in addition to the basic bonus of 10 for having the Shield). Multiple deflections can be done in a single round, but each is a separate instantaneous action (costing 1 AP for every instantaneous action after the first).
SPELL DEFENSE.10: As Protection I, except bonuses are +15.
SPELL DEFENSE.11: As Channeling Shield, except it is effective only against Essence spells.
SPELL DEFENSE.12: As Dispel Channeling I , except it affects spells of the realm of Essence.
SPELL DEFENSE.13: As Channeling Shield, except it is effective only against Mentalism spells.
SPELL DEFENSE.14: As Protection I, except bonuses are +20.
SPELL DEFENSE.15: As Channeling Shield, except it is effective against any two of the three realms.
SPELL DEFENSE.16: As Dispel Channeling I, except it affects spells of the realm of Mentalism.
SPELL DEFENSE.17: As Protection I, except bonuses are +25.
SPELL DEFENSE.18: Dispels a curse if the curse fails an RR. The curse’s level is the level of the caster of the curse. If the curse is not dispelled, the caster cannot try again until he has gone up another level of experience.
SPELL DEFENSE.19: As Channeling Shield, except it is effective against all three realms.
SPELL DEFENSE.20: Target gets +50 to all RRs against Channeling spells.
SPELL DEFENSE.25: Target gets +50 to all RRs against Essence spells.
SPELL DEFENSE.30: Target gets +50 to all RRs against Mentalism spells.
SPELL DEFENSE.35: As Channeling Resistance, except for duration.
SPELL DEFENSE.40: As Channeling Resistance, except is effective against spells from two realms of the caster’s choice.
SPELL DEFENSE.50: As Channeling Resistance, except is effective against all three realms.

SPELL ENHANCEMENT.1: Caster adds +25 to his next Spell Mastery maneuver within 1 minute of casting this spell.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.2: Causes the caster’s next spell cast within 1 minute to have double the normal duration. This is not cumulative with any other Extension spell. Only spells with a defined duration can have their duration changed with an Extension spell. This does not include spells with a duration of concentration (C) or —.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.3: The caster’s next spell (cast within 1 minute of this spell) gains a +25 bonus to the spell-casting roll and to any associated Spell Mastery maneuver.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.4: Causes the caster’s next spell cast within 1 minute to have its range increased by 50'. This is not cumulative with any other Ranging spell. Only spells with a range greater than touch/self can have their range extended with a Ranging spell. For spells with a range increment (directed spells), divide the increase by 5 to get the change to the increment (e.g., for Lesser Ranging, the increase to a range increment would be 10').
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.5: As Extension II, except duration is three times normal.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.6: As Lesser Ranging, except casting time is instantaneous.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.7: Causes the caster’s next spell cast within 1 minute to have its area of effect increase by 5'R. Obviously, this applies only to spells with a radius effect. This is not cumulative with any other Increased Radius spell.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.8: As Lesser Ranging, except range is increased by 100' (or +20' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.9: As Extension II, except duration is four times normal.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.10: As Instant Lesser Ranging, except range is increased by 100' (or +20' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.11: As Increased Radius I , except radius is increased by 10'.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.12: As Ranging I, except range is increased by 200' (or +40' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.13: As Extension II, except duration is five times normal.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.14: As Instant Ranging I , except range is increased by 200' (or +40' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.15: As Increased Radius I, except radius is increased by 15'.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.16: Causes the caster’s next applicable spell (directed spells and those that do a critical) cast within 1 minute of casting this spell to have its Size increased by one size category (typically Medium to Big).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.17: As Ranging I, except range is increased by 300' (or +60' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.18: As Extension II, except duration is ten times normal.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.19: As Instant Ranging I , except range is increased by 300' (or +60' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.20: As Increased Radius I, except radius is increased by 20'.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.25: As Ranging I, except range is increased by 500' (or +100' range increment).
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.30: As Extension II, except duration is increased by 12 hrs.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.35: As Extension II, except duration is increased by 24 hrs.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.40: As Extension X, Ranging III, and Increased Radius II combined.
SPELL ENHANCEMENT.50: As Extension II, except duration is permanent. This spell may only be cast once per month. The caster has a random temporary stat decreased by one. This loss may only be recovered by normal stat gain upon gaining a level.

SPELL REINS.1: Caster may cast this spell with another spell in order to store that second spell. The spell to be stored must be cast within one minute after the Spell Store spell is cast. The stored spell may then be cast later as an instantaneous spell. The Spell Store spell costs the same number of PP as the spell that is stored. No other spell may be cast while a spell is stored.
SPELL REINS.2: If the target of this spell is casting a spell, the caster will know what realm the spell is from and what Type of spell the target is casting.
SPELL REINS.3: The attack spell that is targeted with this spell must make an RR. If the RR succeeds, it will attack its original target as intended; otherwise the effects of the spell are delayed for the duration of this spell. At the end of the duration, if the target of the held spell has moved more than 20', the held spell may randomly affect another target within 10' of the original target point. If a spell is not cast on the original target, the new target gets a +15 RR bonus. Elemental attack spells suffer a -30 modification if not cast upon the original target. An attack spell for purposes of this spell is any spell having a target.
SPELL REINS.4: As Spell Store, except the spell to be stored may be cast by someone (or something if the spell is cast by an item) other than the caster storing the spell. The PP cost of this spell is three more than that of the spell to be stored (with a minimum cost of 4 PP).
SPELL REINS.5: Caster can deflect one Bolt, Ball, or Cone of magical origin. The incoming spell must make an RR. If the RR succeeds, it will attack its target normally; otherwise, the attack roll is modified by a penalty equal to the amount by which the RR was failed.
SPELL REINS.6: As Spell Hold I, except attack spells can be held up to 2 rounds.
SPELL REINS.7: As Spell Store, except other spells may be cast while the stored spell remains in storage. However, such casting requires an additional PP and the spell-casting roll suffers a -10 penalty. The PP cost of this spell is five more than that of the spell to be stored (with a minimum cost of 7 PP).
SPELL REINS.8: As Spell Bending I, except attack roll is modified by twice the amount of RR failure.
SPELL REINS.9: As Spell Hold I, except attack spells can be held 3 rounds.
SPELL REINS.10: The target elemental attack spell must make an RR with a modification of +20 (in favor of the elemental attack spell). If the RR succeeds, the attack is made as normal; otherwise, the elemental attack spell is reversed back on its caster with a +0 OB.
SPELL REINS.11: As Spell Bending I, except attack roll is modified by three times the amount of RR failure.
SPELL REINS.12: As Spell Hold I, except attack spells can be held 4 rounds.
SPELL REINS.13: The caster may “borrow” Power Points from the days to come. When this spell is cast, the caster regains all Power Points to the caster’s normal maximum. Every time the caster casts this spell, he loses all PP for the next two days (the normal duration of the spell). The caster also suffers concussion hit damage equal to 10% of the caster’s normal maximum hits. In addition, for the duration of this spell, he will cast spells as if he were one level lower than he actually is. This spell may be cast multiple times (borrowing PP from many days to come), but all effects are cumulative. For example, if cast twice, the caster will lose all PP for the next four days, take 20% of maximum hits in damage, and cast spells at two levels lower than normal. All penalties will remain in effect until the duration of the spell lapses. The next time the caster sleeps, that rest period will be extended by six hours for each casting of this spell and if this sleep is interrupted, the days of no PP will not begin to count down until after the caster has slept for the full required period of normal sleep requirement plus six hours per casting of Power Loan.
SPELL REINS.14: As Spell Bending I, except attack roll is modified by four times the amount of RR failure.
SPELL REINS.15: As Spell Hold I, except attack spells can be held 5 rounds.
SPELL REINS.16: As Reverse Spell I , except a successfully reversed spell rebounds with an OB equal to half of the original OB.
SPELL REINS.17: As Spell Bending I, except attack roll is modified by five times the amount of RR failure.
SPELL REINS.18: As Spell Hold I , except attack spell is held 10 rounds.
SPELL REINS.19: By using this spell, the caster can transfer the maintenance and control of one spell with a duration of Concentration (C) to a target. The target must then concentrate to maintain the spell (while the original caster is now free of this requirement) and controls any aspects of the spell effects subject to change. Target must have better than animal intelligence (i.e., no Mindless or Animalistic flaw).
SPELL REINS.20: As Spell Hold I, except attack spell is held for 20 rounds.
SPELL REINS.25: As Spell Bending I, except spell can be totally deflected up to 90° in any direction. Caster of this spell may chose to designate another target for the “bent” spell – use half of the original OB.
SPELL REINS.30: As Spell Hold I, except attack spell is held up to 1 round/level.
SPELL REINS.35: As Spell Store, except any number of spells may be stored up to a total number of spell levels equal to the caster’s level. When the stored spell complex is released, all the spells are cast as one instantaneous spell. Each of the component spells may be individually targeted, although only one may have a Directed Spell bonus added to the attack. Spell failure is rolled on the Force spell column, with triple the number of spells added to the spell failure roll (i.e., spell failure when casting five spells in a complex would result in a +15 to the spell failure roll). The PP cost of this spell is equal to the total PP cost of the spells stored plus ten (with a minimum cost of 35).
SPELL REINS.40: As Complex Spell Store , except any or all of the stored spells may be cast by someone (or something) other than the caster storing the spell complex. The PP cost of this spell is equal to the total PP cost of the spells stored plus ten plus the number of stored spells cast by someone else (with a minimum cost of 40).
SPELL REINS.50: As Reverse Spell I , except all spells within a 100'R of the caster are reversed.

SPELL RESISTANCE.1: Caster gains a bonus of +5 to RRs against spells and a bonus of +5 to DB against elemental attacks.
SPELL RESISTANCE.2: When the caster is a target of a spell from the Realm of Mentalism, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
SPELL RESISTANCE.3: Gives the caster a +10 RR bonus against spells with the “mental” subtype.
SPELL RESISTANCE.4: As Cancel Mentalism, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
SPELL RESISTANCE.5: As Protection I, except bonuses are +10.
SPELL RESISTANCE.6: As Cancel Mentalism , except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
SPELL RESISTANCE.7: As Cancel Mentalism, except affects spells from two realms of the caster’s choice.
SPELL RESISTANCE.8: Creates a shimmering shield in front of the caster: it provides a bonus to the caster’s RR or DB of 10 against all frontal Mentalism spell attacks on the caster (force and elemental). If the caster takes an instantaneous action to do so, he can “deflect” one spell attack. This results in adding the caster’s ranks in this list to his RR or, for spells that use an attack table, to his DB (in addition to the basic bonus of 10 for having the Shield). Multiple deflections can be done in a single round, but each is a separate instantaneous action (costing 1 AP for every instantaneous action after the first).
SPELL RESISTANCE.9: As Cancel Mentalism , except affects spells from any realm.
SPELL RESISTANCE.10: As Mentalism Shield, except is effective only against Essence spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.11: As Protection I, except bonuses are +15.
SPELL RESISTANCE.12: As Cancel Mentalism , except this spell only attempts to cancel the next Mentalism spell (not cast by the caster) to target the caster. Only one Waiting Cancel can be in place at a time, but it will last indefinitely until triggered.
SPELL RESISTANCE.13: As Mentalism Shield , except is effective only against Channeling spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.14: As Waiting Mentalism Cancel, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.
SPELL RESISTANCE.15: As Mentalism Shield, except it is effective against any two of the three realms.
SPELL RESISTANCE.16: As Waiting Mentalism Cancel, except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
SPELL RESISTANCE.17: As Protection I, except bonuses are +25.
SPELL RESISTANCE.18: As Waiting Mentalism Cancel, except affects spells from any realm.
SPELL RESISTANCE.19: As Mentalism Shield, except it is effective against all spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.20: Gives a +50 to all RRs against Mentalism spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.25: Gives a +50 to all RRs against Essence spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.30: Gives a +50 to all RRs against Channeling spells.
SPELL RESISTANCE.35: Caster may make a second RR against any spell, if the first RR fails.
SPELL RESISTANCE.40: As Spell Shield True, except if the caster makes a successful RR against the incoming spell while “deflecting”, he can send the spell back at the caster.
SPELL RESISTANCE.50: Gives a +50 to all RRs against all spells.

SPELL WALL.1: Target gains a bonus of +5 to RRs against spells and a bonus of +5 to DB against elemental attacks.
SPELL WALL.2: When the caster of this spell is the target of a spell from the realm of Essence, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.
SPELL WALL.3: As Protection I, except all beings within 10'R of target (sphere moves with target) get the benefits.
SPELL WALL.4: As Cancel Essence, except it affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
SPELL WALL.5: As Protection I, except bonuses are +10.
SPELL WALL.6: As Cancel Essence , except it affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
SPELL WALL.7: As Protection Sphere I, except bonuses are +10.
SPELL WALL.8: As Cancel Essence, except for the area of effect (centered on and moving with the caster). Any spell targeting inside the area of effect may be canceled. If the radius encounters an already existing spell, that spell must make an RR (with a +30 modifier) or be neutralized within the sphere (so long as it is within the sphere).
SPELL WALL.9: Creates a shimmering shield in front of the caster: it provides a bonus to the caster’s RR or DB of 10 against all frontal Essence spell attacks on the caster (force and elemental). If the caster takes an instantaneous action to do so, he can “deflect” one spell attack. This results in adding the caster’s ranks in this list to his RR or, for spells that use an attack table, to his DB (in addition to the basic bonus of 10 for having the Shield). Multiple deflections can be done in a single round, but each is a separate instantaneous action (costing 1 AP for every instantaneous action after the first).
SPELL WALL.10: As Essence Shield, except it is effective only against Mentalism spells.
SPELL WALL.11: As Protection I except bonuses are +15.
SPELL WALL.12: As Cancel Essence Sphere I, except it affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.
SPELL WALL.13: As Essence Shield , except it is effective only against Channeling spells.
SPELL WALL.14: As Essence Shield, except it is effective against any two of the three realms.
SPELL WALL.15: As Protection I, except bonuses are +20.
SPELL WALL.16: As Cancel Essence Sphere I, except it affects spells from the realm of Channeling.
SPELL WALL.17: As Cancel Essence Sphere I, except for radius.
SPELL WALL.18: As Essence Shield, except it is effective against all three realms.
SPELL WALL.19: As Protection I, except bonuses are +25.
SPELL WALL.20: Target gets a +50 bonus for all RRs vs. Essence spells.
SPELL WALL.25: Target gets a +50 bonus for all RRs vs. Mentalism spells.
SPELL WALL.30: Target gets a +50 bonus for all RRs vs. Channeling spells.
SPELL WALL.35: As Cancel Essence Sphere I, except that it affects spells from any realm of magic, and has an enhanced radius.
SPELL WALL.40: As Cancel Magic Sphere II, except for radius.
SPELL WALL.50: As Essence Resistance , except all three realms are affected.

SPIRIT MASTERY.1: Causes target(s) to fall into a natural sleep; the total number of levels that can be affected is 3 (e.g., 3 of level 1; 1 of level 2 and 1 of level 1; 1 of level 3.). All targets must be in caster’s field of vision. Caster should assign target priority, i.e., which is the 1st potential target, the 2nd, etc. The first round of sleep is magical (the target cannot be awakened through normal means). Falling asleep is rapid, but not so instantaneous that the victim cannot slump gently to the ground (no injuries under normal circumstances from falling to the ground).
SPIRIT MASTERY.2: Humanoid target believes the caster is a good friend.
SPIRIT MASTERY.3: As Sleep III, except a total of five levels can be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.4: Target is incapable of making decisions or initiating action, but may continue to fight current foes or in self-defense. The target may also follow orders from the target’s leader (if any), but will only be able to follow them so long as no ambiguities arise.
SPIRIT MASTERY.5: Target will perform a single suggested act that is not self-destructive or diametrically opposed to the target’s personality (e.g., no suicide suggestions, no blinding himself suggestions, no suggestions that a priest pray to an enemy god, etc.).
SPIRIT MASTERY.6: As Sleep III, except a total of 10 levels may be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.7: Humanoid target has its Action Points per round reduced by 3 (i.e., normally, the target will have 1 AP each round).
SPIRIT MASTERY.8: Humanoid target must obey the caster as specified in Suggestion.
SPIRIT MASTERY.9: As Sleep III, except a total of 20 levels may be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.10: As Charm Kind, except any sentient creature may be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.11: Target is given one task; failure results in a penalty determined by the Gamemaster (task must be within capabilities of target). If the target ignores the quest, this incurs the same effects as for failure.
SPIRIT MASTERY.12: [RR Mod.: -10] Target is stunned (-50) for 1 round/10 failure.
SPIRIT MASTERY.13: [RR Mod.: -10] Target takes 50% of remaining concussion hits upon failure.
SPIRIT MASTERY.14: As Hold Kind, except any sentient being can be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.15: [RR Mod.: -10] Target falls into a natural sleep as Sleep III, but there is no level limit.
SPIRIT MASTERY.16: [RR Mod.: -10] Target will not cooperate or agree with anyone for 1 day/10 failure.
SPIRIT MASTERY.17: [RR Mod.: -10] Target is forced to come and face the caster (fighting if necessary) and then remain immobile for 1 rnd/10 failure. Caster must remain immobile, or control lapses.
SPIRIT MASTERY.18: [RR Mod.: -10] Any of the “Words” above can be set to go off at a specified time or if there is movement within a designated 10' radius. Listed duration is maximum wait time. Duration of effect on eventual target is normal for the selected “Word”. The target of the Word is the individual whose movement triggered it, or the closest target in the case of a specified time.
SPIRIT MASTERY.19: [RR Mod.: -10] Target suffers the results of an E critical strike on the table of the caster’s choice.
SPIRIT MASTERY.20: As Quest, except the target is punished for failure (or refusal) by suffering 5 randomly selected E criticals.
SPIRIT MASTERY.25: [RR Mod.: -10] Any three different “Words” are cast on one target in the same round (a separate RR roll must be made for each one).
SPIRIT MASTERY.30: [RR Mod.: -10] Any “Word” may be used and it will affect a number of levels equal to the level of the caster (assign target priority as described in Sleep III).
SPIRIT MASTERY.35: As Master of Kind, except any sentient being can be affected.
SPIRIT MASTERY.40: [RR Mod.: -10] As Waiting Word, except instead of a “Word” spell, the effect is as the Phrase spell.
SPIRIT MASTERY.50: Caster can use one spell/round on this list (10th level or lower) with range improved to 100' where the original range is less.

SUMMONS.1: Caster can instantly summon a first level non-intelligent (i.e., possessing the Animalistic or Mindless flaw) living creature, which appears somewhere within the spell’s range. The caster can specify the general type of creature, but exactly what the creature is should be determined randomly (e.g., the caster could specify “four-legged, hoofed” and could get a zebra, horse, camel, etc.). If the creature can understand the caster’s communication, the creature can be controlled by normal communication. Otherwise, control is maintained through concentration of the caster. If the creature summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in its own perception), the duration for this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the creature is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level. At the end of the spell’s duration, the summoned creature will disappear back whence it came.
SUMMONS.2: As Summons I, except the caster chooses one of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options to the higher level Summons spells, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).
SUMMONS.3: As Summons II, except two options are chosen.
SUMMONS.4: As Summons II, except three options are chosen.
SUMMONS.5: As Summons II, except four options are chosen.
SUMMONS.6: As Summons II, except five options are chosen.
SUMMONS.7: As Summons II, except six options are chosen.
SUMMONS.8: As Summons II, except seven options are chosen.
SUMMONS.9: As Summons II, except nine options are chosen.
SUMMONS.10: Caster summons a Type I Demon that appears over the course of 2 rounds; the caster can control it by concentrating. After the caster stops concentrating, the demon will fade out over the course of 2 rounds, attacking the closest being until gone.
SUMMONS.11: As Summons II, except fourteen options are chosen.
SUMMONS.12: Caster can summon (teleport to his location) a single “guard”, who must be a member of an intelligent race, a follower of the caster’s own religion, and currently doing service to the summoner or the summoner’s religious institution. Guard must also be within range of the spell when it is cast or it will fail.
