SUMMONS.13: As Summons II, except nineteen options are chosen.
SUMMONS.14: As Demonic Gate I, except summons a Type I extraplanar being of a non-hostile nature. Control is not possible, though the summoned being will be helpful and will usually do as the caster requests if communication is possible.
SUMMONS.15: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type II Demon is summoned.
SUMMONS.16: As Summon Guard, except one guard can be summoned for every five full levels of the caster (e.g., a 16th level caster could summon three guards).
SUMMONS.17: As Summons X, except it can be delayed up to 1 day/level or until triggered by one of the following (decided by the caster at the time of casting): specified movements, sounds, touch, or actions. Trigger must be such as the caster would be able to sense it if still at the location of the spell effect. At the time of casting, the caster may specify a single task for the creature(s) to perform when it/they arrive(s).
SUMMONS.18: Caster sends out a telepathic summons heard by all the faithful within range to gather for a holy war. Response is voluntary, except for the Paladins of the faith, who must make an RR if they wish to resist. Use of this spell tends to be strictly monitored by the Cleric’s superiors, both mortal and divine, so it is best used only when there is a real need.
SUMMONS.19: As Divine Gate I, except a Type II entity is summoned.
SUMMONS.20: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type III Demon can be summoned.
SUMMONS.25: As Summons II, except one option per caster level is chosen.
SUMMONS.30: As Summons I, except caster can summon a specific type of creature of a level up to half the caster’s level for more lengthy service.
SUMMONS.35: As Divine Gate I, except a Type III entity is summoned.
SUMMONS.40: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type IV Demon can be summoned.
SUMMONS.50: Caster can pull any being through a “gate” to the caster’s location; if the being is intelligent, it must be willing to come. An intelligent being will know the religious affiliation of the gating person and a vague idea of the reason and the significance for the “gate”.

SURFACE WAYS.1: Caster is healed of 10 concussion hits.
SURFACE WAYS.2: Removes up to -30 in penalties from fatigue.
SURFACE WAYS.3: Caster is relieved of 1 round’s worth of accumulated stun effects.
SURFACE WAYS.4: Reduces damage by 2 hits every round as long as caster concentrates; if caster is unconscious, this spell will operate without concentration.
SURFACE WAYS.5: An injury from burns, chemical burns, scalding, or frostbite incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.
SURFACE WAYS.7: Caster is healed of 25 concussion hits.
SURFACE WAYS.8: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to 40.
SURFACE WAYS.9: Caster is relieved of 3 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
SURFACE WAYS.10: As Regeneration I, except 4 hits are healed every round.
SURFACE WAYS.11: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.
SURFACE WAYS.12: Caster is healed of 50 concussion hits.
SURFACE WAYS.13: Caster is relieved of 5 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
SURFACE WAYS.14: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
SURFACE WAYS.15: As Regeneration I, except 6 hits are healed every round.
SURFACE WAYS.16: As Medium Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
SURFACE WAYS.17: Caster is healed of 75 concussion hits.
SURFACE WAYS.18: As Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.
SURFACE WAYS.19: Caster is relieved of 10 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.
SURFACE WAYS.20: Caster is healed of 100 concussion hits.
SURFACE WAYS.25: Caster is healed of all concussion hit damage.
SURFACE WAYS.30: As Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing, except affecting all such injuries.
SURFACE WAYS.35: As Mass Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.
SURFACE WAYS.40: Caster is relieved of all accumulated stun effects.
SURFACE WAYS.50: Caster is instantly relieved of all accumulated stun effects, concussion hit damage, fatigue penalties, and wounds due to burn, chemical burn, scalding, or frostbite.

SURVIVAL'S WAY.1: Caster receives a +25 bonus to all Survival maneuvers.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.2: Allows caster to set a fire of 1'R; fire will then last as long as there is fuel. A target in the 1'R will take an A Heat critical. Caster may only target a specific location, not an individual, with this spell.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.3: Caster gains a +20 bonus to all Medicine or Herbalism maneuvers.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.4: Caster gains a +50 bonus to any roll to notice a trap.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.5: As Survival I, except the bonus is +50.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.6: Allows caster to purify a number of gallons of water equal to the caster’s level.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.7: Any skill similarity penalty for using Survival in a different biome or any animal skill for a different animal is reduced by -25. This spell may be combined with Survival I or II.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.8: This spell creates a minor, normal trap (constructed magically). The trap can deliver up to a B critical strike (must be either a Slash, Krush or a Puncture; specified by the caster at time of casting) to any one target. The size of the trap may vary from 1'R to 10'R. The time to construct this trap is 1 round per 1'R.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.9: All traces of an encampment vanish. No trace is left of the fire pit, tracks in the area of the encampment are erased, etc. One casting covers the traces of 10 people or animals; additional castings can be used to cover larger camps. This spell does not prevent new traces from being created if the group is still present, it only erases traces that have already accumulated.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.10: Allows caster to monitor animate activity in the area (e.g., the caster will be aware of movements, subtle and overt). Caster cannot move while concentrating. Caster does not receive a picture of what is moving, just an awareness
SURVIVAL'S WAY.11: Caster may locate the nearest natural object of a specified type, e.g. a specified type of animal or plant, a natural spring, a cave, etc. The object can be a specific item if the caster is personally familiar with that item (e.g. the ranger can find his own horse, or the nearest horse, but not a specific horse that has only been described to him). Caster continues to sense the direction and distance for the duration.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.12: Allows caster to set up a natural “alarm system” in the area of effect; any disturbing activity will cause caster to be alerted. This alert will wake the caster, if he is in a natural sleep.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.13: As Common Nature I, except penalties are reduced by -50.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.14: As Lesser Trap, except trap can deliver an E critical hit.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.15: As Traceless Encampment, except that it will actively hide an encampment still in use. Sleeping areas are shrouded by plants or become recessed into the ground, any fire casts light only visible in a 10'R area and produces no smoke, no tracks are left by those staying in the encampment, etc. Hides 1 person/animal per caster level. Any Awareness maneuver to locate the camp suffers a -100 penalty.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.16: Caster has a 95% chance of predicting weather over a 30-day period.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.17: Creates a natural spring producing enough water to supply 1 person/animal per caster level. The spring is created in the most appropriate location within 500' (must be in a natural setting at ground level), and the caster immediately knows its location. If in a location which would not normally support a spring (e.g. in the desert), the spring will last 10 min/lvl. In a suitable location, it may last days or become permanent.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.18: As Common Nature I, except penalties are reduced to zero.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.19: As Locate Natural Object , except the range is 10 miles.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.20: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except detecting activity up to 500' away.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.25: As Waiting Awareness I, except over a 100' radius.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.30: Caster becomes aware of the presence, general location, general type, and number of any actively hostile beings in the area of effect. These beings do not have to be specifically hostile to the caster, but may simply be generally hostile.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.35: Caster’s body adapts to survive in any natural environment. This can involve submersion in freezing water at depths with crushing pressure, traversing molten lava, or enduring exposure to a raging sandstorm. This does not cover any form of deliberate attack (even while comfortable naked in a blizzard, the caster will still suffer normal damage from a Cold Ball). The GM must determine what conditions qualify as “natural.”
SURVIVAL'S WAY.40: As Nature’ s Awareness I, except detecting activity up to 100' per level away from the caster.
SURVIVAL'S WAY.50: Caster can use one lower level spell on this list each round.

SYMBOLIC WAYS.1: Caster learns what spell is emplaced within one Symbol within 50'. The Symbol will not be triggered (unless specifically created to be triggered by an Analyze Symbol spell).
SYMBOLIC WAYS.2: This spell “primes” the next spell cast by the caster – that spell is treated as if it were instantaneous. The PP cost of this spell is equal to the highest level that the caster wants to have primed. For example, if the caster wants any fourth (or lower) level spell to be treated as instantaneous, this spell will cost 4 PP. In addition, when casting this spell, the caster must pray for one minute per PP spent (e.g., if the caster spends 4 PP on this spell, he must pray for 4 minutes before the spell is completed.) The effects of this spell end when the caster casts his next spell (even if the next spell does not benefit from this spell due to being of too high a level or already an instantaneous spell).
SYMBOLIC WAYS.3: Caster can remove one Symbol I (an emplaced 1st level spell). The Symbol’s RR is based on the level of the spell in the Symbol spell (the target level) and the level of the Unsymbol spell (the attack level).
SYMBOLIC WAYS.4: Caster can emplace one 1st level spell on an inanimate, non-mobile slab of stone (block, piece, etc.) weighing at least 2,000 pounds. Only one Symbol may be placed on each continuous slab. The stone may not be moved appreciably without destroying the Symbol. Within one minute, the spell to be emplaced must also be cast (within 10'). A Symbol can be triggered (once per day) by one of the following (decided by caster): time period, specified movements, specified sounds, touch, reading, violent actions in the area, etc. The triggering range is normally the range of the emplaced spell or 10' (whichever is greater). The effective level of the embedded spell (for the purposes of RRs, Duration, etc.) is the level of the highest level spell the Symbol can hold. For example, a first level spell placed in a Symbol using Symbol I is cast at 1st level (regardless of the level of the original caster), while a first level spell place using Symbol VII is cast at 7th level. If the spell is an elemental attack, the OB is normally +0. However, the attack can be focused on a location (as opposed to simply the person who triggers the symbol); in this case, the OB is +50. The Symbol is usually visible, but is only required to be visible if reading is the trigger.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.5: This spell must be cast on a Symbol that has already been triggered this day. Once this spell is cast on the Symbol, the Symbol is recharged and can be triggered again. A given caster cannot recharge the same Symbol more than once per day, but one caster may recharge any number of Symbols and a Symbol may be recharged by multiple casters.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.6: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-II may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.7: As Symbol I, except a 1st-2nd level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.8: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-V may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.9: As Symbol I, except a 1st-3rd level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.10: As Analyze Symbol, except caster learns what spells are emplaced in all Symbols within a 50'R.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.11: As Symbol I, except a 1st-5th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.12: As Unsymbol I , except a Symbol I-VI may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.13: As Symbol I , except a 1st-6th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.14: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-VII may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.15: As Symbol I, except a 1st-7th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.16: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-VIII may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.17: As Symbol I, except a 1st-8th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.18: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-X may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.19: As Symbol I , except a 1st-9th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.20: As Symbol I, except a 1st-10th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.25: This spell allows for the creation of random magic items. See Treasure Law, Section 4.4.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.30: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-X or a Lord Symbol may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.35: As Symbol I, except a 1st-20th level spell may be emplaced.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.40: As Unsymbol I , except any Symbol may be removed.
SYMBOLIC WAYS.50: As Symbol I, except a spell with a level up to half that of the caster (e.g., a 50th level caster can emplace a 25th level spell) may be emplaced.

TELEKINESIS.1: Caster can move one object at a distance, weighing up to 1 pound, up to 1'/second with no acceleration. If the caster stops concentration before the duration is up, the object remains stationary as if it had Staying I (with a 1 pound limit) thrown on it.
TELEKINESIS.2: Exerts 25 pounds of counterforce on a person or object. Object cannot be moved by Staying alone, which can only be used to counter another force, such as gravity, and it can only exert force in one direction.
TELEKINESIS.3: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 5 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.4: As Staying II , except force limit is 125 pounds.
TELEKINESIS.5: As Staying II, except 2 objects may be affected with 25 pounds total force or Staying II can be used to 200'.
TELEKINESIS.6: As Telekinesis I, except 2 objects of 5 lbs. total weight may be moved or Telekinesis I can be used at 200'.
TELEKINESIS.7: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 25 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.8: As Staying II , except force limit is 250 pounds.
TELEKINESIS.9: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 50 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.10: Caster may “hurl” one object (that starts within 10' of the caster) of 1 pound. or less with sufficient force to deliver an attack on the Sling Attack Table with the ranges of a Short Bow. Base the caster’s OB on his Directed Spell: Hurling skill. If a weapon is “hurled”, a medium Spell Mastery maneuver will allow use of the weapon’s normal attack table, retaining the Short Bow range penalties. If this is done, the weapon gets any normal OB bonuses (e.g., from superior materials).
TELEKINESIS.11: As Staying II , except force limit is 500 pounds.
TELEKINESIS.12: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 100 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.13: As Hurling I, except weight limit is 5 pounds and it delivers 3x normal hits. Hit multiplication does not apply if hurling a weapon using its own attack table.
TELEKINESIS.14: As Staying II, except force limit is 600 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.15: As Staying II, except 3 objects may be affected with 25 pounds total force or Staying II can be used to 300'.
TELEKINESIS.16: As Telekinesis I, except 3 objects of 25 lbs total weight may be moved or Telekinesis I can be used to 300'.
TELEKINESIS.17: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 200 lbs.
TELEKINESIS.18: As Hurling II, except limit is 25 lbs. and hits are 5x normal.
TELEKINESIS.19: As Staying II, except force limit is 50 pounds per level.
TELEKINESIS.20: As Telekinesis I, except weight limit is 20 pounds per level.
TELEKINESIS.25: As Hurling I, except the object to hurl may start up to 300' away or the caster may hurl 3 objects that start up to 100' away (at the same target). Directed Spells skill must be divided between multiple objects. Range penalties are based on distance from object starting point to the target.
TELEKINESIS.30: As Hurling II, except limit is 50 lbs. and hits are x5 normal and the OB has an additional +50 bonus.
TELEKINESIS.35: As Hurling III, except the object to hurl may start up to 300' away or the caster may hurl 3 objects that start up to 100' away (at the same target). Directed Spells skill must be divided between multiple objects. Range penalties are based on distance from object starting point to the target.
TELEKINESIS.40: Caster channels telekinetic force in conjunction with the caster’s armed or unarmed melee attacks, resulting in all such attacks doing an additional Impact critical of the same severity as the normal critical.
TELEKINESIS.50: As Telekinesis True, except for duration and caster can manipulate a new object (up to a combined total of 100 pounds per level) each round and a Staying applies to each object while it is not being concentrated on.

TRADE MASTERY.1: Caster receives basic idea of item’s purpose, and receives +20 to its use if it is intended to be operated using any Craft or Technical skill.
TRADE MASTERY.2: When this spell is cast upon a machine, the machine’s efficiency will return to its original level. For example, if cast upon a rusty lock, the lock would return to its original non-rusty operational level.
TRADE MASTERY.3: This spell allows the caster to perform one maneuver using a Craft or Technical skill as if the caster had a complete set of the appropriate tools with a bonus of +10.
TRADE MASTERY.4: Target mechanism (trap, lock, door, machine, etc) is jammed, and has only a 7% chance of operating when used/triggered normally. It remains jammed until repaired.
TRADE MASTERY.5: Caster can assess the value of jewels and metals within 10% of actual value. This spell allows the caster to calculate different values for all of the cultures with which the caster is familiar. This spell evaluates materials only, not craftsmanship or magical properties.
TRADE MASTERY.6: This spell will operate any non-magical machine in its normal capacity for the duration. After the duration, the machine will operate normally. For example, if this spell were used to turn a wheel, the wheel would spin for the duration of the spell, then slowly spin to a stop. This merely provides basic motive power; any additional controls (directional controls, frequency settings, etc.) must be handled separately.
TRADE MASTERY.7: As Jewel/Metal Assessment, except this spell will determine the value of an item, taking into account the craftsmanship of the item.
TRADE MASTERY.8: As Phantom Tools I, except the bonus is +30.
TRADE MASTERY.9: Caster will be aware of any machine activity or stored power for a machine (e.g. capacitors, battery, springs, weights) within the radius of effect. The caster will not know what the machine is or exactly where, though successful Perception may give an idea of the number of machines, vague location, and overall energy involved. A Very Hard Perception maneuver while moving at a walking pace or slower with this spell active will give the caster enough warning that he is starting to trigger a mechanism for the caster to pull back, avoiding a tripwire or pressure plate activation.
TRADE MASTERY.10: Caster receives a +25 bonus to Trading skill and to any Social or Lore skill used to locate a buyer or seller.
TRADE MASTERY.11: Caster gains a general idea of an item’s abilities (e.g., it provides a bonus to Stealth [but not the amount], it allows casting a spell twice daily [but not which spell], or it does an additional critical in combat [but not what kind or information on the severity of the critical]).
TRADE MASTERY.12: As Operate I, except concentration is not required and the duration is 1 minute per level.
TRADE MASTERY.13: As Jam I, but the mechanism has only a 4% chance of operating when used/triggered.
TRADE MASTERY.14: Caster can detect any item in the radius which has cultural or historical significance (e.g. a sacred relic, a item formerly possessed by a great hero), and its magnitude (mild significance, great fame, etc), but not the details. Caster can move the area of effect 5' each round by concentrating.
TRADE MASTERY.15: As Operate II, except duration is 1 hr/level.
TRADE MASTERY.16: As Phantom Tools I, except the bonus is +50.
TRADE MASTERY.17: As Acumen I, except the bonus is +50.
TRADE MASTERY.18: The caster learns the area of origin of the item, the race of the being who made it, and when it was made (within 100 years).
TRADE MASTERY.19: As Operate II, except the duration is 1 day per level.
TRADE MASTERY.20: As Jam II, except affects all mechanisms in the area of effect.
TRADE MASTERY.25: Caster gets a vision of a significant event in the item’s past.
TRADE MASTERY.30: Allows caster to operate and control one machine. This not only provides basic motive power (as an Operate spell), but allows full control of an entire machine by the caster alone. Treat as if as many individuals as needed for normal operations (up to a maximum equal to the caster’s level) were working under the perfect command of the caster. Treat the skill of this crew as either +0 or the caster’s own bonus in the appropriate skill.
TRADE MASTERY.35: As Acumen I, except the bonus is +100.
TRADE MASTERY.40: As Operate II, except affects all machines in the area of effect.
TRADE MASTERY.50: As Operate II, except the duration is permanent.

UNBARRING WAYS.1: Caster can cause any lock visible within 100' to be locked (the lock is just normally locked and can be opened normally).
UNBARRING WAYS.2: A door (or container) can be magically “locked”. The door can be broken normally or the spell can be dispelled; otherwise, the door cannot be opened.
UNBARRING WAYS.3: Gives the caster a +20 bonus for picking the lock analyzed. If the caster takes a round to explain the lock to someone else, that person gets a +10 bonus to pick the lock.
UNBARRING WAYS.4: When cast on a lock there is a 20% chance a normal lock will open, and a 45% chance that a Magic Lock will open (failure means there is a 10% chance of setting off attached traps). Rolls are open-ended, and the quality of the lock may modify the roll (given percentage assumes a “Medium” quality lock).
UNBARRING WAYS.5: As Locklore, except applies to disarming traps.
UNBARRING WAYS.6: As Opening I , except its chances concern disarming traps.
UNBARRING WAYS.7: Causes a door to expand and jam into its frame (roll 1-100: severity ranges from slightly stuck to unopenable).
UNBARRING WAYS.8: Reduces the inherent strength of a door. All attempts to force or break through the door get a +50 bonus; Breakage Rolls for the door are made at -50.
UNBARRING WAYS.9: As Opening I, except that every lock within the radius of effect has a chance of opening. Roll separately for each lock.
UNBARRING WAYS.10: As Opening I, except chances are 40% for normal locks and 90% for a Magic Lock.
UNBARRING WAYS.11: Will vaporize a non-magical door up to 6" thick, 10' high and 10' wide (if the door is thicker than 6" it will vaporize the closest 6 inches).
UNBARRING WAYS.12: As Opening II, except its chances concern disarming traps.
UNBARRING WAYS.13: As Magic Lock, except duration is 1 hr/lvl and the chance that an Opening spell (or Open Prison) will work is reduced by 20%.
UNBARRING WAYS.14: As Magic Lock, except door is unbreakable by normal means (lasts 1 hour/level).
UNBARRING WAYS.15: As Undoor I , except door can be up to 20'x20'x2'.
UNBARRING WAYS.16: As Open Prison I, except chances are 40% for normal locks and 90% for Magic Locks.
UNBARRING WAYS.17: As Undoor I, except door can be up to 50'x50'x3'.
UNBARRING WAYS.18: As Magic Lock, except duration is 1 day/level and the chance that an Opening spell (or Open Prison) will work is reduced by 40%.
UNBARRING WAYS.19: As Undoor I, except any single door is vaporized.
UNBARRING WAYS.20: A doorway (8'x5') is created in any wall up to 6"/level deep.
UNBARRING WAYS.25: Gives the caster a 90% chance of opening a lock. The roll is open-ended and the quality of the lock may modify the roll.
UNBARRING WAYS.30: As Lock Mastery, except its chances concern disarming traps.
UNBARRING WAYS.35: All traps in the radius of effect have a 95% chance of failing to operate. Once the spell duration is over or the trap is outside the effect radius, the trap will function normally. This does require that the trap have some mechanism involved. A pit trap with a trap door opening will fail, but a pit simply covered over with some branches will still not support any more weight than normal.
UNBARRING WAYS.40: As Lock Mastery and Trap Mastery, except applies to every lock and trap within the radius of effect.
UNBARRING WAYS.50: Caster can utilize any one of the lower level spells on this list each round.

WATER LAW.1: Condenses 1 cubic foot of water from the surrounding air. Condensation can be channeled into any suitable receptacle, including caster’s cupped hands.
WATER LAW.2: Creates dense fog within the area of effect. Treat as a heavy fog as described in RMCL Section 15.
WATER LAW.3: Creates a wall of water up to 10'x10'x1'. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Run going in would be a Walk pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty.
WATER LAW.4: A bolt of water is shot from the caster’s palm; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table.
WATER LAW.5: Disperses fog within the area of effect. If not all of a fog is dispersed (i.e., the fog covers a greater area than the area of effect for this spell), the fog will refill the area of effect, reducing the cleared radius by 10' every 1-5 minutes (varying depending on the heaviness of the fog and ambient humidity).
WATER LAW.6: Target’s fluids are immediately replenished, eliminating any dehydration that the target may have been suffering. This spell also meets the target’s water needs for the next 24 hours, not including losses directly due to magic (i.e., it will not defend against a Dessication spell, but will cure its effects if cast after).
WATER LAW.7: As Water Bolt I, except that if the caster knows the Fire Law list to 7th level, this bolt becomes scalding hot, trailing thick tendrils of steam behind it. It uses the Water Bolt Table, but in addition to the normal results, it does an additional Steam critical one level of severity less than the usual critical.
WATER LAW.8: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
WATER LAW.9: An area of water is made hostile to any air-breather who intrudes upon it. Powerful undertows will appear out of nowhere and sudden currents seem to grab at the victim. Swimming maneuvers are made at -100 within the area of effect.
WATER LAW.10: As Waterwall, except the caster need not concentrate and the duration is 1 min/level.
WATER LAW.11: As Water Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.
WATER LAW.12: A region of water becomes slightly denser, making it easier to float. All Swimming maneuvers to avoid drowning or to move at or near the surface of the water gain a +30 bonus.
WATER LAW.13: If there are clouds in the sky, it rains outdoors for the duration of the spell.
WATER LAW.14: As Steam Bolt I, except range increment is 75' and Fire Law must be known to 14th level in order to transform the Water Bolt into a Steam Bolt.
WATER LAW.15: As Water Bolt I, except the range increment is 125'.
WATER LAW.16: Three bolts of water are shot from the palm of the caster. They can strike up to three different targets as long as they are all within 60° of each other and are within the caster’s field of vision. Caster may apply his Directed Spell skill by dividing it as he chooses among the three bolts (e.g., a +120 OB could be split evenly into three +40 bolts or one +120 bolt and two +0 bolts or one +60, one +40, and one +20 bolt).
WATER LAW.17: The area of effect for this spell is the water immediately surrounding the boat that the caster is on (where “boat” should be interpreted loosely; a log is a “boat” for purposes of this spell if the caster is riding it on water). For as long as the caster concentrates, the speed of the boat can be increased by 25' per round (approximately 3 miles per hour).
WATER LAW.18: As Calm Water, except radius is 100'/lvl and waves are cut by 50' in the center and caster need not concentrate.
WATER LAW.19: As Quench, except affects up to one target/level and can target anyone within 100'.
WATER LAW.20: Creates a whirlpool with a 50 foot radius in any liquid body that will draw in any objects in the liquid up to 500' away. Objects capable of moving under their own power may make a Very Hard maneuver to resist the pull of the whirlpool.
WATER LAW.25: Caster can part water up to 100' deep and 100'/lvl long and 10' wide at bottom (50' at top).
WATER LAW.30: Summons forces of nature in a fierce sea storm (must be created over a large body of water). This storm will include waves of 20-50', heavy rain, and winds of 25-60 mph. The storm can be delayed up to 1 hour/level after casting the spell. After the storm has been summoned, it will dissipate normally.
WATER LAW.35: As Command Current, except speed is increased by 50' per round.
WATER LAW.40: As Whirlpool, except caster need not concentrate as the Maelstrom lasts 10 minutes per level on its own and the maneuver to resist the pull is Extremely Hard.
WATER LAW.50: Caster can use one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

WEATHER WAYS.1: Caster may determine air temperature, humidity, barometric pressure, and wind speed. One round of concentration is required per piece of information.
WEATHER WAYS.2: Gives caster a 95% chance of predicting the time and type of rain (or other precipitation) by ±15 minutes over the next 24 hours.
WEATHER WAYS.3: Gives caster a 95% chance of predicting the high and low temperatures over the next 24 hours.
WEATHER WAYS.4: Caster has a 95% chance of correctly predicting time, type, and severity of weather over the next 24 hr period.
WEATHER WAYS.5: No rain, snow, sleet, hail, or fog will come closer than an inch from the caster or what the caster is wearing, allowing the caster to pass through a downpour and come out completely dry.
WEATHER WAYS.6: Caster causes a breeze to come forth which will drive out any gaseous matter (clouds, fog, poison gasses, etc.) and subtracts from all missile attacks (-10). Once set, the direction of the breeze will not change.
WEATHER WAYS.7: Caster causes fog to arise that obscures nearly all vision in and through the radius; treat as a heavy fog as described in RMCL, Section 15.7.
WEATHER WAYS.8: Disperses fog within the area of effect. If not all of a fog is dispersed (i.e., the fog covers a greater area than the area of effect for this spell), fog will refill the area of effect, reducing the cleared radius by 10' every 1-5 minutes (depending on the heaviness of the fog and ambient humidity).
WEATHER WAYS.9: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.
WEATHER WAYS.10: As Weather Prediction I, except that caster has a 95% chance of predicting weather over a 3-day period.
WEATHER WAYS.11: Caster causes rain or snow to fall (depending on temperature). The precipitation obscures vision into the radius (-25 to Perception) and modifies all missile attacks passing through part of the radius by -25.
WEATHER WAYS.12: Alerts the caster any time that the weather within the area of effect is altered by supernatural means.
WEATHER WAYS.13: As Weather Prediction I, except that caster has a 95% chance of predicting weather over a 5-day period.
WEATHER WAYS.14: Caster can adjust the temperature up or down by 18°F (10°C). Once the duration of the spell is over, the temperature will slowly adjust back to the ambient temperature (over the course of an hour).
WEATHER WAYS.15: Caster can control the speed and direction of the wind. Caster can increase or decrease wind speed by 1 mph per caster level (e.g., if the wind speed is normally 20 mph, a 15th level caster could alter the wind speed to between 5 and 35 mph). By directing the wind against missile attacks the caster can cause a -1 modification for each mph of wind speed. Note that caster can also control the direction of the flow of gases and/or clouds.
WEATHER WAYS.16: Caster can summon a dew (or frost, should the temperature be below freezing) the following morning.
WEATHER WAYS.17: Caster can control water movement. Caster can create magical barriers blocking water flow, but intangible to all else, within the limits of the spell. Caster can also control the flow of surface currents on a large body of water, providing up to 1 mph per caster level of current, which can carry boats or ships along.
WEATHER WAYS.18: Caster can make the skies clear of haze, precipitation, clouds, etc. This spell will not affect the wind.
WEATHER WAYS.19: As Weather Prediction I, except that caster has a 95% chance of predicting weather over a 30-day period.
WEATHER WAYS.20: Caster can summon rain of moderate intensity; the rain will obscure short-range vision (-25 to Perception and missile attack out to 100', -75 beyond 100').
WEATHER WAYS.25: Caster may use any of the lower level spells on this list, but with the effects covering a one mile radius.
WEATHER WAYS.30: Caster may call forth a storm of any type with maximum wind speeds equal to twice caster’s level in miles per hour, and intensity according to the caster’s desires. Effects will vary according to circumstance (and the GM’s discretion). For example, a 30th level caster could summon a lightning storm with intense rain and constant wind speeds of up to 60 mph: the GM may wish to have random rolls to determine possible lightning strikes vs. exposed targets.
WEATHER WAYS.35: Caster can cause lightning bolts to strike a region within range, with anyone in the area subject to a 25% chance of being targeted by a lightning bolt during any given round. The results are determined on the Lightning Bolt Attack Table, with the OB equal to the caster’s ranks in this list. This spell requires that there already be clouds in the sky. Any fumbles are resolved based on the clouds launching the lightning, not the caster.
WEATHER WAYS.40: Caster creates a tornado. This spell requires a cloudy sky and at least 20 mph winds. The tornado will be 15' in radius and will move 100' randomly each round that the caster does not concentrate. If the caster concentrates, he can control the movement of the tornado (up to 100' per round). The tornado is resolved on living things and structures as a Large Ram Attack with an OB equal to the caster’s ranks in this list, with an additional Grapple critical. If the tornado ever gets a 100% “grapple” on someone, it will pick him up and drop him from a height of 1-100'. The tornado will pick up and toss around small objects, raise dust and water, and the GM should call for breakage checks on any items he thinks may be threatened. Note that range is for initial formation only; tornado may travel any distance from caster once formed.
WEATHER WAYS.50: Caster may dictate the weather conditions in the area, modifying wind speeds by ±1 mph x level, and setting the level of precipitation, fog, cloud cover etc. Caster may call for shifts in the patterns as he concentrates, but the change will take at least 1 minute.

WIND LAW.1: Causes a light breeze as long as the caster concentrates. The breeze moves any gasses in the area as the caster directs, up to 20' per round.
WIND LAW.2: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty.
WIND LAW.3: By concentrating, the caster can remove most of the oxygen from the area of effect. After three rounds of concentration, all targets in the area of effect must make an RR each round in the area of effect or fall asleep (1 round per 10 failure). All fire spells in the area suffer a -20 penalty (or are 20% less effective in the case of non-attack spells).
WIND LAW.4: Creates a 5'R cloud of charged gas particles. The cloud takes one round to form, so anyone in the radius when it is cast may make a maneuver to move out of the radius without taking a critical; however, after that anyone within the radius at anytime in the round takes the critical indicated (a maximum of one per round). Following the initial formation round, the cloud delivers a C Electricity critical to all within the area of effect on the first and second rounds, a B critical on the third and fourth rounds, and an A critical on the fifth and sixth rounds, after which it dissipates. It drifts with the wind.
WIND LAW.5: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' radius.
WIND LAW.6: As Stun Cloud I, except radius is 10'.
WIND LAW.7: Creates a 5' radius of near vacuum; all within the radius take a B Impact critical as the air leaves and rushes back in (if they fail an RR).
WIND LAW.8: As Air Stop I, except radius is 20'.
WIND LAW.9: As Slumber Mist, except for the duration and that it drifts with the wind.
WIND LAW.10: As Stun Cloud I, except radius is 20'.
WIND LAW.11: As Stun Cloud I, except delivers an E critical on rounds 1 and 2, a D on rounds 3 and 4, a C on rounds 5 and 6, a B on rounds 7 and 8, and an A on rounds 9 and 10, after which it dissipates.
WIND LAW.12: As Vacuum I, except radius is 10'.
WIND LAW.13: As Air Stop I, except radius is 100'.
WIND LAW.14: A 10'R whirlwind is created, taking one round to form. It delivers an A Impact critical to all inside (every round). It requires 2 AP to enter or exit the whirlwind, and pace is limited to a walk within it and all Movement maneuvers are Very Hard at a minimum. The caster can move the whirlwind 10' per round (does not require concentration, but caster must be able to see the whirlwind).
WIND LAW.15: As Death Cloud I, except radius is 10' and range is 40'.
WIND LAW.16: Creates an area of constantly shifting winds. Any missile passing through this region suffers a -100 penalty to its OB and elemental bolt attacks suffer this penalty as well, except for Shock and Lightning Bolts. Any sailing vessel caught in these crosswinds will be unable to make any significant progress.
WIND LAW.17: As Vacuum I, except radius is 20'.
WIND LAW.18: As Vacuum I, except delivers a D Impact critical.
WIND LAW.19: As Stun Cloud IV, except by concentrating the caster can move it 10'/round up to 100' away.
WIND LAW.20: Allows the caster to change the direction of the wind within the radius.
WIND LAW.25: A hard wind comes forth from caster’s outstretched arms, fanning out to reach a 100' wide span at a distance of 200'. All within the cone receive a B Unbalancing critical.
WIND LAW.30: As Vacuum I, except radius is 40'.
WIND LAW.35: Summons forces of nature in a fierce thunderstorm which includes heavy rain, 25-60 mph winds, lightning (random) and a blizzard (if correct climate). Can be delayed up to 1 hour/level after casting.
WIND LAW.40: As Death Cloud I, except radius is 25' and range is 100'.
WIND LAW.50: Caster can use one lower level spell (on this list) each round.

WOUNDING.1: Target suffers 1d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.2: Target suffers the result of a failed Fatigue check (resulting in a -10 fatigue penalty, becoming an injury penalty if the target exceeds -50 fatigue total).
WOUNDING.3: Target will bleed at the rate of 1 hit per round.
WOUNDING.4: Target suffers 3d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.5: One of the target’s external sensory organs (eye, ear, nose, etc.) is damaged. Target will operate without that organ until healed. In addition, the target will have a -10 modification per 10 failure for all actions that partially use that organ (-100 for all actions involving that organ specifically).
WOUNDING.6: Target suffers a hairline fracture in a limb, penalty is -20.
WOUNDING.7: Target will bleed at the rate of 3 hits per round.
WOUNDING.8: Target suffers 5d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.9: As Sensory Damage I , except affects two sensory organs.
WOUNDING.11: Target suffers 7d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.12: Target suffers a -1 penalty to his Constitution bonus.
WOUNDING.13: Target suffers a simple fracture in a limb, penalty is -40.
WOUNDING.14: Target will bleed at the rate of 5 hits per round.
WOUNDING.15: Target suffers 10d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.16: Target suffers a -2 penalty to his Constitution bonus.
WOUNDING.17: Target suffers a compound fracture in a limb, penalty is -60.
WOUNDING.18: Target suffers a spasm in his arms, causing him to drop anything he is holding.
WOUNDING.19: Target suffers a -3 penalty to his Constitution bonus.
WOUNDING.20: Target suffers 15d10 concussion hits of damage.
WOUNDING.25: Target suffers a penalty to his Constitution bonus equal to -1 for every 5 levels of the caster (e.g., if the caster were 25th level, the penalty would be -5).
WOUNDING.30: Target will bleed at the rate of 1 hit per round per every 5 levels of the caster (e.g., if the caster is 30th level, then the target bleeds at a rate of six hits per round).
WOUNDING.35: Target suffers multiple fractures in a limb, total penalty is -100.
WOUNDING.40: Target suffers spinal injury, resulting in paralysis below the waist (if RR failed by 25 or less) or from the neck down (if RR is failed by 26 or more). Target immediately drops to ground and is at -50 for any activity that is still possible.
WOUNDING.50: Target loses all his hits, then takes an additional 1 hit per level of the caster. Note: All spells on this list have a range of 2'. In melee, it is presumed that you are within 2' of your opponent every round. See Section 10.12 for notes on Life Draining.   
